At the same time, the sheer volume and diversity of data in combination with a variety of data quality (DQ) assessment strategies poses challenges for data integration and cross-disciplinary utilization. We introduce a structured (modular), harmonized (comparable) and transferable (reusable) framework for evaluating geospatial data. Common DQ dimensions are integrated with new aspects of upstream validity (Metadata, Input data, Reproducibility), recognizing the importance of data provenance. Together with aspects of downstream usability (Applicability, Data constraints) this supports the integration into operational workflows. The spatial variability of dimensions is considered as key aspect influencing the utilization across space (Transferability). The flexibility of the protocol integrates the two interlinked concepts of general DQ and data adequacy and allows the assessment of the degree of FAIRness through the harmonization with FAIR metrics. Machine-evaluable measures are balanced with expert judgement, essential for contextual insights and critical reflection. Reliability-focused and suitability-oriented assessment needs are reflected in a modular, hierarchical organisation of DQ dimensions in the four domains: Descriptives, Design, Conformance and Context. This enables time-efficient assessments through (1) comparability across data of different processing levels, origin, type and context and (2) reusability of reliability-related results.

title: Enhancement of Partial Discharge Performance and Insulation Stability of XLPE/SiR Heterointerfaces in Cable Accessories by Plasma Silicon Deposition_supp1-3690139.pdf
authors: Yan Li
abstract: In cable accessories, the heterogeneous interface between cross-linked polyethylene (XLPE) and silicone rubber (SiR) is a critical weak point for partial discharge (PD). This study employs dielectric barrier discharge plasma enhanced chemical vapor deposition (DBD-PECVD) to in-situ construct a SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">y</sub>H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">z</sub> functional layer on XLPE, thereby enhancing the interfacial dielectric strength. The evolution of surface morphology, chemical composition, dielectric strength and trap distribution is investigated under different interfacial pressures. Moderate plasma treatment smooths the XLPE surface and optimizes the trap distribution, while keeping the interfacial conductivity within the insulating range of 1.2×10-<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">15</sup>–6.2×10-<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">13</sup> S/m. At an interfacial pressure of 0.4 MPa, the sample treated for 3 min exhibits the highest electrical strength among the investigated conditions. The corresponding partial discharge inception voltage (PDIV) and breakdown voltage (BDV) are 9.79 kV and 21.25 kV, respectively, representing increases of 30.88% and 37.45% compared with the unmodified interface. After thermal aging, the BDV of the plasma treated interface decreases by only 7.62%, less than that of a silicone grease coated interface. These results indicate that a solid SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">y</sub>H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">z</sub> functional interlayer can enhance the dielectric performance of XLPE/SiR interfaces and improve the retention of interfacial insulation performance under thermal aging.

title: Dual-Mode History-Dependent Attack Strategy Design for Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Xiao-Lei Wang, Chen Peng, Dakui Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of designing a dual-mode history-dependent attack strategy for cyber-physical systems (CPSs) modeled by Markov jump systems (MJSs). A crucial challenge lies in the lack of dynamic adaptability in conventional attack strategies, which rely solely on single-time instant mode information, making it difficult to strike a balance between attack effectiveness and stealthiness. To tackle this issue, virtual systems are first established to quantify the attack impacts on asynchronous observers and attack detector, integrating multi-time instant mode evolution information of both the system and observer. Unlike the existing schemes, a dual-mode history-dependent attack strategy is proposed, which exploits historical statistical characteristics of modes to enhance robustness against complex jump behaviors. Furthermore, an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H\_{-}/H\_{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> optimization framework is introduced to maximize the attack's disruptive effect on the observer's estimation performance while minimizing the risk of detection. Finally, theoretical analysis and simulation results demonstrate that the proposed attack strategy achieves superior attack effectiveness and stealthiness compared to the traditional static attack models, thereby validating its practical applicability and advantages.

title: Medical Aerosol Flow Rate Estimation by Vibrational Assessment for Smart Inhaler Development
authors: Ivan Mysovskikh, Mathew Legg, Serge Demidenko, Moi Tin Chew
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The study focuses on flow-rate detection and evaluation using acoustic vibration assessment for the development of a smart inhaler, aiming to effectively manage respiratory diseases. It highlights the importance of adhering to proper inhalation techniques and maintaining consistency in managing respiratory conditions. Methods and relevant commercial and prototype research-type devices for sensing and measuring inhalation flow rate in smart inhalers are evaluated and compared. The findings show that utilising acoustic analysis is a promising approach for detecting and assessing inhalation, ultimately improving treatment outcomes and quality of life for patients with respiratory diseases.

title: 3D measurement of precision parts in complex environments based on line structured light and deep learning
authors: Mengjie Cao, Huakun Jia, Xiangyang Wang, Zhehao Chen, Jingyu Xu, Yang Lu, Liandong Yu
abstract: Line structured light scanning is widely used for the three-dimensional (3D) measurement of precision parts, essentially relying on the optical sensing of laser stripe information by a camera. However, for metal parts, the large curvature and specular reflection of their surfaces can introduce significant noise, reducing the stability of laser stripe perception and thus hindering the accurate extraction of the laser centerline. In order to overcome these challenges, a deep learning-driven algorithm is proposed. An attention mechanism is incorporated into the original U-Net architecture, and the encoder is modified to achieve precise laser stripe segmentation. Subsequently, the region of interest (ROI) is extracted, followed by the application of the Steger algorithm for laser stripe centerline extraction, with the extracted results further refined through non-maximum suppression (NMS), interpolation, and smoothing techniques. The experimental results indicate that, under complex perception environments, a maximum standard deviation (SD) of 0.006 mm, a maximum root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.006 mm, and a maximum absolute percentage error (APE) of 0.069% are achieved by the proposed method. These findings validate the high accuracy and stability of the scheme, especially under high curvature, reflective surfaces, and strong ambient lighting conditions.

title: Performance Degradation of Monocrystalline PV Modules Under Progressive Uniform Shading Conditions: An Experimental Study
authors: Md. Sabbir Alam, Shahariar Ahmed Himo, Md. Ashraful Islam, Md. Mohi Uddin Mohin, Mst. Umme Habiba, ASM Shihavuddin, Ahmed Al Mansur
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) modules frequently experience partial shadowing in real-world installations, which significantly reduces energy production. The effect of uniform partial shadow, which covers 0%, 10%, 20%, 30%, 40%, 50%, 60%, 70%, 80%, and 90% of the module surface area, on the performance of 20W monocrystalline solar modules in outdoor conditions is investigated experimentally in this work. The panels generate 7.82©9.40 W at 10% shading but drop to 3.215.08 W at 90% shading, indicating that higher shading levels disproportionately reduce available power. The results show a significant fall in maximum power with increased shading. From 58.29% at 10% shading to almost 80% at 90%, power loss increases dramatically, with peak sensitivity between 10% and 50% shading. According to this study's results, the shadow has a greater impact on current, reducing it by almost 46% (from 0.369 A to 0.201 A). While the voltage drops by just 11% (from 23.08 V to 20.46 V), this indicates that current loss is the leading cause of the power decline. In addition to providing crucial information for solar system design, performance forecasts, and shade mitigation strategies in both urban and rural environments, this work offers quantitative insights into the consequences of uniform shadowing.

title: PFANet: A Progressive Feature Alignment Network with Local-Global Attention for Joint Classification of Hyperspectral and LiDAR Data
authors: Man Li, Yiyang Tan, Kai Xu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral image (HSI) and light detection and ranging (LiDAR) data contain complementary information for land cover classification. However, the substantial differences in imaging mechanisms often cause cross-modal feature discrepancies, leading to feature misalignment that hinders the formation of consistent joint representations and ultimately weakens fusion performance. Moreover, insufficient consideration of multiscale features results in incomplete integration, preventing the complementary information of the two modalities from being fully exploited. In addition, the severe class imbalance commonly observed in HSI-LiDAR datasets impairs the recognition of minority categories, further reducing overall classification performance. To address these issues, we propose a progressive feature alignment network (PFANet). First, a progressive alignment module is developed to mitigate feature misalignment through semantic distribution calibration and spatial fine registration. Then, a window interval cross-attention module integrates local window attention and interval-based sparse global attention. It jointly captures multiscale spectral-spatial details and elevation information, producing more discriminative fusion features. Finally, a dynamic class-balanced loss is introduced to adaptively enhance minority-class learning and improve robustness under class-imbalanced conditions. Experimental results on three benchmark datasets demonstrate that PFANet outperforms other advanced methods. It achieves overall accuracies of 92.84% on Houston2013, 99.58% on Trento, and 97.50% on Augsburg, and exhibits clear advantages in minority-class performance, thereby confirming its stable performance on three HSI-LiDAR benchmarks and stronger performance on few-shot classes.

title: 1 Transistor-Dynamic Random Access Memory as Reservoir for Temporal Signal Processing
authors: MD Yasir Bashir, C Lalith Adithya Sai, Shubham Sahay
publish: IEEE Journal on Exploratory Solid-State Computational Devices and Circuits
abstract: Reservoir computing (RC), a computational paradigm inspired by the recurrent neural networks, offers a promising framework for efficient temporal processing with minimal training overhead. Hardware implementation of RC primitive requires devices that exhibit short-term memory, nonlinearity, and energy-efficient state-switching dynamics. While emerging non-volatile memory (eNVM) technologies have been explored as physical reservoirs, their limited endurance and wafer scale fabrication challenges hinder widespread application for real-time edge-compatible RC systems. In contrast, 1-transistor dynamic random-access memories (1T-DRAMs) have recently gained significant attention owing to their high scalability, low footprint, and high endurance. In this work, we propose the use of 1T-DRAMs as physical reservoirs for computing and temporal signal processing. We show that the inherent charge storage and its temporal decay in the body of 1T-DRAMs owing to the dynamic recombination efficiently emulates the fading (short-term) memory behavior, making it well-suited for reservoir computing. Furthermore, networks of the 1T-DRAM physical reservoirs achieve classification accuracy of 82.46% and 85.02% on reduced MNIST and Weizmann human action dataset, respectively.

title: VL-BrainNet: A VGG16–BiLSTM-Based Framework for Accelerated Brain Tumor Diagnosis
authors: S M Masfequier Rahman Swapno, Md Basitur Rahman Bappi, A B M Shawkat Ali
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Deep Learning (DL)-based automated systems have emerged as powerful tools for medical image analysis. However, existing models often face limitations when applied to brain tumor classification because the irregular organ shapes, varying sizes, overlapping regions, and low-contrast boundaries significantly affect the performance and generalizability of current classification techniques. To address this challenge, we propose VL-BrainNet, an advanced VGG16 and BiLSTM-based classification network for brain tumor. Here, we introduce an advanced architecture that combines VGG16 for robust spatial feature extraction with BiLSTM for bidirectional context modelling (e.g., across slices/feature sequences), yielding richer, more separable representations and stronger generalization. Using the Kaggle dataset (7,023 images), VL-BrainNet is trained, validated, and tested <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{8 0 \% - 1 0 \% - 1 0 \%}$</tex> split) to classify the four major brain tumors (glioma, meningioma, pituitary, and normal), achieving substantially higher training performance of 99.93% and validation accuracy of 98.67%. Moreover, VL-BrainNet achieved a test accuracy of 98.19% in 5-fold cross-validation. The model also outperformed state-of-the-art methods in terms of higher precision, recall, and F1 scores, reflecting its robust classification quality. Thus, VL-BrainNet has substantial practical implications for the reliable delineation of brain organs in clinical workflows (e.g., radiotherapy planning), thereby contributing to improved healthcare outcomes in the management of brain tumor detection.

title: An Efficient Deep Learning Method for Malware Detection Through Visual Representation
authors: Sourav Adhikary, Kaushik Deb
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Traditional signature-based methods are often ineffective against modern, evolving malware, creating a need for more adaptive detection techniques. While converting malware binaries into images for deep learning analysis is a promising alternative, this approach faces significant challenges, including a lack of consensus on the best image conversion methods and the high computational cost of many models. This study addresses these issues through a systematic evaluation of multiple deep learning architectures and visual representations to identify an optimal configuration for accuracy and efficiency, with interpretability analyzed using Grad-CAM. Our findings show that visual representation is the most critical factor, with entropy-based images consistently providing the most effective features for classification. We propose a hybrid CNN-BiGRU model that, after a fine-tuning process, achieves a state-of-the-art F1-Score of 0.9903 and 99.0 % accuracy on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$224 \times 224$</tex> entropy images. Furthermore, we identify DenseNet121 as a highly efficient alternative for resource-constrained environments, while also providing interpretable insights into model decisions. By analyzing the interplay of representation, architecture, and resolution, this work provides a clear, empirically-grounded framework for building the next generation of transparent and effective malware classifiers.

title: Generation of brain PET synaptic density imaging from MRI and FDG-PET using a 3D Multi-stage Residual U-Net
authors: Xinyuan Zheng, Patrick Worhunsky, Takuya Toyonaga, Qiong Liu, Xueqi Guo, Yinchi Zhou, Xiongchao Chen, Bo Zhou, Adam P. Mecca, Ming-Kai Chen, Ryan S. O’Dell, Christopher H. Van Dyck, Richard E. Carson, Rajiv Radhakrishnan, Chi Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radiation and Plasma Medical Sciences
abstract: Synaptic vesicle glycoprotein 2A (SV2A) in human brains is an important biomarker of synaptic loss associated with several disorders, including Alzheimer's disease (AD). However, SV2A tracers, such as [<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup>C]UCB-J, are less available in clinical practice due to constraints that include cost and the need for an onsite cyclotron. As a result, there is interest in generating synthetic SV2A PET images using a more commonly used tracer, or another widely available image modality. We therefore investigated the feasibility of using a deep learning approach to generate synthetic [<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup>C]UCB-J SV2A PET images from T1-weighted (T1w) Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) and/or [<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F]FDG PET imaging. In our study, we used paired data from 54 participants for training and evaluation, including 22 healthy controls (HC) and 32 AD patients. We implemented two architectures, 3D UNet and 3D Multi-stage ResUNet (MS ResUNet) for translating either MRI or [<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F]FDG PET or both into [<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup>C]UCB-J PET images. Our experiments show that, through progressive learning 3D MS ResUNet achieved less bias compared to a single-stage design. Adopting residual connection, cross-stage feature fusion and supervised attention strategies to our model contributes to better gradient flow, mitigates information loss and enables selective focus, leading to better generation results. Additionally, combining MRI T1w and [<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F]FDG PET images provides the best prediction performance, whereas having [<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F]FDG PET alone produces better synthetic images than using MRI alone. We also evaluated our model’s performance for 14 typical brain regions. The MS ResUNet achieved less than 5% bias in all regions. Furthermore, the differences between AD and HC in certain regions (e.g., hippocampus and entorhinal) are still significant in generated images (p=0.000). In conclusion, the 3D MS ResUNet was able to generate desirable SV2A PET images that are visually consistent with true images in terms of contrast and tracer distribution, quantitatively with low bias (-0.26% ± 5.56%) and high similarity (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.975), for both AD and HC. Compared with the 3D vanilla UNet (FDG: −2.39% ± 5.55%, MRI: −5.03% ± 13.24%, or both: −1.89% ± 6.04%), the modified design produced more robust SV2A images. The synthetic images can potentially provide additional pathological insights into AD and other neurological disorders without increasing radiation exposure and cost.

title: Dynamic Event-Triggered Robust Switching Control for Path Tracking of Electric Semi-Trailer Trucks in Varying-Curvature Paths
authors: Yujie Liu, Ming Yue, Hongbo Song, Xudong Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: To enable electric semi-trailer trucks (STTs) to adapt to path-tracking scenarios with varying-curvature, this article proposes a dynamic event-triggered mechanism (DETM)-based robust switching control method to enhance the overall tracking accuracy and stability. First, the vehicle’s nonlinear dynamics model, brush tire model, and preview dynamics model are augmented into a coupled lateral dynamics model, which is linearized and then transformed into a linear parameter varying (LPV) model with time-varying motion state and tire parameters. Second, a robust switching controller is developed, incorporating two steering modes: a tractor-trailer coordinated steering system and a tractor independent steering system. Simultaneously, a DETM is introduced between the sensors and the controller to conserve communication resources. Then, considering the stability and robustness of the DETM-based robust switching controller against linearization errors, model uncertainties, environmental disturbances, DETM, and mode switching, a codesign criterion for the DETM and controller is established using Lyapunov stability theory, linear matrix inequality (LMI) techniques, and average dwell time (ADT) theory. Finally, hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) tests under comprehensive driving conditions validate the effectiveness and reliability of the proposed method.

title: Retraction Notice: Synergistic Integration of Lead Level, EEG, and Oculomotor Analysis for Advanced ADHD Diagnosis through Machine Learning
authors: Ganesh Arbad, Komal M. Mungekar, Tushar Kuvalekar, Bhagwat Zunzurwar, Nikita Gore
publish: 2024 Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The paper explores the integration of Electroen cephalography( EEG) and advanced machine literacy ways for ADHD discovery. pressing the significance of early identi fication, the study aims to upgrade EEG signal channels and point birth to ameliorate delicacy. Challenges like computational complexity and overfitting are addressed. The exploration aims to give perceptivity into ADHD neural autographs, potentially transubstantiating individual and remedial approaches in internal health.

title: Multimodal Workload Analysis for Developer Burnout Prediction: Integrating Jira and GitHub Activity Data
authors: AI Mohamed Sabeeb, JD Tenushi Erandi, WV Chandima Maduwanthi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Multimodal workload analysis has become extremely important for understanding developer burnout, especially in Jira/GitHub-based software projects to enhance productivity and maintain developer well-being. Developer activity data collected from issue-tracking and version-control platforms such as Jira and GitHub contain rich indicators of burnout, including workload, commit activity, temporal work patterns, and collaboration dynamics. However, existing approaches do not analyze the cumulative effect of assigned tasks and actual development because they solely rely on traditional survey-based methods or analyze single data sources, such as Jira or GitHub in isolation, for burnout prediction. This study addresses this limitation by proposing a proactive, all-inone, data-driven framework that combines task management and issue-tracking data from Jira and code contribution activity from GitHub to predict burnout among software developers. The study extracts multi-modal metrics from the Mozilla-Apache dataset, which consists of issue-tracking and versioncontrol data collected from the Jira and GitHub platforms respectively. Several machine learning models were trained and evaluated for predicting developer burnout. Among all the trained models, Artificial Neural Network (ANN) revealed superior predictive performance, obtaining an accuracy of 98.48% along with a F1-score of 97.41%. Moreover, Spearman correlation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\rho)$</tex> analysis revealed the key predictors with the strongest positive associations to burnout risk including commit count, total lines changed, issues handled, unique components worked on, and commits made during off-work hours. The study also presents a dashboard prototype integrated with the best-performing model (ANN), along with a natural language interface that assists in monitoring burnout risk.

title: Low-Thermal-Budget (300°C) HZO Ferroelectrics with Improved Ferroelectricity and Endurance via Process-Material Co-optimization
authors: Weiqin Huang, Yuejia Zhou, Runteng Zhu, Ru Huang, Kechao Tang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The integration of hafnium oxide-based ferroelectrics into back-end-of-line (BEOL) processes is constrained by the required crystallization thermal budget. This work investigates the interplay between deposition and annealing thermal budgets, revealing a critical trade-off that prevents reference 1:1 HZO from achieving high ferroelectricity at low temperatures. This low-temperature limitation is overcome through a process-material co-optimization strategy. This approach, which combines tuning of the ALD temperature (process) and the Zr concentration (material), enables the fabrication of devices at an ultra-low 300°C that simultaneously achieve improved ferroelectricity (34.1 μC/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) and high endurance (>1×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">10</sup> cycles). This study provides a viable process map for fabricating reliable, BEOL-compatible ferroelectric memories.

title: Hybrid Solar PV and Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting for Smart Roads
authors: G. S. K. Godawela, G. G. G. M. Dilruk, S. K. Jaslin, A. I. S. Juhaniya, M. G. N. D. Weerarathna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Modern road infrastructure requires reliable and sustainable power for applications such as street lighting, traffic signaling, surveillance, and electric vehicle charging. In Sri Lanka, heavy dependence on the national grid results in frequent power interruptions and increased operational costs, highlighting the need for decentralized energy solutions. This paper proposes a hybrid smart road energy system integrating solar photovoltaic (PV) generation with piezoelectric energy harvesting. Bifacial solar PV panels are installed as roof structures along pedestrian walkways, while drum-type piezoelectric tiles are embedded beneath a 1 km road segment to harvest vehicleinduced mechanical energy. The system is modeled and analyzed using MATLAB/Simulink, incorporating intelligent energy management, battery storage, and load-priority control. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate stable DC bus regulation, reliable power delivery to critical loads, and nearly 100 % renewable energy penetration under normal operating conditions. The proposed system generates significant annual energy savings while reducing grid dependency and carbon emissions, confirming its suitability for sustainable smart road infrastructure in tropical regions.

title: Examining the Influence of ChatGPT on Programming Courses in Higher Education
authors: Jonel B. Prado, Thelma D. Palaoag
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: ChatGPT is a Generative Artificial Intelligence (GAI), a large language model that is rapidly reshaping programming education by providing on-demand explanations, code generation, and debugging support. While the potential is immense, empirical evidence on its instructional influence remains fragmented and raises concerns about reliability, over-reliance, and academic integrity. Thus, this study examined ChatGPT’s influence on programming courses in higher education using a sequential exploratory mixed methods design. Phase 1 employed a PRISMA-guided meta-synthesis of empirical studies published from 2022 to 2025, resulting in 20 included studies that were thematically synthesized to identify convergent patterns of integration, limitations, experiences, and instructional implications. Phase 2 administered a theme-derived questionnaire to 440 undergraduate programming students in the Philippines and analyzed responses using descriptive statistics and Pearson correlations. Results indicate moderately high student engagement with ChatGPT in programming learning. Meanwhile, correlation findings show that ChatGPT-assisted practices were strongly and positively associated with perceived learning outcomes, with the strongest association observed for AI literacy and evaluation skills, followed by problem-solving influence and integration in practice. The integrated findings suggest that ChatGPT’s educational value in programming courses depends less on mere usage and more on students’ critical verification behaviors and ethical awareness, supporting the need for explicit AI literacy instruction and assessment redesign toward reasoning and validation.

title: Analysis of the Impact of Service Quality Factors on Customer Satisfaction: A Study of Sri Lankan Freight Forwarding Selection
authors: Thanusha Withana, Chathumi Kavirathna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: This study aims to analyze the impact of service quality factors on customer satisfaction in the selection of sea and air freight forwarders in Sri Lanka, a sector that plays a vital role in supporting the country's import and export performance. The research addresses the problem of fragmented service quality criteria and the absence of a consolidated, context specific framework for evaluating freight forwarders in the Sri Lankan logistics industry. A mixed method research design was adopted, employing a systematic literature review guided by the PRISMA methodology to synthesize evidence from fifty two peer reviewed studies. The reviewed literature was analyzed across three dimensions chronological trends, regional focus, and industry specific applications. The study identifies several critical service quality factors influencing customer satisfaction, including cost effectiveness, reliability, responsiveness, tangibility, timeliness, and operational efficiency. The findings reveal that cost and operational efficiency remain the most influential determinants of customer satisfaction in freight forwarder selection, while technological capabilities and sustainability related practices are gaining increasing importance. The study concludes that enhancing service quality performance across these key dimensions can improve customer satisfaction and operational efficiency competitiveness of Sri Lankan freight forwarders, offering practical implications for industry stakeholders and future research directions.

title: CGP Scaled 3D 2T0C DRAM with Record Write Speed of 400 ps and High Temperature Stability
authors: Tianyue Fu, Qianlan Hu, Xiong Xiong, Qijun Li, Wenjie Zhao, Shiyuan Liu, Yanqing Wu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: As data-intensive applications, particularly in artificial intelligence (AI), demand ever-increasing high-density and high-speed memory, three-dimensional (3D) integration has emerged as a crucial solution. In this work, we present a high-density, 3D 2T0C DRAM architecture based on amorphous oxide semiconductor (AOS) transistors. The device is fabricated using a comprehensive back-end-of-line (BEOL) compatible process with a low thermal budget of less than 300 °C. We demonstrate aggressive contact-gate-pitch (CGP) scaling to 140 nm and successfully stack two AOS transistor layers to form the 3D structure. The fabricated 3D DRAM cell exhibits robust thermal stability, with excellent data retention performance up to 125 °C. Furthermore, the device achieves a record-fast write speed of 400 ps, highlighting its superior performance. These results provide a comprehensive path toward future ultra-high-density and high-speed memory technologies.

title: Readiness Dimensions for Digital Twin Adoption and Process Optimization in Sri Lanka's Large-Scale Apparel Sector
authors: Dulanga Thennakoon, Chalani Oruthotaarachchi, Amila Withanaarachchi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Industry 4.0 positions Digital Twin (DT) technology as critical for manufacturing efficiency through real-time virtual replicas, enabling process optimization. This study examines how organizational readiness dimensions for Digital Twin adoption predict process optimization outcomes in Sri Lanka's large-scale apparel manufacturing sector, which faces substantial barriers to digital transformation despite contributing significantly to the national GDP. Using data from 179 industry professionals analyzed through PLS-SEM, the research model examined four readiness dimensions: Technological Infrastructure, Organizational Capability, Workforce Competency, and Financial Resources, alongside environmental moderators including Market and Competitive Pressure and Government Support. Results reveal that Financial Resources and Technological Infrastructure significantly predict process optimization, while Market Pressure critically moderates the Organizational Capability relationship, demonstrating that competitive intensity activates organizational capabilities for performance gains. Government support shows limited influence, revealing institutional gaps. The model explains substantial variance in process optimization outcomes. Findings emphasize that financial capacity and technological infrastructure drive DT adoption with competition-dependent organizational effects. Recommendations include prioritizing strategic technology investments, strengthening adaptive organizational structures, and implementing targeted policy interventions to address sector-specific barriers to digital transformation in emerging economies.

title:
Influence of Oxidation Temperature on the Electrical and Structural Properties of ZrO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    film on 4H-SiC: An SEM, EDX, CV and IV Investigation
authors: Nurliyana Abu Hasan Sazalli, Roslina Ahmad, Wong Yew Hoong
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This study examines the influence of thermal oxidation temperature on the structural and electrical properties of zirconium dioxide (ZrO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) thin films deposited on 4H-SiC substrates by RF magnetron sputtering. The as-deposited Zr films were oxidized in N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O ambient at 550℃, 600℃, and 700℃ for 15 minutes followed by SEM, EDX, C-V, and I-V analyses. Films oxidized at 600℃ showed the most uniform surface morphology with improved grain coalescence and complete oxidation. This sample exhibited the highest accumulation capacitance (5341 pF) and stable dielectric behavior with minimal frequency dispersion. In contrast, 550℃ resulted in incomplete oxidation, while 700℃ increased leakage due to grain boundary conduction. Overall, 600℃ provided the best balance of dielectric quality and reliability, making it an optimal condition for ZrO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/4H-SiC MOS capacitor fabrication.

title:
Influences of Voltage Cycling on Variations in HfO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -based 3D Trench FeRAM
authors: Weiwen Pan, Fei Yu, Peiyuan Du, Wanmei Zhang, Yutai Man, Xingcheng Jin, Chongyong Guo, Hongbo Li, Huan Liu, Bing Chen, Ran Cheng, Yan Liu, Xiao Yu, Genquan Han
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, we experimentally investigated the variation behavior of HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based 3D trench ferroelectric random access memory (FeRAM) under different voltage cycling conditions. The remanent polarization (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf>) and coercive field (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf>) were extracted to evaluate device-to-device (D2D) and cycle-to-cycle (C2C) variations. The planar-dominated capacitors showed a gradual decrease in variation when the cycling voltage and number increased. In contrast, the trench-dominated capacitors showed irregular changes during cycling. To understand the cause, cross-sectional transmission electron microscopy (TEM) and energy-dispersive spectroscopy (EDS) analyses were performed. The results revealed that the ferroelectric layer at the trench sidewall was thinner and suffered from strong interdiffusion between the electrode and ferroelectric materials. These structural and compositional differences degrade the ferroelectric quality and lead to larger variation in trench-dominated devices. This work clarifies the relationship between cycling behavior and variability in 3D FeRAMs and provides guidance for improving their reliability through interface and material engineering.

title: Reversible Jump MCMC for Neural Network Selection: A Comparative Study Against Information Criteria
authors: Faisal Islam, Aciful Islam Khan, Nushrat Tarmin Meem, Partha Jyoti Roy
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Selecting an appropriate architecture for neural networks in regression problems is an easy but challenging problem, as model complexity directly affects the prediction performance and generalizability. Traditional selection methods like AIC and BIC tend to provide uninformative suggestions, prefer smaller formulas and do not attempt to address model uncertainty. In this respect, to handle these limitations, employment of a Bayesian method with Reversible Jump Markov Chain Monte Carlo (RJMCMC) has been proposed, where the architecture and predictive uncertainty are selected and estimated in a principled way. In the case of Boston Housing dataset, this method provided the intuitive result of a unimodal posterior distribution on network structures, with the most likely structure being that of thirty-nine hidden neurons. This result is diametrically opposite to less complicated architectures that are preferred by information criteria. Moreover, Bayesian model averaging will give well-calibrated and interpretable estimates of the uncertainties that were not available in standard approaches. Even though one of the baseline models had a smaller Root Mean Square Error, it was not as robust and uncertainty measurable than Bayesian approach. This data shows that Bayesian methods with MCMC are a robust and comprehensive model of neural network structure selection with better interpretability and reasonable uncertainty estimation needed for real-world applications.

title: In-pixel Capacitive Droplet Sensing Circuit with Dual-electrode Design for Active-matrix Digital Microfluidics
authors: Shengzhe Jiang, Dongping Wang, Arokia Nathan, Jun Yu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Stable droplet manipulations in large-array active-matrix digital microfluidics requires real-time droplet position feedback. This paper proposes a novel droplet position sensing circuit for low-temperature polysilicon-based active-matrix digital microfluidics. A dual-electrode design is adopted to eliminate the droplet disturbance existed in conventional sensing circuits. Dual-gate thin-film transistors are employed to enhance the high-voltage stability. An amplifier is integrated at the output, producing sensing signals of approximately 0.88 V for silicone oil and 24.27 V for deionized water; the maximum output variance exceeds 23 V, outperforming all previously reported work. This design offers a new approach for precise droplet localization in active-matrix digital microfluidics.

title: Ultrafast Optical Linear and Nonlinear Response of Two-Dimensional CrOCl Materials and Applications
authors: Gaochen Yang, Wei Ju, Langlang Xu, Xinyu Huang, Wenhao Shi, Zili Li, Xiao He, Xiaohan Meng, Shengjie Lv, Mingjun Sun, Ping Wang, Lei Tong, Lei Ye
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Spatial optical computing is one of the promising solutions for achieving high-performance neural network applications. Despite significant progress in spatial optical linear computation, the heterogeneous integration of linear and nonlinear computations has limited computational efficiency. In this work, we propose a spatial optical computing scheme using femtosecond pump-probe laser excitation of low-symmetry 2D material CrOCl. Based on the crystal orientation-dependent ultrafast optical response, we regulate the changes in linear and nonlinear transmittance to realize reconfigurable integration of both linear and nonlinear computations. This demonstrates the potential application of CrOCl material in ultrafast optical spatial computing.

title: Optimizing Efficiency of Perovskite Solar Cells Using Neural Network with MWCNT-Based HTL
authors: Ihtesham Ibn Malek, Hafiz Imtiaz, Samia Subrina
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study introduces a machine learning-based approach to improve the efficiency of perovskite solar cells (PSCs) by optimizing layer parameters and incorporating multi-walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs) as the hole transport layer (HTL). SCAPS-1D simulations of PSCs with an MWCNT-based HTL yield a power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 14.39 %, closely corresponding to experimental results. We analyze the impact of different fabrication factors on efficiency, including the defect density of the absorber, thickness variations in the HTL, active region, and electron transport layer (ETL), as well as doping densities in both transport layers. A dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> samples is generated using SCAPS-1D to train machine learning models for PCE prediction, with the artificial neural network (ANN) achieving the best performance (RMSE: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0439 \%, \mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex>: 0.9999). Using the derived ANN function as an objective function in a genetic algorithm (GA), we further optimize the PCE, reaching a maximum of 23.28 %, demonstrating an effective approach to enhancing PSC efficiency.

title: Hold the Prompt (HODPOT): Defense Design for Securing the System Prompt of LLM Agents
authors: Lilian Tai Do Khac, Marc Fabian Mezger, Daniel Thürck
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Since the public release of ChatGPT, generative AI (GenAI) models have become increasingly functional and autonomous, spurring their integration into a wide range of products. This integration introduces substantial security risks, most notably prompt-injection attacks that can manipulate model outputs and expose proprietary data. A less-studied variant—prompt-theft, where an adversary who fully controls the user prompt extracts the system prompt or a semantically equivalent paraphrase—remains viable even against state-of-the-art models. We formalise this threat under an attacker model that can issue arbitrary user inputs and observe the model’s responses. Our defense is a lightweight post-inference similarity filter: each generated output is compared using text similarity measures with a calibrated threshold that determines whether the response is returned unchanged, sanitized, or blocked. Experiments on eight LLMs and two “guard”-type models show that the filter reduces successful prompt extraction two-third of occurences while preserving above $90 \%$ of benign responses. These findings, given the the increasing usage of fully-autonomous GenAI agents, re-highlight prompt theft as a pressing research problem and motivate further work on adaptive thresholds, multi-modal prompts, and compositional leakage.

title: CALM: Cost-efficient, model Agnostic, Low-latency Modular LLM Deployment framework
authors: Ashish Bansal, Deepkamal Kaur Gill, Rajeev Joshi, Hung Pham, Lee Zamparo, Wenxuan Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: The rapid evolution of deep learning and generative AI has positioned large language models (LLMs) as critical enablers of innovation across industries. This paper underscores the critical importance of architecting optimized LLM deployment strategies that not only meet stringent service-level agreements (SLAs) but also enable faster time to market and meet return-on-investment expectations for enterprises through reduced inference costs. We identify three core challenges faced by AI practitioners: (1) the prohibitive and often unsustainable costs of leveraging thirdparty hosted LLMs for advanced applications such as customer service automation, (2) the need to optimize deployments for specific use cases to meet dynamic SLA requirements, and (3) ensuring always-on production reliability without compromising scalability or development velocity. To address these issues, we introduce CALM - a purpose-built, cost-aware deployment methodology that integrates dynamic autoscaling, workload-aware dedicated tenancy, and intelligent resource orchestration. Our system supports a local development and deployment model that simplifies the end-to-end lifecycle while enabling efficient hardware utilization and robust production performance. Empirical results show inference cost reductions of up to 5x compared to managed hosted solutions in high-load environments. Finally, we provide practical guidelines and deployment patterns that enable AI teams to build and operate scalable, performant, and cost-optimized LLM applications—accelerating their ability to deliver value in production environments.

title: Temporal Personal Fashion AI System with Multi-Component Integration
authors: Raveesha Harshani, Navodya Manamendra, Pabasara Rajapaksha, Shyamantha Godaarawa, Buddika Harshanath
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The rapid growth of e-commerce has reshaped fashion retail but left gaps in personalized sizing, styling, and grooming solutions. This study presents an integrated, AIpowered ecosystem that addresses these challenges through four interoperable, cloud-based modules. First, wardrobe intelligence uses LSTM networks with multi-head attention to track personal style trends over time and recommends sustainable, wardrobe-centric choices, outperforming standard prediction methods. Second, accurate anthropometric measurements are extracted from dual-view images using EfficientNet-B3, enabling privacy-focused and precise body sizing across brands. Third, occasion-based accessory recommendations are generated by combining ResNet-50 for clothing attribute extraction, Transformer-based cultural modeling, and reinforcement learning to adapt to user feedback. Fourth, specialized CNNs offer comprehensive grooming advice—analyzing facial shape, skin, dental, hair, and nail health—validated by expert professionals. The ecosystem leverages large, diverse datasets and federated learning to ensure scalability and user privacy, resulting in notable reductions in return rates, measurable user savings, and high satisfaction across large deployments.

title: Dual-Loss Trained Compact Cascaded ANN for Custom-Range BSIM-CMG Parameter Extraction
authors: Sidhant V Vazarkar, Abdullah M. H Cachhi, Shreyan Ghosh, Girish Pahwa
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper presents a novel deep learning framework that combines dual-loss training with floating normalization for BSIM-CMG model parameter extraction. The framework addresses the fundamental challenge of non-unique parameter-to-characteristic mappings through a cascaded forward-inverse neural network architecture trained with both parameter supervision and curve reconstruction constraints. A flexible floating normalization scheme enables user-defined extraction ranges, allowing multiple valid parameter sets to be extracted from identical I-V characteristics. The dual-loss training mechanism provides superior convergence by simultaneously enforcing parameter accuracy and forward-inverse consistency. Validated with 200 K Monte Carlo samples across 3 nm, 7 nm, and 14 nm FinFET technology nodes, the framework achieves RMSE below 3.75% while offering unprecedented flexibility compared to conventional fixed-range extraction methods.

title: BizQuery: Agentic Financial Question Answering over Structured Data
authors: Akash Mondal, Sourav Karmakar, Shrutendra Harsola, Abhishek Yadav, Vignesh Subrahmaniam, Reeti Sarup
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Financial decision-makers such as CFOs, business owners, and analysts often pose high-level, ambiguous questions that require multi-step data analysis and synthesis. Existing structured-data question answering systems can handle straightforward financial queries but struggle with complex analytical tasks due to limited query decomposition, multi-hop reasoning, and narrative report generation. We introduce BizQuery, a generalizable, iterative agentic framework that orchestrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with a structured data interface to answer complex financial inquiries. BizQuery decomposes high-level questions into actionable sub-questions, builds a knowledge base via parallelized querying, and iteratively tests its sufficiency using a hybrid of LLM-based reflection and embedding-based semantic checks. A secure data analysis sub-agent performs multi-table reasoning through a declarative JSON specification, improving transparency and avoiding code execution risks. BizQuery then synthesizes its findings into coherent, data-grounded narrative reports that users can iteratively refine through natural language feedback. We propose a complementary evaluation framework that measures both sub-question quality and final report accuracy using LLM judges, semantic similarity metrics, and hallucination checks. Across multiple real-world financial datasets, BizQuery achieves consistent improvements over strong baselines in factual accuracy, analytical depth, and report quality, suggesting its potential as a scalable solution for advanced financial analysis.

title: LandChain: A Blockchain-Powered Land Marketplace Using Hyperledger Fabric and IPFS
authors: Israt Jahan Anika, Masnin Juairia, Md. Sadique Mahmud, Md. Aminur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Property transactions in the majority of jurisdictions remain reliant on scattered records, hand-verified and paper files that are slow to validate and easy to dispute. Users cannot easily check title, boundaries, or counterparty authenticity; agencies have duplicate files and conflicting identities and third-party middlemen introduce expense and complexity. We present LandChain, a Blockchain-Powered Land Marketplace which makes each step of a property transaction verifiable while preserving current offline legal processes. Users are authenticated with an X. 509 certificate issued by a Fabric CA which is linked with their national ID; the permissioned ledger stores immutable ownership and event history and government-approved files are anchored in IPFS with hashes secured on-chain. From the moment when a user first transacts on the land's advertisement web page to when the owner-name is finally updated, the system includes: identity verification, seller-buyer direct communication, offline deed signing, auto-authentication and user profile updates in ledger. LandChain reduces intermediaries and builds trust through open provenance. The result is a secure, auditable and human-centered way of land transfer.

title: LLM is Not All You Need: A Systematic Evaluation of ML vs. Foundation Models for text and image based Medical Classification
authors: Meet Raval, Tejul Pandit, Dhvani Upadhyay
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: The combination of multimodal Vision-Language Models (VLMs) and Large Language Models (LLMs) opens up new possibilities for medical classification. This work offers a rigorous, unified benchmark by using four publicly available datasets covering text and image modalities (binary and multiclass complexity) that contrasts traditional Machine Learning (ML) with contemporary transformer-based techniques. We evaluated three model classes for each task: Classical ML (LR, LightGBM, ResNet-50), Prompt-Based LLMs/VLMs (Gemini 2.5), and Fine-Tuned PEFT Models (LoRA-adapted Gemma3 variants). All experiments used consistent data splits and aligned metrics. According to our results, traditional machine learning (ML) models set a high standard by consistently achieving the best overall performance across most medical categorization tasks. This was especially true for structured text-based datasets, where the classical models performed exceptionally well. In stark contrast, the LoRA-tuned Gemma variants consistently showed the worst performance across all text and image experiments, failing to generalize from the minimal fine-tuning provided. However, the zero-shot LLM/VLM pipelines (Gemini 2.5) had mixed results; they performed poorly on text-based tasks, but demonstrated competitive performance on the multiclass image task, matching the classical ResNet-50 baseline. These results demonstrate that in many medical categorization scenarios, established machine learning models continue to be the most reliable option. The experiment suggests that foundation models are not universally superior and that the effectiveness of Parameter-Efficient FineTuning (PEFT) is highly dependent on the adaptation strategy, as minimal fine-tuning proved detrimental in this study.

title: DenseNet201-Based Deep Learning Approach for Automated Classification of Peanut Plant Leaf Diseases
authors: Boyapati Bharathi, Premkumar Dhanasekar, S. Kaliappan, Avani Mittal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: In this work, an auto system for classification of peanut plant leaf disease based on the DenseNet201 deep learning model is introduced. The research utilizes a large dataset of 822 images, divided into three equally distributed classes: Normal Leaf, Diseased Leaf, and Dead Leaf. By using transfer learning, designing dense layers specific to the problem, and applying careful dropout, the new model performs optimally. The dataset was then pre-processed in a systematic way; resized to 224x224 pixels, normalized, and augmented to make the model more robust. Training for 15 epochs with dynamic learning rate management achieved a very good accuracy of 92%. It achieved high precision and recall scores on all higher classes, with scores above 0.90. Specifically, the system reported ideal classification for Normal Leaf samples and robust accuracy in classifying Dead and Diseased classes. Confusion matrix analysis results reported negligible misclassification patterns, such as between the Dead and Diseased classes. This study provides a trustworthy automated disease detection tool for peanut farming that can potentially increase the efficiency of crop monitoring and, hence, aid in timely intervention strategies.

title: Enhanced Stock Trend Prediction Through Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Sheik Rubyeat Ahmed, Fayzul Bari Shikder, Jaheen Md. Abrar Khan, Sheik Erfan Ahmed, Md. Tanvir Hayat
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The stock market's price movements are highly unpredictable, influenced by a wide range of fundamental and technical variables. Despite this complexity, accurately forecasting stock trends remains crucial for investors seeking to make informed and strategic financial decisions. Reliable trend prediction supports the development of robust portfolio management strategies, improves asset allocation, and strengthens risk management practices. This research presents a customized artificial neural network (ANN) specifically designed to capture the intricate patterns and nonlinear dependencies present in stock market data. To ensure a comprehensive assessment of the proposed ANN model, its performance is compared against several wellestablished machine learning models, including Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and XGBoost. These benchmark models are widely recognized for their strong predictive capabilities in various data-driven tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed ANN achieves a notable accuracy of 97.08%, outperforming competing models in stock trend prediction. This study contributes to the growing body of research on machine learning applications in finance and underscores the potential of advanced neural network architectures for enhancing stock market forecasting accuracy.

title: An Adaptive Security Model Based on User Behavior Analysis for Enhancing Protection Against Cyber Attacks
authors: Mohammed Elrasheed Mohammed Salih, Adil Bushra Yahya Yousif, Siddiq Babikir, Osama El Abdein, Ahmed Arbab
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: With cyberattacks increasingly exploiting human vulnerabilities, traditional security systems based on static rules fail to adapt to individual user behaviors. These systems treat all users the same, overlooking differences in awareness, roles, and usage patterns. This research presents an adaptive security model that analyzes user behavior in real-time using artificial intelligence (AI). The model classifies users by risk level and dynamically applies tailored security responses, such as alerts or access limitations. By integrating behavioral science and machine learning, the system proactively identifies anomalies and adjusts defenses accordingly. Evaluated in a simulated environment, the model reduced successful attacks by 42%, cut false alarms by 68%, and responded to threats in under 3.2 hours. It proved capable of adapting to changing user patterns within minutes, enhancing both security and user experience. This behavior-aware approach represents a significant step toward intelligent, personalized cybersecurity systems that protect against modern, human centered threats..

title: Condition Number Analysis for MIMO-OTFS Communication Systems
authors: Zheng Wang, Bodong Shang
abstract: Orthogonal Time-Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation is an innovative modulation technique that operates in the two-dimensional delay-Doppler (DD) domain. It is specifically designed for high Doppler scenarios, where the channel can be transformed into an almost non-fading channel for DD domain symbol transmission. In multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) OTFS systems, the DD domain input-output relation has considerable complexity, especially under fractional delays and Dopplers. The condition number is a key indicator of channel matrix quality as it reflects the sensitivity to noise and disturbance. In this paper, a novel Zak-OTFS modulation in the DD domain has been proposed as a competitive alternative to the conventional multi-carrier (MC) OTFS scheme, where MC-OTFS is implemented in two steps in the time-frequency (TF) domain. We focus on both MIMO-Zak-OTFS and MIMO-MC-OTFS schemes and study the condition numbers for the residual error covariance matrix and the channel matrix. We investigate the condition number under various system parameters, and the results indicate that OTFS outperforms MIMO-orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (MIMO-OFDM) in terms of condition number distributions. Meanwhile, the condition numbers of MIMO-Zak-OTFS matrices also outperform those of MIMO-MC-OTFS, while MIMO-Zak-OTFS shows a better BER performance compared to MIMO-MC-OTFS and MIMO-OFDM using both LMMSE and sphere decoders in the experimental results.

title: Causal Discovery in Education: A Counterfactual Analysis of Student Outcomes using DECI on the OULAD Dataset
authors: Navya Nayer, Misha Kakkar, Neha Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Traditional educational analytics relies on correlation-based models that identify patterns but cannot establish causation, limiting actionable policy insights. We apply Deep End-to-End Causal Inference (DECI) to the Open University Learning Analytics Dataset (31,482 students, 11 features), uncovering causal relationships that differ substantially from correlations. Age exhibits 10x stronger causal effects (0.8) than correlation suggests (0.077), while education’s strong correlation (0.157) masks zero direct causal impact. Counterfactual simulations of six policy interventions highlight practical pathways for academic optimization and disability accommodations. Our results demonstrate why correlation-based policies fail and provide evidence-based guidance, showing that academic factors have stronger causal influence than demographics, enabling targeted interventions and resource allocation.

title: SALG-QCNN-SER: A Noise-Robust Quantum Convolutional Neural Network with Enhanced Cross-Domain Generalization for Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Md. Rifat Hossen, Md. Sarwar Hosain, Akif Mahdi, Tarun Debnath, Md. Najmul Hossain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Emotion recognition in speech is essential for human-computer interaction. This study introduces a refined hybrid Quantum Convolutional Neural Network (QCNN) framework designed to substantially enhance Speech Emotion Recognition (SER). It addresses major challenges such as speaker variability, noise robustness, and cross-domain applicability that often limit classical deep learning models. By Utilizing quantum principles like superposition and entanglement within a 6-qubit, 8-layer parameterized circuit, the model derives highdimensional, noise-resistant features from speech signals. The method includes sophisticated preprocessing steps, such as bandpass filtering and energy-based Voice Activity Detection (VAD), followed by Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) extraction. Evaluated on a diverse dataset of 4,515 samples across eight emotions (Angry, Calm, Disgust, Fear, Happy, Neutral, Sad, Surprised), the QCNN achieved an accuracy of 86 %, setting a new benchmark. It surpasses classical approaches such as a CNN with attention <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(77 \%)$</tex>, a traditional QCNN <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(77.87 \%)$</tex>, and a hybrid quantum-classical network <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(76 \%)$</tex>. The model demonstrated balanced performance, with precision scores ranging from 0.79 to 1.0 (including a perfect 1.0 for Fear) and recall scores from 0.82 to 0.94. These results underscore the promise of quantum-enhanced neural networks in capturing complex, nonlinear speech emotion patterns and establishing a new standard for robust, generalizable SER systems.

title: Design and Analysis of a Symmetric Thirteen-Level Inverter for THD Minimization with Reduced Component Count
authors: Bivas Sarkar, Md. Mohi Uddin Mohin, Mohammad Ibrahim, Shahariar Ahmed Himo, Md. Sabbir Alam, Monjila Afrin Dorothi, Ahmed Al Mansur
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Multilevel inverters (MLIs) have emerged as a pivotal technology for high-efficiency power conversion in renewable energy systems, owing to their superior output waveform quality and reduced dv/dt. However, conventional thirteen-level topologies often incur high component counts and control complexity, limiting large-scale integration. This research proposes a novel symmetric thirteen-level inverter that employs only nine power switches and uses the Optimum Nearest Level Control (ONLC) to minimize the total harmonic distortion (THD). Comprehensive MATLAB/Simulink simulations demonstrate a peak output of 600 V and a THD of 6.24% using a resistive load. The inverter's streamlined architecture reduces cost, simplifies control, and enhances reliability. Key advantages include lower conduction and switching losses, simplified gatedriver requirements, and superior harmonic performance. Comparative analysis confirms the proposed topology's economic feasibility and technical superiority over state-of-theart thirteen-level inverters.

title: Lidar Signal Fusion Method Based on AI-Driven Saturation Detection and Nonlinear Calibration
authors: Long Xu, Xin Lin, Yong Yang, Linmei Liu, Wei Wang, Xuewu Cheng, Kaijie Ji, Jiqin Wang, Faquan Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Ground-based dual-channel Rayleigh lidar provides continuous observations of the middle and upper atmosphere, but reliable full-altitude profiling remains difficult. Strong near-range echoes may drive the low-altitude channel into nonlinear compression or saturation, whereas weak high-altitude echoes are often affected by severe signal-to-noise-ratio degradation. Mismatch between the low- and high-altitude channels can also introduce discontinuities in the overlap region, which may propagate into subsequent density and temperature retrievals. Existing studies have mainly addressed detector-level dead-time correction or local channel stitching, but the complete processing chain from saturation detection and nonlinear correction to overlap-region consistency and retrieval-oriented fusion has not been fully considered. To address this limitation, we propose an AI-driven lidar signal fusion framework that integrates saturation detection, nonlinear calibration, and retrieval-consistent channel stitching. An Anomaly Transformer is first used to identify the onset of nonlinear response and determine the valid fusion interval. A non-paralyzable detector model combined with XGBoost residual learning is then applied to correct saturation-affected segments. Finally, an inter-channel nonlinear mapping model and a three-stage weighted stitching strategy are used to generate continuous fused profiles before physical inversion. Experiments on both isomorphic and heterogeneous photomultiplier-tube systems show that the proposed framework improves signal continuity and retrieval consistency. For the isomorphic configuration, it improves temperature-profile consistency within the overlap region, reducing the maximum temperature uncertainty from 4.8 K to 3.7 K and the RMSE from 2.45 K to 1.62 K. For the heterogeneous configuration, the retrieved temperature profile agrees well with the reference model over 30–80 km, with deviations at most altitudes confined within ±3 K and a maximum deviation of approximately 3.8 K. These results demonstrate the robustness of the proposed framework for retrieval-oriented dual-channel Rayleigh lidar signal fusion under both isomorphic and heterogeneous PMT conditions.

title: Extended Gramian Angular Field: An Efficient Method for Encoding Time Series as Images and Its Application in Power Converter Open Circuit Fault Diagnosis
authors: Yang Zhou, Jin Zhao, Yang Liu, Kang Ren
abstract: The data-driven-based fault diagnostic methods have continued to emerge in power electronics systems in recent years. Among them, though the methods that convert one-dimensional (1-D) electrical signals into two-dimensional (2-D) images for classification have shown promise, they often suffer from information loss or excessive computational burden. This article refines the Gramian angular field (GAF) theory and proposes a lossless and efficient method for transforming time series into images, termed extended Gramian angular field (EGAF). Building upon EGAF, an efficient and robust fault diagnostic method is proposed, with implementation demonstrated on a two-level (2L) three-phase (3P) voltage-source-inverter (VSI). The diagnostic method first extracts the current time series under different faults through Clarke transformation and zero-crossing detection, then converts the windowed time series into 2-D matrices using EGAF to construct the diagnostic dataset, and finally implements fault classification through a lightweight convolutional neural network (CNN). The proposed diagnostic method achieved an accuracy of 98.64%, and the experimental results demonstrate its high performance.

title: Bridging Local and Contextual Features with IChOA-CNN-LSTM for Robust Textual Emotion Recognition
authors: Asif Ahammad Miazee, Ashraful Goni
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The explosive growth of digital communication has transformed social media into a vast repository of human emotion, yet the noisy, informal nature of online language continues to challenge existing natural language processing (NLP) systems. This paper introduces IChOA-CNN-LSTM, a optimised hybrid deep learning architecture designed to bridge the persistent gap between local feature extraction and longrange contextual understanding in textual emotion recognition. The framework integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for capturing fine-grained n-gram patterns, bidirectional long short-term memory networks (BiLSTM) for modeling sequential dependencies, and a multi-head attention mechanism optimized through the Improved Chimp Optimization Algorithm (IChOA) for feature selection. The model achieves an accuracy of 78.19%, outperforming conventional CNN, LSTM, and state-of-the-art hybrid baselines by up to 18%. This research lays a scalable foundation for future multimodal and context-aware emotion analysis.

title: Zero Trust-Based Cyber-Physical Security for Financial Fraud Detection in Engineering Projects
authors: Rakesh Ranjan Swain, Joshila Grace, Manisha Chandna, Divya Sharma, Nivedan Mahato, Kuthalingam Venkadeshwaran
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: As banks come up with new ways to digitize their systems, the implementation of new cybersecurity measures becomes critical. ZTA’s implementation would ensuring there is real-time verification of access requests to inter-connected systems, thus minimizing exposure to the cyber threats. Most contemporary fraud detection systems still utilize manually configured, static, centralized basis, which does not adapt to changing attack scenarios, and are inefficient in rule outputs. Most of all, such mechanisms are privacy invasive. AAD-FRL is the new cyber security fraud detection framework proposed in this thesis. It integrates ZTA and distributed fraud detection poly systems and principles of ZTA to optimally detect and prevent fraud. Using federated learning and encapsulated with reinforcement learning, AAD-FRL can conduct real-time, and lac data fraud detection and prevention across different financial systems without compromising user privacy. AADFRL, when operating within a ZTA paradigm, amplifies the Zero Trust principles of the banking security, strengthens proactive, real-time fraud detection and prevention systems, increases anomaly detection, and misclassification detection, and reframes the security architecture of the systems. The proposed AAD-FRL method accomplished a detection accuracy of 95%, a false positive rate of 3%, a false negative rate of 4%, and an average response time of 70 milliseconds, reflecting excellent real-time performance in fraud detection.

title: A Recurrent Neural Network-Enhanced Supertwisting Pitch Controller for Floating Offshore Wind Turbines: Nonlinear Synthesis and HIL Validation
authors: Flavie Didier, Hedi Basbas, Djelalli Larioumlil, Yong-Chao Liu, Salah Laghrouche, Daniel Depernet
abstract: This article proposes a novel adaptive pitch control strategy for floating offshore wind turbines (FOWTs) operating in Region III. The controller combines a supertwisting algorithm (STA) with a recurrent radial basis function neural network (RRBFNN) to estimate and compensate for external disturbances in real time. The control law is derived from a simplified nonlinear control-oriented model of a 5-MW semisubmersible FOWT, without relying on linearization. A Lyapunov-based framework guarantees closed-loop stability and online neural adaptation. The proposed RRBFNN-STA controller is implemented on a hardware-in-the-loop platform integrating OpenFAST, a CompactRIO emulator, and a Beckhoff industrial controller. Validation is performed under three realistic wind and wave conditions, including extreme turbulence scenarios. Compared to a conventional STA and a gain-scheduled proportional–integral baseline controller, the RRBFNN-STA achieves significantly lower root-mean-square errors (RMSE), reducing rotor speed tracking error from over 1.5 to 0.24 r/min, and platform pitch velocity RMSE to 0.24 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>/s. These results confirm the robustness and real-time applicability of the proposed control approach for pitch regulation in FOWTs under nonlinear and turbulent operating conditions.

title: A Novel Low-Cost 3D-Stackable 2T0C DRAM Cell with Vertical-Double-Gate and Lateral-C-shape-Channel InGaZnO Transistors
authors: Ziyi Liu, Yiwei Du, Liyang Pan, Renrong Liang, Ting Liu, Qi Hu, Zhigang Zhang, Zhaoyan Gao, Jianshi Tang, Bin Gao, Jun Xu, He Qian, Huaqiang Wu, Yuegang Zhang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, we present a novel multi-layer 2T0C DRAM architecture integrating lateral C-shape-channel FETs (LCCFETs) and vertical-double-gate FETs (VDGFETs) for high-density memory applications. The proposed structure employs LCCFET as the write transistor and VDGFET as the read transistor, with the LCCFET source connected to the VDGFET lateral gate (LG) and the VDGFET vertical gate (VG) enabling column access. Key innovations include a one-step ALD process for InGaZnO channel formation, ALD-based InZnO replace source/drain, and sidewall vertical-damascene TiN bitline for read transistors. The design supports n-layer parallel integration akin to 3D NAND, achieving an equivalent feature size of 8F<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/n.

title: FedSP: Privacy-Preserving Malware Detection Model Through Lightweight Federated Learning
authors: Jannatul Ferdous Nuri, Md. Fardin Khan, Abu Rayhan Naeem, Sagufta Sabah Nakshi, Md. Jahidul Islam, Saurav Chandra Das
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has exposed them to serious security loopholes by penetrating them using malware attacks. Federated Learning (FL) offers a solution that is privacy-friendly but existing research such as SIM-FED, CyberForce, and FedDetect is still challenged by the limitations of adversarial robustness, scalability, and class imbalance. This paper introduces FedSP, a lightweight FL-based model for malware detection intended for IoT settings. Unlike existing works, FedSP integrates a privacy-protecting FedSP algorithm with secure aggregation and adversarial training, federated data normalization, and SMOTE-based balancing for imbalanced data distributions, and local computation efficiency for resourceconstrained IoT clients. Moreover, experimental results over the Drebin-215 dataset show that FedSP achieves 98.5% accuracy and outperforms state-of-the-art FL-based approaches in precision, recall, F1-score, and adversarial robustness. These results demonstrate the model's applicability for privacy-respecting and scalable IoT malware detection.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 Artificial Intelligence x Humanities, Education, and Art (AIxHEART)

title: Meta Learning for Healthcare: Few-Shot Diabetes Prediction Across Heterogeneous Populations
authors: Nafiz Nahid, Supto Saha, Meher Farjana, Meher Afsana, Nabil Nahid Ope, Md. Abu Naser Mojumder
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Diabetes prediction plays a crucial role in early diagnosis and effective clinical decision-making. Traditional machine learning (ML) models have been widely applied for this task; however, their performance often declines when evaluated on data from different populations. In this study, we present a comparative analysis of classical ML models-Naïve Bayes, Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA), Quadratic Discriminant Analysis (QDA), k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Support Vector Machines (SVM)-alongside an Artificial Neural Network (ANN) and a Few-Shot Meta-Learning approach (Prototypical Network for Tabular Data, Prototypical Networks). The models were trained on the PIMA Indian Diabetes dataset and tested on an external dataset, DiabeticsSylhet, collected from Sylhet Diabetic Hospital, enabling robust cross-population evaluation. Experimental results demonstrate that SVM and ANN both achieved high test accuracy of 94.9 %, while Prototypical Networks outperformed all models with a 96.0 % accuracy, highlighting its superior generalization capability. Compared to conventional ML and ANN approaches, Prototypical Networks showed greater robustness against domain shifts by leveraging episodic training. The novelty of this work lies in applying and evaluating FewShot Meta-Learning for diabetes prediction across heterogeneous datasets, bridging the gap between controlled experimental settings and real-world clinical data.
