These findings suggest that Meta-Learning frameworks hold strong potential for enhancing the reliability of computer-aided diagnosis in healthcare.

title: Adjustable Accumulation Behavior in 2D Perovskite-Based Artificial Neuron Devices
authors: Jiaqi Li, Qingxiu Li, Shirui Zhu, Yan-Bing Leng, Ye Zhou, Su-Ting Han
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: A two-dimensional (2D) perovskite memristor based on BDAMA<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">n-1</inf>Pb<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">n</inf>I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3n+1</inf> is demonstrated to exhibit an adjustable accumulation-rate (AR) artificial graded-neuron behavior. The incorporation of butane-1,4-diammonium (BDA<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2+</sup>) molecules induces dimensional reduction and defect passivation, increasing the iodide-ion migration barrier. Compared with MAPbI<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> devices, the 2D perovskite shows enhanced stability and tunable current accumulation under repeated pulse stimulation. The AR value can be modulated from 18.03 to 58.54, enables stable and tunable graded-neuron functions for future neuromorphic computing applications.

title: Dynamic Response Characterisation of PDMS-MWCNTs Piezoresistive Sponge for Human Motion Monitoring
authors: Abhinav Sharma, Shivanku Chauhan, Shubham Kumar Mishra, Sabyasachi Talukdar, Mohd. Zahid Ansari
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Flexible sensors have gained considerable attention in recent years for human motion monitoring. Particularly, sponge based, porous piezoresistive flexible pressure sensors are particularly promising. The flexible pressure sensor applications in human motion monitoring require the sensor to be tested against the dynamic response. In this paper, we prepared a flexible pressure sensor based on Polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) with multi walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs) together. The sensor was tested in real time to measure its sensitivity and conducted a dynamic response analysis. The sensitivity analysis showed that at low pressure values, the sensor shows a linear response with a sensitivity value to be 3.03% kPa<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> in the range of 0-20 kPa. Additionally, to mimic the dynamic response, we tested the sensor against dynamic loading conditions, along with repeated cycles at fixed pressure values. The sensor response showed the clear differentiation between applied pressure values as well as stable performance over numerous cycles.

title: Multimodal Deep Learning for Early Sepsis Prediction: Fusion of Physiological Data and Clinical Notes Using Kernel Attention and ClinicalBERT
authors: Redoanul Haque, Md. Nazrul Islam Mondal
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Early identification of sepsis substantially improves patient outcomes, but robust prediction remains challenging due to the heterogeneous nature of clinical data. In this study, we present a multimodal fusion framework that predicts sepsis six hours prior to onset by combining a Kernel Attention module for structured time-series data with ClinicalBERT representations of unstructured clinical notes. Using the MIMIC-III dataset of adult ICU stays, we compute SOFA-based labels and derive early-warning targets by shifting the onset definition six hours earlier. The time-series branch applies kernel attention to capture long-range dependencies across physiological signals, while the text branch leverages ClinicalBERT embeddings to represent clinical notes. The outputs are fused through a joint learning layer to generate sepsis risk predictions. Experimental results demonstrate that compared to the strongest 7 baselines, our model achieves an 8.25% improvement in AUROC, along with gains of 2.9% in F1 score, 2.7% in precision, and 3.2% in recall, demonstrating clear superiority across all evaluation metrics.

title: An Integrated Approach to Forecast Bitcoin Price Incorporating Economic Factors, Strategic Commodities, and X Tweets
authors: Swattic Ghose, Rubaiyet Hossain Jawwad, Nafiz Imtiaz Rafin, Yamin Bin Yahiya, Faiyaz Bin Khaled, Md. Golam Rabiul Alam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Bitcoin is known for its volatility. So, we conducted a diverse market analysis to construct a conclusive market feature dataset and a hybrid model architecture to find better insight and make better forecasts. One key factor that other research overlooked was the market impact of AMD and NVIDIA. These two most prominent tech companies provide consumerlevel GPUs mostly used for cryptocurrency mining. Focusing on these gaps, we conducted an extensive study of the potential markets and their impact on our model training. We provided an extensive data analysis of the impact of crude oil, gold, AMD, NVIDIA, S&P500, NASDAQ, and sentiment analysis of 85 million tweets altogether on Bitcoin price. The volume of tweets that we collected through web scraping is substantial to the other studies. From our comparative analysis, we deduced that A bidirectional LSTM works best for predicting the Bitcoin trend compared to other deep learning and time series models. Thus, we chose Bidirectional LSTM as our base model and used it to build a hybrid architecture ensemble model with Random Forest Regressor. For the proposed model, we have deployed four simultaneous Bayesian Optimized Bidirectional LSTM models, each with its distinct input features, and trained the Random Forest Regressor model using the predictions from those four models. The trained Random Forest model was used to select the best forecast we obtained from the four Bi-LSTM models. Our findings indicate that Bidirectional LSTM predicts more accurately when sentiment analysis and other macroeconomic aspects are incorporated.

title: A Compact Ultra-Wideband MIMO Antenna for Mmwave Applications
authors: Zijian Dong, Zhenzhen Jiang, Mark Leach, Jiafeng Zhou
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This paper presents an ultrawideband (UWB) millimetre-wave (mmWave) multiple-input, multiple-output (MIMO) antenna employing a defected ground structure (DGS) covering the 5G NR bands n257, n258, n259, n260, and n262. The proposed design has a compact volume of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$28 \times 26 \times 0.832 ~\text{mm}^{3}$</tex> and achieves an operating bandwidth from 23.71 to 51.99 GHz, corresponding to a 74.71% fractional bandwidth. The MIMO configuration exhibits strong diversity performance; an envelope correlation coefficient below 0.06 and diversity gain above 9.97 dB. The antenna achieves radiation and total efficiencies exceeding 90% and 80%, respectively, and provides a maximum gain of 6.45 dBi. The measured and simulated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S$</tex>-parameter results are in good agreement, showing the proposed design can be used for 5G mm wave applications.

title: Half Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)

title: Standard CMOS-Compatible Low-Power Ternary Logic with Drain Resistance
authors: Minsu Lim, Jiwon Chang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: We propose a silicon (Si) CMOS-compatible ternary logic (T-CMOS) technology achieved by a simple modification of conventional MOSFETs. By introducing an additional drain resistance, the on-current is limited to a gate-independent constant level, thereby enabling a stable third logic state in the voltage transfer curve (VTC). TCAD simulations calibrated to experimental transfer characteristics of planar FETs and GAAFETs confirm robust ternary operation with low static power consumption and short propagation delay. Among the devices, GAAFET-based T-CMOS achieves the most favorable performance, demonstrating superior current scalability and a large static noise margin (SNM) of 149 mV/dec at V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf> = 0.7 V. Furthermore, the circuit supports hybrid operation, switching between ternary and binary logic depending on the supply voltage.

title: Editorial Board
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)

title: An Explainable Ensemble Learning Framework for Crop Classification with Optimized Feature Pyramids and Deep Networks
authors: Syed Rayhan Masud, SK Muktadir Hossain, Md. Ridoy Sarkar, Mohammad Sakib Mahmood, Md. Kishor Morol, Rakib Hossain Sajib
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Agriculture is increasingly challenged by climate change, soil degradation, and resource depletion, and hence requires advanced data-driven crop classification and recommendation solutions. This work presents an explainable ensemble learning paradigm that fuses optimized feature pyramids, deep networks, self-attention mechanisms, and residual networks for bolstering crop suitability predictions based on soil characteristics (e.g., pH, nitrogen, potassium) and climatic conditions (e.g., temperature, rainfall). With a dataset comprising 3,867 instances and 29 features from the Ethiopian Agricultural Transformation Agency and NASA, the paradigm leverages preprocessing methods such as label encoding, outlier removal using IQR, normalization through StandardScaler, and SMOTE for balancing classes. A range of machine learning models such as Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors, Support Vector Machines, Decision Trees, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and a new Relative Error Support Vector Machine are compared, with hyperparameter tuning through Grid Search and cross-validation. The suggested “Final Ensemble” meta-ensemble design outperforms with 98.80% accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, compared to individual models such as K-Nearest Neighbors (95.56% accuracy). Explainable AI methods, such as SHAP and permutation importance, offer actionable insights, highlighting critical features such as soil pH, nitrogen, and zinc. The paradigm addresses the gap between intricate ML models and actionable agricultural decision-making, fostering sustainability and trust in AI-powered recommendations

title: DREAM: Drain Stress-Induced Reliability Enhancement Method for IGZO-FETs and Performance Improvement of IGZO-based M3D System from 77K to 373K
authors: Zhidong Tang, Yanbo Su, Jianshi Tang, Kexin Wang, Zhixing Jiang, Yuyao Lu, Yijia Fan, Yibei Zhang, Yue Xi, Dong Wu, Bin Gao, He Qian, Huaqiang Wu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: For the first time, we have developed a Drain stress-induced Reliability EnhAncement Method (DREAM) for IGZO-FETs, demonstrating significantly improved positive bias temperature instability (PBTI) reliability across a wide temperature range from 77 K to 373 K. Specifically, the ΔV<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf> of IGZO-FETs is reduced from 210 mV to 40 mV in PBTI measurements respectively (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ox</inf>=3 MV/cm for t<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">stress</inf> =10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> s at T=300 K). By applying DREAM to an IGZO-based monolithic 3D (M3D) system design, the performance and reliability is enhanced, where the typical neural network classification accuracy can be improved by over 10%.

title: Synthetic EEG for Motor Imagery Tasks using Transformer-Guided Generative Adversarial Network
authors: Sabrina Soltana, Arif Istiaque Rupom
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Electroencephalogram (EEG) signals are essential for brain-computer interface (BCI) systems and clinical research. However, the limited availability of EEG data constrains the performance of deep learning models. While existing generative models can replicate time-domain patterns, they often fail to generate complex temporal features. This study introduces a Transformer-Guided Generative Adversarial Network (TGGAN), which integrates adversarial learning with self-attention mechanisms to generate synthetic EEG signals that capture both temporal and frequency domain features. Experiments conducted on the BCI Competition IV 2b dataset indicate that TG-GAN produces realistic signals with statistical properties, temporal dynamics, and spectral patterns comparable to real data in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu(\mathbf{8}-\mathbf{1 3 ~ H z})$</tex> band. Additionally, Fréchet Distance scores confirm the distributional similarity between synthetic and real signals. These findings indicate that TG-GAN is a promising approach for augmenting EEG datasets and enhancing model training in BCI applications.

title: Manufacturing-Oriented Dual Neural Surrogate for Trench MOSFET Fabrication Process and Device Optimization
authors: Mingqiang Geng, Jianming Guo, Yuting Sun, Yixian Song, Dawei Gao, Dong Ni
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper presents a dual-recurrent neural network (RNN) surrogate model for end-to-end semiconductor manufacturing process optimization targeting 150 nm gate-length N-type trench MOSFETs. The forward RNN accurately predicts device electrical characteristics with 98% accuracy, while the inverse RNN infers critical process parameters with 95% precision, effectively avoiding the local-optima issues of conventional multi-objective optimization. Validated against simulated and experimental data of a 150 nm trench MOSFET, our approach establishes a robust inverse-design methodology for global optimization of multi-stage fabrication parameters. This data-driven framework enables precise process control and improved device yield, highlighting its significance for advanced manufacturing.

title: Khelte Khelte Shikhi: A Proposed HCI Framework for Gamified Interactive Learning with Minecraft in Bangladeshi Education Systems
authors: Mohd Ruhul Ameen, Akif Islam, Momen Khandokar Ope
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Game-based learning shows real promise for engaging students in well-resourced classrooms, but what about the millions who study in schools with far fewer opportunities? We propose a practical framework for bringing Minecraft Education Edition into Bangladesh's 130,000 schools, where 55% lack reliable internet, rural areas receive only 12-16 hours of electricity per day, computer access in rural schools is just 8%, and student-teacher ratios can reach 52:1. Our approach addresses these constraints directly through three deployment tiers: cloudbased multiplayer for urban schools with stable infrastructure (15%), local-area network (LAN) solutions with optional solar power for semi-urban schools <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(30 \%)$</tex>, and fully offline, turnbased modes using refurbished hardware for rural contexts (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 5 \%} \boldsymbol{)}$</tex>. We provide eight curriculum-aligned Minecraft worlds with complete Bangla localization, covering topics from Lalbagh Fort reconstruction to monsoon flood simulation. The interface supports first-time users through progressive complexity, culturally familiar metaphors rooted in local farming and architecture, and accessibility features such as keyboard-only controls and 200% text scaling. We outline evaluation benchmarks including <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 \%}$</tex> learning gains, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 0 \%}$</tex> transfer-task mastery, System Usability Scale scores above 70, and a per-student cost below two dollars per hour. Although not yet empirically validated, this work synthesizes game-based learning theory, HCI principles, and contextual analysis to offer implementable specifications for pilot testing in resource-constrained settings. It is presented as a design-oriented conceptual framework rather than a field deployment, providing an implementation-ready blueprint for future empirical validation.

title: Bioinspired Fractal Nanoneedle Arrays for Flexible and Controlled Drug Delivery
authors: Ananya Bhattacharjee, Ratul K Baruah
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This study advances transdermal drug delivery by optimizing hollow micro/nanoneedles (MN/NN) as a minimally invasive alternative to conventional methods. Structural strength analysis using COMSOL FEA examines variations in base design, tip diameter, and length. A spiderweb-inspired silicon hexagonal array coated with PMMA shows linear strength gains under 1-5 kPa loads, with results up to third-order arrays confirming the feasibility of higher-order designs for improved performance. Fractal, web-based nanoneedles, when arranged in robust, scalable hexagonal arrays, enhance mechanical strength and drug delivery efficiency for use in flexible electronics.

title: Enhancing Learning Path Recommendation via Multi-task Learning
authors: Afsana Nasrin, Lijun Qian, Pamela Obiomon, Xishuang Dong
publish: 2025 Artificial Intelligence x Humanities, Education, and Art (AIxHEART)
abstract: Personalized learning is a student-centered educational approach that adapts content, pace, and assessment to meet each learner's unique needs. As the key technique to implement the personalized learning, learning path recommendation sequentially recommends personalized learning items such as lectures and exercises. Advances in deep learning, particularly deep reinforcement learning, have made modeling such recommendations more practical and effective. This paper proposes a multi-task LSTM model that enhances learning path recommendation by leveraging shared information across tasks. The approach reframes learning path recommendation as a sequence-to-sequence (Seq2Seq) prediction problem, generating personalized learning paths from a learner's historical interactions. The model uses a shared LSTM layer to capture common features for both learning path recommendation and deep knowledge tracing, along with task-specific LSTM layers for each objective. To avoid redundant recommendations, a nonrepeat loss penalizes repeated items within the recommended learning path. Experiments on the ASSIST09 dataset show that the proposed model significantly outperforms baseline methods for the learning path recommendation.

title: A Tunable Energy-Efficient DPI-Based Leaky Integrate-and-Fire Neuron for Neuromorphic Applications
authors: Theodoros Kantianis, Apostolos Kardamylas, Vasileios Moustakas, Andreas Papathanasiou, Vassilis Alimisis, Paul P. Sotiriadis
abstract: A low-power analog implemented DPI-based neuron is proposed in this paper. The design was carried out using TSMC’s 65nm CMOS process node, targeting energy-efficient spiking with multi-parameter tunability. The proposed neuron combines a subthreshold differential pair integrator (DPI) membrane for tunable integration and logarithmic membrane dynamics with a modified 1N3P Schmitt trigger that reduces leakage to preserve its efficiency for low-power applications. Additionally, a current starved inverter provides independent control of spike width and refractory duration via a dedicated refractory/reset circuit. Operating from a 0.8 V power supply, the circuit simulations demonstrate a stable leaky integrate-and-fire behavior and controllable firing rates, consuming just 20.42fJ/spike at 51.5kHz spike frequency. These results suggest that the proposed neuron is a well suited building block for largescale neuromorphic systems requiring robust low-power spiking and flexible parameter programmability.

title: Deep Learning Model Splitting for Distributed Inference in Resource-Constrained Edge Environments for Wildlife Monitoring
authors: Md. Auhidur Rahman, Davide Adami, Stefano Giordano
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Deep neural networks have achieved remarkable success in computer vision tasks such as object detection, classification and segmentation. However, deploying these models in real time on resource-constrained edge devices poses significant challenges due to high computational and memory requirements. Split computing enables distributed inference by partitioning the model across multiple devices, thereby balancing workload and reducing per-device overhead. In this work, we split our custom trained YOLOv8m object detection model for European wildlife animal classification into two components: the backbone (backbone.pt) and the neck-head (neck_head.pt). After verifying the correctness of the split model, we deploy it across three NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano 8 GB devices two acting as gRPC clients executing the backbone, and one as a gRPC server completing inference using the neck-head layers. To address the communication bottleneck caused by transmitting high-dimensional intermediate feature maps (P3, P4, P5) over limitedbandwidth edge networks, we compare two tensor serialization strategies: repeated float fields and serialized byte arrays, evaluated in both FP32 and FP16 formats. Our experiments show that the use of byte-based serialization with FP16 reduces network latency by up to 85 % and improves the end-to-end frame rate by over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times$</tex>, achieving up to 9 FPS. In contrast, traditional TCP-based backbone feature maps tensor transmission yields less than 1 FPS, making it infeasible for real-time applications. These results demonstrate that efficient tensor serialization and gRPC-based communication are key enablers for scalable, low-latency, distributed inference in edge AI systems.

title: Explainable Machine Learning for Chronic Kidney Disease Prediction with SHAP and LIME
authors: Md. Shohanoor Rahman, Abid Hasan Mahmud, S. M. Mehedy Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a progressive loss of renal function that often remains clinically silent until advanced, irreversible damage occurs. Conventional diagnostic markers, such as serum creatinine and estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR), usually increase late, which misses the critical window for timely interventions that can slow disease progression. Although machine learning (ML) offers a promising alternative for early prediction, its adoption is hindered by the opaque nature of many models, as physicians require transparent reasoning to trust and act on automated diagnoses. This study introduces an interpretable ML framework designed to bridge this gap. Using a novel dataset of 4,921 patient records, our pipeline first processes data and resolves class imbalance using SMOTE. A rigorous statistical analysis combining Chi-Square, T-Test, and Kruskal-Wallis tests identifies the most influential biomarkers for CKD prediction. Our top-performing models, including RandomForest and XGBoost, are tuned using Bayesian optimization and achieve 99.84% accuracy during 10 -fold cross-validation. Crucially, the framework integrates eXplainable AI (XAI) to provide clinicians with actionable insights. SHAP offers a global view, consistently identifying Creatinine as the most critical predictor, while LIME delivers patient-specific explanations for individual predictions. By combining high-precision prediction with transparent interpretation, this framework serves as a reliable assistive tool that empowers physicians to detect CKD in its early stages with evidence-based confidence.

title: An Intelligent Machine and Deep Learning Framework for Fake News Detection
authors: Amit Sheikh, Soyad Adnan Hossain Pranto, Souhardyo Bhattacharjee, Anamika Rani Nath, S M Samim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of fake news in the digital era has become a critical challenge, undermining public trust and spreading misinformation across online platforms. Accurate and automated detection of fake news has become essential to ensure the reliability of digital information. In this study, we have performed a comparative evaluation of classical machine learning models and a deep learning-based approach for fake news detection. Logistic Regression and Naïve Bayes classifiers have been implemented, achieving accuracies of 98% and 93%, respectively, which has demonstrated the effectiveness of traditional methods in text classification tasks. To better capture semantic relationships and sequential dependencies in textual data, a Long Short-Term Memory network has been developed. The LSTM model has achieved superior performance with 99% accuracy, which has highlighted the advantages of deep learning in modeling complex linguistic patterns. The dataset has consisted of verified fake and real news articles, which have been preprocessed using tokenization, normalization, sequence padding, and feature extraction techniques such as TF-IDF to optimize classification performance. Experimental results have indicated that LSTMbased approaches have not only improved classification accuracy but also offered robustness against noisy and unstructured text, providing a scalable and reliable framework for automated online news verification. This work has contributed to the growing body of research on fake news detection and has served as a benchmark for future studies integrating deep learning and natural language processing techniques.

title: Leveraging AI in Library Science: Enhancing Access to Political Science Literature and Resources
authors: Mahmoud Khalifa, Ammar A. Jalamneh, Amany A. Khodair
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper investigates how artificial intelligence (AI) can enhance access to political science literature within library systems, while critically examining the challenges associated with its implementation. Adopting a broad definition of AI that encompasses a range of technologies and practices, the study explores how AI-driven tools such as intelligent search engines, recommendation systems, and open data infrastructures are reshaping the curation, indexing, and dissemination of political knowledge. These technologies offer new opportunities for evidence-based analysis, policy-oriented research, and user engagement, thereby transforming both library science and political science research methodologies. However, the integration of AI also raises significant concerns regarding equity, transparency, and governance. Issues such as algorithmic bias, platform dependency, and unequal access to advanced tools may intensify existing disparities across institutions and regions. Drawing on case studies and current initiatives in Library and Information Science (LIS), the paper highlights practical implications and outlines future research directions for the responsible and expandable deployment of AI in political science libraries. Ultimately, AI is positioned not merely as a technical enhancement, but as a political and epistemological force within the evolving ecosystem of knowledge production.

title: Enhanced Near-Field Power Beam Scanning in E-Plane and H-Plane Using mmWave Leaky-Wave Antenna
authors: Shan Han, Yuan Ding, George Goussetis, Symon K. Podilchak, Lei Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This paper presents a two-dimensional (2-D) near-field-focusing (NFF) leaky-wave antenna (LWA) array formed by stacking 16 one-dimensional (1-D) NFF LWAs with 5.45 mm spacing and element-dependent feed phases. The derived aperture phase distribution is obtained from the finite-distance pathlength condition, and applied as element-dependent feed phases. The proposed excitation synthesizes a converging aperture field that produces a compact focal spot with a 6 dB power-density enhancement compared with a conventional far-field focusing (FFF) configuration at the same distance. While the NFF design exhibits a lower far-field gain (18.4 dBi) than the FFF case (23.9 dBi), it still maintains strong directional radiation and stable far-field characteristics. This demonstrates that NFF and highquality far-field performance can be simultaneously achieved. The resulting dual-function aperture is well suited for wireless power transfer and communication applications, offering a compact and efficient solution for simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems.

title: QGMR: A New Quantum Mutation Testing Operator
authors: Sanskar Shah, Sangharatna Godboley, P. Radha Krishna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: Mutation testing is an important technique for assessing the quality of test cases and software systems. It involves introducing changes into the code and identifying those changes. In the context of quantum computing, where programs are shown as quantum circuits composed of quantum gates, the traditional mutation testing techniques face unique challenges due to the inherent complexity of quantum algorithms and operations. We propose a novel strategy for higher-order mutation testing of quantum programs. Our main objective is to enhance the reliability of the quantum program. We propose a mutator to replace quantum gates in a quantum program. It identifies the gate combinations and their syntactic equivalence. We compare circuit state vectors to identify equivalent gate combinations and present the results for efficient mutation generation.

title: Plant Leaf Disease Detection Using Deep Learning and Explainable AI
authors: Jose P, Vonesh Kumar K, Chittibabu Y
abstract: Plant diseases lead to major losses in crop yield and quality, posing a serious threat to global food security. This study presents a smart and clear plant disease detection framework that combines Deep Learning with Explainable Artificial Intelligence. We use CNN architectures like VGG16, ResNet50, and EfficientNet for automatic feature extraction and classification, reaching over 95 percentage accuracy on the PlantVillage dataset. To improve transparency, Grad-CAM and LIME create visual explanations that highlight areas affected by disease. This approach blends high accuracy with interpretability, offering a reliable tool for early disease diagnosis and sustainable farming.

title: An Integrated Framework for Feasibility and Risk Assessment of Roadway Energy Harvesting: A Critical Review
authors: Saleem Ahmed Al-Azazi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper conducts a systematic review of decision-support frameworks for roadway energy harvesting projects. Following PRISMA guidelines, we analyze existing literature to synthesize a tripartite assessment model integrating technical performance modeling, Life-Cycle Cost Analysis (LCCA), and formal risk assessment. Our findings reveal that while prototypes are technically feasible, widespread deployment is constrained by a high Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) and significant structural risks to pavement integrity. We identify that while Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (MCDA) is a key tool for balancing these trade-offs, its effectiveness is limited by a lack of long-term field data. This study concludes that technology adoption depends on material and manufacturing advancements to reduce costs and mitigate structural risks.

title: Energy-Efficient and Low-Carbon Model Training Through Adaptive Pruning for AI Training
authors: Ankur Pramanik, MD. Rabiul Islam Asif, Sazia Sharmin, Kamruddin Nur
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has reshaped our daily life. It is reshaping education, healthcare, social media, automation, and large industrial organizations. However, a major problem has emerged because of the rapid development of Large Language Models (LLM) and deep learning technologies. Extensive training consumes a large amount of energy and significantly increases carbon dioxide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex>) emissions. Research found that the amount of carbon released to train a large NLP model is equivalent to the total lifetime carbon emissions of five gasoline-powered cars. In this study, we propose adaptive pruning is proposed as a solution to address the issue of high energy consumption and carbon emissions. Unlike static pruning, which utilizes set criteria, adaptive pruning dynamically discovers and eliminates unnecessary weights and neurons throughout training. This flexibility helps maintain the balance between maintaining accuracy and energy efficiency. We evaluated the CNN and Transformer models in their normal and pruned forms using datasets such as MNIST, CIFAR-10, and WikiText-2. The results demonstrate that adaptive pruning decreases energy usage by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20-40 \%$</tex>, while retaining accuracy within a 2 % decline. This study proves that Green AI is an important step in a sustainable environment for machine learning without sacrificing performance.

title: Vocal Tract Resonance Estimation: An Energy Weighted Singular Spectrum Approach
authors: Mohaimenul Islam, Md Rakibul Hasan, Md Mahbub Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Formant extraction, therefore, the recovery of vocaltract information is central to acoustics-based phonetics, and most vocal tract analysis algorithms operate on spectral envelopes or pole domains. However, the rich time-domain structure and its distribution of energy remain underused. We examine whether singular values, containing energy distribution information, derived from Hankel matrix, which contain temporal information, do in fact encode formant related information or not. In this work, ground-truth formants and bandwidths are obtained from four isolated bengali vowels (অ /ɔ/, আ /a/, ই /i/, and উ /u/) using polynomial decomposition LPC (PDLPC), one of the archetype methods, and formant candidates from the spectral energy domain are calculated from energy-weighted componentspectrum peaks (“weighted peaks,” WP). We then fit linear maps in both directions to test mutual predictability and see whether there is a relation between. Upon application across 160 vowel tokens from 20 speakers, it is observed that Weighted Peaks show strong, monotonic relations with formants, suggesting that they do contain formant information. Theoretically, the findings should provide us with concrete evidence that a few dominant patterns extracted from a short slice of sound (Roughly Singular Pairs 1-10) are enough to predict the mouth's resonant tones (Formants F1 and F2 mainly). Practically, this pipeline offers a lightweight feature for estimating or stabilizing formants and enables singular value decomposition based formant tracking directly from raw waveforms. The procedure is reproducible, language-portable, and easy to integrate into analysis or learning systems.

title: AI-Based Forecasting Model for Sustainable Development in Arab and GCC Countries
authors: Nabil El Kadhi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper predicts the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) Index of Arab and GCC nations based on high-quality AI-processed time-series forecasts to predict its value in 2027 using SDG data (2000 -2022) of the SDG Transformation Center. Findings indicate that GCC countries have better performance when compared to non-GCC countries because of better institutional capacities, governance and investments on sustainability. The United Arab Emirates is expected to be top of the list with a score of above 75 in 2026 , and this is due to its efforts on diversification, renewable energy, and digital governance systems. The SDG Index of Saudi Arabia is expected to increase to 72.5 in 2026 with a steady rise in 2018 (63.7) in relation to Vision 2030 and moderately in Oman and Qatar, focusing on green employment and inclusive society. On the other hand, Kuwait and Bahrain are disadvantaged by their delayed reform and by its dependence on oil. Comparative experiments show that cross-validation has a considerable increase in model performance relative to the 80/20 split, with a 35-60 per cent reduction in MAE and RMSE and an average of 0.9 in R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in a stable economy. As an example, the R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of Saudi Arabia increased by 0.72 to 0.96, and RMSE decreased by 3.34 to 1.13 in Oman. These findings demonstrate the strength of cross-validation in SDG forecasting and the urgency of the implementation of transformational and innovation-led policies to maintain long-term developments towards the 2030 Agenda in the Arab and GCC worlds.

title: Trustworthiness of Legal Considerations for the Use of LLMs in Education
authors: Sara Alaswad, Tatiana Kalganova, Wasan Awad
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: As Artificial Intelligence (AI)—particularly Large Language Models (LLMs)—becomes increasingly embedded in education systems worldwide, ensuring their ethical, legal, and contextually appropriate deployment has become a critical policy concern. This paper offers a comparative analysis of AI-related regulatory and ethical frameworks across key global regions, including the European Union, United Kingdom, United States, China, and Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries. It maps how core trustworthiness principles—such as transparency, fairness, accountability, data privacy, and human oversight—are embedded in regional legislation and AI governance structures. Special emphasis is placed on the evolving landscape in the GCC, where countries are rapidly advancing national AI strategies and education-sector innovation. To support this development, the paper introduces a Compliance-Centered AI Governance Framework tailored to the GCC context. This includes a tiered typology and institutional checklist designed to help regulators, educators, and developers align AI adoption with both international norms and local values. By synthesizing global best practices with region-specific challenges, the paper contributes practical guidance for building legally sound, ethically grounded, and culturally sensitive AI systems in education. These insights are intended to inform future regulatory harmonization and promote responsible AI integration across diverse educational environments.

title: Student Behaviour Analysis in Online Proctoring Using XGBoost
authors: Vijaya Shetty S, Jyoti Neeli, Srujana S N, Tilak S K, Sudeep H M, Thejesh M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The coronavirus pandemic made it clear that trustworthy ways are needed to take exams online for students. This report is about a way to monitor students take exams online using artificial intelligence. The system checks what students are doing during the exam by looking at pictures and videos of them. It uses a tool to look at each frame of the video in real time to see if the student is doing something they should not be doing. This includes things like looking from the screen moving their eyes around trying to trick the system with a fake face talking or making faces having someone else in the room, with them or using things that they are not supposed to have like phones, books or other computers. The data that shows how people behave is put together into organized reports. Then looked at using special computer programs like Random Forest, Decision Tree, Linear Regression and Ridge Regression and Lasso Regression and XGBoost. XGBoost is chosen and implemented using Python and Django. It is private and it runs on its own because it makes the fewest mistakes. It has a low error rate of 4.55. This means it is fast and accurate.

title: Predictive Analytics in Smart Manufacturing with OptiSenseNet and Superior Sensor Data Integration
authors: Syed Hassan Imam Gardezi, Sankarganesh P, Ban Safar Khalaf, Ingilala Sharona, S.T. Santhanalakshmi, I Infant Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The huge amount and diversity of sensor information produced in contemporary settings continue to pose challenges to predictive analytics in smart manufacturing The existing approaches often fail to effectively process and analyze this data, which in turn leads to imprecise outlook and inaccurate decision-making father son It was born to go far. OptiSenseNet is the product that uses CNN and LSTM networks to offer effective solutions to these issues. The model is designed in such a way that it uses CNN layers to detect spatial features in sensor data and the LSTM components detect temporal features. This two-sided approach enables the analysis of both spatial and time elements of the OptiSenseNet in greater detail, which captures more information about how the devices behave and interact with the performance. One of the most important innovations of OptiSenseNet is that a temporal bypass connection between the LSTM module is combined. This aspect maximizes the seize of the long-term period dependencies via leveraging everyday styles which are made of all day fluctuations which supplements the overall predictive performance of the model. The process involves a systematic pipeline, which comprises statistics standardization, processing through CNN and LSTM layers with continuous model refinement through a process of iteration and blunders backpropagation. The OptiSenseNet has an impressive accuracy charge of 99.1, which proves that it can contribute to the proactive choice-making and optimization of business in intelligent manufacturing. OptiSenseNet will be a far-reaching leap in predictive analytics as it will overcome the limitations of traditional methods and adopt Industry four. Zero principles. It stimulates innovation and promotes operational excellence, which makes it a critical tool in the context of sophisticated sensor statistics generation and selection to support ways of production in the state of the art.

title: Neuromarketing Applications: Evolving Trends and the Way Forward
authors: Siddhartha Sarkar, Sachin Patel
abstract: Neuromarketing integrates neuroscience with marketing to uncover subconscious drivers of consumer behavior. As traditional self-report methods often fail to capture implicit processes, neuromarketing offers more accurate insights through neuroimaging and physiological measures. This paper reviews the evolution, tools, and applications of neuromarketing, highlighting its contributions to understanding decision-making. It also outlines emerging trends and future research directions, emphasizing neuromarketing’s growing potential to advance marketing practice.

title: Integrating Metabolic Pattern Analysis and Ketone Dynamics with AI-Based Strength Training for Lower Limb Health Prediction in Working Women
authors: B. Devimano, S Ravikumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The interaction of Multi Omics Brain- Derived Neurotrophic factor insight with personalized AI Strength Training for working women’s lower limb health and Longterm outcome prediction wellness metrics by combining AI models with brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) as a positive wellness factor, hedonic hunger as an negative wellness factor with highly personalized and effective lower limb leg-strengthening metrices with time restricted feedings for working women professionals of advanced middle aged, especially those seeking to improve lower body strength, mobility, fat lose and longevity. BDNF is a protein that, in human is a key molecule involved in plastic changes related to learning and memory, the resistance training, functional exercises, and high intensity interval training can significantly increase the BDNF levels in women, which in turn supports muscle growth, recovery and neural adaptation in the legs which enhances both physical and cognitive outcomes. A regression target of predicting an individual’s hedonic hunger score classification which is the major causes of weakness in lower limb leg among women, caused because of childhood experiences, picky eating addiction can be analysed with the deep learning algorithms. AI powered personalized lower limb leg strengthening training increases the BDNF value of the brain that decreases the hedonic hunger value with help of the time restricting feeding of women of advanced middle aged that promotes fat lose, Lower limb strengthening that leads to healthy lifestyle of longevity.

title: Hardware Security Modules in the Modern Era: Design, Scalability, and Integration with Emerging Technologies
authors: Dinesh Punde, Mandeep Singh, Balwinder Singh
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: In today’s increasingly interconnected digital landscape, securing sensitive data is of paramount importance. Hardware Security Modules (HSMs) have emerged as a critical solution to safeguard cryptographic keys and ensure secure data management. HSMs provide physical protection against cyberattacks and unauthorized access by offering a dedicated, tamper-resistant hardware environment for cryptographic operations. This paper explores the role of HSMs in modern security infrastructures, examining their design, implementation, and challenges in real-world applications. It also discusses the future directions of HSM technology, including advancements in cryptographic algorithms, the integration of machine learning for enhanced threat detection, and their expanding role in securing emerging technologies such as blockchain and IoT. Key challenges such as cost, scalability, and integration with modern technologies are highlighted, along with potential solutions to address these barriers. The paper concludes with recommendations for organizations considering HSM adoption and suggests areas for further research to enhance the effectiveness and accessibility of HSM technology.

title: Privacy-Preserving Edge-Cloud Framework for Real-Time Crowd Detection and Forecasting
authors: Sanidhya Vijay, Dhruv Gehlot, Shikher Tewari, Samarth Mantri, Malvinder Singh Bali
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The conventional cloud-based crowd monitoring system faces issues of latency, bandwidth, and privacy due to the constant transmission of video. This paper introduces a hybrid edge-cloud framework focused on privacy. It combines real-time human detection, short-term crowd forecasting, and proactive timeslot allocation. The YOLOv11 detection runs locally on NVIDIA Jetson devices to create anonymized per-second JSON metadata with centroid-based deduplication. This approach removes the need to transfer raw images. Short-term occupancy estimates are generated via a hybrid forecasting ensemble that combines Transformer, LSTM, and XGBoost models. Bayesian fusion improves these predictions using user-intent signals. The predicted densities then go into an Integer Linear Programming scheduler that assigns visitor timeslots to maintain balanced occupancy and prevent congestion. To support ongoing model improvement without central data collection, the system uses federated learning with adaptive differential privacy and secure aggregation. Only encrypted and noisy model weight updates are shared. The framework monitors privacy loss using Rényi Differential Privacy accounting. This method guarantees privacy while keeping the model effective. As a result, the framework achieves a 95% reduction in bandwidth, less than 50ms inference latency, and a 25% decrease in peak occupancy while ensuring privacy.

title: Text Recognition Using Neural Radiance Fields
authors: Madhav Sharma, Hukam Chand Saini, Samiksha Agarwal, Akash Saxena, Pushpendra kumar Sikarwal, Shalini chaudhary
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Text reputation in complex scenes is still a difficult problem due to various lighting conditions, occlusions, and mental distortions. In three-dimensional environments, text appears on curved or non-planar surfaces, rendering conventional OCR methods useless. Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs), originally designed for three-D scene reconstruction, offer a new method for textual content reputation by allowing immoderate-constancy volumetric rendering. Using deep analysis models to extract text records and reconstruct three-dimensional textual content systems from two-dimensional photographs, this paper explores the possible adaptation of NeRFs for text popularity. We evaluate NeRF-based full text recognition on both synthetic and real-world datasets, demonstrating that it performs better than conventional techniques when dealing with intricate text distortions and occlusions.

title: LOD-Net: Locality-Aware 3D Object Detection Using Multi-Scale Transformer Network
authors: Mustaqeem Khan, Aidana Nurakhmetova, Wail Gueaieb, Abdulmotaleb Elsaddik
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: 3D object detection in point cloud data remains a challenging task due to the sparsity and lack of global structure inherent in the input. In this work, we propose a novel MultiScale Attention (MSA) mechanism integrated into the 3DETR architecture to better capture both local geometry and global context. Our method introduces an upsampling operation that generates high-resolution feature maps, enabling the network to better detect smaller and semantically related objects. Experiments conducted on the ScanNetv2 dataset demonstrate that our 3DETR + MSA model improves detection performance, achieving a gain of almost 1% in mAP@25 and 4.78% in mAP@50 over the baseline. While applying MSA to the 3DETR-m variant shows limited improvement, our analysis reveals the importance of adapting the upsampling strategy for lightweight models. These results highlight the effectiveness of combining hierarchical feature extraction with attention mechanisms in enhancing 3D scene understanding.

title: Data-Driven Electromagnetic Wave Propagation Modeling for Urban and Indoor Wireless Channels
authors: Movva Naga Swapna Sri, Bhiksha Gugulothu, A.P. Pushpalatha, Amaleswari Rajulapati, Anusha Peyyala, Vijayakumar Sivasundar
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: This paper examines how EM wave propagates through urban area and also examines the communication happens indoor. Existing methodologies have been based on the physics of the subject area, however, the application of those same methodologies to actualized versions of real world environments have proven to be time-consuming in a computational sense. Therefore, synthetic data from simulated environments was used to create a model of a neural network model utilizing an attention mechanism to find behavior in wireless communications by identifying propagation characteristics. The neural network model generated predictions concerning the amount of signal attenuation experienced by the signals and the variability in the signal strengths experienced within an environment, and was able to do so using only modeled data and without the need for explicit ray tracing methodologies. Further, the neural network model’s outputs showed spatial continuity, consistency in their patterns, and similar levels of error throughout the environment. The model also was found to demonstrate good generalization capabilities for a number of different environments for which it had no prior knowledge. In comparison to reference baselines, this model displayed superior accuracy and improved flexibility to accommodate a variety of environments. Ablation test results suggest that the performance of models can be significantly influenced by both how they are designed (i.e., their use of attention mechanisms) and how the inputs are processed (i.e., feature selection). The output from this model appears to exhibit many of the properties that one would expect of an output generated as part of simulating a realistic environment even though the input data was entirely artificially created. In general, this research provides evidence for the ability of employing data driven methods to guide propagation of modeling toward informing current wireless system planning and future network design.

title: A Decentralized Crypto Launchpad on Binance Smart Chain
authors: Bidyutmala Saha, Priyanka Chakraborty, Sayan Nath, Debtanu Roy, Arna Saha, Anwesa Ghosh, Dewan Habib, Akash Dey
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: This project is a blockchain-based decentralised application that allows cryptocurrency owners to launch new currencies while investors may make first investments in them. The charge of crypto in the foreign websites is high due to currency differences. This is a Make in India initiative and also supports the Digital India campaign by making Indian people more accustomed to the digital market. It emphasizes security, transparency, and community participation to build a trustworthy and sustainable launch pad ecosystem. A block chain consists of a chain of blocks, where each block contains an immutable record of all the transactions or data. The application can easily access transactions by smart contracts. In this paper, our pinpoint is to build a decentralized crypto launch pad based on the Binance Smart Chain (BSC) block chain technology, which is known for its speed, security and low fees. A decentralized wallet add-on has been integrated with the website, so to buy new coins from us, customers need to have a MetaMusk or trust wallet, which they need to connect with our website.

title: Development of Machine Learning Model for Automated Fruit Classification & Ripeness Detection
authors: Anshika Jindal, Monika Nayak, Rekha Manasa, Medha Shukla, K. A. Chinmaya
abstract: This paper delineates the development of two machine learning models: the first for the classification of fruits into distinct categories using the comprehensive Fruits 360 dataset, and the second for the determination of ripeness levels within a specific category, exemplified by bananas. The Fruits 360 dataset, encompassing over 90,000 images of 131 fruit and vegetable types, provides a robust foundation for the initial classification model. In contrast, the banana ripeness dataset, with its focus on various stages of banana maturity, enables the second model to discern between unripe, ripe, and overripe states with remarkable accuracy. The architecture of the models is based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs). It is meticulously trained and validated to achieve high precision in both fruit categorization and ripeness detection. The results of this study not only demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed models but also highlight the transformative potential of machine learning in automating and enhancing agricultural processes.

title: The Impact of Artificial Intelligence on Financial Decision-Making: An Empirical Study in Commercial Bank Operations
authors: Jiawei Yi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is becoming a part of any commercial banking operation and transforming decision-making and organisational performance. This study set out to investigate the effect of central AI-related variables, system accuracy, perceived usefulness, trust, operational integration, and employee competence, on the quality of financial decisions made by employees working in the banking sector. Quantitative research design has been used since a structured survey with a 5-item Likert scale was given to 300 respondents, who were subsequently analysed in terms of reliability, correlation analysis and multiple linear regression. The findings indicate close positive relationships among all AI-related dimensions and the quality of decision (r is between 0.834 and 0.898), and the regression model has a high explanatory power (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.884) with employee AI competence (b = 0.292) and system accuracy (b = 0.287) appearing as the most significant predictors. The research findings conclude that the technological performance and human capability should meet to achieve high-quality financial decision results in an AI-enabled banking setting. One of the most important suggestions is that banks are advised to focus on employee AI training and optimisation of the systems. The practical implication is that improving human-AI synergy can lead to a significant improvement in the performance of organisational decisions. The fact that the self-reported data is cross-sectional and in itself can lack long-term effects on behaviour and actual performance measures is also a great weakness.

title: A Multilingual AI-Based Conversational Tutor for Language Practice and Grammar Assessment
authors: Sandhiya R, Manasha K, Narmadha G, Genga Lakshmi K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Language learners often face difficulty in achieving fluency due to limited opportunities for real-time conversational practice, inadequate grammar awareness, and lack of pronunciation feedback. Traditional methods largely focus on passive lessons, offering minimal scope for natural interaction or personalized correction, which affects confidence and progress in real-world communication. To address these limitations, an AI-powered language learning platform is proposed and implemented that enables real-time interaction through text and voice across multiple languages. The system integrates speech-to-text and text-to-speech for voice communication and employs AI models to provide instant, context-aware feedback on grammar, sentence structure, and pronunciation. Learners can set proficiency levels, engage in adaptive dialogues on diverse topics, and receive personalized corrections tailored to their performance. Implemented with React.js, Node.js, and Firebase for scalable integration, the platform offers an immersive and effective approach to enhancing language acquisition through interactive practice.

title: Sensing the Neural, Muscular, and Biomechanical Biomarkers to Evaluate Age-Related Neuromuscular Health
authors: Sankhalp NS, A Celestin Jerald
abstract: Age-related neuromuscular changes significantly influence the onset and progression of neuromuscular disorders. This emphasizes the importance of understanding normal age-related neuromuscular variations for improved prevention and intervention strategies. This study aims to analyze neuromuscular dynamics across the aging spectrum using multimodal approaches, including electromyography (EMG), electroencephalography (EEG), and force plate studies. The results obtained in this study portray significant differences in these biomarkers with age. The integration of data from EMG, EEG, and force plate analyses offers a comprehensive framework to identify normal aging, which can be used to identify predictive biomarkers and explore their applications in assessing neuromuscular health. This research holds promise for enhancing diagnostics, advancing targeted therapies, and contributing to the understanding of neuromuscular function in aging populations.

title: Design and Simulation of Quantum-Enhanced Hybrid Machine Learning Models for Accelerated Big Data Classification
authors: Nida Afreen Rizvi, Shadab Pasha Khan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: The research article presents a Quantum-Enhanced Hybrid Machine Learning Model (QEHMLM) designed to accelerate data classification in Large-Scale Big Data Environments (LSBDE). The proposed framework integrates Quantum Computing (QC) with Classical Machine Learning Techniques (CMLT) including Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and Neural Network (Artificial-ANN) to overcome the computational and scalability limitations of classical approaches when processing High-Dimensional Datasets (HDD). The model leverages quantum feature encoding, entanglement-based transformations, and classical optimization to enhance accuracy and convergence speed. Simulations conducted using IBM Qiskit and TensorFlow Quantum on the Mobile Telecom Network Traffic Dataset validate the industrial applicability of the suggested approach in Applied Operational environment. The QEHMLM achieves notable improvements, recording 97% accuracy, 95% F1-score, and nearly 20% reduction in computational time compared to classical counterparts. These outcomes confirm that Hybrid Quantum-Classical Systems (HQCS) provide a scalable, efficient, and high-performance paradigm for next-generation Big Data analytics and intelligent computational ecosystems.

title: Bootstrap-Ensembled Neural Networks for Robust Stellar Temperature and Age Predictions
authors: Marco Tarantino, Loredana Prisinzano, Nicoletta D'Angelo, Francesco Damiani, Giada Adelfio
abstract: This study presents a machine learning approach to predict the effective temperatures and their accuracy of premain sequence stars, which are essential for deriving stellar ages through isochrone fitting and starspot-dependent evolutionary models. We trained a Neural Network on high-quality spectroscopic temperatures from the Gaia-ESO Survey, using Gaia DR3 and 2MASS photometry as input features. To estimate predictive uncertainty, we implemented a Neural Network with bootstrap procedure, where each model was warm-started using the parameters of an initial network trained on the full dataset with K-fold cross-validation. This allowed accurate and robust temperature predictions for large stellar populations lacking spectroscopic data, with strong performance in the lowtemperature regime. The predicted temperatures were used to build Hertzsprung-Russell diagrams and derive stellar ages of young clusters by starspot evolutionary models, achieving good agreement with spectroscopic benchmarks and independent methods, such as lithium equivalent widths.

title: OpenCV-Based Real-Time Multi-Modal Threat Detection and Alert System for Public Safety Applications
authors: S. P. Chokkalingam, Teja Thatiparthi, LaxmiNarayana Kaya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The introduction of efficient systems which can respond quickly and accurately will result in the safety of the people in crowded or dangerous places. A real-time Threat Detection and Alert System is presented in this paper, which is implemented using OpenCV and utilizes computer vision and deep learning to recognize hazards such as guns, fights, or flames. The intention is to create a system that can ’see’, ’hear’ and ’move’ just like a human being to understand its environment. Live video will be taken using OpenCV, and then every frame will be processed to focus the attention on the important parts of the scene. While the YOLOv8 deep learning model identifies weapons, the 3D Convolutional Neural Networks (3D-CNNs) monitor human’s gestures to classify actions as Fighting or Aggressive. An audio-based CNN will similarly be listening for shocking sounds—screams or gunshots—for instance—to corroborate the camera’s detection. The combination of all these inputs is done through a weighted Decision model which gives a single score indicating the likelihood of a threat’s presence. The system will immediately trigger a loud alarm sound and display a red warning on the screen if this score goes beyond a predetermined limit, thus instantly notifying the closest people to either evacuate or stay safely. The tests carried out in real-life situations showed that the system is very effective, with high accuracy and almost no delay. It is rapid, reliable, and easy to implement, thus making it ideal for locations such as public surveillance systems, train stations, schools, or even smart city infrastructure. By combining visual, movement, and sound analysis, this system offers an intelligent and responsive Approach to public safety beyond previous capabilities.

title: InteracTalker: Prompt-Based Human-Object Interaction with Co-Speech Gesture Generation
authors: Sreehari Rajan, Kunal Bhosikar, Charu Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Generating realistic human motions that naturally respond to both spoken language and physical objects is crucial for interactive digital experiences. Current methods, however, address speech-driven gestures or object interactions independently, limiting real-world applicability due to a lack of integrated, comprehensive datasets. To overcome this, we introduce InteracTalker, a novel framework that seamlessly integrates prompt-based object-aware interactions with co-speech gesture generation. We achieve this by employing a multi-stage training process to learn a unified motion, speech, and prompt embedding space. To support this, we curate a rich human-object interaction dataset, formed by augmenting an existing text-to-motion dataset with detailed object interaction annotations. Our framework utilizes a Generalized Motion Adaptation Module that enables independent training, adapting to the corresponding motion condition, which is then dynamically combined during inference. To address the imbalance between heterogeneous conditioning signals, we propose an adaptive fusion strategy, which dynamically reweights the conditioning signals during diffusion sampling. InteracTalker successfully unifies these previously separate tasks, outperforming prior methods in both co-speech gesture generation and object-interaction synthesis, outperforming gesture-focused diffusion methods, yielding highly realistic, object-aware full-body motions with enhanced realism, flexibility, and control. (https://sreeharirajan.github.io/projects/InteracTalker/)

title: AI-Based Technique for Odia-to-English Language Translation Using LSTM: Breaking the Communication Barriers
authors: Sasadhara Sahoo, Rojalina Priyadrashini, Ram Chandra Barik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Neural machine translation (NMT) for low-resource languages remains a significant challenge due to limited parallel corpora and linguistic complexities. This paper presents an LSTM-based Seq2Seq model with attention for Odia-to-English translation. The proposed model integrates a bidirectional LSTM encoder with an attention-enabled decoder, enabling effective capture of contextual dependencies in morphologically rich Odia sentences. The system was trained on a curated parallel Odia–English corpus and evaluated using BLEU scores on a held-out validation set. Experimental results demonstrate that the model achieves reasonable translation quality for short and medium-complexity sentences, while highlighting challenges in handling rare words, long sentences, and domain-specific terminology. Comprehensive analysis, including attention visualizations, loss convergence trends, and sentence-level BLEU distributions, provides insights into model behaviour and translation limitations. Future improvements involving sub word tokenization and Transformer-based architectures are proposed to further enhance translation accuracy. This work establishes a foundational framework for advancing low-resource Odia-English neural machine translation and serves as a benchmark for future research.

title: Detection of Alzheimer's Disease Using Deep Learning and Interpretability Using Grad-CAM
authors: Sanjukta Mohanty, Swaraj Priyadarsini, Arup Abhinna Acharya, Debabrata Sahoo, Soumyaranjan Das
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease is a debilitating neurodegenerative disorder which progressively affects cognition, memory, and daily functioning. Early detection is critical for slowing disease progression and implementing timely interventions, yet current diagnostic techniques including manual MRI analysis and clinical assessments are often timeconsuming, subjective, and dependent on expert interpretation. These limit points highlight the need for an automated, efficient, and objective diagnostic approach. This study introduces a deeplearning based model to classify MRI scans automatically into different Alzheimer's disease stages (Normal, Mild, Moderate, Severe), addressing the inefficiencies of traditional diagnostic methods. The proposed framework integrates EfficientNet for 2D feature extraction and a 3D Convolutional Neural Network (3D CNN) for volumetric analysis, enabling high-precision classification while maintaining computational efficiency. To enhance interpretability and trustworthiness, the model incorporates Grad-CAM visualization, allowing clinicians to pinpoint affected brain regions and understand the model's decisionmaking process. With an accuracy target of more than 98.5%, the model demonstrates robust performance across diverse MRI datasets, ensuring generalizability and reliability. Extensive experimentation and validation show its potential for scalability and real-world deployment in hospitals and research facilities. By leveraging AI and deep learning in neuroimaging, this research presents an advanced, automated diagnostic solution that can significantly improve early Alzheimer's detection, reduce diagnostic subjectivity, and assist medical professionals in delivering more effective patient care.

title: A Comparative Study of Machine Learning Models for Hypoxemia Classification
authors: Sujata Ray, Debasmita Pradhan, Niranjan Kumar Ray
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Hypoxemia, is a critical medical condition which indicates the lower oxygen level in blood. Early prediction of hypoxemia is highly important for timely medical intervention as it may lead to health disasters including brain injury, heart and kidney damage. Machine learning models are widely and effectively used in almost all domains including healthcare. In this work a comparative analysis of machine learning models, including Logistic regression, Gradient Boosting, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machines (SVM), are used to predict hypoxemia severity using dataset including, heart rate, patient oxygen saturation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{SpO}_{2}\right)$</tex>, and other vital signs. Performance metrics such as precision, recall, accuracy, and F1-Score are computed to find the model predicts the severity of hypoxemia efficiently. From the results it is observed that the performance of Random Forest is better than other models compared with an average accuracy of 83% and accuracy of 88% for severe class.

title: Multimodal Hybrid Attention-Gating Fusion Deep Learning Framework for Alzheimer’s Disease Classification
authors: Mohammed Rizwan Shaikh, Andrew Jeyabose, R. Vijaya Arjunan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Accurate diagnosis, staging, and progression assessment of Alzheimer’s disease (AD) remain challenging due to heterogeneous clinical manifestations and substantial overlap among disease stages. While deep learning approaches based on structural magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) have demonstrated encouraging performance, imaging-only models often fail to capture important clinical and cognitive factors that are essential for comprehensive disease characterization. This work proposes a multimodal deep learning framework that jointly integrates MRI-derived features with clinical biomarkers for improved AD classification and staging. Unimodal (MRI-only and clinical only) and multimodal models are systematically evaluated using subject-level-cross-validation and an independent test set across multiple binary and multi-class tasks, including AD vs cognitively normal (CN), AD vs mild cognitive impairment (MCI), stable MCI vs progressive MCI (SMCI vs PMCI), and multi-class disease staging. Experimental results show that multimodal fusion consistently outperforms unimodal models, improving MCI progression prediction from 59% to 75% and increasing MCI vs AD diagnostic accuracy from 92.31% to 93.85% achieving higher accuracy, improved generalization, and reduced performance variability across evaluation settings. These findings demonstrate that combining imaging and clinical information provides a more robust and clinically meaningful approach for Alzheimer’s disease diagnosis and prognosis.

title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Computing for Privacy-Preserving Machine Learning on Edge Devices
authors: B. Dinesh, M. Thangamani, B. Paulchamy, Lavanya K, D. Anandakumar, Rajasekhara Babu L
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The edge computing environment faces a challenge in maintaining data privacy while performing machine learning tasks. The current solutions for privacy preservation are inefficient or do not provide security guarantees, causing a critical gap in the edge computing environment, which is resource-constrained. The aim of this research work is to design a hybrid quantum-classical computing system that addresses the challenges of privacy preservation and efficiency in machine learning tasks. We design a novel architecture combining variational quantum circuits for secure data encoding with classical neural networks for scalable inference, implemented through federated learning protocols enhanced with quantum differential privacy mechanisms. Experimental evaluation demonstrates a 35% reduction in communication overhead compared to traditional federated learning approaches while maintaining 99.2% classification accuracy across diverse datasets. The quantum-enhanced privacy mechanisms achieve provable security guarantees with information-theoretic bounds, outperforming conventional cryptographic methods by 28% in computational overhead reduction. This hybrid approach establishes a new paradigm for secure and efficient machine learning deployment on edge devices, enabling privacy-preserving artificial intelligence in healthcare, finance, and IoT applications.

title: Roadkill Hotspots and Policy Advocacy (RHPA): A Multi-Layered AI and IoT Approach for Real-Time Wildlife Preservation
authors: Lokesh Patra, Indigibilli Harshit, Saumendra Kumar Mohapatra, Apurva Singh, Mihir Narayan Mohanty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Roadkill episodes are a crucial point where vehicle safety risks and ecological damage converge. The frequency of wildlife-vehicle incidents has increased due to rising urbanization, posing a threat to commuters and causing a major loss of biodiversity. In this study, a novel three-dimensional (3D) approach called “Roadkill Hotspots and Policy Advocacy” (RHPA) is proposed. In contrast to conventional radar-based systems, RHPA creates a preventive and responsive ecosystem by combining Computer Vision (YOLOv5xu), Deep Learning (DenseNet121 and EfficientNet-B3), and the Internet of Things (IoT). Both CNN architectures are trained individually to categorize subjects as “Alive” (High Risk) or “Dead” (Low Risk), achieving an overall accuracy of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\approx} {9 5 {\%}}$</tex>, and YOLOv5xu for real-time detection. Additionally, the system incorporates a policy-level dashboard to detect blackspots and an ESP32-based actuation layer for real-time driver warnings. Pilot testing indicates a potential 36% reduction in roadkill incidents.

title: Network Traffic Classification Based on Hadoop Using Gradient Boosting Algorithm
authors: Hmway Hmway Tar, Thiri Shwe Sin Htun
abstract: The proposed system presents a system for network traffic classification that combines Apache Hadoop for processing large data with the Gradient Boosting Classifier (GBC) algorithm. The proposed framework processes web server logs to categorize traffic into High, Medium, and Low priority flows, aiming to improve resource allocation and anomaly detection. The research highlights a gap in existing systems and handles with Gradient Boosting that gets high accuracy for complex pattern recognition in traffic flows. By leveraging the distributed processing power of Hadoop, the system transforms raw, messy network data into structured insights. The integration significantly improves decision-making, optimizes resource allocation, and helps identify critical trends and anomalies in network traffic, offering a robust advancement in big data and machine learning applications for network management.

title: PALMS+: Modular Image-Based Floor Plan Localization Leveraging Depth Foundation Model
authors: Yunqian Cheng, Benjamin Princen, Roberto Manduchi
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Indoor localization in GPS-denied environments is crucial for applications like emergency response and assistive navigation.
