Electrochemical analytical techniques such as Cyclic voltammetry (CV) and differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) techniques were employed, achieving limits of detection (LoD) and quantification (LoQ) of 0.035 ng/mL and 0.105 ng/mL for cTnI, and 1.39 ng/mL and 4.25 ng/mL for myoglobin, respectively. The fabricated electrodes exhibited excellent stability and reproducibility, supported by physicochemical characterizations confirming detailed electrode morphology. Furthermore, this dual-detection platform was successfully tested for real-time detection in human blood serum, yielding high recovery values and demonstrating its potential for practical, real-world applications.

title: Bangla Visual Question Answering Using Gated Cross-Attention and Reinforcement-Optimized Fusion
authors: Md. Fariaz Islam, Md. Mostafizur Rahman, Mohammad Isfar Nayen, Md. Mahmudul Arif, Hasib Hasnat, Refatul Islam Roni, Tonmoy Das, Md. Emran Hossain Dipu
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Visual Question Answering (VQA) in low-resource languages like Bangla poses dual challenges: effectively modeling complex linguistic structures and integrating visual and textual information to generate accurate answers. Traditional supervised methods often fail to capture intricate multimodal dependencies and rely heavily on large labeled datasets, which are scarce for Bangla. To address these challenges, we propose a reinforcement learning-based Bangla VQA system that combines MobileViT for visual feature extraction and Bangla BERT for question understanding, fused via a gated cross-attention mechanism to capture fine-grained interactions between image and text. The model is trained using a hybrid strategy: supervised pretraining with cross-entropy loss followed by self-critical REINFORCE optimization, allowing iterative improvement through reward-driven feedback. Experiments on the BVQA dataset demonstrate superior performance across all question types, achieving 75.60% overall accuracy and outperforming state-of-the-art baselines. This approach provides an efficient solution for low-resource, multimodal learning in Bangla.

title: Dataset for DDoS Detection in 5G-Enabled Connected and Autonomous Vehicle Systems
authors: Mirza Akhi, Md. Shalha Mucha Bhuiyan, Alfonso Ariza, Lubna Luxmi Dhirani
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: The intelligent Autonomous Vehicles (AVs) rely on 5G connectivity to exchange positional data, perception outputs, and control commands in real time. This communication enables coordinated motion and safety-critical response. However, it also increases the environment's exposure to Distributed Denial-ofService (DDoS) attacks, disrupting message flow and impairing operational safety. To investigate this risk, a simulated driving-and-DDoS-attack scenario is developed in the CARLA simulator under a 5 G -enabled AV setting. This setup yields the UL-ECE-5G-AV-DDoS2025 dataset, which contains regular driving traffic and DDoS attack traffic. The dataset includes Global Positioning System (GPS) coordinates, speed, acceleration, throttle, steering, braking values, and network-layer metrics. Hence, this UL-ECE-5G-AV-DDoS2025 dataset supports reproducible benchmarking for Generative-AI (Gen-AI), machine learning, deep learning, and traffic-flow analysis in 5 G -connected transport environments.

title: Artificial Intelligence Based Smart Glass for Navigating Vision Impaired Individuals
authors: MGT Sandaruwan, MKP Madushanka
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The proposed research is an AI-based indoor navigation system that helps visually impaired people navigate with the assistance of object detection, depth estimation, and natural-language guidance. The system operates on a stereo-vision camera with a YOLOv8 model which has been trained to locate two important objects of the interior space, namely, tables as a navigation object and chairs as an obstacle object, to model mobility in controlled conditions. The depth estimation is carried out based on stereo disparity mapping, which allows measuring the distance and obstacle avoidance in real-time. A rule-based navigation module converts spatial knowledge to step-by-step voice instructions produced by a Rule-based language model and passed on to TTS. Several indoor configurations of different table-chair layouts were used to test the system with different multiple configurations to assess the performance of the system in terms of accuracy in detection, reliability in navigation and performance in terms of user-safety. The findings indicate that the system has high accuracy in object detection and is able to circumvent obstacles and direct users to the target point. This prototype shows that it is possible to use vision-based indoor mobility aids at low cost and give them a basis to be expanded to multi-object environment, autonomous path finding, and wearable embedded implementation.

title: A Novel Dopant-Segregated Si FeFET with a Superlattice Gate Stack Featuring Low-Temperature Gate-First Compatibility and Enhanced Memory Window
authors: Shaodi Xu, Yue Yu, Qianqian Huang, Ru Huang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this paper, a novel dopant-segregated (DS) Si FeFET featuring low-temperature gate-first compatibility is experimentally demonstrated for the first time. The implant into silicide (IIS) process under optimized conditions is adopted for the dopant segregation, leading to a pronounced dopant pile-up and a high on/off current ratio. Concurrently, the gate stack was co-optimized with the IIS process, where a thin interfacial layer (IL) and superlattice (SL) HZO ferroelectric (FE) layer were fabricated, and the rapid thermal annealing (RTA) temperature is optimized. The DS FeFET shows improved memory window (MW) of 1.3V at low operation voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OP</inf>). This work provides effective solutions of integrating front-end-of-line (FEOL) Si HZO FeFETs and expands their potentials for high-reliable embedded memory applications.

title: Rain Streak Removal: A Streamlined Hierarchical CNN-Based Approach for Image Enhancement
authors: Nushrat Tarmin Meem, Sk. Md. Masudul Ahsan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Rain streaks can potentially degrade the image quality acquired during outdoor scenes and lead to poor visibility and low performance of downstream vision tasks. The rain removal task aims at recovering clean images by effectively eliminating rain artifacts and thereby improving visual quality and ensuring the reliable operation of vision-based systems in adverse weather conditions. On this basis, this paper has proposed a new Hierarchical CNN-based ResNet architecture for image de-raining that generalizes a weakly supervised data distillation approach to improve the performance in smalldata scenarios. The first-stage encoding network can effectively capture spatial and contextual features with eight dilated convolutional blocks with residual connections and a two-layered convolutional sub-network for feature transformation within each block. To fight against data scarcity, wanted rainy-clean image pairs were created by retrieving real rain streaks from real-world rainy images, applying geometric augmentations and merging them with synthetic rain patterns to create realistic and diverse training samples. This approach enables better generalization while being data-efficient. Real-world rain generation enhances the model's ability in handling natural rainy images significantly. The performance of the model is increased with PSNR and SSIM rising from 25.72 to 26.41 and 0.78 to 0.82, respectively. The approach achieves high computational efficiency with per-image times of 0.027 seconds (CPU) and 0.006 seconds (GPU).

title: A scalable neuromorphic platform with four neural motifs featuring <1 ms Per-Neuron Latency
authors: Xiaolong Ma, Ziye Di, Jing Tian, Xianghong Zhang, Shuiyuan Wang, Peng Zhou
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The first electronic neural circuit motifs are demonstrated by integrating a 1 KB multi-gate 2D ferroelectric floating-gate transistor (MG 2D FeFGT) synapse array (20 ns switching, yield of ~99.6%) with leaky-integrate-and-fire (LIF) neuron circuits. The architecture combines four fundamental neural motifs (feedforward excitation/inhibition, feedback inhibition, mutual inhibition) and exhibits <1 ms neuron signal processing latency (NeuroSP Latency, approaching biological timescales), feature extraction, dynamic gain, and rhythm regulation. Devices show excellent uniformity (>6-bit weight states) and robust operation from -25 to 85 ℃. This work establishes a scalable platform for bionic neuromorphic systems with biological-timescale signal processing capabilities.

title: Designing an Intelligent Protection Scheme for Solar Power Plants Based on Power Prediction and Asymmetrical Fault Detection
authors: Nima Kadivaryan, Milad Jaliliyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: In recent years, the increasing penetration of solar power plants into distribution networks has posed new challenges to the performance of protective relays. The limited current injection behavior of inverters, rapid power fluctuations, and the occurrence of asymmetrical faults make digital relay settings unreliable, especially under conditions such as reverse power flow and high-impedance faults. This research proposes an adaptive protection scheme for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 M W}$</tex> solar power plant in Samangan-e-Sahneh, which is based on a combination of short-term power output prediction and negative sequence component analysis. The proposed adaptive logic is able to synchronize relay settings with the operating conditions of the network in real-time and distinguish between actual faults, transient fluctuations, and reverse power flow phenomena. The system modeling and simulation were performed in PowerFactory software based on actual data for the transformer, lines, relays, and the power profile of the power plant. The results show that the proposed method increases the speed of fault detection, reduces unnecessary tripping due to reverse power flow, and significantly improves accuracy in identifying high-impedance faults. This protective structure can be implemented in Iranian distribution networks without requiring additional hardware, by utilizing the capabilities of existing digital relays.

title: Resonance Stability and Bandwidth Variation in Fabricated X-Band Microstrip Resonator
authors: Shah Faisal, Arbab Abdur Rahim, Aneesa Razaq, Tahseen Amin Khan Qasuria, Husnul Maab
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: This paper presents the fabrication and experimental characterization of a microstrip resonator designed for X-band applications. The structure was previously simulated, exhibiting a narrow bandwidth of 152 MHz at a resonance frequency of 10.64 GHz. In this work, the same design was fabricated on a single side copper coated FR4 substrate and tested using a Vector Network Analyzer in transmission mode. The measured results show excellent agreement with the simulated data in terms of absorption and resonance frequency, while revealing a significant increase in bandwidth. Various factors contributing to this bandwidth broadening are analyzed and discussed in detail, emphasizing the influence of practical fabrication parameters on the performance of the microstrip resonator.

title: WGB-VM: Uncertainty-Aware Virtual Metrology Framework for Semiconductor Manufacturing
authors: Weiping Xie, Zixuan Huang, Yining Chen
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In semiconductor manufacturing, virtual metrology (VM) techniques have been widely adopted to enhance process monitoring and reduce measurement costs. However, most existing approaches primarily focus on point predictions while neglecting the reliability of predictions. To address this gap, we propose an uncertainty-aware VM framework based on Wasserstein Gradient Boosting Trees (WGB-VM). Experimental validation on 1329 wafers from a HDP-CVD system demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed approach, achieving an R-squared value of up to 0.616 and a negative log-likelihood score of 4.372, thereby confirming its strong predictive accuracy and uncertainty quantification capability.

title: A Graph Transformation-Driven TCAD Method for Efficient Device Characteristic Prediction
authors: Minrong Zhang, Yuxuan Zhai, Ziyi Hu, Dashan Shang, Rui Chen, Ling Li
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Deep learning continues to make significant breakthroughs in computational science, demonstrating immense potential for applications in semiconductor device simulation. This study proposes a deep learning model based on the Graph Transformer for the rapid prediction of the electrical characteristics of TCAD devices. Considering the importance of the topological relationships between nodes in the device's physical structure, a positional encoding is incorporated into the model to endow the network with a perception of the device's spatial layout. Experimental results demonstrate that our model, even when trained on a small dataset (less than 1000 samples), yields predictions with a high degree of consistency with commercial TCAD software (MSE: 8×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−4</sup>). This provides a viable new pathway to accelerate the iterative design and optimization process for semiconductor devices.

title: Graphene Inversion Layer for Enhanced Photoresponse in MXene-Si Schottky Photodiodes
authors: Muhammad Malik, Xuan Ye, Muhammad Abid Anwar, Yu Bin, Wenzhang Fang, Srikrishna Chanakya Bodepudi, Yang Xu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: MXene-semiconductor heterojunctions are highly promising platform for realizing cost-effective, broadband and multifunctional photodetectors. However, the presence of functional groups and polar surface-terminations in MXenes can induce non-uniform interface potential and large leakage current when placed over bulk semiconductors. In this work, we proposed a van der Waals interfacial engineering approach, employing CVD graphene as a p-type inversion layer between MXene (Ti<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf>) and the n-type Si interface. This modified structure (MXene/Gr-Si), significantly suppressing carrier recombination and enlarging built-in potential, leading to low dark current and enhanced photoresponse. This simple van der Waals integration of 2D layered materials with silicon open new pathways for high-speed, low-noise optoelectronic devices.

title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in Enhancing Decision-Making Processes
authors: Ammar Salamh Alrawahna, Hasan Alhanatleh, Hussain Awad, Amro Alzghoul, Imad Al Muala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in decision-making processes has revolutionized organizational operations across all industries, facilitating more informed, efficient, and precise decision-making. This document analyzes the role of AI in improving decision-making, emphasizing its applicability, advantages, and related ethical implications. Artificial intelligence methodologies, including machine learning, natural language processing, and reinforcement learning, provide many decision-making approaches, ranging from aided to entirely automated systems. These methods enhance operations by facilitating real-time data analysis, limiting human error, and improving resource allocation, resulting in substantial cost savings and increased operational efficiency. The integration of AI in decision-making presents ethical obstacles, such as algorithmic bias, transparency, and data privacy issues, which must be resolved to guarantee equitable and responsible AI implementation. The future of artificial intelligence in decision-making is contingent upon upcoming technologies such as quantum computing and edge AI, which provide accelerated and more advanced data processing capabilities. Moreover, a collaborative framework that integrates human creativity with AI's analytical prowess highlights the necessity of ethical governance to address the intricacies of AI-driven decision-making. This synthesis utilizes recent research (2020-2025) that emphasizes AI's significant influence on decision-making and highlights the necessity for ethical supervision to optimize its potential while preserving public confidence and accountability.

title: Benchmarking ML and DL Models for Bangla Sentiment Analysis in Student Movement
authors: Chayti Saha, Zannatul Mawa Koli, Dristi Saha, Yadab Sutradrar, Md Fayejullah, Umme Ayman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Sentiment analysis of social movements offers important insights into public opinion, collective feelings, and speech that is motivated by equality. Nonetheless, although being spoken by more than 230 million people, Bangla is still underrepresented in Natural Language Processing (NLP), particularly when it comes to scenarios including activism. We provide Echoes of Equity, a manually curated Bangla sentiment analysis dataset centered on Bangladesh's Anti-Discrimination Student Movement, in order to fill this vacuum. The dataset is a multilingual resource for scholars, comprising <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3, 1 6 2}$</tex> statements that are categorized as positive, negative, or neutral and accompanied by English translations. Using this dataset, we evaluate a variety of deep learning (ANN, BiLSTM) and machine learning (Naïve Bayes, Logistic Regression, SVM) models. The findings indicate that while neural architectures, especially BiLSTM, enhance classification to 79%, they have trouble with neutral and context-dependent words, whereas classic machine learning techniques achieve competitive performance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$73-76 {\%}$</tex> accuracy). This paper illustrates the function of natural language processing (NLP) in computational social science, provides a new dataset and baseline assessments, and provides tools to better understand how collective emotions are expressed in activist movements. Echoes of Equity establishes the groundwork for future studies on transformer-based sentiment models, fairness-aware AI, and cross-lingual activism by making it possible to systematically analyze texts pertaining to activism in Bangla.

title: Automated Cooked Rice Dispenser with Weight Accuracy and Spoilage Monitoring
authors: D. M. K. I. Dissanayake, H. L. Subasinghe, Kaushalya Wijayasekara, I.R Muthumala, R.D.S.K. Karunathilaka, I. M. Jafran, K. A. M. D. Jayaweera
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Cooked rice is widely served in institutional settings, yet manual portioning commonly used in cafeterias and hospitals leads to inconsistent servings, hygiene risks, and reduced operational efficiency. This study presents an IoT enabled automated cooked rice dispenser designed to address these limitations by integrating precision weight-based dispensing, thermo-regulated storage, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex>-based spoilage detection, ultrasonic volume sensing, and an intuitive HMI interface. The screw-based dispensing mechanism, controlled through real-time load-cell feedback, ensures accurate and repeatable portions, while a PID-regulated heating system maintains rice more than 65° C to prevent microbial growth. An MH-Z19B CO2 sensor enables early detection of spoilage, enhancing food safety. Experimental results demonstrate high performance, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1.3 \mathrm{g}$</tex> dispensing accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1{}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> thermal stability, and early microbial activity detection at around six hours. The system reduces serving time by over 50 % and significantly improves hygiene and consistency, offering a robust solution for modern institutional food-service automation.

title: Interannual Linkage Between LOD and ENSO: Insights From Geodetic Observations, Geophysical Models, and Climate Indices
authors: Małgorzata Wińska, Dominika Staniszewska, Justyna Śliwińska-Bronowicz, Jolanta Nastula
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: This study addresses interannual linkages between variations in Earth rotation (Length of Day, LOD), atmospheric dynamics (AAM), and key climate indices related to El Niño Southern Oscillation (ENSO) events, highlighting that the mechanisms and strength of these couplings at interannual scales are still not fully quantified or consistently characterized across different datasets. This study investigates interannual linkages between Length of Day (LOD), Atmospheric Angular Momentum (AAM), and key climate indices, including the Southern Oscillation Index (SOI), Northern Oscillation Index (NOI), Global Mean Temperature (GMT), and Global Mean Sea Level (GMSL). Using advanced statistical and spectral methods, we analyze interannual oscillations (with periods between 2 and 8 years) along with variations of geodetic, atmospheric, and climatic time series to quantify correlations, lead–lag relationships, and phase coherence with respect to El Niño Southern Oscillation (ENSO) events. Strong dynamical coupling and significant bidirectional Granger causality is observed between LOD and AAM, as well as robust in-phase correlations with major climate indices, particularly during pronounced El Niño and La Niña episodes. Our findings highlight the potential of LOD and AAM as integrative geodetic indicators for monitoring and forecasting the impacts of climate variability, underscoring the necessity of considering Earth rotation dynamics in climate system analysis.

title: Freelancer-Client Matching System with AI-Based Skill Assessment
authors: Md. Takbir Al Tanim, Md. Ataur Rahman, Mahady Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Freelancing platforms have constantly faced issues like mismatched skill and job, fake or inflated profiles, the recruiting lag, and the bias in hiring processes. In an attempt to resolve the above issues, this paper proposes an intelligent freelancer-client matching system with skill assessment. This system uses natural language processing and machine learning models to evaluate freelancer profiles and job descriptions as well as their performance data to optimize matching on freelancer job platforms. An autonomous verification module is incorporated to validate as well as authenticate the skill attributes while a real time recommender system is integrated to offer personalized job and freelancer sponsorships. Proprietary fairness-aware algorithms are incorporated to eliminate any form of demographic and geographic bias for equal opportunity employment. Other system enhancing products include conversational AI to facilitate client-freelancer communication, content analysis to determine response sentiment to evaluate and enhance trust scores, and the use of blockchain technology to issue certificates for increased trust and privacy. With a modular and scalable architecture, the system is able to migrate and customize for diverse industry demands and global markets. This approach combines accuracy, fairness and efficiency, which aims to increase client-freelancer collaborations, lower the recruitment expenditure, and improve the trustworthiness of the digital freelancing ecosystem.

title: GenoPIM: Enhancing Genome Alignment using Digital Processing-in-Memory
authors: Dhruv Kulkarni, Sai Manoj Pudukotai Dinkarrao
abstract: Genome alignment is an emerging application in the field of genomics. There have been several hardware chips that have been introduced that aim to optimize Genome sequencing, but none of them utilize Look-up table based approaches. We implement a Look-up-Table based PIM (Processing-in-Memory) chip for Dynamic Programming based algorithms, such as Needleman-Wunsch. Our results indicate up to 4.5× higher substrate-level throughput potential than a CPU baseline under an architectural upper-bound model, with substantially lower estimated energy per DP cell than contemporary PIM designs and a naive GPU baseline.

title: Personalized Tabular Transformer with Explainable AI for Mental Health Assessment
authors: Amit Hasan Sikder, Sumya Akter, Md Amir Hamja, Md. Iftekhar Hossain Tushar, Rokaiya Tasnim, Mahmudul Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: In technology workforce the mental health issues are continuously growing and impacting both employee wellbeing and organizational productivity. This study presents a predictive framework for mental health assessment using a publicly available dataset from Kaggle. A preprocessing pipeline is developed to handle missing values, normalize categorical responses, and construct composite indicators. Various ML models (LR, RF, GB, SVM, NB, DT, KNN) and DL models (MLP, LSTM, BiLSTM, TabT) are utilized, however results show only moderate performance (accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx \mathbf{7 0}-\mathbf{7 3} \boldsymbol{\%}, \mathbf{F 1} \approx \mathbf{0. 6 7}-\mathbf{0. 7 2}$</tex>) due to weak correlations in self-reported features. To mitigate this, we propose the Personalized Tabular Transformer (PTabT), which integrates feature specific embeddings, attention pooling, and token level personalization to capture heterogeneous feature interactions. PTabT achieves superior performance over others with 0.86 accuracy with minimal variance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.02$</tex>) in cross validation, F1score of 0.86, and Cohen's Kappa of 0.70. Beyond prediction, both levels of explainability are emphasized, with SHAP used for global and LIME for local interpretation. SHAP identified family_history, work_interfere, care_options, and benefits as the most influential global predictors, while LIME highlighted how protective and risk factors balance differently across individuals. These findings demonstrate that PTabT, combined with XAI, provides both reliable predictions and interpretable insights, supporting proactive decision making for HR professionals and mental health practitioners.

title: X-RF-DETR: A Framework for Explainable and Trustworthy PPE Decision Support
authors: Ezzaldeen Al-Tayar, Saleem Ahmed Al-Azazi, Mogeeb A.Saeed
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Hazardous industries such as construction report high accident rates, where non-compliance with Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) is a significant contributing factor. While AI-powered computer vision systems are increasingly used for automated monitoring, their "black box" nature often impedes trust, user adoption, and accountability. This paper proposes a novel conceptual framework, X-RF-DETR, designed to provide not only accurate but also explainable and trustworthy real-time PPE compliance monitoring to aid decision-making in occupational health and safety (OHS). The proposed framework integrates the RF-DETR Transformer-based detector with a dedicated Explainable AI (XAI) module. This module generates visual evidence to explain the model's detection results, thereby enhancing transparency and interpretability. Although conceptual, the framework’s feasibility is supported by a proposed validation plan using public PPE data and standard explainability metrics. The primary contribution is the synthesis of a high-performance detection model with an XAI layer to address the critical gap of trustworthiness in AI safety systems, aligning with AI risk management standards like ISO/IEC 23894:2023. Ultimately, the X-RF-DETR framework promises to enhance human-AI collaboration, facilitate more informed decisions for safety managers, and increase the acceptance of automated monitoring technologies in high-risk environments.

title: 28 GHz Indoor Continuous-Space Channel Measurements and AI-Enabled 6G Channel Map Construction
authors: Tianrun Qi, Cheng-Xiang Wang, Chen Huang, Junling Li, Xiping Wu, John Thompson
abstract: Conventional wireless channel measurements and modeling typically study channels via discrete spatial sampling. As the sixth-generation (6G) wireless communication places higher demands on the accuracy of channel state information, this discrete approximation becomes insufficient and motivates research on continuous-space channels. In this work, 28 GHz indoor continuous-space channel measurements are conducted, and key channel characteristics are analyzed. Based on the analysis, the necessity of continuous-space channel research is validated, and the role of channel maps is demonstrated. Furthermore, a continuous-space channel map construction method using the Graph SAmple and aggreGatE (GraphSAGE) algorithm is proposed. By the learning on spatial aggregation function rather than performing a passive weighted sum, the proposed GraphSAGE-based map construction method can reduce the performance bias caused by discrete spatial sampling and recover the continuous-space channels accurately. Continuous-space measurements are used as benchmarks to compare the performance of the GraphSAGE-based channel map. Extensive experiments confirm the superiority of the proposed GraphSAGE-based method over existing artificial intelligence (AI) algorithms, providing a robust approach for the 6G continuous-space channel map construction.

title: Real-Time Breast Cancer Classification Employing GMM-HMRF Segmentation and PCA-Optimized Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Mohammad Asif Hasan, Nafis Ahmed, Maruf Ahmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Holding one of the highest mortality rates among cancer-related deaths, breast cancer still necessitates rigorous research into advanced diagnostics. This work presents a comprehensive pipeline for breast cancer classification using mammographic imagery through classical preprocessing, intricate segmentation, extensive feature extraction, dimensionality reduction, and diverse machine learning classifiers. Encompassing two distinct datasets, the initial image refinement incorporated Adaptive Median Filtering (AMF) and Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE), augmenting lesion segmentation and image clarity. Advanced segmentation is achieved via Gaussian Mixture Models conjoined with Hidden Markov Random Fields (GMM-HMRF), fostering spatial coherence. Texture, shape, and frequency domain features are extracted and reduced via PCA while retaining 95% variance. Comparative classifier evaluation and ablation studies featuring Probabilistic Neural Network (PNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and Enhanced Neural Network (ENN) are performed. Results indicate significant accuracy improvements through the application of segmentation and PCA. Additionally, an interactive Streamlit-based web application manifests real-time predictive proficiency, bolstering prospects for clinical integration. While peak CNN accuracies in literature are higher, our method achieves competitive performance (e.g., 83.3% SVM accuracy), with strong generalizability, minimal computational cost, and improved feature-space explainability, which are critical for practical deployment in limited-resource clinical setups. The SVM classifier showed the best accuracy of 83.3%, with a 0.98 AUC, while the ENN reached 75% accuracy, with a p-value of 0.05.

title: IPnest: A Permissioned Blockchain Framework for Secure and Transparent Intellectual Property Management Using Hyperledger Fabric, NFTs, and IPFS
authors: Gazi Maliha Raisa Noor, Md. Ashikul Islam, Saha Reno, Saba Al Mukter Mahin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Managing intellectual property (IP) in the digital age presents significant challenges, including unauthorized duplication, unclear ownership, and insufficient verification mechanisms. This paper proposes a blockchain-based framework that integrates Hyperledger Fabric, Interplanetary File System (IPFS), and Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs) to ensure secure, transparent, and tamper-proof IP management. Hyperledger Fabric serves as the permissioned blockchain platform, using the RAFT (Replicated And Fault-Tolerant) consensus protocol to achieve fast, fault-tolerant agreement with low latency and moderate resource consumption. IP assets are stored off-chain using IPFS, with their metadata encapsulated in NFTs for unique identification, traceability, and automated licensing via smart contracts. The system enables decentralized IP registration, verification, and transfer while preserving content integrity. Performance analysis shows that RAFT consistently outperforms PoW, PoS, and Clique in throughput, latency, and CPU usage, making it well-suited for high-performance enterprise applications. A cost breakdown also confirms the system's affordability and scalability. Finally, command-line validation demonstrates correct ledger operations and smart contract functionality. The proposed solution offers a practical and efficient alternative to traditional IP systems, with future improvements targeting enhanced access control and support for large-scale IP ecosystems.

title: Fast CU Splitting in VVC: A DCT-Based Homogeneity-Aware Approach for Encoding Complexity Reduction
authors: Md. Zahirul Islam, Fatema Tuz Zohra, Noman Amin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The surge in demand for audiovisual content has exceeded the capabilities of existing communication systems, prompting the development of the Versatile Video Coding (VVC) standard. Compared to its predecessor, High Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC), VVC offers approximately 40% bitrate savings; however, this benefit comes at the cost of significantly increased encoding complexity. To address this challenge, we propose a Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT)-based framework aimed at enhancing the efficiency of Coding Unit (CU) partitioning. Within this framework, DCT proves highly effective in intra-frame signal representation by partitioning frames into non-overlapping homogeneous and non-homogeneous rectangular regions. Leveraging DCT's ability to capture spatial homogeneity and its computational efficiency, this work introduces a homogeneity-driven method for predicting CU split structures in VVC. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves up to a 22.29% reduction in intra-mode encoding time relative to the standalone VVC encoder, with minimal impact on bitrate and perceptual quality, as evaluated by Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM), and Video Multi-Method Assessment Fusion (VMAF) metrics.

title: xLSTM-Based Framework for Accurate Rainfall Forecasting Across Bangladesh: A Comparative Analysis with LSTM and GRU
authors: Rakib Ahammed Diptho, Pial Ghosh, Sarnali Basak
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Bangladesh is extremely vulnerable to flood and drought due to its unpredictable monsoon rainfalls. As such, accurate forecasts of rainfall will greatly aid in disaster management and agricultural planning. The study used three different advanced deep learning architectures, namely xLSTM, Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and Long Short Term Memory (LSTM), to predict rainfall based on a 70 -year historical weather data set which was gathered at 36 different locations across Bangladesh. We also addressed the common problem of class imbalance in rainfall data by using the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE). Experimental results show that xLSTM achieved the best performance with 93.86 % accuracy, 98.39 % precision, 93.96 % recall, and a 96.12 % F1 score, while the other two architectures performed lower overall. This performance improvement results from xLSTM's enhanced ability to capture long-term dependencies and nonlinear relationships within meteorological variables. Its strong predictive capability highlights its potential for developing more accurate and reliable rainfall forecasting models. Overall, our findings reflect the model's strengths and the growing advancement of deep learning in environmental prediction.

title: Common-Mode Analysis and Suppression for Asymmetrical Six-Phase PMSM Drive Under Single-Phase Open-Circuit Fault
authors: Guangyu Wang, Hongyang Liu, Zicheng Liu, Junnan You, Rongpei Zhu, Changhao Huang, Dong Jiang, Ronghai Qu
abstract: Compared with healthy electric drive systems, open-circuit faults break the symmetry of the electric drive system, thereby inducing unique common-mode (CM) characteristics. As a result, traditional definitions and analyses of CM voltage (CMV) for health-symmetrical electric drive systems are difficult to quantitatively evaluate the CM noise of fault-asymmetric electric drive systems or guide the design of suppression methods. Therefore, this article models and analyzes the CMV under single-phase bridge open-circuit fault, revealing the formation mechanism of CMV in fault-asymmetric electric drive systems and achieving an accurate definition of CMV. On this basis, an optimized CMV suppression method suitable for faulty electric drive systems is proposed. It employs a dynamic zero-sequence injection scheme to actively cancel the CMV generated by the remaining healthy bridges, and adjusts the carrier phase based on the unique CMV characteristics of the faulty phase to suppress the CMV of the system. The experimental results verify the effectiveness of the proposed CMV modeling and analysis method for the fault-asymmetric system. Meanwhile, compared with traditional methods, the proposed CMV suppression method achieves lower CMV and CM electromagnetic interference (EMI) under single-phase open-circuit fault, providing a reference for the CM evaluation and optimal design of fault-asymmetric electric drive systems.

title: Machine Learning Approaches to Classify and Predict Water Quality Status in the Surma River, Sylhet, Bangladesh
authors: Md. Anamul Hossain Chowdhury, Nishad Mahmud Opu, Md Fazla Rabbi, Nabil Mahmud Sakib, MD Shofiur Rahman, Pujan Dey Anik, Rayhan Mahmud Rakib
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Water is essential for sustaining human, animal, and ecological life. However, river water quality in Bangladesh has deteriorated in recent years, posing risks to public health, aquatic ecosystems, and long-term development. Accurate prediction of water quality is therefore critical for effective management. This study evaluates several machine learning (ML) models for water quality classification (WQC) of the Surma River in Sylhet using 669 samples collected from three sites over 18.5 years (January 2007-July 2025). Eight physicochemical parameters (pH, DO, BOD, COD, TDS, SS, EC, and temperature) were used to train six ML classifiers: KNN, SVM, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, XGBoost and LightGBM. LightGBM achieved the best performance with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 5 2} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 5 3 \%}$</tex> precision, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 5 2 \%}$</tex> recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 5 0 \%}$</tex> F1-score. To ensure robustness, 5-fold cross-validation was conducted, and a temporal hold-out validation (training on 2007-2020 and testing on 2021-2025) was performed to assess predictive capability on future data. Results indicate that the river's overall water quality is marginal, with suspended solids and temperature identified as the most influential factors affecting WQI. This study is the first to apply ML-based models to the Surma River and provides a data-driven framework to support policymakers in designing effective pollution control and sustainable water resource management strategies.

title: Set-Based Asynchronous State Estimation for Networked Switched Neural Networks
authors: Weizhong Chen, Haoxu Wang, Xiaohua Wang, Ying Zhao, Xudong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: This study addresses the zonotopic interval estimation for a class of discrete-time switched neural networks (NNs) under an event-triggered mechanism. Unlike the existing studies, it adopts zonotopic set-membership estimation and considers general asynchronism, thereby enhancing the accuracy and applicability of the estimation results. First, by constructing appropriate observer-mode-dependent Lyapunov functions, certain sufficient conditions are established to guarantee the stability and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\ell }_{\infty }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> performance of the augmented error system. Based on these conditions, we present a co-design approach for the switching signals and event-triggered nonlinear observer. Furthermore, a time-varying state zonotope is established, and the corresponding estimated bounds are derived. Finally, the efficacy of the proposed interval estimation approach is validated through two illustrative examples.

title: A Complementary Positive Feedback-Assisted Current Mirror-Based Level Shifter for Energy-Efficient Level Conversion for Wide Voltage Ranges
authors: Shin Han, Gicheol Shin, Jongmin Lee, Yoonmyung Lee
abstract: A complementary positive feedback-assisted current mirror-based level shifter (CPFLS) is proposed to reliably convert subthreshold signals to higher voltages. By adopting a positive feedback structure, the CPFLS mitigates the delay degradation and short-circuit current issues inherent in a prior art, WCMLS, due to its negative feedback design. Additionally, the CPFLS addresses the instability caused by the dynamic node in the CMLS-LED by incorporating a current-limiting circuit. Moreover, the CPFLS effectively prevents the delay degradation observed in diode-MOSFET insertion-type LSs with split-controlled inverter, when the voltage difference between the input and output is small, leveraging its positive feedback structure to maintain fast and stable operation. The measurement results from a test chip fabricated in 28-nm CMOS technology demonstrate that the CPFLS successfully converts a 0.28-V input signal to a 1.0-V nominal voltage with stable operation, achieving an 89% improvement in the measured delay compared to WCMLS. Furthermore, the CPFLS achieves up to 53%/75% faster measured delay and 63%/89% better energy-delay product (EDP) than the split-level converter (SLC) at V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {DDH}} = 1.0$</tex-math> </inline-formula>/0.7 V, respectively.

title: Flow-Induced Vibration-Based Liquid Flow Rate Measurement With Optical Carrier Microwave Interferometry
authors: Songlin Li, Ting Xue, Yan Wu, Bin Wu
abstract: Accurate liquid flow rate measurement is essential for efficient energy management and sustainable development. In this paper, a novel non-intrusive liquid flow rate measurement method based on optical carrier microwave interferometry (OCMI) combined with flow-induced vibration (FIV) is developed. The theoretical principle of FIV is presented, and the liquid flow rate mathematical model based on FIV is established. In the experiment, the sensing fiber is helically wrapped around the outer surface of the pipeline to effectively capture FIV signals. The Fabry-Perot interferometers (FPIs) are categorized into two types: sensing FPIs incorporating the helical fiber and reference FPIs without it. Experimental results demonstrate that sensing FPIs exhibit a strong response to liquid flow rate variations through pipeline vibrations, with a pronounced quadratic relationship between the dip frequency and the flow rate. The fitted curve achieves high accuracy, yielding a mean absolute error and root mean square error of less than 4 kHz and 5 kHz, respectively. The comparative analysis between sensing and reference FPIs not only corroborates the validity of the derived theoretical model but also confirms the effectiveness and reliability of the proposed approach, which presents a promising, non-intrusive solution for liquid flow rate monitoring in practical industrial applications.

title: Machine Learning Assisted Tilt/Azimuth Estimation for Passive Stylus Pens using Position-Based Model Selection
authors: Junseo Jo, Hyungcheol Shin, Youngjoo Lee
abstract: This paper presents an advanced machine learning (ML) model to calculate the tilt and azimuth angles of passive stylus pens on display panels. Based on real-world sensing data, preprocessing steps were developed to automatically capture data windows centered on the peak-value positions, and a custom convolutional neural network (CNN) was applied to estimate the tilt/azimuth information in detail. Considering the missing data samples in edge areas, novel training and inference procedures are newly introduced by adopting the position-based model selection, successfully increasing the estimation quality by assigning the dedicated CNN model per input data window. Compared to the conventional approaches, experimental results reveal that our optimization methods lead to an attractive ML-based tilt/azimuth calculation by significantly reducing the estimation errors when the input position belongs to edge regions.

title: GNNLicense: An Active Intellectual Property Protection Technique for Graph Neural Networks based on Autoencoder
authors: Mingfu Xue, Longlong Hu, Linji Zhang, Yang Shi, Liquan Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are widely utilized in areas such as social network analysis, recommendation systems, and fraud detection. Protecting the intellectual property (IP) of GNN models is crucial to avoid potential economic losses. However, existing researches on GNN IP protection rely on watermarking techniques for passive copyright verification. In this paper, we propose GNNLicense, the first active GNN IP protection and user’s identity management method against GNN piracy. The proposed method employs encoder-decoder models to encode and reconstruct graph features. Since the graph encoded by the encoder can only be decoded by the corresponding decoder, this design achieves active IP protection. In addition, the proposed method leverages fingerprint to manage and distinguish between different authorized users’ identities. The encoder is held by the user, while the decoder is retained by the service provider. The user encodes the input graph data through the encoder, and then submits it to the server for normal predictions. This mechanism makes unauthorized users can only obtain very poor model performance while ensures the normal use of authorized users. Experimental results on five graph datasets and five popular GNN models across node classification and graph classification tasks demonstrate the method’s effectiveness. Moreover, the method maintains its robustness when subjected to fine-tuning and adaptive attacks.

title: A Framework for Energy-Efficient Hybrid Transceiver Design in Multi-Hop Communications
authors: Hanyu Yang, Heng Liu, Shiqi Gong, Chengwen Xing, Nan Zhao, Dusit Niyato
abstract: In this paper, we propose a general energy efficiency (EE) optimization framework for the hybrid analog-digital transceivers design in multi-hop communication systems. The analog and digital beamforming matrices are jointly optimized considering two kinds of practical power constraint models, i.e., sum power with box eigenvalue constraints (SPBECs) and multiple weighted power constraints (MWPCs), and unit-modulus constraints on analog beamforming matrices. For both the SPBECs and MWPCs cases, to tackle the challenging problem involving highly-coupled variables, an effective decoupling approach is first proposed. Specifically, a set of auxiliary variables are introduced to equivalently transform the original problem into a decoupled form with respect to the variables of each node. Then, for each node, we propose an efficient two-stage analog and digital beamforming optimization algorithm. To be specific, we optimize the analog beamforming matrices in the first stage by jointly exploiting the matrix-monotonic optimization framework and channel-alignment strategy. Then, we optimize the digital beamforming matrices in the second stage based on the multi-node water-filling methodology. Furthermore, in order to compute the parameters involved in the multi-node water-filling solutions for the SPBECs case, we propose two novel strategies, i.e., the Dinkelbach based strategy and the per-node penalty based strategy, which derive the parameters in closed-forms and offer clear physical interpretations. Moreover, the per-node penalty based strategy is effectively extended to the MWPCs case by additionally employing the Lagrangian duality theory. Simulation results demonstrate the superior performance and high efficiency of our proposed algorithms.

title: A Narrative Review of Decision Support Approaches for Teaching Mode Selection in Higher Education: Evidence From Sri Lankan and South Asian Contexts
authors: P.A. Janendra Chamodi, Dinesh Asanka
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic accelerated the largescale adoption of online learning in higher education, intensifying institutional challenges related to selecting appropriate teaching modes for diverse academic modules. While online learning offers flexibility and accessibility, concerns regarding student engagement, learning effectiveness, and contextual suitability remain prominent when compared with traditional physical instruction. This study presents a structured narrative literature review examining how student feedback and module-specific characteristics, including credit count, interaction level, class size, and assessment type, can inform structured decision support for teaching mode selection in higher education. This study presents a structured narrative review examining how student feedback and module-specific characteristics, including credit count, interaction level, class size, and assessment type, can inform decision support for teaching mode selection in higher education. Relevant literature was identified from major academic databases and synthesized to capture key trends and insights. The reviewed studies are organized into four thematic areas: (1) student feedback and perceptions, (2) comparative effectiveness of online and physical learning, (3) analytical and computational approaches in education, and (4) Sri Lankan and South Asian contextual studies. The findings indicate that student feedback is primarily used for descriptive evaluation rather than as an input for structured decision-making. Furthermore, institutional approaches rarely integrate pedagogical parameters in a systematic manner. This review highlights the need for a feedback-driven decision support perspective that incorporates both student perceptions and module characteristics. The study contributes a conceptual foundation for developing contextaware and evidence-informed approaches to teaching mode selection in higher education.

title:
A Novel Ferroelectric-based SnO
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    FET with Non-volatility and Symmetrical Ambipolarity for In-Memory-Searching toward Edge AI
authors: Runze Han, Zening Gao, Weikai Xu, Bingrui Song, Jin Luo, Qianqian Huang, Qian Xin, Ru Huang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, a novel ferroelectric (FE) based non-volatile ambipolar FET (NV-AFET) with tin monoxide (SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf>) channel is proposed and experimentally demonstrated with enhanced symmetry and back-end-of-line (BEOL) compatibility for area-efficient in-memory-searching (IMS) system. The NV-AFET leverages the symmetrical symmetric transport properties of SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf> oxide semiconductor to achieve non-monotonic ambipolarity, enabling XNOR operator-based content addressable memory (CAM) cell within only one transistor. Moreover, the ambipolar symmetry of NV-AFET is further enhanced via synergistic optimization of the gate-channel interface and area ratio, boosting matching accuracy and enabling multi-level expansion for high area-efficiency IMS. It presents a promising solution for edge AI hardware.

title: Balancing Accuracy and Efficiency: CNN Fusion Models for Diabetic Retinopathy Screening
authors: Md Rafid Islam, Rafsan Jany, Akib Ahmed, Mohammad Ashrafuzzaman Khan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) remains a leading cause of preventable blindness, yet large-scale screening is constrained by limited specialist availability and variable image quality across devices and populations. This work investigates whether featurelevel fusion of complementary convolutional neural network (CNN) backbones can deliver accurate and efficient binary DR screening on globally sourced fundus images. Using 11,156 images pooled from five public datasets (APTOS, EyePACS, IDRiD, Messidor, and ODIR), we frame DR detection as a binary classification task and compare three pretrained models (ResNet50, EfficientNet-B0, and DenseNet121) against pairwise and tri-fusion variants. Across five independent runs, fusion consistently outperforms single backbones. The EfficientNet-B0 + DenseNet121 (Eff+Den) fusion model achieves the best overall mean performance (accuracy: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 2. 8 9 \%}$</tex>) with balanced class-wise F1-scores for normal (83.60 %) and diabetic (82.60 %) cases. While the tri-fusion is competitive, it incurs a substantially higher computational cost. Inference profiling highlights a practical trade-off: EfficientNet-B0 is the fastest (approximately 1.16 ms/image at batch size 1000), whereas the Eff+Den fusion offers a favorable accuracy-latency balance. These findings indicate that lightweight feature fusion can enhance generalization across heterogeneous datasets, supporting scalable binary DR screening workflows where both accuracy and throughput are critical.

title: Position-Aware Beam Steering and Antenna Adaptation Using LiDAR Sensing for 6G Wireless Links
authors: Praveen Kumar Sharma, Mitesh Solanki, Rashmi Jangid, Tapan Nahar, Chandrasinh Parmar
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Wireless communication systems are rapidly moving towards 6G networks, which provide ultra-reliable, high-speed, and low-latency connectivity, however signal degradation, interference, and physical barriers at high frequencies cause serious issues in urban areas. The proposed research integrates LiDAR (Light Detection and Ranging) technology with beamforming techniques to enable dynamic beam steering, optimize antenna positioning, and improve line-of-sight (LoS) predictions. An improvement in the signal to noise ratio and coverage has been shown with MATLAB simulations and the obtained results have been validated with the hardware implementation through the ESP32 microcontroller that is interfaced with a VL53L0X LiDAR sensor. The proposed approach has been very beneficial for the antenna positioning and beamforming optimization for applications such as smart cities and autonomous vehicles as it is improving the network connectivity in the extremely populated urban environments.

title: Keynote 3
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)

title: Non-Intrusive Speech Intelligibility Measures Using Multi-Metric Approach
authors: Refatul Islam Roni, Israt Jahan Muna, Nursadul Mamun
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Most existing objective speech intelligibility measures require access to a clean reference signal, which limits their use in practical acoustic environments where clean speech is usually unavailable. This motivates the need for nonintrusive intelligibility assessment methods that operate directly on noisy signals. In this work, we focus on two complementary non-intrusive intelligibility metrics-Short-Time Objective Intelligibility (STOI) and the Spectrogram Orthogonal Polynomial Measure (SOPM) and highlight their conceptual similarity in capturing speech degradation under noisy conditions. Building upon this relationship, we propose a deep learning framework that jointly predicts STOI and SOPM using an encoder, convolutional feature extractors, Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Units (BiGRU), and a self-attention mechanism to model spectral-temporal dependencies. Experimental evaluations demonstrate strong predictive performance when each metric is estimated individually (SRCC: 0.9672 for STOI and 0.9650 for SOPM), while joint prediction yields even higher correlation (SRCC: 0.9747) and lower error (MSE: 0.0019). These findings reveal that combining both intelligibility metrics improves prediction accuracy compared to learning them independently. Overall, the proposed non-intrusive approach bridges the gap between traditional reference-based evaluation and real-world acoustic conditions while demonstrating the benefit of integrated intelligibility estimation.

title: Detecting User Stances on AI-Generated YouTube Content
authors: Rohit Raj Gunti, J.M.Shalani Dilinika
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: There is a surge of digital users on several online platforms relying on AI-generated content to engage with other users or their followers. On video platforms, there are neutral or mixed opinions, leading to an uncertain user stance on the perception of AI-generated content. Understanding user stances on platforms like YouTube is essential for interpreting public discourse accurately. Hence, in this study, we analyze the viewer engagement to reflect real societal perspectives. Specifically, we collect several user interactions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 7}$</tex> highly subscribed channels that primarily rely on AI-generated content. Then, we manually code five percent of the data collected for each channel in seven themes. Parallelly, we employ a BERT-based model for semantic topic discovery and an ensemble of Large Language Models for selective stance disambiguation. Using this automated coding, all the user interactions are coded in three stances, i.e., Infavor, Against, and Unclear. Finally, we compare manual coding with automated coding to validate the reliability of automated classifications. Findings showed that there is strong evidence of reliability for themes coded and stances detected while also revealing additional user perspectives on AI on YouTube. These perspectives will contribute to enhancing the existing AI techniques employed to identify user perceptions and misinformation risks on online platforms.

title: Exploring Regional Variation in Bangla: Automating Dialect Classification Using Deep Learning
authors: Mahmudul Hasan Badhan, Rubel Ahmod, Promit Purkayastha, Sandwip Dash Talukder, Munshaibur Rahman Mahin, Md. Abu Naser Mojumder
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Language plays an important role in expressing human emotions and identity, with dialects serving as rich reflections of cultural and regional diversity. Although Bangla is the official language of Bangladesh, numerous dialects are spoken across different regions, often posing challenges in mutual understanding and communication. This research proposes a deep learning-based approach for Bangla dialect classification using character-level sequences and attention mechanisms. We manually collected a dataset from various online sources and native speakers, then preprocessed and balanced it to ensure consistent representation. Multiple deep learning models—CNN, GRU, LSTM, and BiLSTM—were implemented and evaluated. The BiLSTM with Attention model achieved the highest performance, reaching 88.43% accuracy and an F1 score of 0.88, demonstrating its effectiveness in capturing both past and future context. This research contributes to the preservation of dialectal variation in Bangla, offers a strong baseline for future studies in low-resource NLP tasks, and highlights the importance of attention-enhanced architectures in dialect classification.

title: Power Management Circuit Building Blocks Based on 2D Molybdenum Disulfide
authors: Jingde Xu, Xinrong Shan, Jieya Shang, Weijie Sun, Zhengjie Sun, Zhejia Zhang, Mingrui Ao, Xinliu He, Haojie Chen, Yuchen Tian, Yufei Song, Yuexi Wang, Yue Zhang, Zihan Xu, Xiangqi Dong, Wenzhong Bao
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This study employs a top-gate process to fabricate monolayer MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> transistors. By leveraging the work function difference between Au gate (5.1 eV) and Al gate (4.08 eV), enhancement-mode (E-mode) and depletion-mode (D-mode) devices are realized, enabling precise control over the threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</inf>). Using these devices, we fabricated a five-transistor operational transconductance amplifier (OTA), a 3-bit resistor-divider digital-to-analog converter (DAC), as well as 2-stage and 4-stage positive/negative charge pumps. The 3-bit DAC exhibits excellent linearity, while the E-D NMOS-based five-transistor OTA can be flexibly controlled via bias signals applied through the DAC. The MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> diodes exhibit a rectification ratio >10⁴. The 2-stage charge pump achieves stable output within 0.1 s under a 100 Hz clock signal, while the 4-stage charge pump demonstrates excellent stability with output fluctuation as low as 0.012 V. This research provides a viable solution for integrating two-dimensional materials into power management circuits, advancing the development of low-power system-on-chip (SoC) applications for 2D semiconductors.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Network for Inverse Design of LDMOS Devices with Ultra-high FOM via Wafer-Level Validation
authors: Yixian Song, Yan Pan, Qiao Teng, Xiaoyun Huang, Dawei Gao, Kai Xu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The design of Laterally Diffused MOSFETs (LDMOS) inherently faces a trade-off between breakdown voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</inf>) and specific on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf>). This work proposes a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) framework that embeds governing partial differential equations （PDEs） directly into the loss function, enabling accurate prediction of V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</inf> and R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf> with significantly reduced data requirements. Compared to conventional neural networks, the PINN achieves superior accuracy and generalization, maintaining interpolation and extrapolation errors below 3%. Furthermore, the differentiable nature of the model allows an inverse design approach, which efficiently identifies optimal device geometries and implantations. Finally, the model's strong practical applicability is rigorously confirmed through 12-inch full-wafer tape-out data. It achieves a superior trade-off between V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</inf> (66.5 V) and R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf> (22.6 mΩ•mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), resulting in the state-of-the-art Baliga’s figure of merit (FOM，V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</inf> <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf>) of 19.56 MV/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, demonstrating substantial reductions in both development time and computational cost.

title:
A BSIM4-based SPICE Model for MoS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Transistors Incorporating Trap Effects for Integrated Circuit Applications
authors: Jiangchuan Tian, Yu Geng, Kuanglei Chen, Xiaoyu He, He Jiang, Jiankun Xiao, Xiankun Zhang, Zheng Zhang, Yue Zhang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: For the first time, we employ the industry-standard BSIM4 framework to develop a compact model for two-dimensional (2D) molybdenum disulfide (MoS2) field-effect transistors. We present the first integration of dynamic trap effects into 2D semiconductors compact model by incorporating a dedicated RC subcircuit. This approach accurately describes the capture and emission processes of interface traps, enabling highly precise transient circuit simulations. Such simulations lead to the largest number of logic circuit types reported to date in the field of 2D integrated circuits. Furthermore, we successfully construct a comprehensive MoS2 logic cell library, demonstrating the potential of the model for the large-scale integrated circuit design. This work establishes a novel methodology for developing compact models tailored to emerging semiconductor technologies.

title:
DC bias-tunning strategy to optimize Sc
                    <sub>0.3</sub>
                    Al
                    <sub>0.7</sub>
                    N cantilever-type MEMS Speakers performance and wafer-level uniformity
authors: Prakasha Chigahalli Ramegowda, David Sze Wai Choong, Shyam Trivedi, Yul Koh
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The performance of Lead Zirconate Titanate (PZT) and Scandium-doped Aluminum Nitride (ScAlN) Piezoelectric Micromachined Ultrasonic Transducers (pMUTs) and speakers is heavily influenced by residual stresses from thin-film fabrication. In cantilever-based designs, these stresses induce significant out-of-plane deflection, causing the membrane to vibrate around a biased offset. This displacement creates structural gaps that facilitate air leakage, leading to substantial acoustic loss and degraded Sound Pressure Level (SPL). Due to the residual stress variation of a piezoelectric layer in a wafer, this offset deflection is one of the bottlenecks for the high yield manufacturing. To address this problem, this study demonstrates that applying a DC bias effectively counteracts residual stress, flattening the membrane and restoring acoustic performance. To validate this, we fabricated Sc<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf>.<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf>.<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</inf>N triangular cantilever-type speakers exhibiting high tensile residual stress. This stress resulted in upward tip deflections of 68–100 µm across the wafer, creating leakage paths. We show that a DC bias (-125 V to -175 V) can compensate for this stress, flattening the membrane at a rate of 0.5 µm/V. Experimental results confirm that optimal DC bias tuning achieves a 2× increase in tip displacement and ~12 dBSPL sound pressure increases under 2Vpp AC excitation at resonance. While DC bias significantly enhances the sound pressure and allows for frequency tuning from 13.8 kHz to 12.6 kHz, we observe that polar directivity remains largely unaffected due to diffraction at the sharp cantilever tips. This study provides a comprehensive wafer-level analysis essential for improving the manufacturability and acoustic reliability of PiezoMEMS speakers.

title: DABBiT: A Drift-Adaptive Behavioral Biometrics Framework for Continuous Authentication in Tanzanian Internet Banking
authors: Festus Edward Ndalama, Yuzo Taenaka, Patrick Mutabazi, Youki Kadobayashi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Tanzania’s internet banking sector has seen rapid growth but also an 84% rise in digital fraud, exposing the weakness of static authentication. Behavioral biometrics, which are statistical patterns in how a user interacts with a device (e.g., keystroke dynamics and mouse movements), provide continuous verification but suffer from concept drift, where user behavior changes over time or under attack. We present DABBiT (DriftAdaptive Behavioral Biometrics Framework), a system that integrates anomaly-based drift detection with adaptive classifiers to sustain authentication accuracy. Natural concept drift was evaluated using the Probabilistic Concept Drift Measure (PCDM), revealing moderate stability in keystrokes ($\mathbf{8 8 9}$ drifts; false alarm rate (FAR)=4.45 %, recovery accuracy (RA)=2.20 %, average error rate (AER)=9.01% but high volatility in mouse dynamics (8,054 drifts; FAR=3.19 %, RA=1.48 %, AER=26.40%). Adversarial drift simulations showed that the adaptive Hoeffding Adaptive Tree (HAT) and strong non-adaptive models, namely k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), consistently outperformed a static Support Vector Machine (SVM) baseline; under drift, keystrokes achieved accuracy above 0.98 (false rejection rate (FRR) ≈ 4-7%) with faster recovery for HAT, while mouse dynamics incurred longer adaptation delays (up to $\sim 250,000$ samples) and higher error rates. A survey of 107 Tanzanian users highlighted strong privacy concerns (43%) but clear support (83 %) for multi-factor authentication. These findings demonstrate that drift-aware biometrics not only detect and adapt to evolving behaviors but also enhance fraud resilience while aligning with user expectations in high-risk financial environments.

title: An Effective Cardiovascular Risk Prediction Using PCA and mRMR Feature Selection with Multi-Model Explainable AI Framework
authors: Md Ishtiaq Ahmed, Razi Amin Rizvee, Moumita Tasmim Labonno, Arpon Sarkar Choity, Tanver Ahmed, Sadekatul Shihab
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) continues to be the leading cause of death worldwide, claiming around 18 million lives each year. Predicting risk early and intervening promptly is especially important in resource-limited healthcare settings. This study introduces a machine learning framework for cardiovascular risk prediction using the Framingham Heart Study dataset, which includes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4, 2 4 0}$</tex> records. The proposed framework combines dimensional reduction technique PCA (Principal Component Analysis) and feature selection method mRMR (minimum Redundancy Maximum Relevance) with seven machine learning classifiers Extra Trees Classifier, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, CatBoost, XGBoost, Decision Tree, and Multilayer Perceptron. To make the predictions interpretable for clinicians, SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations) were applied as a dual explainable AI (XAI) framework. Overall, the framework performed best when PCA was combined with the Extra Trees Classifier, reaching 0.9763 accuracy, 0.9797 recall, 0.9780 precision, 0.9762 F1-score, and 0.9763 ROC-AUC using 10 -fold stratified cross-validation with standard deviation. XAI analysis highlighted age, systolic blood pressure, and glucose as the most important predictors. By balancing high predictive performance with interpretability, this framework provides a transparent and scalable tool for cardiovascular risk assessment, which could be particularly useful in developing regions.

title: MedViT: A Vision Transformer-Based Model for Kidney Disease Detection
authors: P H T Mandhika, S Sotheeswaran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Kidney disease remains a major global health challenge, where early and accurate diagnosis is critical for effective treatment. However, conventional diagnostic methods are often time-consuming and subject to interpretive variability, highlighting the need for automated, reliable alternatives. This study investigates the potential of transformer-based deep learning models for automated kidney disease detection, utilizing a publicly available Kaggle dataset comprising four categories: normal, cyst, tumour, and stone. We propose a MedViT transformer model-a hybrid architecture that integrates convolutional layers with transformer blocks to efficiently capture both local and global features in medical images. This strategic combination within a unified architecture enables efficient capture of fine-grained details and long-range dependencies in medical images, delivering robust classification performance across various diagnostic tasks. The model was evaluated using a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex>-fold cross-validation scheme, achieving a mean validation accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.66 \% (\pm 0.0036)$</tex> and a validation loss of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0109 (\pm 0.0120)$</tex>, demonstrating consistent robustness across all folds. Class-wise evaluation yielded high precision, recall, and F1-scores, confirming the model's ability to reliably distinguish among cysts, stones, tumours, and normal tissue with minimal variability. Comparative analysis against other transformer architectures-including ViT, Swin, and Bi-Former-shows that MedViT delivers comparable accuracy while offering notable improvements in model stability and generalization. Additionally, data augmentation was employed to enhance generalization, the dataset is limited to CT images from a single source, which may restrict the model's performance in real-world clinical settings. Future work will explore advanced augmentation strategies, multimodal data integration, and enhanced attention mechanisms to further improve classification reliability and clinical applicability.

title: Approximation and Implementation of the Lung Bio-Heat Transfer Electrical Model
authors: Ifigeneia-Galini Koulentianou, Julia Nako, Costas Psychalinos
abstract: The lung bio-heat transfer electrical equivalent which is described by a fractional-order impedance is approximated through the utilization of a curve-fitting based method. The resulting rational integer-order impedance function is realized using a Foster type-I RC network. The derived MATLAB and OrCAD PSpice simulation results confirm the validity of the introduced approximation concept as well as the correct operation of the presented circuitry.

title: E80: An Open-Source 8-bit CPU and Toolchain based on Textbook Digital Design Theory
authors: Panos Stokas, Vassilis Fotopoulos, Konstantinos Giannakopoulos
abstract: Educational soft-cores often obscure hardware execution through high-level behavioral abstractions. We present the design methodology of the E80, a single-cycle 8-bit CPU developed to bridge the gap between digital design theory and computer architecture. We discuss the decision to rely on structural VHDL without arithmetic libraries, a choice that prioritizes gate-level transparency over synthesis efficiency. Furthermore, we detail the choices we made for our instruction set architecture, our assembly language and the development of a toolchain to automate the flow of the assembler, simulator, and waveform viewer in a free open-source package, to provide “one-click” hardware simulation for novices. Synthesis across three distinct platforms validates the design’s portability and quantifies the resource-performance trade-offs inherent in an implementation that impedes the inference of vendor components.

title: Tomato Leaf Disease Classification Using RHN-EfficientNet: A Regularized Deep Learning Architecture
