We describe the modeling, hardware, and software used to measure and render IGE-based haptic feedback, and evaluate participants' ability to discriminate heights in a user study. Results show users can distinguish different ground distances using only the haptic cues, highlighting the potential of IGEbased feedback in UAV teleoperation. Finally, we present a demonstration experiment where a user is flying the UAV blindly along a path through IGE haptic feedback, showcasing our system's ability to aid navigation under low-visibility conditions.

title: Wavelet-Machine Learning analysis for combined hybrid energy sources with SVC associated microgrid protection under IoT supervision
authors: K.V.Dhanalakshmi, P.Ramakrishna Reddy, K.V.Sowmya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Designing and implementing new security systems to safeguard the power industry integrated with renewable energy sources is essential for the power sector and there is also a need to address many difficulties. With the integration of digital machines and the internet, the Internet of Things is regarded as a revolutionary technology. Wavelet techniques transform essential data into accurate coefficients. Machine learning (ML) techniques are suggested to make the digital reform process simpler. The stability and safety of the electrical system is likely to improve with machine learning techniques. The recommended method generates crucial information with wavelet-machine learning analysis for combined hybrid energy sources against a variety of faults and fault inception angles.

title: Enhancing SME Financial Accuracy through Prompt Engineering Services to Address Manual Recording Challenges (Phenomenological Study)
authors: Khalid Muhammad Alfarisi, Riza Rizqiyah
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: The majority of Indonesian Small to Medium Enterprises (SMEs) still remain on using manual financial recording despite digital tools being widespread and available to access, resulting in hindered efficiency and data inaccuracies. AI technology are currently on the rise, creating new jobs on tuning AI to fit individual needs. Earlier studies predominantly focuses on AI adoption in large enterprises, overlooking prompt engineering potentials as a bridge on smaller enterprise digitalization. This research gaps are addressed through this research by studying the lived experiences and operational workflows of seven Small to Medium Enterprise (SME) owners and accountant to identify resistance factors and adoption perquisites through a phenomenological approach. High AI enthusiasm and low technical readiness are revealed in this research, currently 70% of the participants uses manual/hybrid recording. The research also reveal that tiered prompt engineering service monetization to be crucial, it helps improving financial accuracy by 27.3% and predictability by 34.5%. This research shows scalable monetization processes that match highlevel computational intelligence with technical and resource-based limitations of emerging economies, therefore supporting SDG 8 and SDG 9.

title: A Federated Learning Framework for Quality Inspection and Anomaly Detection in AI-Enabled Manufacturing Systems for Sustainable production
authors: K. Thiruppathi, Gururama Senthilvel P, M. Arivukarasi, P. Kalaivani, G. Vennila
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This study introduces a blockchain-enabled federated learning system for intelligent, privacy-preserving quality assessment in dispersed industrial contexts. To handle non-IID and heterogeneous datasets, the suggested system uses multimodal feature extraction from visual, vibration, and temperature data with adaptive federated optimisation. Smart contracts and a blockchain trust layer avoid data tampering and model poisoning by verifying model modifications transparently. Experimental assessments employing casting, electronics, and assembly datasets show 32% accuracy, 14.2% precision, and 16.8% recall advantages over centralised models, coupled with 40% lower communication costs. Results confirm system scalability, energy efficiency, and resilience, making it suitable for real-time industrial implementation. The proposed system provides safe, interpretable, and decentralised Industry 4.0 quality assurance and predictive maintenance.

title: Hybrid NLP Framework for Detecting Anxiety and Depression on Social Platforms
authors: Prinoor Kaur Buttar, Priyal Dargan, Ritu Rani, Arun Sharma
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The fast-paced changes in society have left a drastic impact on the physical as well as the mental state of human beings. Within the previous decade, people have experienced an overwhelming turn of events such as the COVID-19 pandemic which generated a feeling of secludedness leading to a sudden rise in the number of cases of depression and anxiety. Thereby, this motivated us to conduct a comprehensive study by analyzing mental health-related text collected from raw data from multiple online sources such as Reddit, Twitter and from chatbot conversations. The initial stage involved cleaning and compiling collected data after which TF-IDF features were applied to classical models like Logistic Regression and Random Forest, sequence embeddings for LSTM, and contextual embeddings from DistilBERT. To further enhance the performance, model explainability was incorporated using LIME to interpret predictions. The results obtained suggested that DistilBERT with Logistic Regression provided an optimal solution with an accuracy of 96.66 %, hence providing a special emphasis on the semantic analysis of the text.

title: Fuzzy Logic-Based Cognitive Model for Adaptive Cybersecurity Threat Prediction
authors: Gnanajeyaraman Rajaram, M. Samanvi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The growing complexity of digital infrastructures has intensified the need for adaptive and intelligent cybersecurity mechanisms. Traditional threat detection systems often rely on rigid rules or static signatures, which struggle to respond to evolving attack patterns and uncertain data. This paper presents a Fuzzy Logic-Based Cognitive Model for Adaptive Cybersecurity Threat Prediction, designed to emulate human-like reasoning in assessing network behavior. The proposed model integrates fuzzy inference with entropy-driven learning to interpret vague and imprecise indicators of threat activity, such as irregular access frequencies, failed login attempts, and abnormal communication flows. By measuring uncertainty through entropy and processing it using fuzzy membership functions, the system continuously refines its understanding of potential risks. This adaptive reasoning framework enables dynamic adjustment of threat levels without requiring explicit retraining, offering a more resilient defense against novel attacks. The approach remains lightweight, interpretable, and suitable for real-time implementation within existing software-based monitoring systems. Evaluation focuses on accuracy, adaptability, and false alarm reduction, demonstrating that the model maintains a balanced trade-off between sensitivity and stability. Beyond its technical contribution, the study highlights how cognitive modeling principles can bridge the gap between human analytical intuition and computational threat analysis, paving the way for transparent, explainable, and self-learning cybersecurity solutions.

title: Real-Time Tracking of Flexible Markers in Low-Contrast Fluoroscopy Using a Deep Neural Network Trained Solely on Synthetic Data
authors: Tomoki Uchiyama, Yukinobu Sakata, Ryusuke Hirai, Hitoshi Ishikawa, Shinichiro Mori
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: In radiation therapy, fiducial markers implanted in a patient’s body are tracked using X-ray fluoroscopy to estimate tumor positions. However, flexible markers, such as Gold Anchor<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> (Naslund Medical AB, Sweden), deform within the body, making conventional template matching challenging. While deep learning offers a promising solution, the extensive collection and annotation of clinical data required for training pose a significant barrier to adoption. To address this, we propose a tracking framework that utilizes a lightweight Siamese CNN trained exclusively on synthetic fluoroscopy images. Our method generates synthetic data simulating diverse marker deformations under low-contrast and high-noise conditions, employs dynamic programming for stable initial detection, and performs real-time tracking with a particle filter. In evaluations using clinical data, our method achieves a tracking accuracy of 0.42 ± 0.12 pixels for prostate cancer cases and 0.97 ± 0.53 pixels for pancreatic cancer cases. This significantly outperforms conventional methods, particularly in challenging low-contrast pancreatic cancer cases. With TensorRT optimization, the framework achieves a processing speed of 3.8 ms/frame. This work presents a practical solution for high-accuracy tracking, reducing data collection costs and facilitating the use of deep learning in clinical applications.

title: Analytical Technology Infrastructure and Decision Making Efficiency in the Retail Sector
authors: Arya Kemal, Chato Meyer Alexander, Muhammad Ridho, Robertus Nugroho Perwiro Atmojo
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: This study aims to determine the extent to which the proposed model and data-driven technological aspects influence decision-making effectiveness in the retail industry. The analysis focuses on three independent variables-Big Data, Data Quality, and Data Analysis-evaluated against the dependent variable of decision-making effectiveness. The empirical findings demonstrate that all three variables have a significant impact on decision-making effectiveness, as evidenced by the hypothesis testing results: Data Analysis (T-statistics <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=4.550; \mathrm{P}$</tex>-value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.000$</tex>), Big Data (T-statistics <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=2.246; P$</tex>-value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.025$</tex>), and Data Quality (T-statistics <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=3.255; \mathrm{P}$</tex>-value <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.001$</tex>). These results provide strong empirical evidence that data analytics capabilities and data management quality play a crucial role in supporting strategic decision-making processes within the retail sector.

title: NeuroSpeechNet: Hierarchical Attention-Enhanced EEG-to-Speech Synthesis via Deep Neural Mapping
authors: Hanumanthappa H, Vidyadevi G Biradar, Megha Arakeri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: This study introduces NeuroSpeechNet, a novel end-to-end neural framework for transforming non-invasive EEG signals into intelligible speech. The approach integrates a hierarchical attention-based feature extraction mechanism, enabling the selective amplification of speech-relevant neural patterns at both local and global levels within the EEG signal. These enriched representations are processed by a deep sequence-to-sequence model employing self-attention to capture complex temporal dependencies, thus facilitating a robust mapping from neural activity to acoustic space. The output acoustic representations are synthesized into continuous speech waveforms, fully automating the EEG-to-speech process without manual feature engineering or intermediate labels. Experimental evaluations on benchmark EEG-speech datasets demonstrate that NeuroSpeechNet achieves superior performance in terms of correlation accuracy, reconstruction error, and speech intelligibility compared to classical and recent deep learning baselines. Objective metrics show notable improvements in both feature prediction and audio quality, while subjective listening tests confirm the enhanced naturalness and clarity of the synthesized speech. These findings substantiate the efficacy of attention-driven neural modeling for advancing brain-computer interface-based speech prostheses.

title: FARMARK-AI Powered Seamless Recommendation System For Farm-to-Consumers Market
authors: Nagaraj R, Sandhya M, Divyadharshini B, Janani M
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Farmers often face challenges in selling their produce at fair prices, relying heavily on middlemen, while consumers struggle to access fresh and reliable products. Farmark is a digital platform developed to bride this gap by connecting farmers directly with consumers. The platform uses smart features like AI-assisted freshness scoring and geo-mapping to ensure that produce reaches buyers in the best conditions at fair prices. Farmers can list their products, get insights on pricing, and build credibility through ratings, while consumers can easily find nearby farms, check freshness, and make informed purchases. By simplified the farm-to-table process, Farmark not only empowers farmer but also enhances trust and convenience for consumers, offering a practical solution to the challenges in today’s agricultural supply chain.

title: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence for Efficiency in Software Engineering and Banking
authors: Nikhil Teja Gurram
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: AI is changing the landscape of software development and banking, allowing us to work more efficiently, accurately, and quickly by taking advantage of real-time datadriven decision-making. In this paper, I surveyed the AI methods and tools in Software Development methodologies, including Banking. Software Development in line with the accelerated development velocity, AI along the software cycle assists in generating code automatically, predicting anomalies on already implemented code, “predictively” (not preventively) maintaining applications, testing more smartly and to optimize a project management, lowering overall time and rolling costs of development - but still providing more quality. Banking does it in the field of fraud detection and risk analysis, as well as complex data analysis / IP management products, personalized customer service, workflow automation, and quicker implementation speed backed by enhanced security to ensure a better client experience. This paper also explained real-life application areas and effects grounded in the two fields with leading AI technologies, including machine learning (ML), natural language processing (NLP), and robotic process automation (RPA). This paper addresses the struggle related to privacy, ethical concerns, regulation, and provisioned system. AI adoption is employed strategically to achieve not only operational benefits but also innovation and competitive advantage in software engineering and in banking, thereby supporting firms' capability to address continuous technological and business change.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Skin Cancer Detection Using Convolutional Neural Networks and Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Manicka Senthamarai N, Nivetha I, Kavitha V, Sureshkumar T, Durga P, Gokula Priya S
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: In the world, skin cancer is a major and most prevalent types of cancer and can pose serious health risks if not diagnosed quickly or accurately. Recently, the diagnostic methods currently used can require extensive work and the accuracy can greatly depend on the clinician performing the diagnosis which can sometimes lead to misdiagnoses. The current work introduces a Hybrid Deep Learning Framework (HDLF) incorporating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) in various types of Machine Learning (ML) classifiers for improving accuracy in disease detection. While using CNNs to obtain discriminative things in dermoscopic lesion, and extracted deepfeature images will be sent through an RF classifier and a SVM classifier for differentiation. Evaluation of the suggested framework's performance against both a CNN-based framework and an ML classification on hand-crafted features. The performances of both classification methods were provided through an experimental design. Analyses show that the proposed HDLF achieved an accuracy across classes of 93.28%, exceeding the performance of the whole CNN (85.12%), that of ML on hand-crafted (78.45%) feature classification methods, with an average AUC of 0.978 and Melanoma recall rate of 99.00%. The efficiency of the proposed system can provide an automated, scalable, efficient, and clinically feasible method to aid in the early detection in dermoscopic lesion.

title: Knowledge Distillation for Cataract Classification Using a ViT Densenet Framework
authors: Shobana T S, Rashmi K B, Megh Mayur M R, Mohd. Viquaruddin Anas, Vedant Khandelwal, Karthik N H
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Cataracts are one of the major causes of preventable blindness globally and the elderly are particularly at risk for the disease, especially in low and middle income countries. The World Health Organization(WHO) reports that cataracts cause almost half of all blindness worldwide, and it is predicted that this will rise because of aging populations and inadequate access to early healthcare services. Conventional diagnosis through slit lamp examination requires trained professionals and specialised equipment that makes large scale screening difficult in resource limited settings. Fundus imaging is a workable alternative for large-scale screening as it is inexpensive, widely available, and easy to implement. In this work, a strong binary cataract classification framework with deep learning is proposed in a Knowledge Distillation paradigm. A Vision Transformer ViT B 16 is applied as the teacher network to train a compact DenseNet 121 student model. To overcome the extreme class imbalance that exists in the ODIR 5K dataset, a balanced training corpus was developed that includes 7000 images of the fundus, 3000 of which are of a cataract case or 4000 of a non cataract case, based on clinically valid data augmentations. Model evaluation was carried out on a totally isolated test set of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2, 5 4 7}$</tex> images to ensure unbiased and reliable performance evaluation. Through the knowledge distillation process, the student model exhibited improved feature robustness and achieved a test accuracy of 98.19%. This marks a significant increase compared to the baseline DenseNet 121 model and its CLAHE enhanced version. The proposed system attained a precision of 98.96% and a sensitivity of 97.41%, both of which are particularly important for automated screening systems where reducing missed diagnoses is important. Comprehensive benchmarking against traditional machine learning techniques like PCA based Random Forest and XGBoost in addition to modern convolution neural networks architectures shows that the proposed method outperforms existing techniques. These results confirm that the knowledge acquired by transformer based models can be successfully transferred to light models, which can achieve accurate results in cataract detection in the hardware with lesser resources.

title: A DeBERTa-Based Claim Verification Framework with Integrated Retrieval and NLI Reasoning
authors: Manthri Parthiv, Kethavath Sai Naik, Ms. Prachi Dusa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: In today’s world, internet usage has gone up drastically, and we have a rapid spread of misinformation online, which is difficult for users to identify as factual content or fabricated claims, leading to large-scale public confusion and reducing trust in digital news sources. To address this issue, this paper presents a DeBERTa-based claim verification framework that integrates multi-source evidence retrieval with Natural Language Inference (NLI), which enables real-time fact-checking. The system retrieves evidence from trusted news platforms like GNews, Wikipedia, and research corpora through a hybrid pipeline that combines keyword-based and semantic search methods.The retrieved documents are then ranked and passed to a fine-tuned DeBERTa model trained on the FEVER dataset, which helps classify claims as SUPPORTS, REFUTES, or NOT ENOUGH INFO. To enhance explainability, the framework provides brief evidence snippets and an LLM-generated explanation of the model’s reasoning, thus making this system a reliable approach for fact verification in dynamic news environments.

title: Remote Healthcare Monitoring for the Aging Population: A Systematic Review of Datasets, Techniques, and Challenges
authors: Abha Tewari, Dhananjay Kalbande, Sanchi Khopkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The rapid growth of the aging population has increased the demand for innovative healthcare solutions, particularly in fall detection, vital sign monitoring, and activities of daily living. Older adults are more vulnerable to health concerns, and timely intervention through remote monitoring systems Can help mitigate the risks that come with sudden illnesses. and falls. The paper describes a systematic literature survey. (SLS) of remote monitoring technologies for elderly healthcare. focusing on the datasets, techniques, and methodologies published. During the period 2019-2025, the survey highlights the integration of deep learning models and telehealth technologies to provide continuous and real-time care to the elderly, supporting global health objectives. We analyze 15 publicly available datasets, examining their characteristics, sensor modalities and preprocessing techniques, and applicability to machine learning and deep learning. (DL) models. Main challenges include diversity of datasets, real-world Applicability, and relevant privacy concerns are discussed along with potential solutions such as multi-sensor fusion, hybrid models. and edge computing. The findings provide guidelines for researchers and practitioners, while providing a roadmap for future advances in Remote healthcare monitoring of the aging population.

title: Humanizing the Machine: A Study of Chatbot Continuance Intention in the Indonesian Banking Sector
authors: Keith Kanto, Nathanael Joshua Heriyando, Valdo Reyes Kurniadi, Santy
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: While banking chatbots are widely deployed, the drivers of long-term continuance in emerging markets remain under-researched. This study addresses this gap by integrating the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) and the Expectation-Confirmation Model (ECM) to examine chatbot's continuance usage in Indonesia. Analyzing survey data via PLS-SEM, the results show that Information Quality and Expectation Confirmation are critical to Customer Satisfaction, while Perceived Usefulness, Trust, and satisfaction most strongly predict continuance intention. The findings reveal that service quality does not significantly impact trust or satisfaction, suggesting a fundamental shift toward functional and cognitive evaluations in AI driven environments. This research demonstrates that conventional service quality metrics have limited explanatory power for autonomous agents. Practically, banks should prioritize information accuracy and expectation management over traditional service dimensions to ensure sustainable digital strategies.

title: Ephemeral Bastion: A Lightweight Zero-Trust Framework utilizing Blockchain-Backed Auditing and JIT Certificate-Based Authentication
authors: Ramu Para, S.M. Udhaya Sankar, Aswanth Babu, N Kishan Jai Soorya, G Harikumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Cloud-natives infrastructure and perennially remote administration has broadened the attack surface inside regular perimeter based security types. Static SSH keys and longlived passwords remain as a major form of credential, despite the security risk they carry for theft, brute force and lateral movement attacks. Compounding this problem, centrally managed SQL audit logs can be easily modified by privileged insiders and destroy forensic non-repudiation. In response, we present Ephemeral Bastion, a lightweight zero trusting lightweight just in time (JIT) certificate based SSH authentication and SHA-256 hash chained audit ledger. A custom Python Certificate Authority is used to issue short lived session certificates ($\Delta t=300 \mathrm{~s}$), and thus reduces the credential exposure window length by 98.4% compared with static-key deployments. Kernel level EXECVE events received by Linux auditd are sent over an append-only and single-node ledger over TLS secured sockets with 100% tamper detection. When compared with static SHH keys, SQL-based logging, and Ethereum-based private blockchain (EPB), the framework consumes 68.2% fewer resources than EPB and increases the service load balancing index by 15.57%. By having data on authenticated identities experimentally visually tied to unchangeable command data, Ephemeral Bastion paints over that blind spot to identity audit with out actually incurring the consensus costs that ground within the construct of fully decentralised blockchains.

title: Enhancing Cloud Reliability Through Multi Agents Based Self-Healing Architecture
authors: Rajat Pundir, Yuvraj Kabadwal, Sujal Thapa, Chandradeep Bhatt, Anupam Singh, Rohan Verma
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: India is one of the major economies across the world but intensively hit by a peculiar challenge which is traffic congestion - high pedestrian density, diverse mix of vehicles, and unpredictable choke points. This leads to increased waiting times, CO2 emissions, and fuel use. This system leverages real time data camera feeds to perceive the current traffic state and uses Computer Vision (CV) which acts as an eye of this system, converts this raw data into actionable data on vehicle count and speed, then model this structure into interconnected graph, to recognize these intersection a graph Neural Network(GNN) with an attention mechanism (GAT) is used; this identifies which intersection is the most significant at any given time, Structured data is transferred to the main Muti Agent (DRL) framework. Through this framework each traffic signal acts as an autonomous agent and trained with a Multi Agent Proximal Policy Optimization (MAPPO) algorithm, which helps to decentralize the control, and to learn cooperative control policies that helps to effectively work as coordinated team to minimize the congestion and delays. The proposed framework is validated through SUMO simulations that replicate the diverse traffic conditions characteristic of Indian metropolitan cities, including varied vehicle categories, seasonal demand patterns, and pedestrian flow. In a comparative analysis model resolves the contemporary RL based controller and fixed time, achieving a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 25\%$</tex> in queue length, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 15\%$</tex> reduction in estimated emission, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 20 \%$</tex> reduction in average travel time. The proposed system is modular, scalar, and infrastructure-agnostic, providing pragmatic and future-proof architecture for intelligent traffic.

title: Smart Vehicle Black Box for Enhanced Road Safety and Crash Investigation
authors: M Dinesh Kumar, R Srikumar, S Sahaya Sam, P Sharon Raj, M Indumathi, S Revathy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Growing severity of road accidents and the lack of reliable post-crash evidence require smart vehicular surveillance systems that can provide real-time analytics and forensic data storage. A smart vehicle black box design has been introduced, which combines the concepts of multi-sensor signal imprinting, physics-guided impact modeling, and embedded crash intelligence to achieve road safety and accuracy in investigation. Its system utilizes a tri-axial inertial sensor, forceenergy severity estimation, collision discrimination, and probabilistic collision, as well as on-device classification to confirm the crash events and activate the emergency communication. The accuracy of collision detection was more than 98%, and false event suppression was more than 95%, as experimentally tested. The latency of impact confirmation was less than 12 ms, whereas embedded inference took not more than 4.1 ms. The response time to dispatch emergency alerts with end-to-end was 214 ms, and the forensic data integrity was 99.97% under the high-impact conditions. The proposed framework is highly effective in enhancing crash recognition reliability, response timeliness, and evidentiary strength. It can be deployed in intelligent transportation systems that need reliable safety analytics and legally sound crash reporting.

title: Q-AuthNet: A Hybrid Quantum-Classical Neural Framework for Deepfake Authentication with Explainability
authors: Uroosa Fatima Zaidi, Rakesh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: This paper presents a Quantum-Enhanced Hybrid Machine Learning Model (QEHMLM) designed to accelerate data classification in Large-Scale Big Data Environments (LSBDE). The proposed framework integrates Quantum Computing (QC) with Classical Machine Learning Techniques (CMLT) including Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and Neural Network (Artificial-ANN) to overcome the computational and scalability limitations of classical approaches when processing High-Dimensional Datasets (HDD). The model leverages quantum feature encoding, entanglement-based transformations, and classical optimization to enhance accuracy and convergence speed. Simulations conducted using IBM Qiskit and TensorFlow Quantum on the Mobile Telecom Network Traffic Dataset validate the industrial applicability of the suggested approach in Applied Operational environment. The QEHMLM achieves notable improvements, recording 97% accuracy, 95% F1-score, and nearly 20% reduction in computational time compared to classical counterparts. These outcomes confirm that Hybrid Quantum-Classical Systems (HQCS) provide a scalable, efficient, and high-performance paradigm for next-generation Big Data analytics and intelligent computational ecosystems.

title: Multimodal Deep Learning for Dissociation Detection: Window-Based, Sequential, and Attention-Augmented Architectures
authors: Nischal V Pattedar, Punav Anirudh Potluri, Raunak Choudhury, Sravan Kumar Reddy, Deepa Gupta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Accurate assessment of dissociative states remains difficult due to dependence on subjective self-reporting and clinician interpretation. This work introduces a multimodal deep learning framework that integrates visual behavioral features, Mel-spectrogram representations of audio, and linguistic cues to enable objective dissociation detection. Seven architectures were examined, including CNN-based models, sequential models, and a lightweight Deep Neural Network (DNN) that fuses a 64-dimensional latent vector extracted from a convolutional autoencoder with 16 normalized tabular features. Among all models, an EfficientNet-B0 backbone equipped with a Squeeze-and- Excitation attention mechanism achieved the highest window- level performance with 99% accuracy. A rigorous preprocessing pipeline grounded in feature-wise knee-point analysis established a 1.5σ Z-score threshold for outlier detection, ensuring robust multimodal inputs. Overall, the proposed system highlights the potential of deep learning to support more reliable dissociation assessment.

title: Post-Quantum Cryptography Linux Distribution
authors: Varnitha Venkatesh, Shreyaa S M Kudremane, Vismitha H, Ritika S Nair, Syed Matheen Pasha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: In this research work, the classic cryptography methods on which modern digital security relies are threatened by an existential risk due to the development of quantum computing. When large-scale quantum computing emerges, algorithms such as RSA, ECC, & Diffie-Hellman, having protected data for several decades, might soon turn vulnerable. This has created a direct need across the globe to migrate to post-quantum cryptographic techniques that can also resist attacks by adversaries, both classical and quantum. In this paper, we authors use the Yocto Project to develop and deploy a PQC-enabled Linux distribution. The system presents a hybrid encryption architecture for quantum-safe file security, combining symmetric encryption using AES-256 with simulated key encapsulation based on CRYSTALS-Kyber. The creation of a special meta-layer known as meta-pqr allows Yocto to be expanded with PQC-specific recipes, system services, and cryptographic tools. A completely automated file encryption daemon transparently encrypts newly created files in real-time while monitoring filesystem events without requiring user input. Platform independence and reproducibility are assured since the entire system is designed, packaged, and tested in a controlled embedded environment built around a QEMU. With thorough functional verification, runtime evaluation, and security assessment, the proposed PQC Linux distribution exhibits reliability, low-latency encryption, stable resource consumption, and seamless integration with embedded Linux workflows. This work provides a validated, modular, and extensible operating system blueprint for PQC-enabled systems that can be used as a foundation for additional research and deployment in quantum-resilient infrastructures.

title: Industrial Worker Safety Enhancement Through IOT-Driven Fatigue Detection Using Machine Learning
authors: K. Elavarasi, D. Dhivya, B. Sabeshna
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: The Industrial Worker Safety Enhancement through IoT-Driven Fatigue Detection Using Machine Learning project is aimed at uplifting the level of occupational safety and health of workers in industrial environments by an intelligent, real-time fatigue monitoring system. The proposed system uses several IoT-based wearable sensors, like a heart rate monitor, body temperature sensor, motion sensor, and eye-blink or EEG sensor, to record the physiological and behavioural changes of the workers. The recorded data are sent by the ESP32 microcontroller to the cloud platform, where continuous monitoring is done. A machine learning model, which is already trained, compares the data from the sensors to find out the earliest signs of fatigue, stress, or any abnormal activity patterns that may lead to danger to the worker’s safety. The system sends the worker and supervisor the alerts about the detected condition that is fatigue or irregularity through mobile or webbased dashboards along with a buzzer or vibration signal for the onsite notification. The entire information is stored for subsequent analysis and prediction of the trend. By the integration of IoT-driven data acquisition with machine learning-based prediction, the system provides the means for the prevention of the employee’s interventions; thus, the fatiguerelated accidents are reduced and productivity is improved. This is a project that shows the successful and scalable approach of the management of industrial safety by the use of intelligent technology-driven fatigue detection and monitoring.

title: CHIMERA: An Agent-Based Business Intelligence Framework
authors: Muskan Sethi, Arshit Goel, Santan Ratna
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Business intelligence (BI) is a critical component of modern decision-making, enabling organizations to transform raw data into actionable insights. However, traditional BI systems often rely heavily on manual processes, specialized technical expertise, and static reporting workflows, which limit accessibility and slow down insight generation. In this paper, we present Chimera, an agent-based business intelligence framework designed to automate the end-to-end BI pipeline from raw tabular data to multi-modal analytical outputs. Chimera employs a coordinated set of specialized agents responsible for data cleaning, schema inference, automated exploratory data analysis, visualization generation, narrative summarization, and conversational querying. By integrating large language models with structured data processing, Chimera enables natural language interaction with datasets while reducing human intervention and analytical overhead. The framework produces interactive dashboards, textual summaries, and conversational insights from a single CSV input. We evaluate Chimera through qualitative analysis, structural ablation studies, and comparative evaluation against traditional BI workflows, demonstrating its potential to improve accessibility, interpretability, and efficiency in business intelligence tasks. This work contributes toward the broader goal of democratizing data analytics by lowering technical barriers and accelerating insight delivery.

title: Kullback-Leibler Adversarial Training for Robustness Against Word-Substitution Attacks in Transformer-Based Arabic Text Classifier
authors: Hanin Alshalan, Banafsheh Rekabdar
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: Transformer-based models significantly advanced natural language processing (NLP), yet they remained vulnerable to adversarial attacks. While existing research primarily focused on English, a notable gap existed in evaluating and defending models for morphologically rich languages like Arabic. To address this, we proposed a Kullback-Leibler divergence-based adversarial training method (KL-AT) to enhance the robustness of Arabic Transformer-based models against score-based wordsubstitution attacks. KL-AT regularized the learning process by aligning model predictions between clean and adversarial inputs, enabling the model to resist perturbations to the greatest extent possible. We evaluated our method using the AraBERT model on two large-scale Arabic datasets, HARD and BRAD. Experimental results showed improved classification accuracy and resilience against two strong score-based attacks, TextFooler and PWWS. Specifically, KL-AT achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 \%}$</tex> increase in accuracy under both attacks TextFooler and PWWS, respectively. These findings highlighted the effectiveness of KL-AT in defending Arabic NLP classifiers against adversarial attacks at the word level.

title: Customer Behaviour Analysis Using Data Mining Techniques with AI-Driven Recommendation System
authors: Koumudi Prasanna Dasari, Sarika Muda, Sathwika Valija, Thrisha Vijayalakshmi Chenna
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: E-commerce platforms require businesses to understand customer behavior because it enables them to provide better personalized experiences and achieve higher user engagement. The paper describes a customer behavior analysis system which combines data mining methods with an AI-powered hybrid recommendation system to investigate customer behavior and purchasing habits. The system uses customer browsing history and transaction records together with demographic information to create personalized product recommendations based on identified behavioral patterns. The system uses Python together with the Django framework and MySQL database system to handle data efficiently. The data mining process uses association rule mining and content-based filtering techniques to discover important patterns which help increase the accuracy of recommendations. The researchers tested their system using an openaccess e-commerce dataset which includes customer demographic information and their purchasing history. The results show that the hybrid model developed by the researchers achieves 91 percent recommendation prediction accuracy while successfully detecting valuable customer segments. The system enables users to analyze visual data which shows how different demographic groups make purchasing decisions. The proposed approach helps businesses achieve better marketing outcomes through enhanced customer interaction and data-based decision support.

title: Trajectory Tactics: When Transformers Learn Exploration to Generate Online Signature
authors: Anurag Pandey, Aditya Nigam, Arnav Bhavsar, Ashutosh Sharma, Basu Verma, Divya Acharya, Mohd Amir
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: The increasing need for robust digital signature verification systems has amplified interest in realistic online signature generation to counter digital forgeries. In this work, we propose a novel Decision Transformer based framework that learns Reinforcement Learning output to generate diverse online signatures. Departing from traditional RL approaches that rely on policy gradients or value function estimation, we formulate signature generation as a sequence-modeling problem. Our framework addresses varied free-form signature styles, demonstrating adaptability across linguistic and stylistic variations. Initially, an RL model generates signature trajectories, which are then fed to Decision Transformer, employing an autoregressive sequence modeling approach. To further personalize the generated signatures, we introduce a Q-learning-based module that produces user-specific variations while mitigating noise. By operating in an offline reinforcement learning setting, the proposed method reduces the dependency on extensive online interactions, improving scalability. Experimental results on a publicly available online signature dataset in multiple linguistic script styles show that our approach significantly outperforms traditional generative methods in terms of realism, variability, and mimicry accuracy. These results highlight the potential of Decision Transformers for structured sequence generation tasks beyond their conventional domains. Decision Transforme

title: An Intelligent IoT-Enabled Physiological Surveillance Framework for Early Cardiac Risk Detection and Emergency Intervention
authors: V. Gomathi, K Nandha, M Dhinesh Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The Heart Attack Monitoring and Controlling System has been proposed because it offers real-time monitoring of crucial physiological variables, such as heart rate, body temperature, heart beat rate (hbr), and SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, in a network of integrated sensors with a NodeMCU microcontroller. The wireless transmission of the data is through Wi-Fi to an Android application, which allows users to configure the individual settings of every parameter. Above and below those readings, instant alerts are sent to the patient and other emergency contacts, which is important to avert serious consequences of the situation. An Electrodermal Activity (EDA) sensor tracks the variation in the skin conductance with stress or pain, as an extra indication of patient discomfort in conjunction with vital signs. Its portability enables it to be monitored under a variety of settings, which improve patient comfort and autonomy and reduce dependence on hospitals. Taking several sensor sensors and an interactive display, this system allows the early warning against health hazards, especially heart attacks, facilitating proactive healthcare control and quick emergency calls in a dependable and convenient way.

title: Knowledge to Sight: Reasoning over Visual Attributes via Knowledge Decomposition for Abnormality Grounding
authors: Jun Li, Che Liu, Wenjia Bai, Mingxuan Liu, Rossella Arcucci, Cosmin I. Bercea, Julia A. Schnabel
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: In this work, we address the problem of grounding abnormalities in medical images, where the goal is to localize clinical findings based on textual descriptions. While generalist Vision-Language Models (VLMs) excel in natural grounding tasks, they often struggle in the medical domain due to rare, compositional, and domain-specific terms that are poorly aligned with visual patterns. Specialized medical VLMs address this challenge via large-scale domain pretraining, but at the cost of substantial annotation and computational resources. To overcome these limitations, we propose Knowledge to Sight (K2Sight), a framework that introduces structured semantic supervision by decomposing clinical concepts into interpretable visual attributes, such as shape, density, and anatomical location. These attributes are distilled from domain ontologies and encoded into concise instruction-style prompts, which guide region-text alignment during training. Unlike conventional report-level supervision, our approach explicitly bridges domain knowledge and spatial structure, enabling data-efficient training of compact models. We train compact models with 0.23B and 2B parameters using only 1.5% of the data required by state-of-the-art medical VLMs. Despite their small size and limited training data, these models achieve performance on par with or better than 7B+ medical VLMs, with up to 9.82% improvement in mAP<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">50</inf>. Code and models: https://lijunrio.github.io/K2Sight/.

title: Learning spatio-temporal feature representations for video-based gaze estimation
authors: Alexandre Personnic, Mihai Bâce
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Video-based gaze estimation methods aim to capture the inherently temporal dynamics of human eye gaze from multiple image frames. However, since models must capture both spatial and temporal relationships, performance is limited by the feature representations within a frame but also between multiple frames. We propose the Spatio-Temporal Gaze Network (ST-Gaze), a model that combines a CNN backbone with dedicated channel attention and self-attention modules to fuse eye and face features optimally. The fused features are then treated as a spatial sequence, allowing for the capture of an intra-frame context, which is then propagated through time to model inter-frame dynamics. We evaluated our method on the EVE dataset and show that ST-Gaze achieves state-of-the-art performance both with and without person-specific adaptation. Additionally, our ablation study provides further insights into the model performance, showing that preserving and modelling intra-frame spatial context with our spatio-temporal recurrence is fundamentally superior to premature spatial pooling. As such, our results pave the way towards more robust video-based gaze estimation using commonly available cameras.

title: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
authors: N/A

title: Dynamic Global Illumination for Interactive Gaussian Splatting Scenes in Real Time_supp1-3689199.mp4
authors: Sheng Li
abstract: We present a real-time pipeline to approximate global illumination in interactive or dynamic scenes, including both 3D Gaussian models and conventional meshes. Building on a formulated surface light transport model for 3D Gaussians, we address key performance challenges through a fast compound stochastic ray-tracing algorithm and a hardware 3D Gaussian rasterizer, and we implement multiple RTGI (real-time global illumination) techniques for 3D Gaussians. Our pipeline enables real-time rendering of interactive scenes featuring editable materials, lights, meshes, and 3D Gaussian models, effectively capturing multi-bounce diffuse and one-bounce glossy light transport. Our approach covers a wide range of dynamic light sources, including area lights, directional lights, and environmental lighting. Comprehensive experimental results demonstrate that our approach can efficiently render global illumination for both 3DGS models and hybrid models combining meshes and 3DGS, all in real time. Our pipeline highlights the potential of 3D Gaussians in real-time global illumination, and offers insights into performance optimization.

title: Enhancing D2D communication through RIS-assisted NOMA networks:An Underlay Framework with Imperfect SIC
authors: P Baskar, Abhijit Bhowmick, Yogesh Kumar Choukiker
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: This paper investigates a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA)-enabled device-to-device (D2D) network operating under cellular spectrum sharing with an interference constraint. In the considered network, direct links are modeled as Rayleigh fading channels to characterize the obstructed propagation environments, whereas RIS-assisted links follow Rician fading. A comprehensive analytical framework is developed to derive closed-form expressions for both the users outage, overall system outage probability, and ergodic sum-rate under both perfect and imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC). To characterize the fundamental performance limits, asymptotic expressions for outage parameters and throughput are derived in the high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) regime, revealing both achievable diversity order and throughput ceiling behavior. To capture practical deployment scenarios, a mobility-aware channel model is incorporated to evaluate system performance under dynamic user movement. Since system performance strongly depends on RIS placement, a capacity maximization problem is formulated to determine the RIS optimal location within a predefined deployment area, and an efficient optimization algorithm is proposed to obtain the solution. Simulation results shows that increasing the RIS elements significantly decreases the outage probability, while SIC imperfections introduce an outage floor, thereby limiting the achievable diversity order and creating throughput ceilings at high SNR. For example, at 6 dB SNR with a fading parameter (K) of 6 dB, the proposed RIS-NOMA scheme achieves a 23.70% overall outage improvement compared to orthogonal multiple access (OMA). Furthermore, the impact of key system parameters—including the SNR, Rician K-factor, number of RIS elements, and user velocity—on outage and throughput performance is thoroughly investigated.

title: An Improved and Provably Secure EDHOC Protocol Supporting the Extended Canetti–Krawczyk (eCK) Security Model
authors: Awaneesh Kumar Yadav, Madhusanka Liyanage, An Braeken
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Transport Layer Security (TLS) is considered to be the most used standard security protocol for the Internet of Things (IoT). However, as TLS was originally designed for computer networks, it is not optimal with respect to efficiency. Therefore, a new protocol called Object Security for Constrained RESTful Environments (OSCORE) has been standardized for securing constrained devices. Currently, the Ephemeral Diffie Hellman Over COSE (EDHOC) protocol, which is a key exchange protocol to define a session key used in OSCORE, is also in the process of being standardized. This paper shows that the four authentication modes of the EDHOC protocol are vulnerable in the extended Canetti–Krawczyk (eCK) security model, which is a common security model used in IoT. In addition, also resistance to Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks is weak. Taking this into account, we propose two new variants of EDHOC. The first variant, EDHOC2, is able to overcome both issues but has a slightly higher cost for communication, computation, storage, and energy consumption. The second variant, EDHOC3, offers only additional protection in the eCK security model and has, on average, similar, even better performance in one authentication mode, compared to EDHOC. Additionally, the Real-Or-Random (ROR) logic and Scyther validation tool are employed to ensure the security of the designed variants. Furthermore, a prototype implementation is conducted to demonstrate the real-time deployment of the designed versions.

title: Joint Activity Detection and Channel Estimation for Asynchronous mMTC: A Model-Driven GNN Approach
authors: Junyu Xie, Jing Guo, Zhong Zheng, Lou Zhao, Zesong Fei
abstract: This work investigates the joint activity detection and channel estimation (JADCE) problem in asynchronous up-link massive machine-type communications (mMTC), where a model-driven graph neural network (GNN) approach is developed under the variational inference framework. Specifically, we first formulate the JADCE problem as an equivalent channel estimation task and derive its optimal variational distribution. Then, all potential access time slots of each device are modeled as nodes to construct a homogeneous graph, and the learnable GNN layers are integrated with the variational inference process to efficiently infer the associated hyperparameters. To further reduce the computational complexity and enhance estimation performance, a residual-based aggregation strategy and a multi-task update architecture are incorporated into the GNN design. Finally, simulation results show that our approach achieves better performance in both channel estimation and activity detection compared with benchmarks, while maintaining acceptable computational complexity and strong scalability.

title: When ‘YES’ Meets &lsquo;but&rsquo;: Can AI Comprehend Contradictory Humor in Comics?_supp1-3688191.pdf
authors: Yu Yin
abstract: Understanding humor, especially when it involves complex and contradictory narratives requiring comparative reasoning ability, remains a significant challenge for large vision language models (VLMs). This limitation hinders AI's ability to engage in human-like reasoning and cultural expression. In this paper, we investigate this challenge through an in depth analysis of comics that juxtapose panels to create humor through contradictions. We introduce the YESBUT, a novel benchmark with 1,262 comic images from diverse multilingual and multicultural contexts, featuring comprehensive annotations that capture various aspects of narrative understanding. Using this benchmark, we systematically evaluate a wide range of VLMs through four complementary tasks spanning from surface content comprehension to deep narrative reasoning, with particular emphasis on comparative reasoning between contradictory elements. Our extensive experiments reveal that even the most advanced models significantly underperform compared to humans, with common failures in visual perception, key element identification, comparative analysis, and hallucinations. We further investigate text-based training strategies and social knowledge augmentation methods to enhance model performance. Our findings not only highlight critical weaknesses in VLMs' understanding of cultural and creative expressions but also provide pathways toward developing context-aware models capable of deeper narrative understanding through comparative reasoning.

title: Exponential-type variable parameter finite-time ZNN for solving joint-drift-free inverse kinematics of redundant robots
authors: Yu Zhang, Peng Chen, Xiongxiong He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper proposes an exponential-type variable parameter finite-time zeroing neural network (EVPFTZNN) that incorporates a finite-time bounded activation function (BAF). The proposed variable-parameter function exhibits exponential growth dependent on the error signal within prescribed bounds, while its boundary parameters remain fully configurable to accommodate specific application requirements. For example, most parameters are time-varying and are subject to physical constraints, meaning they cannot exceed certain maximum values. Consequently, unbounded activation functions and variable parameters that grow infinitely with time may be somewhat challenging in actual hardware implementation. The EVPFTZNN model studied in this paper is capable of circumventing this problem. Theoretical derivation proves the finite-time convergence of the EVPFTZNN and provides the analysis of its disturbance rejection capability. Furthermore, to verify the practicality of the EVPFTZNN, this paper applies it to solve the joint-drift-free inverse kinematics problem of redundant robots. Compared with several existing ZNNs, computer simulations and physical experiments validate the superior performance of the EVPFTZNN for solving joint-drift-free inverse kinematics of redundant robots.

title: Gate-All-Around Nanosheet Oxide Semiconductor Transistor with Crystalline InGaOx
authors: Masaharu Kobayashi, Anlan-Chen, Ki-Woong Park, Kota Sakai, Sunbin Hwang, Xingyu Huang, Takuya Saraya, Toshiro Hiramoto, Takanori Takahashi, Mutsunori Uenuma, Yukiharu Uraoka
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this paper, we introduce our recent research progress on gate-all-around nanosheet (GAA NS) oxide semiconductor (OS) FETs for LSI applications such as 3D DRAM and monolithic 3D integration. We demonstrated performance enhancement of poly-crystalline InOx FET by Ga-doping in ALD process and anneal with mobility of 81cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/Vs, novel technique of selective crystallization for the InGaOx in an OS stack, and process integration and device operation of a GAA NS InGaOx (IGO) FET. Transconductance of 689μS/μm has been achieved in the GAA NS IGO FETs. Bias-stress reliability is significantly improved by GAA NS FET. This work provides a direction for scalable high-performance OS FETs toward LSI applications.

title: Electrohysterogram-Based Identification of Gestational Period Variations in Uterine Electrical Activity
authors: Muhammad Omar Cheema, Anum Rashid, Zia Mohy-Ud-Din, Jahan Zeb Gul, Hassan Ali, Muhammad Zeeshan Ul Haque
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: Electrohysterography (EHG) is a noninvasive technique of measuring uterine electrophysiological activity, through which the functional maturation of the myometrium in pregnancy is assessed. This paper focuses on examining gestational age-related changes in the electrical behavior of uterine during pregnancy, through the application of high-order signal decomposition and feature selection processes. The records of EHG were obtained in 24 pregnant women (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22-43$</tex> weeks of gestation) in a BIOPAC MP 36 system under standardized clinical conditions. The records of each of the three channels were first processed by a bandpass filter (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.08-4.0 ~\text{Hz}$</tex>) to isolate uterine activity and decomposed by Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) via Continuous Wavelet Transformer (CWT) validation. Various temporal and spectral characteristics were calculated out of every mode, such as energy, root mean square (RMS), instantaneous frequency, and entropy. The statistical comparison demonstrated that the energy of the third VMD mode of Channel 1 (M3 energy) had a strong positive correlation with the gestational age (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.4528, p=0.0263$</tex>), and it showed a significant progressive increase in the energy of the uterine electrical energy at term. The KruskalWallis test (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}=0.1877$</tex>) was not significant because of a small sample, but the Cohen's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d$</tex> indicated a statistically significant change in the position of physiological escalation of contractile readiness (week 37-38, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d=-0.15$</tex>). These observations indicate that VMD-based features have the capacity to represent significant electrophysiological maturation of the uterus and indicate their possible use as quantitative biomarkers of gestational monitoring and preterm risk.

title: Securing Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks Using Quantum Sensing: Open Issues, Challenges and Future Directions
authors: Ubaid Ullah, Lubna Luxmi Dhirani
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks (UWSNs) face persistent challenges such as high attenuation, limited bandwidth, and environmental interference, which constrain their reliability and performance. This survey investigates the potential of quantum sensing technologies to overcome these limitations. We systematically review and analyze quantum-enhanced detection methods, entanglement-based communication protocols, and quantum magnetometry for underwater applications. Our study highlights how quantum techniques can conceptually improve signal detection, extend communication range, and enhance positioning accuracy. By comparing existing surveys, identifying key research gaps, and outlining a roadmap for practical implementation, this work provides a comprehensive perspective on advancing UWSNs through quantum technologies.

title: Multi-Task Deep Learning Framework for Leukemia Cell Classification and Segmentation: A Joint Learning Approach
authors: Israt Jahan Suma, MD. Ashikul Islam, Ahmad Nafees Tihami, Muslima Akter
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The classification of leukemia and segmentation of cells are tasks that have medical image analysis with the potential to enable early diagnosis and treatment. This paper propose a novel multi-task deep learning framework for leukemia cell classification and segmentation via a shared convolutional neural network backbone. We design a singular architecture that overcomes the inefficiencies of traditional single-task methods by jointly optimizing both tasks. The model proposed provides incredibly good performance with a macro-averaged F1-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 0 9 \%}$</tex> for classification across four leukemia classes (Benign, Early, Pre, Pro). It also provides segmentation performance with IoU of 55.12% and Dice score of 67.70% on test data. The model achieves 1.87 GFLOPs indicating its computational effectiveness and high accuracy for both tasks. In tests on a comprehensive leukemia dataset, our multitask method not only saves on computing time but also enhances generalization through shared features making it suitable for real-time clinical use.

title: Towards Climate-Resilient Farming: A Critical Review of Adaptive Decision Support Systems and User-Centered Design Approaches
authors: Nayab Nadeem, Amina Khalid, Marium Jalal Chaudhry, Awais Yasin
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: Due to climate change, Pakistan's agriculture has become critically vulnerable to climate related hazards, yet limited adoption has been seen regarding existing digital advisory tools due to their topdown nature and poor configuration with farmers' localized requirements. Identifying key barriers to effective decision support, this paper reviews the landscape of climate-resilient farming practices and digital agriculture in Pakistan. It has been found out that current systems are largely static, they also fail to incorporate indigenous knowledge and lack mechanisms for contextual adaptation. To address this gap, specifically for Pakistani farmers, we propose an integrated framework for an Adaptive Decision Support System (ADSS). The features of proposed farmer-centric framework are: (1) participatory incorporation of indigenous and scientific data; (2) tailored, context aware advice provided by a dynamic recommendation engine; and (3) an iterative feedback loop empowering continuous system learning from farmer decisions. By putting the ADSS as a collaborative platform rather than a prescriptive tool, farmers as active co-creators of resilience strategies are empowered by this framework. This paper delivers an actionable roadmap for developing comprehensive, adaptive digital tools to build genuine climate resilience in Pakistan's agricultural division.

title: The Effect of Halal Label on the Purchase Intentions of Thai Rice among Non-Muslim Consumers in Thailand
authors: Rizal Justian Setiawan, Praphawee Chaiwarit, Khakam Ma’Ruf, Rachkanok Sukhavalli, Patcharida Leepairojkul, Viola Indriana Sofie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: In recent years, the halal label has expanded beyond its religious foundations to represent a broader standard of product quality, hygiene, and ethical assurance. This growing perception has made halal certification increasingly relevant in multicultural and non-Muslim consumer markets. This study investigates how the presence of a halal label influences the purchase intentions of non-Muslim consumers toward Thai rice, a product deeply embedded in Thailand’s economy and cultural identity. Drawing on responses from 108 non-Muslim consumers across Thailand, selected through convenience sampling, the research employed a mixed-methods approach combining structured surveys and brief semi-structured interviews. Quantitative data were analyzed using Spearman’s rank correlation to examine the relationship between halal perception and purchase intention, while qualitative feedback provided contextual interpretation of consumer attitudes. The findings reveal that while many respondents view the halal label as a mark of cleanliness, safety, and product reliability, it does not significantly determine their purchasing decisions. Instead, practical considerations such as price and product familiarity tend to exert a stronger influence. These results suggest that the halal label functions more as a secondary cue of quality than as a primary motivator for purchase among non-Muslim consumers. The study contributes to a better understanding of how religiously derived certifications are interpreted in secular markets and provides guidance for marketers promoting Thai rice to diverse consumer segments.

title: Mel-XNet: A Dual-Architecture Ensemble for High-Accuracy Melanoma Detection with Integrated Explainability
authors: Sudip Sarker, Sourav Das, Mehedi Hassan Bappy, Fatema Rahman Niha
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Skin cancer, particularly melanoma, is a significant global health concern where early detection is paramount for patient survival. While deep learning models have shown dermatologist-level accuracy, their inherent “black-box” nature hinders clinical adoption due to a lack of transparency and trust. To address this dual challenge of accuracy and interpretability, we propose Mel-XNet, an eXplainable ensemble framework for melanoma classification. Mel-XNet integrates two powerful CNN architectures, ResNet-50 and DenseNet-121, trained using a robust 5-fold cross-validation strategy. The final classification is derived from a soft voting ensemble of the ten resulting models, leveraging their diverse feature representations. A core contribution of our framework is the integration of Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME), which provides visual justifications for the model's predictions, demystifying its decision-making process. Evaluated on a hold-out test set, Mel-XNet achieves an impressive accuracy of 95.25% and an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.9904, significantly outperforming its constituent base models. By delivering both high performance and transparent reasoning, Mel-XNet represents a significant step towards developing a trustworthy and reliable AI-powered tool for assisting clinicians in the early diagnosis of melanoma.

title: An Improved End-to-End Deep Learning-Based Approach to Steering Angle Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Md. Shakib Hasan Rudro, Faria Parvin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Autonomous vehicles rely heavily on accurate perception of roads and control systems to ensure safe navigation in a dynamic environment. The traditional modular approach to the system is developing perception, prediction, and planning in separate models then connecting them with the framework. This increases the likelihood of error propagation, computational cost, and probability of the vehicle drifting away from the lane. However, an end-to-end learning approach, where the system takes raw images as input and delivers control actions as output, can be a viable alternative. This study proposes a deep learning-based Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model and a transfer learning-based VGG16 model for end-to-end steering angle prediction and compares them to the state-of-the-art NVIDIA PilotNet baseline. Additionally, it introduces temporal information to the model to ensure vehicle stability on the lane. Microsoft AirSim was used for data acquisition, evaluation, and testing of the proposed model. During the test run, the CNN and VGG16 model achieved an autonomy of 92 % and 94 %, respectively. Both the proposed CNN and VGG16 models achieve up to 10 % higher autonomy than the NVIDIA PilotNet baseline while requiring approximately 40 % fewer training samples. This highlights the potential for CNN-based end-to-end autonomous driving systems.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)

title: AI Adoption and Organizational Agility: Rethinking Business Development for the Digital Era
authors: Abeer Tarawneh, Mohammed H. M. Qeshta, Mohammed Abusweilem, Amineh A. Khaddam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper seeks to respond to the issue of urgency of the application of artificial intelligence (AI) in altering the approach of business development in the digital era. It puts the implementation of AI as one of the contributors in enhancing organizational agility, the capacity to sense and respond swiftly to change, and consequently prosper in turbulent and unpredictable markets. We synthesize the already available literature on AI adoption and organizational agility to come up with a new conceptual model. The model assumes that AI-based capabilities (e.g. automation, decision-making augmentation, generative AI) may help an organization to be more agile strategically, operationally, and on the marketplace, which in turn will result in the realization of the quality of business development. The article introduces a new model, which relates the adoption of AI, agility, and the business development outcomes. What we propose is that the adoption of AI enhances agility (e.g. speed, flexibility, adaptability), and that agility mediates AI effects on business development success, and situational considerations like organizational culture and leadership preparedness precondition agility. This theoretical work sheds light on the AI-agility nexus, that can be utilized in subsequent research works and practitioners as a roadmap in reconsidering the business development in the digital age.

title: Exploring Key Drivers of User Satisfaction in Mobile E-Grocery Applications: A Comprehensive Analysis
authors: Muhammad Lizuardi, Sastra Dinar Bayu Sasmita, Christopher Joshua Leksana, Stefanus Rumangkit
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study seeks to examine the determinants of user satisfaction in post-purchase behavior of mobile e-grocery applications by investigating Performance Expectancy, Effort Expectancy, Behavioral Intention, and Intention to Recommend. A quantitative methodology was utilized, relying on primary data as the principal source. Data were gathered via an online questionnaire. The sample had 256 respondents, as calculated by SPSS Sample Power. This research employed a stratified random sample method, analyzed with SPSS software. The analysis utilized Partial Least Squares-Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The results demonstrate that performance expectancy and effort expectancy do not significantly affect behavioral intention. perceived usefulness significantly positively affects behavioral intention, which in turn significantly and positively effects the intention to recommend.

title: On the Characterization and Limits of 4D Radar for Aided Inertial Navigation
authors: Morten Nissov, Kostas Alexis
abstract: Frequency Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW) radar is a promising sensor for aided inertial navigation, due to its robustness in environments that challenge traditional alternatives, such as LiDAR and vision. However, its widespread adoption is hindered by complex, noisy measurements, which make reliable estimation difficult. This manuscript addresses these challenges by analyzing the fundamental measurement relations of FMCW radar sensing and developing a reliable estimator. Noise models are derived by applying first principles to the underlying signal processing of a typical radar sensor. These models guide the design of a factor graph-based estimator, utilizing a first-order approximation for the measurement noise propagation. The approach is first examined through simulation, evaluating the significance of different noise sources, the validity of the first-order approximation, and the state-dependent nature of the covariance expressions. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superior robustness and accuracy of the proposed method across diverse field environments and flight profiles, including beyond the radar’s standard operating range. Furthermore, the experiments confirm the insights from the simulation regarding the behavior and performance of different estimator configurations relative to their operating conditions. The evaluation data and estimator implementation are made available at https://github.com/ntnu-arl/rig.

title: Low Crosstalk Hollow-Cladding Multicore Fiber for Wideband 600+ OAM Modes Transmission
authors: Rosni Sayed, Abdul Khaleque, Md. Sarwar Hosen
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: A hollow-cladding multicore fiber (HC-MCF) is proposed, specifically engineered to support efficient orbital angular momentum (OAM) mode transmission. The design employs several high-index ring cores in a hollow cladding platform, yielding strong index contrast along with enhanced mode confinement. The fiber supports 636 modes at 1550 nm while maintaining 612 modes across the entire O-L bands. Numerical results demonstrate the adjacent ring crosstalk beneath −97 dB after 100 km propagation, with an effective index difference above <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.64 \times 10^{-4}$</tex>, ensuring suppressed inter-core coupling. The fiber also offers a large effective mode area, minimum confinement loss, and mode purities exceeding 99%. These features establish the proposed design as a formidable contender in the forthcoming, high-capacity optical networks of communication.

title: Pricing Transport Infrastructure in Thailand: Evidence from Multifactor Asset Pricing Models
authors: Tran Trong Huynh, Bui Thanh Khoa, Duong Kim Ngoc
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study investigates the asset pricing behavior of the Transport Infrastructure sector in Thailand over the period January 2009 to September 2025, a period characterized by major economic transitions, infrastructure expansion, and post-pandemic recovery. Using monthly adjusted returns of 17 listed transport infrastructure firms from the Stock Exchange of Thailand (SET) and the 1-year government bond yield as the risk-free rate, the study applies four benchmark asset pricing models, including CAPM, Fama–French three-factor (FF3), Carhart four-factor (FF4), and Fama–French five-factor (FF5) with Newey–West corrections. While numerous studies have examined infrastructure valuation in developed economies, empirical evidence from emerging ASEAN markets remains limited, particularly concerning whether regulated infrastructure assets generate systematic pricing anomalies. This study fills that gap by testing for the presence of an "infrastructure premium" within Thailand’s capital market using a multifactor framework. The regression results reveal a positive and statistically significant alpha across all models, while the market factor remains the dominant driver of returns. Other factors exhibit limited explanatory power, reflecting the stable, regulated, and capital-intensive nature of infrastructure firms. Robustness tests controlling for liquidity, capitalization weighting, and the COVID-19 subperiod confirm the persistence of the positive alpha..

title: I Will Try to Fix You: Large Language Models for Mobile GUI Test Repair
authors: Tommaso Fulcini, Alessandro Poletti, Anna Arnaudo, Riccardo Coppola
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
