authors: Nilesh Dnyaneshwar Bhandarwar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The rapid escalation of zero-day attacks has become one of the most critical threats in modern digital ecosystems, exposing vulnerabilities even in advanced cybersecurity infrastructures. These unpredictable and evolving attacks demand adaptive, intelligent, and proactive defense mechanisms capable of identifying zero-day attack. To address this challenge, this paper introduces a hybrid generative AI-powered framework named FusionGuardNet, with the DualInstinct Optimizer (FGN-DIO), designed to enhance the predictability and resilience of next-generation cybersecurity systems. The proposed FusionGuardNet integrates multiple deep learning principles, enabling simultaneous learning of spatial, temporal, and contextual dependencies for robust pattern recognition. To achieve efficient convergence and adaptive parameter optimization, the DualInstinct Optimizer, combining swarm intelligence and cooperative search strategies, ensures stable learning and superior generalization. Experimental evaluation on the CICIDS2017 dataset demonstrates that the proposed model achieves higher accuracy (97%), precision (97%), recall (97%), and F1-score (97%) compared to conventional techniques. Overall, the FGN-DIO provides a scalable and intelligent solution for zero-day attack prediction, establishing a new direction for proactive and resilient next-generation cybersecurity systems.

title: An Adaptive Quantum Key Distribution Framework Using Fuzzy Reinforcement Learning for Secure Communication
authors: D Nagarajan, J C Kavitha, Subitha D
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) has the main advantage of providing unconditional security, which is a consequence of the fundamental principles of quantum mechanics. For decades, key quantum distribution system has been the technological heart of security systems. However, the existing real QKD approaches have performance limitations. The channel experiences interference, photon loss, and fluctuations in eavesdropping conditions that reduce the efficiency and reliability of key generation. To address these issues, a fuzzy reinforcement learning-enhanced quantum key distribution (FRL-QKD) system is proposed to obtain secure and adaptable quantum communications. The integrated network combines fuzzy logic with reinforcement learning (RL) to expedite the localization of the photon polarization basis selection, control of key rates, and identification of error correction thresholds for optimized key generation. Fuzzy inference mechanisms are utilized within the FRL-QKD model to interpret an ambiguous environment. This first-stage implementation of the symbiosis of quantum cryptography, fuzzy intelligence and adaptive learning is a potent resource for future quantum-secure communications.

title: BHARTI Brain Hyperspectral Attention ResNet U-Net with Transparent Interpretation Using Grad-CAM and Guided Grad-CAM
authors: Hariharan R, Anna Sian, Parv Gupta, Harsh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Accurate brain tumor segmentation from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is vital for diagnosis, surgical planning, and patient monitoring. Manual annotation is laborious and subject to observer variability, highlighting the need for automated methods. While deep learning models such as U-Net have achieved strong results, their limited interpretability reduces clinical trust. This work presents an interpretable segmentation framework that combines an Attention Residual U-Net with explainable AI techniques. Residual connections ensure stable training, while attention gates enhance tumor localization. A lightweight preprocessing pipeline employing CLAHE, Otsu’s thresholding, and morphological operations is introduced for skull removal and image enhancement. Data scarcity is addressed through synthetic augmentation using random rotations and Gaussian noise. Model transparency is achieved via Grad-CAM and Guided Grad-CAM, which generate multi scale heatmaps to visualize decision critical regions. Experimental results demonstrate that the framework achieves nearly 98.915% training accuracy with a reduced loss of 0.045. The Dice coefficient (0.919) and IoU score (0.850) demonstrate strong segmentation performance, enabling accurate isolation of brain structures and precise tumor boundary delineation. By combining spectral spatial learning with explainability, the method remains robust to noise and intensity variations while producing interpretable results consistent with radiological assessment.

title: An Approach for the Classification of Images of Different Spatial Sizes
authors: Nitish Singla, Tammam Tillo
abstract: Convolutional Neural Networks are widely used for computer vision tasks especially for image classification and recognition. There is always the need to classify images of various spatial sizes without having to train the model again and again. There are various approaches that can be used to classify images of various spatial sizes such as designing the model from scratch for a certain image size or using an image resizing tool. In this paper, an efficient approach has been presented. The main idea is to have one trained network without the need for a resizing tool. The framework has been developed to restructure the input unit of a convolutional neural networks and train the restructured network on images of small sizes thus to try to reduce the amount of training. Various experiments are done on this proposed framework. To test the proposed framework, experiments have been done using various batch sizes and learning rates for different epochs.

title: An Evolutionary Optimized Explainable AI Framework for Accurate and Reliable Diabetic Retinopathy Severity Classification
authors: Vivekanand Aelgani, Akansha Singh, V. A. Narayana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is the primary cause of irreversible blindness among individuals with diabetes. Early identification of DR severity can significantly help to maintain people's eye health and prevent irreparable damage to the eye. Retinal images are generally blurry and dull, which makes it more challenging to make predictions in advance. It is hard to find DR severity level because there aren't enough skilled retina doctors. To address the issues in diabetic retinopathy (DR) diagnosis, we present XDRnet. This accurate and reliable deep learning model utilises a residual inception network as its backbone, enabling efficient detection of both minor and major structural abnormalities. The proposed model is fine-tuned using the Crested Porcupine Chaotic Lévy Optimisation (CPCLO) algorithm. The LIME explainer is used to explain the XDRnet model predictions. When tested on the EyePACS dataset, XDRnet achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.3 \pm 0.2 \%$</tex> accuracy and an AUC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.63 \pm 0.01$</tex>, outperforming several commonly used benchmarks. More crucially, it made these improvements in an efficient manner, which means it can be easily integrated into real-world screening procedures, primarily when a reliable automated system assists doctors in their work without compromising the quality of diagnosis or putting patients at risk.

title: Investigation of Switching Characteristics of Ferroelectric Tunnel Junctions by TCAD Simulation
authors: Jingchao Lei, Qiang Zhang, Di Lu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: A systematic, quantitative study of switching characteristics of ferroelectric tunneling junctions (FTJs) is investigated by using technology computer-aided design (TCAD) simulations. This study reveals the relationship between the on-off ratio, one of the most key FTJ performance factor, and multiple design factors, such as work function / carrier concentration of the electrodes, and electron affinity / remnant polarization (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf>) of the ferroelectric layer. Given that the remnant polarization of the ultrathin ferroelectric layer has a fundamental significance for the performance of the FTJs yet difficult to be directly measured, we show the deduction of the P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</inf> of actual Cu-BaTiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>-La<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.67</inf>Sr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.33</inf>MnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> FTJs by TCAD simulation, as an application. Furthermore, a MOSFET-FTJ 1 transistor - 1 resistor (1T1R) structure was simulated, providing references for the future construction of FTJ arrays in practical applications.

title: Forecasting India's Exports to the United States Under Exchange Rate Fluctuations Using VECM and LSTM Models
authors: Dulanji Samarakoon, Dilushika Anton Michael, Ruwantha Perera, Yasmi Rajapaksha, Kavindi Vinurika, Anuradha Hewaarachchi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: As a leading economy in South Asia, India plays a central role in shaping the region's trade dynamics, making its export patterns and currency movements highly influential for neighbouring countries and overall regional economic stability. This study explores the relationship between Indian exports to the United States and the USD/INR exchange rate. These two factors have a strong positive relationship with a correlation of 0.83, suggesting a potential long-run relationship suitable for multivariate modelling. A Vector Error Correction Model (VECM) was initially employed to analyse this relationship, but the results were unreliable. Alternatively, a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) deep learning model was used, which better captured the nonlinear patterns and offered higher performance accuracy with an R -squared of 0.7536. The findings highlight how currency changes can affect export performance, offering useful insights for business leaders, investors, traders, and policymakers not only in India but also in South Asian countries, especially Sri Lanka and Bangladesh. This research supports the idea that combining classical statistical models with modern methods can improve understanding of real-world economic trends. The study also opens the door for future research on how changes in U.S. trade policies and tariffs may affect exports from South Asia across different countries.

title: DMX: A Weighted Softmax Fusion of Heterogeneous CNNs for Burmese Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Faruk Ahmed, Saifuddin Sagor, Dewan Mamun Raza, Md Masum Billah
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Burmese grape (Baccaurea ramiflora) crops are highly susceptible to leaf diseases, which significantly impact yield and quality. Accurate and early detection of these diseases is critical, yet conventional visual inspection and single-model deep learning approaches often lack robustness, fail to generalize under natural field conditions, and are sensitive to class imbalance. To overcome these challenges, we propose DMX, a novel weighted softmax score fusion framework that strategically combines three complementary convolutional neural networks, DenseNet201, MobileNetV2, and Xception, leveraging their unique strengths in depth, efficiency, and feature extraction. The framework assigns optimized weights to individual model predictions, integrating diverse feature representations for enhanced robustness and precision. A primary dataset of 4,063 field-collected images was preprocessed with resizing to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$224 \times 224 \times 3$</tex> pixels, normalization, and quality enhancement, and class imbalance was mitigated using SMOTE. Comprehensive ablation studies highlight the contribution of each base model and input resolution, confirming the superiority of the weighted ensemble over single models. Experimental results demonstrate that DMX achieves 99.33 % overall accuracy, with per-class F1-scores up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 0 0}$</tex>, outperforming all individual models. These findings establish DMX as a highly precise, computationally efficient, and generalizable framework, offering a practical solution for real-world deployment in smart agriculture.

title: Development of Quantum Computers with Electron Spins in Silicon Quantum Dots
authors: Takashi Nakajima
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Silicon is an excellent platform for integrating billions of quantum dots that serve as artificial atoms on a chip. We aim to use the electron spin state in a quantum dot as a qubit to realize a quantum computer, which potentially outperforms traditional supercomputers in certain tasks. However, practical quantum computation requires precise control of fragile qubit states and quantum error correction. We have investigated dominant error sources and implemented qubit control with low error rates, which opens the path toward fault-tolerant quantum computation.

title: Pre-Implementation Readiness for AI in the Success of ERP Implementation: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: N.T.D.T. Sandamini, T.D.K.L. Galappaththi, K.D.N. Dhananjana, M.B.J.M.P.N. Jayasekara, Ruwan Wickramarachchi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems is reshaping enterprise operations, yet implementation failure rates remain high due to inadequate pre-implementation readiness. This study conducts a systematic literature review to examine readiness factors influencing the success of AI-enabled ERP implementations. Based on the analysis of 39 peer-reviewed studies published between 2014 and 2025, five dominant readiness dimensions are identified: technological, organizational, environmental, agile-process, and AI-ethical readiness. These dimensions are synthesized into an integrated Technology, Organization, Environment, Agility, AI Readiness (TOE-A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>IR) framework, extending traditional ERP readiness models by incorporating adaptability and ethical governance aligned with Industry 5.0 principles. The findings indicate that readiness for AI-enabled ERP is not a static condition, but a dynamic, multidimensional capability shaped by technology, leadership, agility, and ethical awareness. This study contributes by consolidating fragmented literature, advancing readiness theory for intelligent ERP systems, and offering a structured framework to support informed and responsible AI-driven ERP transformations.

title: The Museum That Feels: AI-Driven Affective Computing for Emotion-Adaptive AR Experiences
authors: Eleftheria Iakovaki, Markos Konstantakis, John Aliprantis, Konstantinos Ordoumpozanis, Stavroula Pyroti, Athanasios Angioplastis, Alkiviadis Tsimpiris
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: This paper presents a conceptual and architectural framework that integrates Artificial Intelligence (AI), affective computing, and augmented reality (AR) to enable emotionadaptive cultural heritage experiences. Building upon advances in deep learning and human-AI interaction, the proposed system introduces a non-intrusive and ethically aligned approach capable of recognizing visitors’ affective states in real time and dynamically adapting digital narratives and immersive visualizations. The framework extends traditional personalization by embedding emotion recognition, context awareness, and adaptive reasoning directly into the cultural heritage experience, transforming static interpretation into an interactive, empathic dialogue between users and content.A comprehensive review of the state of the art situates this proposal within current research on smart museums, affective analytics, and AR-driven storytelling. The envisioned system architecture includes multimodal sensing, CNN-based emotion analysis, reinforcement-learning-based content adaptation, and AR rendering through mobile or head-mounted devices. Although conceptual, the framework outlines a roadmap for developing emotionally responsive and human-centered AI systems in cultural heritage environments. It contributes a structured model for future empirical validation and highlights the potential of AI to augment empathy, engagement, and inclusivity while adhering to principles of ethics, privacy, and open science.

title: Profit-Optimal Machine Learning for Purchase Intent Prediction: Calibration, Temporal Validation, and Economic Constraints
authors: Rakib Hossain, Lasker Ershad Ali, Kazi Shah Nawaz Ripon, Md. Farhad Bulbul, Md. Mehedi Hasan Tonmoy
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Predicting customer purchase intent is a core machine learning task with direct financial impact. Conventional metrics such as the area under the ROC curve (AUC) or the F1-score focus on discriminative ability rather than the business goal of maximising economic return. This work proposes a decision-analytic framework that combines four standard classifiers, logistic regression, linear support vector machines (SVM), random forests, and gradient boosting, with post-hoc calibration (Platt scaling and isotonic regression) and three often overlooked components: probabilistic calibration, temporally aware validation, and explicit economic constraints. We formulate purchase-intent prediction as maximising expected profit under margin, contact cost, and capacity limits, and use realised net benefit (RNB) and decision curve analysis (DCA) for profit-aware threshold selection. On the UCI Online Shoppers dataset, random forests achieve the strongest predictive ranking, gradient boosting with isotonic calibration provides the most reliable probability estimates, and linear SVM yields the highest profit under baseline economics. Sensitivity analyses show that calibration choices and economic context can matter more than small AUC gains, underlining that calibrated probabilities, temporal validation, and profit-aware evaluation are essential for business-ready intent prediction systems.

title: Optimization and Benchmarking of Distribution Costs in a Sri Lankan Paint Manufacturing Company Using Vehicle Routing Problem Method
authors: WDT Alwis, PM Edirisinghe
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: This research examines the application of Vehicle Routing Problem (VRP) algorithms to optimize the distribution operations of a well-recognized Paint manufacturer in Sri Lanka. The company's logistics relied generally on a manual process, and the logistics were done based on 21 regions island wide. Even though, the process is operationally smooth, it is inefficient in terms of mileage, fleet utilization, and fuel consumption. By using the Clarke-Wright Savings Algorithm and the Sweep Algorithm, two well established heuristic approaches are considered for this study in terms of developing a theoretical optimization model that tailored to the company's specific operational context and to provide a benchmark for the logistical operational cost of the company. Further, it gives an insight into the digital tools that can be utilized in their route planning. Substantial efficiency gains were revealed by a two-month comparative analysis of operational data. The data reported that in February, optimized routing achieved a 38.82% of mileage reduction <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(45,680 \text{km}$</tex> saved), while in the month of March, the figure increased up to 56.07% where it saved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$91,735 \text{km}$</tex>. It also contributed to reduce the number of trucks used in logistic operations and refined algorithmic performance. These savings are translated into fuel reductions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7, 0 2 7}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 4, 1 1 3}$</tex> liters, respectively. Moreover, it amounted to cost saving of over 7 million rupees. Additionally, the optimized model suggested a potential 23.8% reduction in fleet size, importantly lowering maintenance, labor, and operational overhead. This study concludes that a hybrid approach while combining algorithmic planning with human operational insight. That can also preserve the company's service reliability and achieve long-term efficiency, cost savings, and sustainability.

title: Deep Learning Based Crack Detection MLOps Pipeline using Incremental Learning
authors: Samarth Rai, Roshni Mathur, Ahmed Saad, Marwah Tonse, Imran Zualkernan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Infrastructure crack detection is critical for public safety and structural integrity assessment. Traditional methods are labor-intensive, time-consuming, and prone to human error. This paper presents a scalable Machine Learning Operations (MLOps) pipeline leveraging deep learning and incremental learning for automated crack detection in concrete surfaces. We developed an end-to-end system comprising a mobile application, backend server, middleware, and a deep learning model trained on the SDNET2018 dataset. The key innovation lies in implementing incremental learning through knowledge distillation, enabling continuous model improvement without complete retraining. Our approach achieved 86.78% accuracy on SDNET2018 while reducing training time by 70.9% compared to batch retraining, demonstrating the feasibility of deploying machine learning models in production environments with limited initial data.

title: Sentiment Prediction for Market Volatility
authors: Meet Amin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: This undertaking offers an automated framework for creating sentiment metrics from SEC 10-K filings, directing to predict stock market yields and volatility at the sector, portfolio, and stable levels. The system contains two core models: an SEC Filing Extraction Model, which preprocesses filings, and a Supervised Lexicon Learning Model, which examines sentiment employing a four-step process. This incorporates recognizing sentiment-related terms, allocating forecasting weights, combining sentiment scores, and employing the Kalman Filter for progression analysis. Empirical results display the performance of sentiment metrics from 10-K filings, notably the Item 1A risk component segment, in forecasting market shifts.

title: A Wearable Inertial Sensing Platform for Personalized Health and Motion Monitoring
authors: Sachintha Adhikari, Praveen Rathnayaka, Sanjula Pathirana, Thevindu Dilmith Samarasekera, Roshan Godaliyadda, Parakrama Ekanayake, Chanaka Senanayake, Asela Ratnayake, Vijitha Herath
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Accurate measurement of human joint kinematics is fundamental to modern health diagnostics, rehabilitation, and performance optimization. Although vision-based motion capture systems can achieve high accuracy in controlled laboratory environments, their reliance on cameras, controlled lighting, and unobstructed line-of-sight limits their ability to accurately capture true biomechanical motion, including segment-level kinematics and coordinated muscle group responses during dynamic tasks. To address these limitations, this paper presents a synchronized inertial measurement unit (IMU)-based wearable platform for field-deployable joint angle analysis in wearable motion monitoring applications. The proposed system integrates high-rate inertial sensors, precision timing, a real-time operating system, and a high-speed local data logging framework to ensure temporally consistent and lossless biomechanical data acquisition. Sensor orientation is estimated using a quaternion-based Extended Kalman filtering algorithm, and relative joint angles are computed from synchronized body segment orientations. Experimental validation involved 2D planar motion and a clean and jerk task to extract knee and elbow joint angles for evaluation of system performance. Accuracy was assessed by comparing IMU derived joint angles with those obtained from a video-based pose estimation method. The preliminary results of the tests showed a comparable trajectory in controlled experimental environments. These findings provide preliminary support for the feasibility of the proposed wearable framework for motion analytics beyond laboratory environments.

title: Transparent Quality Assurance for Low-Code/No-Code Workflows via Explainable AI and Automated Test Generation
authors: W. M. N. S. K. Yatigammana
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Low-code/no-code (LC/NC) platforms speed up application delivery through visual workflows and reusable components, but their abstraction and limited observability make rigorous quality assurance challenging, especially for boundary conditions, exception handling, and stakeholder-friendly defect diagnosis. This paper presents an AI-driven Explainable Testing Framework for systematic and transparent validation of LC/NC workflows without relying on traditional source-code-centric methods. The framework (i) extracts visual workflow logic into a platform-neutral intermediate representation, (ii) automatically generates and executes functional, boundary, and exception-oriented test cases for comprehensive path exploration, and (iii) converts execution traces into multi-modal explanations using an explainable AI reasoning engine and role-specific interfaces for citizen developers, QA engineers, and executives. A prototype implemented on Microsoft PowerApps workflows shows strong extraction reliability across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=147$</tex> workflows and generates an average of 24 test cases per workflow while reducing redundant executions. The explainability module also achieves high-quality results in readability, trace fidelity, decision-point coverage, and workflow-to-visual correspondence, helping stakeholders understand not only what failed, but why. Comparative evaluation against manual and baseline scripted validation shows better defect detection, lower testing and resolution time, and improved stakeholder understanding. Overall, the approach strengthens LC/NC quality assurance by combining automated rigor with stakeholder-oriented transparency, supporting governance-aligned adoption of citizen-developed workflows in enterprise settings. In doing so, it helps bridge the gap between automated workflow validation and stakeholder interpretability, enabling more evidence-driven release readiness in LC/NC systems.

title: Privacy-Preserving Real-Time Crowd Counting on IoT Enabled Edge Devices Using YOLOv8n and Norfair
authors: W.T.D. Thilini Hansika, M.A. Pramod Madhawa, N.T.M. Sajith, M.N.M. Aashiq, M.N.A. Hinas
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The implementation of smart crowd management and intelligent infrastructure systems necessitates real-time crowd monitoring to ensure safety, efficiency and responsiveness. Recent developments in deep learning methods and IoT devices have opened new avenues for addressing real-world problems. This study presents a lightweight framework for real-time crowd counting, that utilizes YOLOv8n model for human head detection and the Norfair tracking algorithm for multi-object tracking. The complete system is deployed on a Raspberry Pi 5 edge device connected to a USB camera for video input. Integrating deep learning models on edge devices significantly reduces reliance on high bandwidth data communication and cloud processing. A ThingsBoard-based dashboard provides real-time occupancy visualization and historical crowd data. Experimental evaluations conducted across diverse indoor and outdoor environments demonstrate up to 94% counting accuracy under varying lighting and crowd conditions. The proposed edge-AI solution is scalable, cost-effective and privacy-preserving, as it avoids raw video data transmission for smart crowd monitoring applications.

title: Comparing the Performance of Serverless vs. Traditional Cloud Models for AI-Powered Tasks
authors: Theekshana Fernando, Janaka Wijayanayaka
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Cloud computing serves as the primary infrastructure for scalable Artificial Intelligence (AI) workloads, yet selecting the optimal deployment paradigm remains a critical challenge for practitioners. This research presents a structured empirical comparison between Function-as-a-Service (Serverless) and Virtual Machine (VM)-based models using a “single-image, dual-deployment” benchmarking framework on Microsoft Azure. We evaluate these architectures across diverse AI tasks, including MobileNetV2 image classification, Librosa audio feature extraction, and NLP training. Experimental results demonstrate that Azure VMs provide superior temporal stability and performance, completing image classification epochs <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5 \%}$</tex> faster (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3. 6 7 s}$</tex>) than serverless functions (18.40s) with significantly lower batchlatency jitter (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SD}=0.0031 ~\mathrm{s}$</tex> vs. 0.0106 s). However, Azure Functions achieve approximately 60 % higher cost-efficiency, with a training cost of $0.000015 per image compared to $0.000038 for VMs. Scalability assessments contrast the deterministic linear growth of VMs against the elastic but volatile burst handling of serverless models. Crucially, the study identifies a resource ceiling for serverless computing, where memory-intensive NLP training tasks resulted in 100 % failure rates due to Out-of-Memory (OOM) errors, while VMs maintained stable execution. This research contributes a Structured and Repeatable Benchmarking Framework (SRBF) and a practitioner-focused decision matrix. Our findings provide actionable guidance for optimizing AI deployment strategies, recommending persistent VM clusters for heavy model training and serverless architectures for lightweight, cost-sensitive inference tasks.

title: Reviewing Model Collapse and Countermeasures
authors: Xihao Xie, Beichen Hu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Driven by massive amounts of web-scale data, generative AI (GenAI) has achieved remarkable progress, enabling various applications in diverse sectors. The advances of GenAI have actuated practitioners to use AI-synthesized data for training next-generation AI models. Undeniably, using synthetic data has alleviated the increasing stringent demand for data supply. Unfortunately, it also introduces a new critical issue: in a selfconsuming cycle between model and data, the model ultimately collapse, raising more trustworthiness concerns to GenAI. In recent years, increasingly more studies have investigated the phenomenon of model collapse (MC) and explored potential solutions to mitigate it. However, the review of the phenomenon of MC still remains blank. To fill this gap, this paper provides an up-to-date overview of these studies for consolidating and reviewing the progress of MC in different application scenarios and countermeasures for mitigating MC. We also highlight challenges and future research opportunities.

title: Robust Dual-Domain ANN-Based Compact MOSFET Modeling Across Technology Nodes
authors: Abdullah M.H Cachhi, Sidhant V Vazarkar, Shreyan Ghosh, Girish Pahwa
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper presents an artificial neural network model for the current-voltage and capacitance-voltage characteristics of advanced transistor technology nodes. We propose a dual-domain loss function combining ratio and log-scale error terms for multi-scale current modeling. Different activation functions are evaluated, including tanh, ISRU (alpha values: 0.5, 1.0, 2.0), and Mish. The results show that tanh delivers the best current-voltage performance with smooth third-order derivatives, while Mish significantly outperforms other activations for capacitance-voltage modeling through self-regularization, superior gradient flow, and infinite differentiability. Training on BSIM-CMG data for 1000 epochs with early stopping achieves high accuracy and computational efficiency.

title: Nile Tilapia Pathogens Detection Using CNN Model Infused with Efficient Channel Attention Mechanism
authors: Md. Enamul Haque, Alok Das, Mohammad Nurul Huda, Md. Rayhan Ahmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Nile Tilapia, a highly resilient fish species crucial to aquaculture, faces significant health threats from various infectious diseases. These diseases are caused by bacterial, viral, and parasitic pathogens that affect fish health, productivity, and overall sustainability, as the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) reported. Parasitic infections from monogeneans, streptococcosis from Streptococcus agalactiae, columnaris disease from Flavobacterium columnare, Tilapia Lake Virus (TiLV), and Motile Aeromonad Septicemia (MAS) from Aeromonas species and so on. Effective disease treatment in aquaculture depends on prompt and accurate identification due to the overlapping symptoms of these infections. Though reliable, traditional diagnostic methods, like PCR and histology, can be time-consuming and require specialized equipment and expertise, limiting their accessibility. In this study, we used several pre-trained deep learning models to address the identification challenge, including VGG16, MobileNetV2, DenseNet201, ResNet101, and ResNet152 for disease detection in Nile Tilapia. Additionally, we proposed a customized CNN model enhanced with an Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) mechanism to focus on the most relevant features in the input data. Our proposed model demonstrated strong generalization capabilities, achieving a training accuracy of 99.85%, validation accuracy of 93.44%, and 96.86% test accuracy with precision 96.08%, recall 95.8%, and f1-score of 95.88% on the Enhancing Disease Detection in Nile Tilapia (EDDNT) dataset. For the ablation study we have employed Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM), Squeeze and Excitation Module (SE-Net) with CNN model and Dense layers.

title: Fuzzy Decision Tree Framework for Hospital Mortality Prediction: Integrating ADASYN Balancing and Explainable AI
authors: Syeda Somiya Tasnim, Naima Tabachchum Meem, Ibtida Hossain, Maisha Maliha, Shrabosti Jahan Dina
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Predicting hospital mortality is crucial for improving outcomes and resource allocation, especially for vulnerable patients with addiction and comorbidities. Current models struggle with nonlinear relationships, lack transparency, and exhibit class imbalance, leading to high false negative rates in minority mortality cases. This study addresses these fragmented solutions by presenting a comprehensive framework that integrates advanced data preprocessing, class balancing through ADASYN technique, multiple machine learning algorithms including Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Fuzzy Decision Trees, and explainable AI methods using SHAP and LIME for transparent clinical decision-making. Utilizing a dataset of 437 patient records with 23 physiological, biochemical, and comorbidity features, experimental validation through 10-fold stratified cross-validation demonstrates that the Fuzzy Decision Tree significantly outperforms baseline models with 97% accuracy, 98% precision, 97% recall, 97% F1-score, 0.9858 ROC-AUC, and lowest cross-entropy loss of 0.1799, while explainability analyses confirm model focus on clinically meaningful predictors including urea nitrogen, renal failure, bicarbonate, and creatinine that align with established medical reasoning. The proposed approach offers effective ADASYN balancing for minority class detection, advanced fuzzy rule-based decision structures for managing clinical uncertainty, and interpretable AI to enhance transparency and trust. This creates a robust framework for predicting hospital mortality, improving healthcare AI accuracy and clinical adoption, ultimately leading to better patient outcomes and increased efficiency in the healthcare system.

title: Optimized YOLOv12 for Robust Object Detection in Complex Satellite Scenes
authors: Sadek Al Prince, Gazi Mehraj Sakib, Mohammad Shoaib Uddin, Mohammed Shorfuddin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Object detection in satellite imagery remains challenging due to small object sizes, dense backgrounds, illumination variation, and multi-scale spatial patterns. To address these challenges, this paper presents an optimized YOLOv12 framework enhanced with Residual Attention Local Aggregation Networks (R-ALAN), an Adaptive Multi-Scale Fusion (AMSF) module, and region-aware A2C2f attention. A curated 19,254-image dataset covering eight infrastructure and environmental categories is introduced. Extensive experiments show that the optimized YOLOv12 achieves Precision = 0.85, Recall = 0.74, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{F} 1=0.79, \text{mAP} {@} 0.5=0.78$</tex>, outperforming YOLOv10 and YOLOv11. Ablation studies confirm the contribution of each module, while external validation on subsets of DIOR and DOTA demonstrates improved generalization. Grad-CAM and attention visualizations improve interpretability. This revised framework provides a robust solution for remote-sensing applications including environmental monitoring, urban planning, and disaster response. Our results demonstrate that YOLOv12 is capable of attaining a better balance between localization accuracy and detection completeness, making it a competitive solution for realtime remote sensing.

title: Multi-bit Programming Scheme of 2T Gain Cell with Near-Ideal Linearity
authors: Yanbo Su, Yide Li, Jianshi Tang, Zhidong Tang, Kexin Wang, Yijia Fan, Yibei Zhang, Yue Xi, Yirui Ma, Siyu Xu, Bingjun Cao, Renrong Liang, Bin Gao, He Qian, Huaqiang Wu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, we present a novel multi-bit programming scheme of 2T gain cell (GC), which effectively improves the current response linearity by biasing the read transistor in the saturation region. The high linearity significantly benefits the readout circuitry, especially the analog-to-digital conversion (ADC), and is also highly desired for computing-in-memory (CIM) or processing-in-memory (PIM) applications. Using the proposed programming scheme, we have successfully demonstrated 4-bit/cell capability in the fabricated InGaZnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf> (IGZO) based 2T GCs. The output current of the read transistor exhibit a near-ideal linear relationship with the input voltage, achieving a R-squared (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) value up to 0.9955. In contrast, the conventional programming scheme exhibits a much worse linearity with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> ≈ 0.9070. Furthermore, the outstanding linearity of the proposed programming scheme can be retained over 80 seconds, while the multi-bit states become nearly indistinguishable for the conventional scheme.

title: AMAF: An Adaptive and Coupling-Sensitive Framework for Cross-Language Software Maintainability Analysis
authors: P. Vishaliney
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The assessment of software maintainability is vital for controlling development costs and technical debt, thereby supporting long-term software evolution. However, existing maintainability measures and composite indices are typically based on isolated metrics, fixed aggregation schemes, or language-specific tools. As a result, they fail to adequately capture cognitive effort, design-level dependencies, and the nonlinear maintainability risks inherent in modern multi-language systems. This study proposes the Adaptive Maintainability Assessment Framework (AMAF), a language-independent and interpretable composite measure of software maintainability. AMAF integrates cognitive complexity, structural complexity, software size, maintainability indicators, and coupling within a unified framework. The framework employs dynamic metric weighting to adaptively increase or decrease the influence of individual metrics, along with a non-linear coupling penalty to model disproportionate maintenance costs arising from intercomponent dependencies. The use of a language-neutral intermediate representation enables the consistent extraction of metrics across Java, C, and C# using a common methodology. Empirical evaluation, based on functionally equivalent implementations and sensitivity analysis, demonstrates that AMAF provides a stable, consistent, and more realistic assessment of maintainability compared to conventional linear aggregation models.

title: Front-End Readout Circuit Design for DEPFET Pixel Detectors in 65-nm CMOS
authors: V. Gogolou, H. Krüger, J. Dingfelder
abstract: This work presents a differential readout integrated circuit for DEPFET pixel matrices, with a systematic evaluation of trade-offs among key performance metrics such as power consumption, noise, and silicon area. Each readout channel integrates a cascode transimpedance amplifier followed by a compact single-ended-to-differential conversion stage, optimized to fully drive the input range of a high-speed, low-power analog-to-digital converter (ADC). The proposed readout architecture is designed in an advanced 65-nm CMOS technology and is designed to interface seamlessly with such ADCs, enabling a system that meets stringent constraints on power density and pixel pitch while delivering the precision required for future high-rate, high-resolution experiments. The circuit targets DEPFET matrices operated in rolling-shutter mode, supporting a minimum input current of 2 μA and a signal range of interest extending up to 8 μA (4 MIPs about 20,000 electrons). The readout operates with a total power consumption of 900 μW per channel and an input-referred noise of 80 nA, corresponding to approximately 200 electrons. The achieved noise performance ensures a comfortable signal-to-noise margin across the full dynamic range.

title: Lightweight Deep Learning Framework for Pothole Detection and Classification Using CNNs and YOLO Models
authors: Tanjilur Rahman, Yeamin Hossain Shihab, Tasfiya Bushra, Mohammad Tahmid Shams, Dewan Ziaul Karim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Potholes are a major cause that contributes to road accidents around the world. They cause vehicle damage, traffic congestion, and substantial economic losses. These are more common in countries such as Bangladesh, as heavy traffic congestion and monsoon rains accelerate road deterioration, making pothole formation frequent and severe. Manual inspection methods are slow and inefficient, making real-time automated detection solutions necessary. In this paper, we have created a custom dataset of 1,522 pictures, which we gathered from different places of Bangladesh. We tested a number of deep learning methods to classify and detect potholes, aiming for lightweight architectures to deploy it on edge devices such as Single-Board Computers (SBCs). We trained a custom Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) that achieved 97% classification accuracy and compared it with other classification-based models. Moreover, we also analyzed detection-based performance among multiple YOLO (You Only Look Once) models such as YOLOv5, YOLOv8, and YOLOv11. According to our evaluation, the YOLOv5 achieved the highest detection performance with a mean Average Precision at IoU (Intersection over Union) threshold 0.50(mAP@50) of 0.899. To enhance interpretability, we used Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) and Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) to visualize model decisionmaking processes. The results show that lightweight CNN and YOLO-based models offer robust performance and are suitable for real-time, cost-effective deployment, paving the way for scalable and automated road maintenance solutions.

title: From Sensors to Decisions: A Distributed Software-Hardware Stack for Hydroponic Cultivation
authors: Komninos Lazaros Lekkas, Antonios Mavrakis, Achilles D. Boursianis, Charalampos Bratsas, Sotirios K. Goudos, Maria S. Papadopoulou
abstract: Hydroponic cultivation offers substantial gains in water-management efficiency, plant growth rate, and input utilization, yet dependable operation requires continuous supervision of chemical and environmental variables (pH, Total Dissolved Solids/Electrical Conductivity TDS/EC, water temperature, flow, water level, ambient temperature, and relative humidity T/RH). Manual monitoring is error-prone and slow to react, while many low-cost systems lack real-time sensing, automated control, and integrated analytics, limiting reliability and scalability. This work presents Hydroponic Ecosystem (HECO), a low-cost, IoT-enabled hydroponics platform that unifies multi-parameter environmental variables, local safety control, and cloud-backed supervision with a single mobile application for managing multiple installations. Long-running trials demonstrate stable telemetry, responsive closed-loop actuation, and robust behavior under transient sensor and network faults. The modular design of the platform, secure messaging, and edge-prioritized safety make it a practical foundation for scalable, data-driven hydroponic management and a reusable reference for embedded IoT control systems.

title: Tellurium Nanowire Transistors as Ion Sensors with Near-Nernstian Sensitivity
authors: Heping Cui, Kai Zheng, Sven Ingebrandt
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Semiconducting nanowires are promising building blocks for nanoelectronics and biosensing, yet scalable synthesis and stable device operation of nanowires remain challenging. In this work, we present a scalable synthesis and integration of ultrathin tellurium nanowires (TeNWs) in electrolyte-gated field-effect transistors (FETs). Using a controlled hydrothermal method, TeNWs with uniform diameters, high aspect ratios, and high crystallinity are synthesized. Spin-coated TeNW networks exhibit excellent electrical conductivity and form Ohmic contact with Au electrodes. TeNW FETs operate in both back-gated and electrolyte-gated configurations, consistently showing p-type field-effect characteristics of TeNWs. Notably, electrolytegated devices show strong ion sensitivity, with threshold voltage shifts following near-Nernstian dependence (~54 mV/dec for KCl and ~51 mV/dec for NaCl). These results establish TeNWs as a promising platform for electrolyte-gated nanoelectronics.

title: Annealing Activation Effect of Pt Electrodes on SAW Devices with Langasite Substrate and the Mechanism Analysis
authors: Runli Liu, Yang Li, Xudong Cai, Zhen Li, Jun Fu, Tianling Ren
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: High-temperature-resistant surface acoustic wave (SAW) devices commonly employ a structure where Pt electrodes are grown on langasite (LGS) substrates. This paper compares the resonance performance of LGS/Pt SAW devices before and after annealing, revealing that devices of this structure require annealing activation to become functional. Comparative experiments with Au electrodes confirm that the annealing process primarily affects the Pt electrodes. Analysis of Pt microstructures before and after annealing reveals the formation of larger, irregular grains in the Pt films, which would lead to changes in their mechanical properties. Nanoindentation testing comparing load-displacement curves suggests that the annealing process may improve performance of SAW devices by relieving residual stresses within the Pt films.

title: Analysis of Double Gate AlGaN/GaN HEMT with Increasing Quantum Wells for Emerging Electronics Applications
authors: Padmakshya Kar, Nitish Kumar, Niranjan Jyoti Borah, Trupti R. Lenka, Sudhanshu Choudhary, Hieu P. T. Nguyen
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This research presents a detailed DC analysis of a normally-on double-gate AlGaN/GaN HEMT integrated with one, two, and three quantum wells in the channel. The study shows how embedding multiple quantum wells improves carrier confinement and enhances gate control. As the number of wells increases, the device exhibits a favourable shift in threshold voltage, higher drain current, and optimized channel modulation. The appearance of a second slope in transfer characteristics confirms sequential activation of the wells, enabling multi-channel transport. While a minor drop in transconductance is observed for the two quantum well case, the three quantum well structure balances confinement and mobility, offering superior current capability for emerging high-power and RF electronics applications.

title: A Novel Shallow-Trench Structure SiC power MOSFET for Trade-off Among Performance, Reliability and Process Complexity
authors: Yanghao Wang, Zhen Wang, Jingjing Tan, Peng Liao, Jianbin Guo, Yafen Yang, Qingqing Sun, David Wei Zhang, Hang Xu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper presents a novel silicon carbide power MOSFET featuring a JFET shallow-trench structure (ST-MOSFET). By introducing a shallow trench etch within the JFET region of a conventional planar device, an additional gate oxide capacitor structure is formed, which effectively reduces the specific on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf>). The gate oxide reliability is enhanced through the protection offered by the flanking P-base regions and the thick bottom oxide layer. Compared with commercial deep-trench devices, the proposed structure offers lower process complexity. Furthermore, by partitioning the shallow trench into a split-gate configuration (SG-MOSFET) and applying a fixed bias voltage, the miller capacitance can be significantly reduced without compromising the low on-resistance. Simulation results demonstrate that the ST-MOSFET achieves a 37% reduction in R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf> and a 61% improvement in Baliga’s figure of merit (BFOM). Additionally, the miller capacitance is effectively reduced by 94%, resulting in a total switching loss reduction of 62% for the SG-MOSFET when compared to the C-MOSFET. The proposed architecture offers an improved trade-off among performance, reliability, and process feasibility.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of a Slotted Dual-Band MIMO Antenna for X-Band Satellite Communication
authors: Hrittik Raj Barua, Arpan Dey, Imtiaz Akber Chowdhury
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study introduces a dual-band MIMO antenna designed for X-band satellite communication systems. The antenna is fabricated on a Rogers RO4003 substrate with overall dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 ~\text{mm} \times 30 ~\text{mm} \times 0.508 ~\text{mm}$</tex>. The radiating element, composed of copper, incorporates I-shaped and U- shaped stubs along with a centrally embedded circular slot to enhance resonance characteristics. Excitation is provided through a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0} \boldsymbol{\Omega}$</tex> microstrip feed line. Electromagnetic simulations were performed using CST Studio Suite, and the results demonstrate dual resonances at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8. 6 5 ~ G H z}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9. 5 9 5}$</tex> GHz. The proposed design achieves a return loss of -27.63 dB, a gain of 2.98 dBi, and a directivity of 6.59 dBi for 8.65 GHz and a return loss of -33.985 dB, a gain of 5.51 dBi, and a directivity of 7.08 dBi for 9.595 GHz respectively. These outcomes confirm the antenna's suitability for compact, high-performance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{X}$</tex>-band satellite communication applications. Furthermore, the analysis was extended to MIMO configurations, where different alignments for MIMO antennas were represented and analyzed via simulation. The Envelope Correlation Coefficient (ECC) remains consistently low across the observed frequency range, reaching values as small as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.4025 \times 10^{-5}$</tex> between 8.65 and 9.595 GHz.

title: Orientation Dependent Global Stress Analysis for Nanosheet based CMOS Device Technology
authors: Amit Kumar Singh Chauhan, Kunal, Pankaj Kumar, Harsh Raju, Sanjeev Kumar Manhas
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This study analyzes the influence of surface orientation and channel direction on stress distribution in epitaxially grown Si/SiGe superlattice nanosheets. SiGe nanosheets (Ge = 0.35) exhibit compressive stress of 2.17 GPa for (100)/<100> and <110>, 2.39 GPa for (110)/<100>, and 2.94 GPa for (110)/<110>, while Si nanosheets remain initially stress-free. Post-patterning, elastic relaxation in SiGe induces tensile stress in Si nanosheets, contributing to residual stress in GAA NS-FETs. Electrical analysis reveals that switching from (100) to (110) orientation for a <110> channel enhances p-type drain current by ~21%, while reducing n-type current by ~14%. These results highlight the role of stress engineering in optimizing nanosheet CMOS and heterostructure device performance.

title: Effect of Fermentation Time on Bioethanol Production from Coconut Milk Waste for Sustainable Biofuel Development
authors: Slamet Reza Nasrullah, Khakam Ma’ruf, Rizal Justian Setiawan, Andini Jihan Ningrum, Ika Kapti Andarbeni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The ongoing depletion of fossil fuel reserves, coupled with the rising global demand for energy, underscores the urgency of developing sustainable and renewable energy sources. This study investigates the feasibility of utilizing coconut milk waste (Cocos nucifera) as a novel substrate for bioethanol production via a hydrolysis–fermentation pathway. Coconut milk, characterized by a lipid content of 34.3 g/100 g, was subjected to hydrolysis using citric acid (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">8</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</inf>) derived from lime (Citrus aurantifolia) extract as a natural acid catalyst. This process facilitated the breakdown of triglycerides into glycerol (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">8</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) and free fatty acids. The glycerol-rich hydrolysate was subsequently fermented using Saccharomyces cerevisiae to yield ethanol (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</inf>OH), which was then purified through distillation. Hydrolysis using a natural citric acid catalyst derived from lime extract successfully converted coconut milk lipids into fermentable glycerol. However, no detectable ethanol was found at all fermentation times, indicating that the glycerol-to-ethanol conversion process is not yet optimal and requires further control of fermentation parameters. The findings highlight the potential of coconut milk waste as a viable alternative feedstock for bioethanol production, offering a sustainable approach to organic waste valorization and contributing to the diversification of renewable energy resources.

title: A Slope Calibration Technique for Time-mode Delay Tap Accuracy Improvement
authors: Aikaterini I. Chronopoulou, Konstantinos P. Pagkalos, Spyridon Vlassis
abstract: In this paper, a calibration topology for the unity delay tap suitable for time-mode signal processing is proposed. The delay tap is based on the constant slope discharging capacitor concept which is used in time-domain time-mode signal processing. The proposed topology calibrates the impact of both process and temperature variations improving the dynamic range and the accuracy of the delay tap. It contains a digital loop that calibrates the discharging slope. The novelty of this work is that the slope calibration is achieved by adjusting the supply voltage of a driving CMOS inverter avoiding the perturbation of the signal path. All circuits are designed using the 65 nm TSMC process under a supply voltage of 1.2 V. The current consumption of the delay tap is 50μW for a sampling frequency 100MHz. The accuracy of the delay tap after calibration is better than 2.5ps for 80% of the input full scale range. The input range of the delay tap dramatically improves compared to the uncalibrated tap.

title: Developing a Decision Support Framework to Minimize Loss Mileage and Its Impact on Sri Lanka's FMCG Transportation Sector: Delphi-ISM Approach
authors: Induwara Sahasraka, Janaka Wijayanayake, Chathumi Kavirathna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) sector in Sri Lanka depends on responsive transport, yet loss mileage (non-value-adding distance) increases fuel, labor, and maintenance costs and reduces fleet utilization. This paper proposes a decision support framework (DSF) to minimize loss mileage using a case-based Delphi-interpretive structural modeling (ISM) approach, triangulated with observations from transport management system (TMS) logs. A purposive expert panel (a panel of ten experts, each with more than 10 years of experience) refined 20 loss-mileage factors and provided pairwise judgments to form the structural self-interaction matrix, which was transformed into ISM reachability matrices and a MICMAC based driver-dependence classification. The results indicate that structural/ network and coordination/ technology factors such as distribution-network design, collaboration/consolidation practices, and logistics IT adoptionact as primary drivers, while operational issues including lowcapacity utilization and loading/unloading delays are dependent outcomes. The DSF converts these insights into staged intervention priorities: address key drivers first, mid-level enablers (e.g., maintenance reliability and driver capability), and monitor outcomes through routine TMS reporting. Facevalidity evaluation with the expert panel confirmed the framework's usability and practical relevance for Sri Lankan FMCG transport, and future work should quantify relationship strengths and embed DSF rules as TMS alerts.

title: Monitoring Mental Stress Using Multimodal Physiological Signals from Wearable Device
authors: Md Rokonuzzaman Mim, Ashraf Al- Khalique, Mohammod Abdul Motin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Mental stress is closely linked to numerous medical and psychological disorders; early detection can help individuals take proactive steps to protect their well-being and maintain a creative and effective response to daily challenges. With the advent of wearable technologies and intelligent systems, machine learning-based approaches to stress detection have emerged as a promising solution to support early interventions. This study examines the feasibility of utilizing blood volume pulse (BVP) and electrodermal activity (EDA) signals collected from a wearable sensor to monitor stress automatically using machine learning models. Various machine learning algorithms were explored and evaluated for stress classifications. To improve classification performance, a comprehensive analysis of features in both the time domain and frequency domain was conducted. Using publicly available datasets, the proposed approach achieved a binary classification accuracy of 90.00 % and an F1-score of 0.88 on BVP data, while EDA-based classification attained an accuracy of 83.41 % and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of 0.81. For the combined data using both BVP and EDA signals, the model achieved an accuracy of 95.10 % and an F1-score of 0.94, highlighting the effectiveness of multimodal integration. This study also explores a multimodal framework that integrates various domains of features, demonstrating promising potential in advancing the development of intelligent and real-time health-monitoring systems using wearable devices.

title:
Investigation of Oxidative Doping in Multilayer WSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    FETs
authors: Suwen Song, Xin Feng, Zeyang Ren, Weihang Zhang, Xianhe Liu, Hong Zhou, Zhihong Liu, Yue Hao, Jincheng Zhang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, a novel spatially controlled oxidative doping technique is proposed to prepare the high performance 2D p-type WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> FETs, which are based on the bottom-contact and back-gated configuration. To find out the optical device, five kinds of device structure are adopted with full top WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> layer oxidation, full bottom WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> layer oxidation, dual-side WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> layer oxidation, patterned top-layer WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> oxidation and patterned bottom-layer WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> oxidation. It is found that all the oxidative doping WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> FETs show a high hole injection efficiency with a maximum on-state current of 35.1 μA/μm on the dual-side WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> layer oxidation WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> FET. Besides, the patterned top oxidation WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> FET achieves a good gate control (SS:142mV/dec) and 4-fold current growth of 10.9 μA/μm, offering an optimal performance. This study provides key insights for designing high-performance 2D p-type transistors.

title: Electricity Theft Protection Plan Using Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI)
authors: Ehsan Aghababagoli
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: In this paper, a theft alarm scheme based on a smart metering system is presented, based on which theft or destruction of overhead power distribution network facilities, especially wires and transformers, can be detected. This scheme is based on data sent from the Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) at the consumer's location and is based on telecommunication platforms. In this project, by creating the required filters and analyzing the data, the issue of power outages caused by theft or destruction of power facilities is distinguished from other incidents such as power outages caused by transient faults, and by sending timely warnings to the operators of the power company, it protects the power facilities against theft or destruction by thieves. The algorithm presented in this scheme has been simulated in a standard 33-bus IEEE network in MATLAB software. The replication of the simulation results shows the appropriate accuracy of the presented alarm algorithm. Also, since 2021, the described scheme has been implemented within the scope of the Isfahan Province Power Distribution Company, and the results obtained from it show the appropriate accuracy of the presented system.

title: The Role of Supplier Diversification and Supplier Rationalisation in Mitigating Supplier Risk: a Study of Large-Scale Manufacturing Firms in Sri Lanka
authors: Managi Fernando, Chathumi Kavirathna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Supplier related risks pose significant challenges for large-scale manufacturing firms operating in volatile and resource constrained environments such as Sri Lanka. Complex supply networks, import dependency and limited supplier availability increase exposure to delivery disruptions, quality failures, cost volatility and dependency risks. Although supplier diversification and supplier rationalisation are widely used risk mitigation strategies, empirical evidence on their individual and combined effectiveness in developing economy contexts remains limited. This study addresses this gap by examining how these strategies contribute to supplier risk mitigation in Sri Lankan large scale manufacturing firms. A sequential mixed method approach was adopted, combining thematic analysis of prior literature with a survey based empirical study. Primary data were collected from 138 professionals and analysed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLSSEM). The findings show that supplier rationalisation plays a dominant role in mitigating supplier risk through complexity reduction, relationship management and supplier performance management. Supplier diversification contributes mainly by enhancing flexibility and market responsiveness but exhibits lower explanatory power when applied independently. The combined model demonstrates the strongest explanatory strength, indicating that a balanced strategy anchored in rationalisation and supported by selective diversification provides the most effective approach to supplier risk mitigation. The study offers context specific insights to strengthen supplier governance and supply chain resilience in developing economy manufacturing sectors.

title: Miniaturized Antenna Structure Supporting C and X Band Resonances for Integrated Wireless System
authors: Saikat Barua, Nirzar Barua, MD. Akter Hossain, Sujoy Barua, Ayon Dhar, Hridoy Banik
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Advancement in wireless communication now heavily depends on the compact high frequency radiating devices. It increases the importance of antenna design to support different operating bands. In this consequence, this article introduces a triple band compact microstrip patch antenna, operating at 4.27 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{GHz}, 7.52 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and 8.5 GHz frequency ranges. This device is engineered on a 1.6 mm thick Rogers RO4305B <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\epsilon_{r}=3.7, \tan \delta\right. =0.0037$</tex>) substrate and copper metallization is used for patch implementation. At the resonating points, the antenna performs <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-35.6 \text{dB},-32.18 \text{dB}$</tex> and -37.4 dB of reflection gains and covers the bandwidths of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$120 \text{MHz}, 190 \text{MHz}$</tex>, and 240 MHz. With a sustainable gain up to 7.92 dBi, the antenna shows efficiency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 3 \%}$</tex> for the first band and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 3 \%}$</tex> for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7. 5 2 G H z}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8. 5 G H z}$</tex>. With these characteristics, the proposed antenna effectively supports operation across the designated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C}$</tex> - and X-band frequency ranges, ensuring compatibility with a broad spectrum of present and future wireless communication systems.

title: High-Efficient Theta-Slotted Patch Antenna with Low SAR for Early Breast Tumor Detection
authors: Md Absar Siddiqui, Md. Musfequr Rahman, Mahmudul Hasan, Md. Imran Hossen Rony, Niloy Goswami, M. Tanseer Ali
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Breast cancer is one of the major causes of deaths in women and early detection can play a crucial role in their survival. Traditional techniques like ultrasound and mammography face challenges such as expense, radiation exposure, and limited accuracy in dense tissues. Microwave-based sensing has emerged as a potential alternative because of its affordability, non-ionizing waves to detect dielectric contrasts between healthy and malignant tissues. This paper focuses on the design and performance evaluation of a theta-slotted patch antenna (TSPA) specifically designed for breast tumor detection within the ISM band. The proposed antenna integrates two theta-shaped slots and five parasitic elements to enhance impedance matching, bandwidth, and tumor detection sensitivity. The antenna was designed and simulated in CST Studio Suite, demonstrating a -10 dB operational bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 \text{MHz}(2.42-2.52 \text{GHz})$</tex>. The antenna achieved an excellent return loss of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 5 5. 7 8 ~ d B}$</tex> and a VSWR of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 0 0 3}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 4 7 9 ~ G H z}$</tex>. A maximum specific absorption rate (SAR) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.34 ~\mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex> is obtained in off body conditions, far below the FCC limit of 1.6 W/kg, ensures safety compliance. Comparative analysis validates the proposed TSPA's enhanced performance, positioning it as a strong candidate for reliable and efficient early breast tumor detection systems.

title: Flexible electrochemical devices integrating zinc-manganese oxide battery with symmetric supercapacitor
authors: Zanxiang Nie, Pritesh Hiralal, Gehan A.J. Amaratunga, Yue Liu, Shiwei Liu, Shiqiang Luo
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The realization of a unified energy storage device that synergistically combines the high-power attributes of supercapacitors with the high-energy characteristics of batteries represents a pivotal advancement toward enabling robust and versatile energy solutions for flexible electronics. Herein, we present a novel approach involving the electrodeposition of Zn and MnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> onto activated carbon electrodes within a symmetric supercapacitor configuration. This strategy facilitates the seamless integration of the supercapacitor with a Zn-MnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> battery system. The resultant integrated hybrid device exhibits superior overall performance compared to its individual battery and supercapacitor counterparts operating in isolation. Furthermore, the incorporation of this auxiliary battery system is demonstrated to serve as an effective mechanism for preventing overcharging in aqueous supercapacitors through precise control of the charging voltage.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning and Handcrafted Feature Fusion Framework for Tea Leaf Disease Classification with Explainable AI
authors: H.M Imtiaz Uddin, Farah Jahan, Mohammad Khairul Islam, Machbah Uddin, Anjuman Ara Rashid
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Tea is a widely consumed beverage and a key export crop in Bangladesh, yet its yield and quality are threatened by leaf diseases such as Gray Blight, Algal Spot, Brown Blight, Helopeltis, and Red Spot, etc. Early detection is critical, but traditional visual inspection is slow, labor-intensive, and prone to error. This paper presents a lightweight hybrid model for automated tea leaf disease classification. A dataset of 5,867 images across six classes, including healthy leaves, is preprocessed, augmented, and split into training (80%), validation (10%), and testing (10%) sets. Handcrafted features such as Local Binary Patterns (LBP) for texture and color histograms are fused with deep features from a fine-tuned MobileNetV2 via transfer learning to boost accuracy while maintaining efficiency. Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) techniques, including Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) and SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), are incorporated to provide spatial and feature-level interpretability. The proposed system achieves 99.17% classification accuracy with low computational cost, making it suitable for mobile and Internet of Things (IoT) deployment. This research offers a practical and interpretable solution to support farmers with timely and sustainable tea disease management.

title: Innovative Technologies, Smart Manufacturing, and Machine Learning in Semiconductor Supply Chain
authors: Tsung Yao Wen, Yuanbo Wang, Adrian Crisan, Angelo Pinto
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Modern semiconductor supply chains face unprecedented complexity that requires smarter strategies across companies. Leverage of automation tools and new advances in Artificial Intelligence (AI) can be beneficial for enhanced efficiency and stability. To this end, this paper introduces a practical framework focused on three key areas: engineering, planning, and operations. With the automated processes and Machine Learning (ML) integration in each component, manufacturers can significantly improve overall workflow efficiency and system resilience. By pinpointing high-impact business areas for optimization, the proposed framework has demonstrated its ability to identify issues in a timely manner, enable targeted technology deployment, and provide actionable insights for building intelligent and adaptive supply chain solutions in volatile markets.

title: Real-World Detection of Full and Partial Bangladeshi Banknotes: A Deep Learning Based Comparative Analysis
authors: Md Nazmul Hossen, Samim Ahmed, Mohammad Shahidur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study evaluates the automatic classification of Bangladeshi banknotes, focusing on full and partial-note detection to support assistive technology for visually impaired individuals. Unlike previous works that primarily considered full notes in controlled datasets, our research addresses challenging real-world conditions, including complex backgrounds, diverse orientations, folds, and occlusions. The dataset covers multiple denominations, including newly issued 1000, 50, and 20 Taka notes, ensuring practical relevance. To evaluate recognition performance, we compared five deep learning architectures: EfficientNetB0, ResNet50, MobileNetV2, Xception, and a custom CNN.
