title: CGM-Led Multimodal Tracking with Chatbot Support: An Autoethnography in Sub-Health
authors: Dongyijie Primo Pan, Lan Luo, Yike Wang, Pan Hui
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 International Conference on Human-Engaged Computing
abstract: None

title: Exploring Engagement, Interaction, and Community-Building Among Content Creators and Viewers in RedNote’s Autism-Related Content
authors: Yiming Fu, Sichang Su, Jiaan Li, Shuming Yang, Zhicong Lu, Xin Tong
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 International Conference on Human-Engaged Computing

title: DermEVAL: A Dermatologist-Reviewed Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Hongjin Zhao, Weihao Li, Zhenyue Qin, Ge-Peng Ji, Yang Liu, Tom Gedeon, Nick Barnes
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Clinical photographs play a vital role in conversational computer-aided diagnosis, particularly in dermatology. However, existing skin disease benchmarks contain limitations like insufficient dataset size, the sole presence of categorical labels, the lack of expert inspections, and limited diversity in annotations. To address these shortcomings, we introduce DermEVAL, a large-scale benchmark specifically designed to evaluate the performance of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in dermatology. Our benchmark includes image-text pairs depicting 16 distinct skin diseases, featuring a total of 11,347 representative images drawn from various dermatological datasets, carefully selected and annotated with the guidance of dermatologists. DermEVAL enables two primary tasks: visual question answering (VQA) and medical report generation (MRG), designed to simulate real-world medical diagnostics. We evaluate the performance of MLLMs in dermatology using multiple metrics, including traditional metrics and GPT-4V-based assessments. Our results indicate that accurately diagnosing skin diseases remains challenging for state-of-the-art MLLMs. We also demonstrate that fine-tuning MLLMs using DermEVAL significantly improves their performance on dermatology-related image–text tasks.

title: JOCA: Task-Driven Joint Optimisation of Camera Hardware and Adaptive Camera Control Algorithms
authors: Chengyang Yan, Mitch Bryson, Donald G. Dansereau
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: The quality of captured images strongly influences the performance of downstream perception tasks. Recent works on co-designing camera systems with perception tasks have shown improved task performance. However, most prior approaches focus on optimising fixed camera parameters set at manufacturing, while many parameters, such as exposure settings, require adaptive control at runtime. This paper introduces a method that jointly optimises camera hardware and adaptive camera control algorithms with downstream vision tasks. We present a unified optimisation framework that integrates gradient-based and derivative-free methods, enabling support for both continuous and discrete parameters, non-differentiable image formation processes, and neural network-based adaptive control algorithms. To address non-differentiable effects such as motion blur, we propose DF-Grad, a hybrid optimisation strategy that trains adaptive control networks using signals from a derivative-free optimiser alongside unsupervised task-driven learning. Experiments show that our method outperforms baselines that optimise static and dynamic parameters separately, particularly under challenging conditions such as low light and fast motion. These results demonstrate that jointly optimising hardware parameters and adaptive control algorithms improves perception performance and provides a unified approach to task-driven camera system design. Code is available via our project page at https://roboticimaging.org/Projects/JOCA/.

title: Conjuring Positive Pairs for Efficient Unification of Representation Learning and Image Synthesis
authors: Imanol G. Estepa, Jesús M. Rodríguez-de-Vera, Ignacio Sarasúa, Bhalaji Nagarajan, Petia Radeva
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: While representation learning and generative modeling seek to understand visual data, unifying both domains remains unexplored. Recent Unified Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) methods have started to bridge the gap between both paradigms. However, they either extract information from discriminative pretrained models or rely solely on semantic token reconstruction, which requires an external tokenizer during training — introducing a significant computational overhead. In this work, we introduce Sorcen, a novel Unified SSL framework, incorporating a synergic Contrastive-Reconstruction objective. Our novel Contrastive objective, leverages the generative capabilities of Sorcen and eliminates the need for additional image crops or augmentations during training. Sorcen "generates" contrastive positive samples, called Echoes, directly in the semantic token space using the reconstruction objective. This on-the-fly Echo generation, enables Sorcen to operate exclusively on precomputed tokens, eliminating the need for an online tokenizer during training. Sorcen significantly reduces the computational overhead by 60.8% compared to token reconstruction SoTA. Extensive experiments on ImageNet-1k demonstrate that Sorcen outperforms the previous Unified SSL SoTA by 0.4%, 1.48 FID, 1.76%, and 1.53% on linear probing, unconditional image generation, few-shot learning, and transfer learning, respectively. Additionally, Sorcen establishes as a new single-crop MIM SoTA in linear probing and achieves SoTA performance in unconditional image generation, highlighting significant improvements and breakthroughs in Unified SSL models <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>.

title: 3D Superquadric Splatting
authors: Daniel MacSwayne, Aleš Leonardis, Jianbo Jiao
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Gaussian Splatting has proven to be an effective algorithm for novel view synthesis and 3D reconstruction from multi-view images. However, the underlying volumetric primitive—the ellipsoidal Gaussian—has limited expressive capabilities, leading to difficulties in 3D modelling (especially geometry such as edges, corners, and high curvature). To address this limitation, in this paper, we introduce Superquadric Splats (SQS), an extended class of volumetric primitives, as a superset of Gaussian Splats, to model more detailed geometry. We treat superquadrics as volumetric distance functions rather than level-set surfaces. A non-trivial differentiable rendering pipeline is developed to support this. Extensive experimental analysis on multiple datasets validates the effectiveness of the proposed SQS approach, showing both enhanced visual and geometric performance compared to Gaussian-based splatting (with more than 1dB in PSNR and prominent geometric improvement). Project page can be found at: https://daniel-macswayne.github.io/3DSQS/

title: Forget Less by Learning Together through Concept Consolidation
authors: Arjun Ramesh Kaushik, Naresh Kumar Devulapally, Vishnu Suresh Lokhande, Nalini Ratha, Venu Govindaraju
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Custom Diffusion Models (CDMs) have gained significant attention due to their remarkable ability to personalize generative processes. However, existing CDMs suffer from catastrophic forgetting when continuously learning new concepts. Most prior works attempt to mitigate this issue under the sequential learning setting with a fixed order of concept inflow and neglect inter-concept interactions. In this paper, we propose a novel framework -Forget Less by Learning Together (FL2T) - that enables concurrent and order-agnostic concept learning while addressing catastrophic forgetting. Specifically, we introduce a set-invariant inter-concept learning module where proxies guide feature selection across concepts, facilitating improved knowledge retention and transfer. By leveraging inter-concept guidance, our approach preserves old concepts while efficiently incorporating new ones. Extensive experiments, across three datasets, demonstrates that our method significantly improves concept retention and mitigates catastrophic forgetting, highlighting the effectiveness of inter-concept catalytic behavior in incremental concept learning of ten tasks with at least 2% gain on average CLIP Image Alignment scores.

title: TM-Adapter: Temporal Merge Adapter for Efficient Global Temporal Modeling
authors: Woo Joo Hahm, Seungwoo Jang, Hyeon Tak Kim, Daeun Lee, Kwangsu Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We propose the Temporal Merge Adapter (TM-Adapter), a novel framework for image-to-video parameter-efficient transfer learning (PETL), specifically designed for temporal representation learning in video understanding. PETL has emerged as a practical strategy for adapting large-scale vision models to video tasks under limited computational budgets. However, existing PETL approaches suffer from local redundancy caused by highly similar consecutive frames, which limits the modeling of diverse temporal dependencies. To address this limitation, we introduce a lightweight merge-unmerge mechanism that temporally aggregates and redistributes token embeddings, enabling the model to capture diverse temporal patterns by mitigating redundancy. Furthermore, to effectively handle diverse temporal dependencies across different time scales, TM-Adapter introduces a single adapter module with two parallel branches, local and global adapters, each specialized in capturing complementary patterns at different temporal ranges. We validate our approach through experiments on Kinetics-400, Something-Something V2, and HMDB-51, demonstrating competitive performance compared to existing methods while maintaining high parameter efficiency.

title: Cluster-based Pseudo-labeling for Semi-Supervised LiDAR Semantic Segmentation
authors: Qingju Guo, Shuang Li, Jing Geng, Binhui Xie, Jiawei Shan, Wei Li
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: The costly annotation process has driven the development of semi-supervised learning (SSL) approaches. Existing semi-supervised LiDAR segmentation methods typically process entire point clouds directly, aiming to assign labels to all points at the scene scale. However, the large number of points, combined with their sparse and irregular nature, makes it challenging to learn scene-level optimization objectives, especially in SSL settings where labeled data are insufficient. This paper presents a Cluster-based pseudo-LAbeling Semi-Supervised technique, called CLASS. CLASS is designed to divide point clouds into several small, pure clusters, thereby decomposing challenging scene-scale segmentation task into more manageable cluster-scale classification and segmentation tasks, enabling the generation of high-quality pseudo labels for unlabeled data. CLASS possesses three key properties. i) Task simplicity: our pseudo-labeling process is based on simpler cluster-scale classification and segmentation tasks, resulting in ease of learning. ii) Labeling effectiveness: CLASS can generate pseudo-labels comparable to ground truth using only approximately 10% labeled data. iii) Universal versatility: CLASS exhibits flexibility regarding LiDAR representations (e.g., BEV, voxel, and range view). Comprehensive experiments on popular LiDAR segmentation benchmarks demonstrate its superiority.

title: HiMix : Hierarchical Visual-Textual Mixing Network for Lesion Segmentation
authors: Soojin Hwang, Jaeyoon Sim, Won Hwa Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Lesion segmentation is an essential task in medical imaging to support diagnosis and assessment of pathologies. While deep learning models have shown success in various domains, their reliance on large-scale annotated datasets limits applicability in the medical domain due to labeling cost. To address this issue, recent studies in medical image segmentation have utilized clinical texts as complementary semantic cues without additional annotations. However, most existing methods utilize a single textual embedding and fail to capture hierarchical interactions between language and visual features, which limits their ability to leverage fine-grained cues essential for precise and detailed segmentation. In this regime, we propose Hierarchical Visual-Textual Mixing Network (HiMix), a novel multi-modal segmentation framework that mixes multi-scale image and text representations throughout the mask decoding process. HiMix progressively injects hierarchical text embedding, from high-level semantics to fine-grained spatial details, into corresponding image decoder layers to bridge the modality gap and enhance visual feature refinement at multiple levels of abstraction. Experiments on the QaTa-COV19, MosMed-Data+ and Kvasir-SEG datasets demonstrate that HiMix consistently outperforms uni-modal and multi-modal methods. Furthermore, HiMix exhibits strong generalization to unstructured textual formats, highlighting its practical applicability in real-world clinical scenarios.

title: AnyBald: Toward Realistic Diffusion-Based Hair Removal In-The-Wild
authors: Yongjun Choi, Seungoh Han, Soomin Kim, Sumin Son, Mohsen Rohani, Edgar Maucourant, Dongbo Min, Kyungdon Joo
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We present AnyBald, a novel framework for realistic hair removal from portrait images captured under diverse in-the-wild conditions. One of the key challenges in this task is the lack of high-quality paired data, as existing datasets are often low-quality, with limited viewpoint variation and diversity, making it difficult to handle real-world cases. To address this, we construct a scalable data augmentation pipeline that synthesizes high-quality hair and non-hair image pairs across diverse real-world scenarios, enabling effective generalization and scalable supervision. With this enriched dataset, we present a new hair removal framework that reformulates pretrained latent diffusion in-painting using learnable text prompts, removing the need for explicit masking at inference. In doing so, our model achieves natural hair removal with semantic preservation via implicit localization. To further enhance spatial precision, we introduce a regularization loss that guides the model to attend specifically to hair regions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AnyBald outperforms in removing hair while preserving identity and semantics across various in-the-wild domains. Our project page is here: https://vision3d-lab.github.io/anybald/.

title: DoTA: Latent Distribution Conditioned Data Attribution for Diffusion Models
authors: Ninad Joshi, Vivek Srivastava, Shirish Karande
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Diffusion models have emerged as the backbone of several modern generative AI models for effective visual content generation. However, their opaque nature raises fundamental questions about which training samples are responsible for specific generations, especially in applications involving bias detection, model auditing, and dataset curation. Data attribution seeks to identify the training samples that highly influence the output of generative models, a task that becomes especially challenging when targeting fine-scale attributes for attribution. Prior work has focused on broad concepts such as global features or entire images, often overlooking the nuances of fine-grained attributes and relying on group-based strategies that dilute individual influence. We propose a novel latent distribution conditioned method DoTA for data attribution. DoTA presents an effective search space pruning technique based on the latent distribution matching between the generated and training data for effective and controlled attribution. We demonstrate the effectiveness of attribution through extensive quantitative and qualitative evaluations across challenging settings such as counterfactual evaluation and robustness to adversarial attack.

title: Automated Down Syndrome Detection from Fetal Ultrasound Images Using Deep Learning: A MobileNetV2-Based Approach
authors: Vidhyasagar B S, Veeramallu Naga Sai Sandeep Kumar, Katta Naga Manikanta
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Down syndrome is one of the most common chromosomal abnormalities affecting fetal development. Early detection through ultrasound screening is essential for clinical decision-making and parental counseling. This paper presents a deep learning-based automated system for detecting Down syndrome from fetal ultrasound images using transfer learning with MobileNetV2 architecture. The approach analyzes nine critical anatomical markers: nuchal translucency (NT), nasal bone, intracranial translucency (IT), cisterna magna (CM), thalami, midbrain, palate, nasal tip, and nasal skin. The proposed model was trained and validated on a comprehensive dataset of 1,684 ultrasound images with 9,433 anatomical annotations and achieved an accuracy of 75.32%, sensitivity of 96.02%, specificity of 48.53%, and AUC-ROC of 0.8577. The high sensitivity reported herein indicates that the model is very effective as a screening tool because it identified 96% of cases of Down syndrome, with a negative predictive value of 90.41%. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms baseline methods and provides clinically relevant insights for prenatal screening programs.

title: CraftSVG: Multi-Object Text-to-SVG Synthesis via Layout Guided Diffusion
authors: Ayan Banerjee, Nityanand Mathur, Josep Lladós, Umapada Pal, Anjan Dutta
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Generating SVGs from text is a challenging vision task, requiring diverse yet realistic depictions of the seen as well as unseen entities. Existing research has been mostly limited to generating single-object rather than comprehensive scenes comprising multiple elements. In response, CraftSVG introduces an end-to-end framework for creating SVGs depicting entire scenes from a textual description. Utilizing a pre-trained LLM for layout generation from text via iterative in-context learning, CraftSVG introduces a per-box mask latent mechanism for accurate object placement. A fusion mechanism is developed to integrate the attention maps, employing a diffusion U-Net for coherent composition, which accelerates stroke initialization. Recognizing the importance of abstract SVGs in communication, we incorporated an MLP-based mechanism to simplify SVGs, with alignment and perceptual loss via differential rendering and opacity modulation to improve aesthetics. CraftSVG outperforms previous methods in abstraction, recognizability, and detail, as depicted by its CLIP-T: 0.5013, Aesthetic: 7.0779, score. The code is available at github.com/CraftSVG.

title: Occlusion Boundary and Depth: Mutual Enhancement via Multi-Task Learning
authors: Lintao Xu, Yinghao Wang, Chaohui Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Occlusion Boundary Estimation (OBE) identifies boundaries arising from both inter-object occlusions and self-occlusion within individual objects. This task is closely related to Monocular Depth Estimation (MDE), which infers depth from a single image, as Occlusion Boundaries (OBs) provide critical geometric cues for resolving depth ambiguities, while depth can conversely refine occlusion reasoning. In this paper, we aim to systematically model and exploit this mutually beneficial relationship. To this end, we propose MoDOT, a novel framework for joint estimation of depth and OBs, which incorporates a new Cross-Attention Strip Module (CASM) to leverage mid-level OB features for depth prediction, and a novel OB-Depth Constraint Loss (OBDCL) to enforce geometric consistency. To facilitate this study, we contribute OB-Hypersim, a large-scale photorealistic dataset with precise depth and self-occlusion-handled OB annotations. Extensive experiments on two synthetic datasets and NYUD-v2 demonstrate that MoDOT achieves significantly better performance than single-task baselines and multi-task competitors. Furthermore, models trained solely on our synthetic data demonstrate strong generalization to real-world scenes without fine-tuning, producing depth maps with sharper boundaries and improved geometric fidelity. Collectively, these results underscore the significant benefits of jointly modeling OBs and depth. Code and resources are available at HERE.

title: SmartScore: A Deep Learning Approach for Predicting Final Scores in IPL Matches
authors: M V L N Raja Rao, Lakshmi Sathvika Devanaboina, Koppaka Gnanendra Chowdary, Karra Geethika, Mugata Dhanunjay Prabhakar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The dynamic and sequential nature of Twenty20 cricket means that predictions of final scores in Indian Premier League matches are still difficult because of the nature of the game. Conventional statistical models are normally unable to reflect the temporal correlations and quick momentum changes that take place within an innings. To overcome this drawback, it introduces SmartScore, a deep learning-based system of score prediction that predicts match progression with the help of Long Short-Term Memory networks. The system processes ball-by-ball IPL data and features like over, runs, wickets, teams, venue, pitch, and toss. Experimental analysis of the historical IPL dataset shows that the model proposed has an average absolute error of about 12 runs, and the majority of its predictions lie within a range of ±15 runs. The comparative analysis indicates that the LSTM model is better than linear regression and random forest in that it has a higher consistency score of 0.59. Prediction correctness can further be confirmed using venue-wise evaluation, which has a close value of 62%. These findings suggest that SmartScore is a valid and real-time score predictor and is a valuable analysis tool in the assessment of IPL matches.

title: AI-Based Document Leak Attribution System
authors: Kayalvizhi P, Jayaprakash J, Dharanidharan C, Balasunil P
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: With the increasing flow of organizational operations digitalization, the threat of sensitive data leakage has increased immensely, and it is frequently posed by the actions of malicious insiders, the unwitting error, or the external cyberattack. The conventional security controls that include role-based access control, history records, and encryption are not effective outside of the controlled environment and ultimately become useless after the release of documents to third parties. The paper suggests an AI-based Multi-Mode Document Leak Attribution System that has the capability of offering accurate and post-distribution accountability. The system inserts invisible, unnoticeable watermarks into all of the documents by using an integration of natural language processing (NLP)-based text manipulations, metadata addition, and steganographic algorithms and produces durable and resistant fingerprints. The documents have a secure Token ID which is stored in encrypted database and hence they can be traced. When a leaked document is detected, the forensic engine looks at all the layers of the watermark, compares the patterns extracted with the database and creates a high-confidence attribution report, which contains the source of the leak, the time of the leak and the confidence. In unmatched cases, this system enables deep analysis and human-assisted review to make sure that everything was investigated. This framework can improve the security of an organization, increase its forensic, and offer scalability in identifying and attributing insider-induced leaks of documents because it closes the gap between the access control and post-distribution monitoring.

title: MSA Unet A Dual Scale Mining Self Attention Network for Accurate Segmentation in Echocardiographic Images for MI Diagnosis
authors: Anushalin P.S, P Arunachalam
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of cardiac structures from echocardiographic images is essential for the quantitative assessment of heart function. However, the low contrast, noise, and complex structure make the segmentation process more challenging. Recently, deep learning models with attention mechanisms have been widely used for segmentation. However, there is still room for improvement in boundary areas. To overcome the boundary segmentation problem, we propose a novel Dual-Scale Mining Self-Attention U-Net (DS-MSA U-Net) architecture that effectively addresses boundary issues by integrating multi-scale feature extraction, mining modulation, and self-attention mechanisms into the conventional U-Net model. The proposed Mining Self-Attention (MSA) module dynamically modulates the self-attention computation by incorporating a learned mining map, which highlights spatial regions of high uncertainty in cardiac boundaries and noise-affected areas. This mining map is derived from feature-level statistics, such as variance or estimated uncertainty, and is used to control both the query and key representations within the self-attention computation. In order to better represent features, at every level of the network a dual-scale extraction module is employed to obtain contextual information at different receptive fields and enhance the border delineation of cardiac chambers like the left ventricle and myocardium. The incorporation of multi-scale learning here guarantees that both global shapes and fine boundary details are maintained. Experimental assessments performed using publicly available echocardiography datasets confirm that the proposed DS-MSA U-Net performs better than baseline and state-of-the-art models. Having greater segmentation accuracy, the proposed approach presents a promising solution for accurate automated cardiac image analysis in clinical settings.

title: CAMP-VQA: Caption-Embedded Multimodal Perception for No-Reference Quality Assessment of Compressed Video
authors: Xinyi Wang, Angeliki Katsenou, Junxiao Shen, David Bull
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: The prevalence of user-generated content (UGC) on platforms such as YouTube and TikTok has rendered no-reference (NR) perceptual video quality assessment (VQA) vital for optimizing video delivery. Nonetheless, the characteristics of non-professional acquisition and the subsequent transcoding of UGC video on sharing platforms present significant challenges for NR-VQA. Although NR-VQA models attempt to infer mean opinion scores (MOS), their modeling of subjective scores for compressed content remains limited due to the absence of fine-grained perceptual annotations of artifact types. To address these challenges, we propose CAMP-VQA, a novel NR-VQA framework that exploits the semantic understanding capabilities of large vision-language models. Our approach introduces a quality-aware prompting mechanism that integrates video metadata (e.g., resolution, frame rate, bitrate) with key fragments extracted from inter-frame variations to guide the BLIP-2 pretraining approach in generating fine-grained quality captions. A unified architecture has been designed to model perceptual quality across three dimensions: semantic alignment, temporal characteristics, and spatial characteristics. These multimodal features are extracted and fused, then regressed to video quality scores. Extensive experiments on a wide variety of UGC datasets demonstrate that our model consistently outperforms existing NR-VQA methods, achieving improved accuracy without the need for costly manual fine-grained annotations. Our method achieves the best performance in terms of average rank and linear correlation (SRCC: 0.928, PLCC: 0.938) compared to state-of-the-art methods. The source code and trained models, along with a user-friendly demo, are available at: https://github.com/xinyiW915/CAMP-VQA.

title: Concord: Concept-Informed Diffusion for Dataset Distillation
authors: Jianyang Gu, Haonan Wang, Ruoxi Jia, Saeed Vahidian, Vyacheslav Kungurtsev, Wei Jiang, Yiran Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Dataset distillation (DD) has witnessed significant progress in creating small datasets that encapsulate rich information from large original ones. Particularly, methods based on generative priors show promising performance while maintaining computational efficiency and cross-architecture generalization. However, the generation process lacks explicit controllability for each sample. Previous distillation methods primarily match the real distribution from the perspective of the entire dataset, whereas overlooking concept completeness at the instance level. The missing or incorrectly represented object details cannot be efficiently compensated due to the constrained sample amount typical in DD settings. To this end, we propose incorporating the concept understanding of large language models (LLMs) to perform Concept-Informed Diffusion (Concord) for dataset distillation. Specifically, distinguishable and fine-grained concepts are retrieved based on category labels to inform the denoising process and refine essential object details. These concepts can be applied to any diffusion-based DD framework to enhance both the controllability and interpretability of the distilled image generation, without relying on pre-trained classifiers. We demonstrate the efficacy of Concord by achieving state-of-the-art performance on ImageNet-1K and subsets. Code is released at Concord.

title: Learning Compact Video Representations for Efficient Long-form Video Understanding in Large Multimodal Models
authors: Yuxiao Chen, Jue Wang, Zhikang Zhang, Jingru Yi, Xu Zhang, Yang Zou, Zhaowei Cai, Jianbo Yuan, Xinyu Li, Hao Yang, Davide Modolo
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: With recent advancements in video backbone architectures, combined with the remarkable achievements of large language models (LLMs), the analysis of long-form videos spanning tens of minutes has become both feasible and increasingly prevalent. However, the inherently redundant nature of video sequences poses significant challenges for contemporary state-of-the-art models. These challenges stem from two primary aspects: 1) efficiently incorporating a larger number of frames within memory constraints, and 2) extracting discriminative information from the vast volume of input data. In this paper, we introduce a novel end-to-end schema for long-form video understanding, which includes an information-density-based adaptive video sampler (AVS) and an autoencoder-based spatiotemporal video compressor (SVC) integrated with a multimodal large language model (MLLM). Our proposed system offers two major advantages: it adaptively and effectively captures essential information from video sequences of varying durations, and it achieves high compression rates while preserving crucial discriminative information. The proposed framework demonstrates promising performance across various benchmarks, excelling in both long-form video understanding tasks and standard video understanding benchmarks. These results underscore the versatility and efficacy of our approach, particularly in managing the complexities of prolonged video sequences.

title: Distributed Computing Paradigms: Comparative Review of Cluster, Cloud, and Grid Technologies
authors: Venkatesh Bhat, Diya Shetty, Fredrina Saldanha, Chandushree B S, Daneshwari S Jammaladini
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: As distributed computing continues to evolve, it has become a cornerstone for efficiently processing massive data and executing complex workloads across diverse infrastructures. Among the dominant paradigms—cluster computing, grid computing, and cloud computing—each offers distinct architectures, operational models, and performance characteristics. This paper presents a structured comparative review of these paradigms, emphasizing their strengths, limitations, and application domains. Clusters deliver high performance and low latency for tightly coupled workloads; grids enable large-scale collaboration across heterogeneous, federated environments; and clouds provide elasticity, scalability, and cost-effective provisioning. Furthermore, the study discusses hybrid and emerging models driven by AI-based scheduling, containerization, and sustainability-oriented designs. The analysis highlights that no single paradigm universally outperforms the others; instead, optimal selection depends on workload characteristics, scalability requirements, and operational goals.

title: ClusterMine: Robust Label-Free Visual Out-Of-Distribution Detection via Concept Mining from Text Corpora
authors: Nikolas Adaloglou, Diana Petrusheva, Mohamed Asker, Felix Michels, Markus Kollmann
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Large-scale visual out-of-distribution (OOD) detection has witnessed remarkable progress by leveraging vision-language models such as CLIP. However, a significant limitation of current methods is their reliance on a pre-defined set of in-distribution (ID) ground-truth label names (positives). These fixed label names can be unavailable, unreliable at scale, or become less relevant due to in-distribution shifts after deployment. Towards truly unsupervised OOD detection, we utilize widely available text corpora for positive label mining, bypassing the need for positives. In this paper, we utilize widely available text corpora for positive label mining under a general concept mining paradigm. Within this framework, we propose ClusterMine, a novel method for mining positive labels. ClusterMine is the first method to achieve state-of-the-art OOD detection performance without access to positive labels. It extracts positive concepts from a large text corpus by combining visual-only sample consistency (via clustering) and zero-shot image-text consistency. Our experimental study reveals that ClusterMine is scalable across a plethora of CLIP models and achieves state-of-the-art robustness to covariate in-distribution shifts. The code is available at https://github.com/HHU-MMBS/clustermine_wacv_official.

title: SCALEX: Scalable Concept and Latent Exploration for Diffusion Models
authors: E. Zhixuan Zeng, Yuhao Chen, Alexander Wong
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Image generation models frequently encode social biases, including stereotypes tied to gender, race, and profession. Existing methods for analyzing these biases in diffusion models either focus narrowly on predefined categories or depend on manual interpretation of latent directions. These constraints limit scalability and hinder the discovery of subtle or unanticipated patterns.We introduce SCALEX, a framework for scalable and automated exploration of diffusion model latent spaces. SCALEX extracts semantically meaningful directions from H-space using only natural language prompts, enabling zero-shot interpretation without retraining or labelling. This allows systematic comparison across arbitrary concepts and large-scale discovery of internal model associations. We show that SCALEX detects gender bias in profession prompts, ranks semantic alignment across identity descriptors, and reveals clustered conceptual structure without supervision. By linking prompts to latent directions directly, SCALEX makes bias analysis in diffusion models more scalable, interpretable, and extensible than prior approaches.

title: Training-free Detection of Text-to-video Generations via Over-coherence
authors: Jonathan Brokman, Oren Rachmil, Omer Hofman, Roy Betser, Amit Giloni, Roman Vainshtein, Hisashi Kojima
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Text-to-video generative models have emerged as powerful tools in content creation, capable of synthesizing highly realistic videos from textual prompts. However, the rapid advancement of these models introduces significant security and trust concerns - adversaries can now fabricate convincing videos, while existing detection methods struggle to generalize to unseen generative techniques. This is due to existing approaches’ reliance on supervised learning, requiring continuous dataset curation and retraining, which is impractical given the fast-paced evolution of generative models. In this work, we introduce the first training-free detection method for text-to-video generations, eliminating the need for labeled training data or prior exposure to generation techniques. Our approach exploits a fundamental weakness in text-to-video models: Unnatural temporal over-coherence in frame transitions. By leveraging a novel time-coherence detection criterion, our method identifies subtle inconsistencies in video embeddings, capturing temporal artifact patterns that reliably distinguish AI-generated content from real videos. We extensively evaluate our approach on an extended combination of benchmarks, amounting to 23 video generative models and 54.4K diverse videos, demonstrating that it outperforms the existing baselines, and shown to be effective on unseen generative models. This work establishes a new direction for training-free detection of text-to-video generated content, providing a scalable and time resilient solution.

title: Double-Sided Laser-Induced Graphene Memristors for Thermally Tunable Neuromorphic Computing
authors: Soha Maqbool Bhat, Fazian Tariq Beigh, Dhiman Mallick
abstract: In this work, the fabrication and characterisation of double-sided laser-induced graphene (D-LIG) memristors on non-adhesive polyimide (PI) substrates are presented. SEM, XRD, and Raman analyses confirm the formation of few-layer, porous graphene with low defect density (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> ~0.57, I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2D</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> ~0.62) on both surfaces. Electrical measurements show non-linear hysteresis indicative of memristive switching, while infrared thermography under 8 mA bias reveals self-regulated Joule heating and efficient vertical thermal transport through the PI layer. The strong electro-thermal coupling between dual LIG layers enables controlled resistance modulation and stable operation. These findings highlight the potential of D-LIG memristors as scalable, thermally adaptive platforms for neuromorphic and reservoir computing applications.

title: Smart AI-Driven Logistics Optimization and Sentiment Analysis the Role of Technology Trust and Organizational Readliness in Enhancing Business Performance
authors: Muhammad Irvan Firdaus, Murniati
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: The adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) in logistics is expected to enhance efficiency and business performance, yet empirical evidence remains inconsistent, particularly in emerging economies. This study investigates the roles of technology trust and organizational readiness in shaping the relationship between AI adoption and logistics outcomes, while viewing sentiment analysis as a perceived AI-enabled analytical capability rather than a technical tool. Using a quantitative explanatory approach, data were collected from 100 logistics and supply chain professionals in Indonesia and analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The findings show that organizational readiness has a strong positive impact on logistics efficiency, while AI adoption and technology trust exert weaker, indirect effects. Although perceived sentiment analysis capability supports efficiency, it does not directly enhance logistics performance. Overall, the results emphasize that successful AI-driven logistics transformation relies more on organizational preparedness and user trust than on technology adoption alone.

title: EfficientNet-DR: A Deep Learning Approach for Diabetic Retinopathy Detection and Classification
authors: Navyansh Kothari, Aryan Anand, Sandeep Kumar Satapathy, Shruti Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is one of the main causes of preventable vision loss, especially among working-age populations. In this paper, an automated detection and multiclass classification system for DR is proposed, which is optimized for deployment on low-end devices with very confined hardware and limited software capabilities. For the purpose of this paper, we relied on the first ever published dataset representing the Indian population, IDRiD, that comprises pixel-level annotations of DR lesions and normal retinal structures. All images are also classified on the basis of DR severities ranging from 0 (no DR) to 4 (proliferative DR), which are essential while giving precise diagnoses and the need for intervention. Preprocessing techniques including data cleaning, resizing, normalization, transformations, and data augmentation form an integral part of our methodology to significantly improve model performance. The trained and validated model of EfficientNet-B0 achieved a testing accuracy of 84.2 % for all the DR stages. The clinical utility of these results highlights the necessity to apply the idea of deep learning instruments to ophthalmology to more adequately screen system DR and patient outcomes.

title: A Unified ViT-BiLSTM-CTC Framework for Multilingual Handwritten Text Recognition
authors: Gande Varun Kumar, G Harshavardhan, Lekshmi S. Raveendran
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: In India many people still use handwritten documents in many daily scenarios for example in the form of notes,forms,medical documents and agreement papers,so when these documents are being referred by somebody else there might be a chance of misread and misunderstandings,so converting these documents and making a digital version is a crucial task to deal with this problem. Existing systems or website do not provide a one stop solution for this problem, these existing systems only provide support for english language but not for indic languages. In our OCR system,we tested Telugu,Tamil,Hindi and English scripts,and our model handles most of the characters correctly,our system uses a VIT(vision transformer) for extracting visual features of a image and a BiLSTM-model for analyzing front and back sequences and arranging characters in the output as a sequence. The total system consists of preprocessing,segmentation,recognition,and document generation. Results show that the approach performs well across different styles of handwriting and languages.we achieved a character error rate of 5 % and a word accuracy of 75 %, these results show it performs acceptably for many real life situations.

title: Grid-Based Spatial Encoding for Multimodal LLMs in Facility Management
authors: Viviana Crescitelli, Koji Maeda, Mitsuki Ikuta
abstract: This paper presents ongoing research on a spatial analysis framework designed to improve the performance of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in real-time facility management, with parking lot monitoring as a representative use case. Although MLLMs perform well in object recognition, they often fail to capture precise spatial relationships, limiting localization accuracy. Existing methods address this issue but typically depend on specialized sensors, prebuilt maps, or complex embodied systems, which hinder deployment in resourceconstrained environments. To overcome these limitations, we introduce a grid-based spatial encoding method that embeds structured layouts directly into prompts, anchoring visual elements to explicit reference zones. This improves localization, obstruction detection, and reasoning in occluded or ambiguous scenes. In real-world parking scenarios, our approach increased space identification accuracy from 29% to 86% and obstruction detection from 15% to 85%. The system transforms ordinary surveillance into actionable spatial insights, supporting frontline workers and advancing safe, efficient, and sustainable operations in line with Japan's Society 5.0 vision. These preliminary findings demonstrate that prompt-based spatial grounding can significantly improve the spatial reasoning capabilities of MLLMs without retraining or additional sensors, and future work will extend validation to broader facility contexts.

title: AI-Powered Visibility Enhancement System for Safe and Intelligent Driving
authors: Vangimalla Roopeswara Sai Reddy, Nivethitha Vijayaraj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Road safety is an overriding issue in all parts of the world, especially where visibility is highly undermined, thus increasing the risks of vehicle crashes and loss of lives. Quite often, conventional driver aid technologies do not provide any uniform functionality during nighttime, foggy, or rainy weather when visual obscuration is high. Therefore, in this paper, a visibility enhancement system reinforced with AI is outlined, which aims to optimize the perception of driving in real-time and thus, counteract poor video quality and alleviate bad weather conditions. The suggested architecture will use convolutional neural networks along with generative adversarial networks to reconstruct these images with high quality in visual imagery based on noisy or opaque inputs. The system supports low-latency inference using edge-based processing, and the cloud orchestration, which should be based on the usage of the Google Cloud AI, supports the large-scale optimization of the model. Empirical validation results of the system on benchmark datasets like KITTI, BDD100K, and Foggy Cityscapes evidenced that the system is higher in terms of performance measures, like the highest signal-to-noise ratio, structural similarity index, and speed of inference. The conclusions drawn from the experiment show that there are significant improvements in the accuracy of object detection and overall scene interpretability in cases of unfavorable visibility. The implications of these findings are that AI-mediated visibility improvement has a huge potential to advance the next-generation advanced driver-assist systems and autonomous driving platforms, which in turn would help to develop a safer transportation ecosystem.

title: Very-Large-Scale Integration Implementation using Multiplier Topologies with BTC Algorithm and Threshold Optimization for Efficient Computation
authors: R Manasa, Ahmad Al-Qerem, B Mouli Chandra, R Govindaraj, G. Karthik, Bharathi Panduri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) refers to the technology of designing and manufacturing integrated circuits that can accommodate thousands to millions of transistors on a single chip. This paper presents a VIMBTE model that achieves very high performance and hardware efficiency by integrating a drastically improved Block Truncation Coding (BTC) algorithm, a radix-4 Booth multiplier, and a highly customizable VLSI platform suitable for real-time multimedia processing. The main breakthroughs are an adaptive threshold optimization method that enhances Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) without adding to the compression cost and the implementation of $4: 2: 2$ subsampling to reduce the data redundancy level and retain the visual quality. The radix-4 Booth multiplier speeds up arithmetic operations and at the same time reduces the hardware complexity. The hardware implemented in VHDL employs open-source EDA tools for RTL analysis, optimization, binding, and verification. The performance analysis consists of normalized delay, power dissipation, delay, power consumption, and delay metrics.

title: Benchmarking Traditional and Deep Sequential Models for Text Sentiment Analysis
authors: Silvi Avayan, Anna Mikayelyan, Sachin Kumar, Pradeep Kumar Mallick
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Sentiment analysis (SA) helps identify people's opinions and emotions from written text. This paper aims to test and compare machine learning, deep learning, and sequential modeling techniques for sentiment classification by utilizing the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 K}$</tex> Movie Reviews (IMDb) dataset. Four standard baselines (Naive Bayes, Logistic Regression, Linear SVM, Random Forest) are used for comparison and further extended to deeplearning and sequential models. Moreover, an attention-based encoder-decoder is also included in the analysis. The results reveal that the classical models, such as Linear SVM (90.22 %) and Logistic Regression <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(90.00 \%)$</tex>, achieved strong accuracy with less training time and computational cost. However, deep learning architectures performed better overall, with the Feedforward Neural Network and CNN reaching 97.92 % and 97.27 % accuracy, respectively. The attention-based encoder-decoder and Bi-LSTM-GRU also performed well by capturing contextual and sequential relationships. Overall, the study highlights the balance between computational efficiency and predictive performance, and provides useful guidance for selecting appropriate sentiment analysis models based on accuracy and resource availability.

title: Low-Power Intelligent Safety Tool Cabinet: Integration of Electro-Osmotic Dehumidification and IoT Management System
authors: Ke Feng, Daoze Lin, Jinlan Xu, Zhongxiu Weng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: This paper presents the development of a lowpower zero-drainage intelligent safety tool cabinet integrating electro-osmotic solid adsorption dehumidification technology and IoT-enabled management system. Aimed at addressing the limitations of traditional cabinets, including high energy consumption, manual drainage requirements, and inefficient dehumidification <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.3 \mathrm{L} / \mathrm{D})$</tex>, this study introduces a novel system architecture combining electro-dynamic dehumidification and smart tool lifecycle management. The core innovations include: (1) a composite adsorbent material optimized through molecular dynamics simulation and 3D printing, achieving 0.51L/D dehumidification capacity in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex> space at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$35^{\circ} \mathrm{C} / 85 {\%} \text{RH}$</tex>; (2) a fuzzy PID control algorithm enabling ± 3%RH humidity accuracy with energy consumption reduced by 85%; (3) an IoT platform with RFID-based tool tracking and two-factor authentication for automated inventory management. Field validation in 15 cabinets across 7 power tool warehouses demonstrated a 40% reduction in tool damage incidents and 60% improvement in operational efficiency. This work provides a sustainable solution for real-time humidity control and digital traceability of safety tools, meeting industry standards while opening new avenues for AI-driven predictive maintenance and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> integration.

title: The Role of Error in Enhancing Probabilistic Quantum Deletion
authors: A. Nancy, S. Balakrishnan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: In this paper, the probabilistic quantum deletion machine constructed from the NB-deletion machine, controlled by the deletion probability p and the error probability q. It is observed that, under specific conditions, controlled errors can enhance the deletion process: when p is minimal and q is maximal, both deletion and retention fidelities reach their optimal values. Compared to the original NB-machine, this probabilistic machine performs better in both deleting and preserving states. Although the output states do not violate Bell’s inequalities, they still achieve teleportation fidelities above the classical limit. The entanglement of machine’s output state appears only when p > 0, and the mixedness increases with p. Altogether, these results indicate that controlled error can enhance certain non-local features of a probabilistic deletion machine.

title: Empowering Advanced Analytics: The Role of NLP and Semantic Modeling in Self-Service Risk Intelligence
authors: Vinoth Kumar Arumugam, Jeevitha Sakkarai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The growing tendency toward the use of unstructured textual data in organizational settings has added to the pressure to develop intelligent and user-friendly risk analytics that would be able to extract contextual and semantic risk indicators. To overcome this difficulty, a superior selfservice risk intelligence architecture is introduced, based on transformer-based natural language processing, ontologydriven semantic representation, and reinforcement-based prioritization. Its architecture applies the use of contextual semantic encoders, ontology-aligned concept mapping, semantic query interpretation, and adaptive risk scoring to facilitate explainable and autonomous risk intelligence. The evaluation using experiments shows that the ratio of the semantic concept alignment is $94.87 \%$, the contextual embedding stability is 0.931, and the latent risk discovery density is 0.882. The framework has a semantic query interpretation accuracy of $93.54 \%$, a self-service interaction index of 0.918, and a latency of risk insight production of 184 ms. The comparative analysis shows that the risk intelligence maturity index is higher, 0.94, and is characterized by better semantic awareness, flexibility, and explainability. These findings support the usefulness of semantic and NLP-based self-service analytics in terms of proactive, scalable, and context-driven risk intelligence.

title: Intelligent Meeting Insights Generator Using Speech-to-Text and LLM-Based Semantic Categorization Algorithm
authors: Madhushree. R, T.N. Prabhu, Gopalakrishnan. K
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Meetings produce large amounts of unorganized audio data, and therefore, manual data recording of decisions, action items, and discussion outcomes are inefficient, and prone to error. The given paper offers a Meeting Insights Generator powered by AI, which combines the speech recognition (ASR), semantic reasoning with a large language model (LLM), and sentiment analysis to turn meeting audio into structured and actionable insights automatically. The proposed framework uses semantic categorization and reasoning multi-stage pipeline, not using text-only summarization as traditional systems: modules of the proposed system are deployed in a hybrid local-cloud mode. It is a scalable, interpretable and cost-effective design. Simulated meeting and benchmark datasets like the AMI and ICSI Meeting Corpora are experimentally tested to provide better contextual relevance, lower redundancy and competitive results under ROUGE-L, Word Error Rate (WER), and sentiment accuracy measures. The findings have shown that the proposed system is quite suitable to the actual analysis of meetings in academic and organizational settings.

title: Intelligent Vehicle Engine Lock Using Alcohol and Heart Rate Monitoring
authors: M. Ishwarya, AnandhajothiNalini. R, Visalatchi. K, Vicshiya Sherin. V, Manimehalai. S, Velan. K
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The Connected Vehicle safety framework and monitoring system that aims to provide driver comfort and safety, as well as prevent the occurrence of accidents that are a common cause of the impairment of the driver's abilities or health anomalies. The system has a heart rate sensor, temperature sensor, and alcohol detection module besides an ESP8266 Wi-Fi controller for on-the fly monitoring and control. Once the driver puts his finger on the sensor module, body temperature and heart rate are measured by the system, and an alarm is activated if the temperature is above 45 degrees Celsius. At the same time, an alcohol sensor is looking for the level of intoxication. Using the Blinkit application, all sensor data are sent to a web dashboard, which shows the car's health and the driver's condition in real time. The principle of a car will only be activated when all three parameters such as heart rate, temperature, and alcohol are at normal levels and gives accuracy up to 98 %. If there is any abnormality, the engine will be kept at a standstill, and a warning will be sent through the web interface. This is an excellent and very allowed way of demonstrating the implementation of IoT driven safety system that can help in accident prevention, remote control, and the promotion of responsible driving through technology immersion.

title: Scalable Fabrication of Double-Sided Laser-Induced Graphene for High-Sensitvity Triboelectric Pressure Sensors
authors: Tausif Kamal, Faizan Tariq Beigh, Ankur Goswami, Dhiman Mallick
abstract: Laser-induced graphene (LIG)-based sensors are durable, lightweight, and highly versatile, making them ideal for a wide range of potential applications, including healthcare, environmental monitoring, and industrial sensing. This study investigates the feasibility of utilizing LIG as both the electrode and the triboelectric layer in self-powered pressure sensors. Using CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> laser technology, LIG is fabricated via a single-step, chemical-free laser ablation process on polymeric substrates, offering a scalable and cost-effective method for developing triboelectric nanogenerators (TENGs). In this work, double-layer LIG structures with tailored resistances were fabricated on polyimide (PI) substrates, enabling independent control over the conductivity and charge storage properties of the electrode and triboelectric layers. This approach eliminates the need for additional materials, as distinct layers are formed directly on the top and bottom surfaces. A double-layer LIG-based TENG (DTENG) was developed using this configuration, with CYTOP employed as the counter triboelectric material. The performance of the DTENG was compared with that of a conventional copper-based TENG (CTENG) and a single-layer LIG-based TENG (STENG). Comparative analysis revealed that the DTENG exhibited a significantly enhanced peak voltage of 4 V. Finally, the fabricated devices were evaluated for pressure sensing, and their responses were analytically compared. The high sensitivity of 29.31 mV/kPa demonstrates that DTENGs are promising candidates for high-performance self-powered sensing systems.

title: Explainable Multimodal Emotion Recognition Using Integrated Eye-Tracking and Galvanic Skin Response Signals
authors: Abhinav Rana, Kiran Jot Singh, Divneet Singh Kapoor
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Emotion recognition is an important part of affective computing and human-computer interaction. This paper suggests a multimodal emotion recognition system that can be explained with eye-tracking and galvanic skin response (GSR) measures to enhance the accuracy and understandability of classification. Eye-tracking capabilities are used to record visual attention and cognitive involvement, whereas GSR measures are used to estimate autonomic emotional arousal. The collection of synchronized multimodal data was performed with the stimuli which evoked emotions, they were preprocessed and features were extracted, and multimodal fusion was performed. Emotion classification was performed with the help of machine learning models, and explainable artificial intelligence methods were also implemented to explain the decisions of the models. The experimental findings indicate that multimodal approach is much better than the unimodal systems, and early fusion is the most accurate. The predominant role of the GSR amplitude and eye fixation measures is pointed out by explainability analysis and is expected according to the existing psychophysiological theories. The suggested architecture is strong, transparent, and has prospective applications in the real world like affect-aware systems, mental health monitoring, and adaptive human-computer interaction.

title: Framework for Explainable Learning in Federated 3D Multimodal Cancer Detection
authors: D.Nagadurga Naveen, N.Ankith Yadav, P. Rajalakshmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The uneven nature of the multimodal data, absence of sizable, centralized datasets brought on by privacy and data-sharing regulations across healthcare organizations make it difficult to diagnose cancer early using medical imaging. Traditional deep learning models frequently use centralized training and single-modality data, which restricts generalization and puts private patient data at risk. In order to get over these restrictions, this research suggests a Federated 3D Multimodal Explainable Learning Framework that uses distributed 3D medical imaging and clinical data to enable collaborative and privacy-preserving cancer diagnosis. The framework enables efficient learning from multimodal inputs like CT, MRI, and patient metadata by integrating Transformer-based encoders for cross-modal feature fusion and Convolutional neural networks(CNNs) in three dimensions for the extraction of spatial features. A federated aggregation strategy (FedAvg) synchronizes local models from many medical institutions without sharing raw data, protecting patient privacy.Additionally, by emphasizing the important areas and characteristics affecting the diagnosis, explainable AI modules like Grad-CAM and attention visualization offer interpretability. According to experimental assessment, the suggested strategy offers transparency and complies with healthcare privacy regulations while outperforming conventional centralized and unimodal methods for accuracy, F1-score, and resilience. This technique creates a new path for safe and explicable AI-based cancer detection by bridging the gaps between data privacy, multimodal integration, and model interpretability.

title: Personalized Healthcare Assistant for Lung Cancer Detection Using Hybrid Machine Learning Approach
authors: M Sitha Ram, Bhanu Chander Balusa, Phaneendra Kanakamedala, Vejendla Meenakshi, Chikoti Trisha, Allada Shankar, Vemuri Praveena
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Lung cancer is one of the main cause of cancer related death globally so the early detection techniques are really essential for reducing the deaths globally every year. In order to diagnose lung cancer, our research proposes a hybrid machine learning technique which uses XGBoost for tabular data analysis and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for histopathological image classification. The suggested approach makes use of two datasets: a histopathological image dataset with 25,000 images of lung cancer, and a lung cancer dataset with 15 clinical and demographic variables. While the CNN model showed 91.06% training and validation accuracy for image classification whereas the XGBoost model achieved 98.39% accuracy for tabular data set. we used Streamlit to create a web based tool for this research project for real time prediction. The hybrid approach is great for early lung cancer screening programs and early lung cancer detection and support systems.

title: MooTrack360: A Novel Fisheye Camera Dataset for Robust Multi Dairy Cow Detection and Tracking
authors: Rasmus Gjerlund K. Christiansen, Toan Van Nguyen, Lasse Rose Malskær, Leon Bodenhagen, Dirk Kraft
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: MooTrack360 is a novel top-down fisheye dataset designed to support the development of robust camera surveillance and monitoring systems in large-scale, real-world environments. While centered around continuous livestock monitoring of Holstein dairy cows, the dataset addresses general challenges in computer vision such as fisheye distortion, overlapping fields of view, variable lighting, and occlusions. It includes 102,747 annotated cow instances across 1,500 images, each labelled as "standing" or "lying", along with a 1-hour annotated video sequence for tracking evaluation. Several unannotated sequences are included for development and analysis. The dataset enables detection and tracking under conditions ranging from daylight to infrared-assisted nighttime imaging, and facilitates both application-specific and generalized model evaluation. A calibration pipeline based on the Double Sphere Camera model is provided to support distortion correction and precise spatial localization. An ac companying end-to-end training framework further addresses challenges such as illumination changes and occlusions. Benchmarks using state-of-the-art detection and tracking methods demonstrate the dataset’s potential to advance research in non-invasive, camera-based monitoring across domains. The complete dataset—including annotated images, tracking sequences, sample videos, calibration footage, and the full codebase, is available at: https://zenodo.org/records/15470268.

title: Enhanced Liver Tumor Segmentation and Feature Extraction Using Gaussian Filtering and LICA on CT Images
authors: D. Anandkumar, S. John Justin Thangaraj, C. Alamelu, M. Vallikkannu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Liver cancer is among the major causes of cancer related deaths in the world and this is partly caused by the late stages of diagnosis and inability to detect it early through effective means. Effective segmentation and sound feature detection of liver CT images play a significant role in enhancing the credibility of the diagnosis in addition to therapy choices. This paper will offer a superior approach that combines Gaussian filter and Linear Iterative Clustering Algorithm (LICA) to effectively demarcate and extract quantitative characteristics of liver tumors. The image pre-processes start with the use of Gaussian filtering on the CT images to suppress noise and preserve important anatomical information. The ROIs are manually defined based on local intensity minima to enhance focused segmentation. LICA is used to perform effective clustering according to pixel intensity and spatial closeness, and thus, accurate description of tumor extent is achieved even in complex and low-contrast regions. Following segmentation, key geometric properties (such as area) are defined using the technique, and entropy, energy, contrast and homogeneity from Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM) based texture descriptors are used to quantitatively describe the shape of the tumor. The experimental results over the multiple CT image samples have demonstrated that the proposed method can achieve good segmentation accuracy and refined features for further classification. Tumor detection is consistently achieved over a wide range of tumor sizes and contrast levels with low false detection rates. In addition, its high computational efficiency allows the possibility of real-time integration in the clinical environment. Unlike deep learning models, the proposed system is fully interpretable thus making it more suitable for healthcare industry, where the need for explanation is crucial.

title: Design and Comparative Analysis of PID Controllers on FPGA for Power-Sensitive Applications Using Various Arithmetic Architectures
authors: Nowreen Mukhtar, Liyaqat Nazir, Asifa Mehraj Baba
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: This study investigates the design and implementation of Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) controllers on FPGA hardware, focusing on the trade-offs between various arithmetic architectures (Ripple Carry Adders (RCA) and Carry Look-Ahead Adders (CLA)) across variable bitwidth architectures. The results demonstrate different performance characteristics by comparing area, power consumption, delay, and Area Delay Product (ADP). RCA demonstrates superior area efficiency, utilizing 2.35% fewer Look-Up Tables (LUTs) than CLA (e.g., 26942 vs. 27575 LUTs for 64-bit) and lower power consumption (1.255W vs. 1.398W for 64-bit). Conversely, CLA achieves higher operational speeds (155.424 MHz vs. 98.154 MHz for 8-bit) and reduced delays (6.434 ns vs. 10.188 ns for 8-bit). However, CLA incurs a 11.35% power penalty at 64-bit and exhibits 3.25% higher ADP at 64-bit, underscoring a critical speed-resource trade-off. These results emphasize the necessity of selecting adder architectures aligned with application priorities: RCA for power, and area-sensitive systems and CLA for latency-critical implementations. The study provides actionable insights for optimizing FPGA-based PID controllers, paving the way for future exploration of hybrid architectures to balance efficiency and performance.

title: Perceptually Weighted Late Fusion for Robust Binocular Gaze Estimation in Unconstrained Environments
authors: Ngawang Choeda, Biranchi Panda, Phub Namgay
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: The variation in asymmetric illumination and artifacts in ocular regions poses a significant challenge for gaze estimation when moving from controlled laboratories to a realworld environment, despite its promising applications in multiple domains. The standard gaze estimation methods either assume a perfect ocular symmetry or use simple fusion techniques, often overlooking minor interocular asymmetries. This paper introduces a perceptually weighted late fusion framework inspired by human perception for binocular gaze estimation, which implements a SoftMax-weighted fusion of each eye's gaze estimates within a dual-stream LightGazeNet architecture, using the Weber contrast between the sclera (white) and iris (colored) regions of the eye as a perceptual quality metric. Experiments on 15,000 samples from 15 participants in the MPIIGaze dataset show that the proposed method reduces the mean Euclidean error to 60.63 mm, achieving a 6.67% and 12.53% improvement compared to “Left-Eye-Only” and “Right-Eye-Only” baseline models, in addition to reducing the error variance on both axes.

title: Digital Forensic AI You Can Explain: A Case Study on Video Source Camera Identification
authors: Maryna Veksler, Kemal Akkaya, Selcuk Uluagac
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: In recent years, artificial intelligence (AI) has significantly impacted digital forensics, yet its broader deployment remains limited due to the difficulty of explaining AI decisions. Explainable AI (XAI) presents a promising solution to increase transparency and trust, but its application in digital forensics is still underexplored. In this work, we present a practical and structured explainable digital forensics AI (xDFAI) approach tailored to the forensic task of video source camera identification (VSCI). Our method enables forensic examiners to interpret the behavior of AI models, assess whether decisions are driven by intended logic or arise from random or content-dependent artifacts, and establish the integrity and reliability of explanations. We implement and evaluate this approach on two state-of-the-art VSCI models, providing step-by-step analysis of explanation quality, spatial consistency of high-impact features, and content dependence. Our results reveal that although models achieve strong classification accuracy, their explanations lack spatial stability and are impacted by video content, raising concerns about forensic reliability. To support reproducibility and future research, we provide an open-source implementation. This work underscores the potential of XAI to improve transparency in digital forensics and highlights the challenges of interpreting and presenting results. Our study takes an important step toward the operational deployment of xDFAI in multimedia forensics.

title: Hybrid EfficientNet-SVM Model for Analysis of Health Insurance Contract Breaches: An Academic Perspective
authors: J. Gayathri, Behin R.V, M. Sandeep Kumar, D Prerna, Sri Supraja S N, Vardini Venket
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Health insurance contract breaches are a critical issue, often leading to legal disputes between policyholders and insurers. This study presents a novel hybrid model combining EfficientNet and Support Vector Machine (SVM) for the legal analysis of health insurance contract breaches. The model is designed to identify and classify breaches in health insurance contracts, leveraging advanced feature extraction and classification techniques. Data was sourced from public legal records, judgments, and dispute summaries, followed by preprocessing steps such as anonymization, tokenization, and vectorization using TF-IDF and Word2Vec. The hybrid EfficientNet-SVM model integrates deep learning and machine learning to extract features from text-image data and classify cases into “Valid Breach,” “Invalid Breach,” and “Ambiguous” categories. The model achieved an overall classification accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2. 8 \%}$</tex>, outperforming standalone EfficientNet (89.5 %) and SVM (85.3 %). This demonstrates the effectiveness of the hybrid approach in capturing the complexities of legal contract analysis. The study provides a scalable and interpretable solution for legal practitioners and policymakers, aiding in dispute resolution and regulatory compliance. Future enhancements include expanding the dataset to cover additional regions and integrating the model into user-friendly tools for real-time legal analysis.

title: Eff-GRot: Efficient and Generalizable Rotation Estimation with Transformers
authors: Fanis Mathioulakis, Gorjan Radevski, Tinne Tuytelaars
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We introduce Eff-GRot, an approach for efficient and generalizable rotation estimation from RGB images. Given a query image and a set of reference images with known orientations, our method directly predicts the object’s rotation in a single forward pass, without requiring object- or category-specific training. At the core of our framework is a transformer that performs a comparison in the latent space, jointly processing rotation-aware representations from multiple references alongside a query. This design enables a favorable balance between accuracy and computational efficiency while remaining simple, scalable, and fully end-to-end. Experimental results show that Eff-GRot offers a promising direction toward more efficient rotation estimation, particularly in latency-sensitive applications. Code is available at: https://github.com/fmathiou/eff-grot.

title: GPU Accelerated Near-Field Electromagnetic Simulations for Microwave Hyperthermia Treatment Planning Using GprMax
authors: Rahul Choudhary, Kavitha Arunachalam
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Hyperthermia (HT) treatment elevates tumor temperature to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0}-\mathbf{4 4}{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, enhancing the effectiveness of conventional cancer therapies. Microwave antennas, or applicators, are widely used to achieve localized heating, and they require patient-specific hyperthermia treatment planning (HTP) based on electromagnetic (EM) simulations. Most reported work relies on commercial solvers, which add cost and limit accessibility. In this study, we propose a GPU accelerated opensource HTP framework using gprMax, a finite-difference timedomain (FDTD) solver originally developed for groundpenetrating radar. A cavity-backed patch antenna and a twoelement array operating at 434 MHz were simulated in a muscle equivalent digital phantom. Antenna reflection coefficient and specific absorption rate (SAR) distribution were extracted from the computed electric field distributions and compared with results from commercial solver Ansys HFSS. GPU acceleration resulted in an eight-fold decrease in simulation time relative to CPU-based computation in gprMax. The study demonstrates that gprMax can provide accurate and efficient EM simulations for microwave hyperthermia applicators, offering a cost-effective, and accessible alternative for clinical treatment planning.

title: A Novel Multi-Priority Encoder with Efficient Dynamic Reordering and Broad Input Compatibility
authors: A. Subbulakshmi, K Ebinesh, V Sankaranarayanan, D. Denceli
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: The Priority encoders are fundamental components in high-speed digital design and facilitate effective data processing and generation of control signals. Traditional topologies of priority encoders are hindered by high latency and power consumption due to high switching activity and suboptimal logic structures. Adhering to these limitations, this work presents a new topology of a multi-bit priority encoder with new dynamic approaches to priority selection. The suggested architecture includes a strong input selection framework with smooth compatibility support for different input sizes (8-bit, 16-bit, and 32-bit). A leading-one detector (LOD) system is also employed to enhance priority detection, and a masking decoder is employed to eliminate the highest-priority bit, enabling progressive extraction of priorities until the entire set is processed.
