Implementation and simulation were carried out using Cadence Virtuoso at a 90 nm technology node. The priority encoder proposed has a very low latency of 0.272 ns, with a total power consumption of 0.6 mW. Furthermore, the design shows a Power-Delay Product (PDP) of $\mathbf{2 5 f J}$, representing very high energy efficiency in comparison to traditional methods.

title: Bio-inspired Soft Crawling Robot HISOYAKA II: Adaptive Behavior Emerging from the Dynamics of a Redesigned Soft Arm
authors: Koki Hirota, Kazuyuki Ito
publish: 2026 International Conference on Image Processing and Robotics (ICIPRoB)
abstract: We investigate a mobile robot that locomotes using two long octopus-like soft arms in environments with many protruding objects. In this study, we improve the previous robot by redesigning the arms to enable non-uniform motion. A prototype is developed, and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed arms achieve more complex and adaptive motion than conventional designs, despite employing the same simple controller. The reachable area of the arm is approximately three times larger than that of the conventional design.

title: Expanded Deep Embedding Clustering With Adversarial Learning and Adaptive Graph Constraint
authors: Xiaoyu Zheng, Shiyuan Han, Jin Zhou, C. L. Philip Chen, Yingxu Wang, Yuehui Chen, Lin Wang, Tao Du, Cheng Yang, Bowen Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: The autoencoder (AE) is an efficient feature extraction tool that learns latent representations from raw data by minimizing the reconstruction loss. Building upon the AE architecture, deep clustering models are designed to jointly optimize the deep neural network and perform unsupervised clustering. However, existing methods directly impose the clustering objective on the latent features produced by the AE network, thereby neglecting the potential conflict between data clustering and data representation. Specifically, data clustering aims to enhance data aggregation, whereas data representation focuses on ensuring that latent features faithfully reflect the manifold structure of the raw data. To address this issue, this article proposes an innovative expanded deep embedding clustering (E-DEC) model, in which the AE network is employed to seek better latent representations, and a novel residual expansion module (REM) is integrated to construct an expanded feature space that better serves clustering tasks. Furthermore, adversarial learning between the soft cluster assignments and a prior one-hot distribution is adopted in lieu of the conventional Kullback–Leibler (KL) divergence, so as to enhance the discrimination of different clusters and avoid the degeneracy problem. Finally, an entropy regularization technique is incorporated to adaptively refine the affinity graph throughout the clustering process, thereby reducing the sensitivity of clustering performance to the initial affinity graph. Extensive experiments on real-world benchmark datasets demonstrate the superiority of the proposed model over state-of-the-art deep clustering methods.

title: Performance of an IRS-Assisted Underwater Optical Communication System With Energy Harvesting
authors: Himani Mehta, Rohit Kumar, Shankar Prakriya
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Underwater Internet of Things (IoT) sensor nodes face critical energy limitations, as battery replacement or recharging is often impractical. Underwater optical wireless communication (UOWC) offers high-speed data transmission but suffers from severe impairments such as path loss, turbulence, and pointing errors. Intelligent reflecting surfaces (IRS) have recently emerged as a promising solution to enhance signal quality and extend coverage in challenging environments. This paper presents the performance analysis of an UOWC system featuring downlink energy harvesting (EH) at an IoT sensor node and uplink data transmission assisted by an IRS. A novel composite channel model, incorporating both direct and IRS-reflected links, is proposed and characterized for the UOWC environment. Closed-form expressions are derived for key performance metrics including energy outage probability (EOP), outage probability (OP), and ergodic rate (ER). Extensive analysis is conducted for various water types, demonstrating that the proposed IRS-assisted system significantly outperforms conventional setups without IRS, enabling reliable operation of IoT sensor nodes at greater depths.

title: Decoupling Axial Strain and Torsional Twist in Polarimetric Optical Fiber Sensors through Stokes Parameter Analysis
authors: Ana Teixeira, Vinícius Piaia, Paulo Robalinho, Susana Silva, Orlando Frazão
abstract: Polarimetric optical fiber sensors offer a promising approach for simultaneous measurement of multiple physical parameters, such as strain and twist, due to their sensitivity to changes in birefringence. However, their practical application is often restricted by cross-sensitivity between different parameters. In this work, we experimentally investigate the cross-sensitivity of a polarimetric sensor to axial strain and torsional twist. Two independent setups were used to apply controlled strain and twist to a 0.20-meter fiber section, while monitoring the output state of polarization via Stokes parameters. The response of the Stokes parameters S2 and S3, as well as the orientation angle Ψ and ellipticity angle α, were analyzed to construct sensitivity matrices correlating optical changes to mechanical inputs. A cross-sensitivity analysis was performed using the inverse of these matrices, enabling the discrimination of strain and twist contributions in combined loading scenarios. The results demonstrate a clear linear response and provide a quantitative basis for decoupling strain and twist, advancing the development of robust multi-parameter sensing systems. Therefore, this work shows the feasibility of decoupling strain and twist effects using polarimetric interrogation, supporting the potential of such sensors for multi-parameter sensing in practical environments.

title: A detailed analysis and design of a 1kV piezo-driver with precise fast-time response for hydraulic system
authors: Nassim Rayane Bennacer, Christian Martin, Loris Pace, Pascal Bevilacqua
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents the study of a 1kV DC/AC unipolar converter designed to drive piezoelectric actuators with a precise time response of 1ms, used in a hydraulic system. The selected topology includes a PWM current control to adjust the time response and enables energy recovery from the actuator. This paper presents a detailed analysis, design, and control strategy of the proposed topology. Simulation and experimental results are also included in this work to evaluate time response accuracy, alongside efficiency measurements and loss estimation of the DC/AC converter. The experimental results demonstrate a time response adjustment accuracy of 96.52%(corresponding to 1.036ms), with loss estimation accuracy of 99.04%.

title: Adversarial Machine Learning: A 20-Year Survey of Attacks, Defenses, and Standards
authors: Benhur Tekeste, Khalil Al-Hussaeni, Benjamin C. M. Fung, Ibtesam Alawadhi, Claude Fachkha
abstract: Adversarial Machine Learning (AML) presents a significant barrier to the large-scale deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in safety-critical environments. While early research focused on algorithmic robustness, the field has evolved into a complex intersection of security, assurance, and policy. This paper presents a comprehensive, multidisciplinary survey of the AML landscape, covering over 250 peer-reviewed contributions. We implement a lifecycle-oriented taxonomy that maps attack vectors and defense mechanisms to specific stages of the AI pipeline from data collection to deployment, expanding the traditional Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability (CIA) triad to include Governance and Regulation.We identify critical research gaps, including certified robustness for Natural Language Processing (NLP), and emerging threats in Generative AI. To ground these theoretical insights in practice, we analyze five domain-specific case studies: Autonomous Vehicles, Medical AI, Financial Systems, Natural Language Processing (NLP), and the Internet of Things (IoT). Uniquely, this survey bridges the gap between academic literature and industrial practice by mapping technical AML findings to emerging standards, including the NIST AI Risk Management Framework (RMF), MITRE ATLAS, and ISO/IEC 42001. We conclude by providing a roadmap for researchers, practitioners, and regulators to build verifiable, trustworthy, and compliant AI systems.

title: Automated Mode Locking in an All-Normal-Dispersion Fiber Laser Using Polarization Feedback.
authors: Ebin Joseph, Simon Tremblay, Hanan Anis
abstract: We experimentally demonstrate real-time automation of nonlinear polarization evolution (NPE)-based mode locking (ML) in an all-normal-dispersion (ANDi) fiber laser cavity using polarization feedback alone. The system not only achieves stable mode locking but also distinguishes between fundamental (FML), harmonic (HML), and Q-switching (QS) regimes. The automation setup combines an electronic polarization controller (EPC) with a Raspberry Pi 4 (RPi). Output polarization measurements are used to calculate the first Stokes parameter (S1); its response to small bias perturbations provides both fitness and a regime classifier. FML is identified by a dominant polarization switch followed by a quiet plateau, HML by a polarization switch preceded by strong pre-switch variation, while QS is flagged by a low S1 baseline with isolated spikes. This technique is reliable, easy to implement, and low in cost, making it well suited for moving NPE-based mode-locking from research laboratories toward practical and commercial applications. In practice, the controller converges within a few closed-loop refinement stages: FML is typically achieved within two to three genetic algorithm (GA) iterations, HML within a few additional refinements, while QS requires longer stabilization using adaptive dithering.

title: Digital Twin-assisted Handover Scheme for Mobile Networks using Generative AI
authors: Lal Verda Cakir, Mehmet Ali Erturk, Mehmet Ozdem, Berk Canberk
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Handover management in mobile networks is challenged by high latency and reduced reliability in dense deployments and under user mobility. Here, existing schemes improve handover initiation by optimising the candidate handover at the decision time. However, these are applied after a non-negligible delay due to the control-plane signalling. Then, when applied, it may become invalid or degrade performance. To address this, we propose a Digital Twin (DT)-assisted handover scheme that performs predictive execution-time validation prior to the preparation of the Next Generation (NG)-based handover. To this end, the DT-What-If Generator (DT-WIG) is used to emulate short-horizon future network states under uncertainty. Here, the DT-WIG is a spatiotemporal graph generative model that uses variational latent sampling to generate counterfactual post-handover trajectories for the candidate handover decision. Then, the AMF estimates the failure and QoS risks associated with the candidate handover and approves/rejects it via standard-compliant signalling. With this, we form a policy-agnostic mechanism that runs on the underlying handover policy. Consequently, we evaluate performance using ns-3/5G-LENA trace generation and replay-based policy analysis, with OpenAirInterface-based signalling evaluation. The results show that the proposed method reduces the handover failure rate and handover interruption time while improving latency, jitter, throughput, and packet loss.

title: A Novel Filtering Method for Industrial Target Tracking Using Millimeter-Wave Radar
authors: Chenwei Fan, Rui Zheng, Shuhong Liang, Min Wu, Xilin Rui, Zibao Lu, Fangdong Li, Xiaoyao Zheng
abstract: In this paper, a novel target tracking and filtering method is proposed to address the challenges of millimeter-wave radar perception in industrial environments, including multipath interference, high noise levels, and stringent real-time requirements. A velocity-constrained multidimensional Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN) clustering algorithm is developed to improve clustering accuracy and stability by exploiting the triangle inequality to accelerate processing and reduce false alarms. For target tracking, a constant-velocity (CV) motion model is adopted, and radial velocity information is incorporated to derive a nonlinear measurement equation for constructing an extended Kalman filter (EKF) framework. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method improves clustering accuracy by 19%, achieves 94% clustering stability, and increases clustering speed by approximately eight times. Additionally, compared with the DBSCAN+KF-based tracking method and the GTRACK method, the proposed method achieves a higher tracking success rate and more accurate robot tracking.

title: Hyde: Inferring Cardiovascular Hemodynamics Using BCG for Cross-user Hypertension Detection
authors: Yandao Huang, Cong Li, Chenggao Li, Lin Chen, Junyao Peng, Weibin Cheng, Qian Zhang, Kaishun Wu
abstract: Hypertension, a leading contributor to global mortality, affects 1.28 billion adults worldwide, and its early detection is essential to prevent adverse outcomes. However, relying solely on blood pressure for hypertension detection proves insufficiently accurate. Recognizing this limitation, we have identified a range of multi-dimensional hemodynamics as novel biomarkers that can offer more comprehensive cardiovascular insights for detecting hypertension. Specifically, we propose Hyde, a cross-user hypertension detection system that leverages ballistocardiogram (BCG) signals to infer multi-dimensional hemodynamics and naturally detect hypertension without disrupting users' daily routines. Hyde features a multi-task, multi-branch, unsupervised domain adaptation learning framework, enabling simultaneous prediction of five hemodynamic biomarkers in the auxiliary regression task. This design addresses several challenges, including unlabeled target domain, single-source bias, and complete negative transfer. We implement a flexible optic fiber sensor mat to collect high-quality BCG signals and deploy it in a four-month clinical trial involving 85 subjects with different conditions. The results show that Hyde can accurately identify hypertension with an average accuracy of 97.65%, outperforming state-of-the-art approaches by 21.65% $\sim$ 38.16%. To further validate Hyde's reliability, we test the system with another 33 participants under natural, unconstrained sleep conditions, revealing consistent performance. These findings highlight Hyde's potential for long-term, non-contact hypertension assessment, offering valuable clinical implications for personalized healthcare and improving cardiovascular outcomes.

title: Integrating Employer Branding Strategies To Attract Job Applications Through Company Reputation In The Healthcare Industry Among Generation Z
authors: Nova Nur Anisa, Velani Choirunisa, Oktavia Aini Budiningsih, Kartika Aprilia Benhardy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Considering corporate reputation as a mediator variable, this study intends to examine how company branding tactics affect Generation Z's desire to seek for jobs in the healthcare sector. The healthcare industry in Indonesia is currently experiencing rapid growth, driven by increased public awareness of healthcare services and the growing dominance of Generation Z in the productive age group. This research uses a quantitative approach, utilizing a questionnaire distributed to more than 200 respondents aged 21– 28 years. The results show that employer branding has a positive and significant effect on both corporate reputation and the intention to apply for jobs. Moreover, corporate reputation is proven to mediate the relationship between employer branding and job application intention

title: Ensemble Machine Learning Models for Treatment Response Prediction and Adaptive Patient Allocation in Cancer Clinical Trials
authors: Umme Saima Tasnim, Shanjida Hossain, Md Mehedi Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Traditional clinical trial methodologies face limitations in predicting individual patient responses to oncological therapeutics, necessitating advanced predictive approaches for personalized treatment selection. To develop and validate machine learning models for predicting treatment response and implement adaptive randomization algorithms to optimize patient outcomes in clinical trials. This retrospective cohort study analyzed 2,156 patients from phase II-III oncology trials (20182024). Multiple machine learning algorithms including logistic regression, random forest, support vector machines, XGBoost, and ensemble methods were developed using clinical, molecular, and laboratory features. External validation was performed on 856 independent patients. The ensemble model achieved superior discriminative performance (AUC-ROC: 0.862, 95% CI: 0.8370.887) compared to traditional clinical scoring (AUC-ROC: 0.698, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}<0.001$</tex>). External validation maintained robust performance (AUC-ROC: 0.831). Mutation burden and performance status were the most predictive features. The adaptive randomization algorithm allocated 68.4 % of patients to optimal treatments versus 50 % with conventional randomization, improving overall response rates by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12.3 \%(\mathbf{p}<0.001)$</tex>. Machine learning significantly enhances clinical trial efficiency and patient outcome prediction, demonstrating clear clinical utility for treatment optimization and patient stratification in precision oncology.

title: Preface
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This volume contains the papers presented at iWAT2026: 2026 International Workshop on Antenna Technology held on March 25-27, 2026 in Liverpool.

title: A New Localization Algorithm for a Two-Layer Magnetic Sensor Array in Capsule Endoscopy
authors: Omid Yaghoobian, Khan A. Wahid
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate localization of wireless capsule endoscopes (WCE) is essential for gastrointestinal (GI) diagnostics, yet natural peristalsis introduces abrupt capsule motion that challenges conventional optimization-based methods. We propose a magnetic localization framework that integrates a particle filter (PF) with a two-layer sensor array. The PF provides global search capability, reducing sensitivity to poor initial estimates caused by sudden movements, while the two-layer array improves spatial sensitivity and robustness when the capsule shifts away from one layer. Sensor placement and inter-layer separation are further optimized using the Cramér-Rao Lower Bound (CRLB) to minimize uncertainty within the sensing volume. Experimental and simulation results show that the proposed system—optimized for realistic magnet and sensor characteristics-achieves 2 mm position and 2.4° orientation accuracy across a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15 \times 15 \times 25 \text{cm}^{3}$</tex> region. Compared with single-layer arrays and local-optimizer-based approaches, the method demonstrates superior accuracy and robustness under dynamic motion, highlighting its potential for practical WCE tracking.

title: Enhancing TV APPs Authoring With LLM Agents
authors: Paulo Victor Borges, Daniel de S. Moraes, Simone D. J. Barbosa, Sergio Colcher
abstract: Developing interactive hypermedia applications using the Nested Context Language (NCL) presents significant challenges due to its declarative nature and strict syntactic and semantic requirements. This paper introduces a multi-agent tool powered by large language models (LLMs) to assist in the authoring of NCL 3.0 documents. The proposed tool leverages agents specialized in generating, validating, and refining NCL elements, ensuring syntactic correctness and structural alignment. The tool’s usability, workflow efficiency, and perceived user satisfaction were evaluated through a structured observational study. This work contributes to the field of multimedia systems by providing a scalable, efficient, and user-friendly solution for NCL development, paving the way for the broader adoption of declarative languages in digital TV and multimedia applications.

title: An Adaptive Confidence-Weighted Transformer Ensemble Framework for Tomato Leaf Disease Recognition
authors: Md. Fatin Nibbrash Nakib, Md. Shahid Uz Zaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Global economic growth relies on agricultural output, as it ensures food security, raises incomes, and supports employment. However, crop diseases can drastically reduce yields, disrupt supply chains, and strain financial resources, especially in developing countries. Tomato leaf infections are among the most serious threats to the agricultural industry and local farmers. This study proposes a deep learning-based method for the early and precise identification of tomato leaf diseases, covering 10 distinct classes using 18,160 images from the PlantVillage dataset. Two pre-trained transformer-based architectures: Vision Transformer (ViT) and Data-efficient Image Transformer (DeiT) were used as feature extractors, followed by evaluation with multiple classifiers, including logistic regression, XGBoost, and a custom dense layer. Experimental results show that the custom dense layer achieved the highest classification performance among all classifiers. The outputs of the ViT and DeiT models were further combined using a confidence-based weighted ensemble, enabling dynamic prioritization of predictions with higher certainty. The proposed framework achieved 99.95 % classification accuracy with an exceptionally low error rate of 0.0005, outperforming individual models. These results highlight the effectiveness of transformer-based feature extraction combined with ensemble learning for reliable, scalable, and accurate plant disease detection, offering valuable support for sustainable agriculture and global food security.

title: AI-Based Urban Risk Modeling: Leveraging Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Real-Time Structural Stress Simulation of a Structure
authors: Mohammad Arafat Alamgir, Dipra Barua, MST. Khadija Khanom, Md Shahriar Ahmed, MD. Fardin Ahmed, Md. Osman Goni, Mayaz Uddin Gazi
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate and efficient prediction of structural stress in urban high-rise clusters is essential for seismic risk assessment and resilience planning in urban areas. This study proposes a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) framework that embeds elasticity and strength constraints directly into the learning process to ensure physically consistent and interpretable predictions. A curated dataset of 2,500 samples with 11 engineered features was used to benchmark the PINN against conventional machine learning models, including Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, Support Vector Regression, and Multilayer Perceptrons. The results demonstrate that the proposed PINN achieved superior performance (MSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx \mathbf{5. 0 9}, \mathbf{R M S E} \approx \mathbf{2. 2 5}$</tex>, MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 1.81, \mathbf{R}^{2} \approx \mathbf{0. 9 3}$</tex>), outperforming all baselines while maintaining consistency with the established physical laws, with physics-residual violations remaining below 3%. The framework shows potential for near-real-time deployment in digital-twin or rapid assessment workflows, although further validation on larger and real-world datasets is required. Although the study is limited by the dataset size and simplified material assumptions, it establishes a robust physics-grounded baseline and outlines clear pathways for improvement using expanded and real-world datasets, ensemble or hybrid modeling, and region-specific applications. Overall, the findings highlight the novelty and practical utility of physics-informed learning for advancing scalable and reliable urban risk management systems.

title: Enhanced Visual Retrieval and Decision Support
authors: Abdelkrim Saouabe, Said Tkatek, Doha Banoui, Khalid Tizra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The exponential growth of visual data across digital platforms, industrial monitoring, and healthcare applications has intensified the demand for intelligent and interpretable image retrieval systems. Traditional Content-Based Image Retrieval (CBIR) methods, based on handcrafted or convolutional descriptors, have laid the foundation for visual search but remain limited by low semantic interpretability, domain dependency, and restricted adaptability. In contrast, the rise of Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) has introduced a new paradigm that redefines image retrieval as a process of semantic reasoning, synthesis, and decision support rather than simple feature matching. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of recent advances in generative AI–driven retrieval architectures, emphasizing their ability to bridge the semantic gap, enhance multimodal understanding, and provide explainable results. The review demonstrates that generative approaches consistently improve retrieval accuracy, robustness to domain variations, and interpretability of results, outperforming traditional CBIR in multiple application scenarios. Beyond performance evaluation, this work contributes to the conceptual unification of generative retrieval systems and positions them as intelligent decision-support tools for applications in medical imaging, industrial inspection, smart surveillance, and event understanding. The findings highlight a decisive shift from perception-driven to reasoning-driven retrieval systems, underscoring the potential for adaptive, transparent, and explainable visual search frameworks that can support complex decision-making across domains.

title: Secure Searchable Attribute-Based Encryption Scheme for Multimodal Data in Satellite Internet
authors: Ran Hou, Jiayi Liu, Zheyu Zhang, Xiaofei Zhang, Zhen Xu, Yushu Wu, Zhiquan Liu, Yongwang Liu, Tiejun Wu
abstract: With the continuous optimization of data transmission technologies in Satellite Internet, the transmission objects have expanded from initially simple text data to multimodal data, which faces multiple challenges, such as privacy protection and efficient processing. To address these issues, we propose the searchable attribute-based encryption scheme for multimodal data in Satellite Internet (SABE-MD-SI). Firstly, by specifically extracting and quantifying the features of multimodal data, the fusion of cross-modal data is achieved, effectively enabling the fusion and protection of heterogeneous multimodal data in Satellite Internet. Secondly, the scheme adopts searchable encryption technology to allow ground or satellite nodes to search multimodal data in an encrypted state, thereby enhancing the security of data transmission in Satellite Internet. Finally, the scheme incorporates an attribute update function based on attribute-based encryption technology, ensuring that data access permissions can be dynamically adjusted as required while achieving fine-grained access control. The privacy and security analysis indicates that the security of the ciphertext has been rigorously verified.

title: A Multi-Criteria Decision Framework for Selecting Sustainable Local Materials in Post-Crisis Reconstruction
authors: Sufyan Sufyan, Amin Saif, Farag Qasem, Aq Ahdal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Post-crisis reconstruction often defaults to imported materials, undermining long-term sustainability and local economic recovery. Although vernacular materials can reduce embodied emissions and strengthen local livelihoods, decision makers lack a transparent way to weigh competing criteria and justify choices. This paper presents a four-phase quantitative decision framework that integrates the Analytic Hierarchy Process to derive criteria weights from experts and the Technique for Order Preference by Similarity to Ideal Solution to order prospective materials. The framework is demonstrated on a flood-affected housing scenario comparing bamboo and rammed earth. The analysis quantifies decision thresholds and shows how shifts in priorities—such as higher emphasis on initial cost versus durability—change the preferred option. Sensitivity analysis tests robustness and clarifies the drivers of each recommendation. By making trade-offs explicit, the framework operationalizes the "build back better" principle, enabling stakeholders to make defensible, locally appropriate material selections for a resilient and sustainable recovery.

title: Deep Learning and Ensemble Techniques in Fake Image Detection
authors: Md. Nabil Hasan Mumat, Rakibul Hasan Showrov, Nashita Nawar Prapti, Md. Shahriar Hussain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The growth of deep learning has resulted in the creation of deepfake images that are remarkably realistic, posing substantial risks to data integrity, security, and privacy. Detecting such manipulated content is crucial for preventing misuse in social media, journalism, and authentication systems. This study employed advanced feature extraction techniques and neural network architectures to explore machine learning-based deepfake detection. It analyzes spatial inconsistencies, such as artifacts and unnatural blending, through models like EfficientNet-B3, ConvNeXt, Swin Transformer, ViT, and ensemble architectures combining features from ConvNeXt, Swin Transformer, ViT, and EfficientNet-B3 with an NN-Classifier using soft voting. The models were trained, validated, and tested on comprehensive datasets, achieving the highest accuracy with soft voting: 99.43% in training and validation and 99.43% in testing. These findings illustrate the effectiveness of the proposed methods in enhancing detection accuracy and addressing generalization challenges, ultimately leading to reliable systems that combat misinformation and deepfakes.

title: Trustworthy Smishing Detection in Code-Mixed Bangla-English SMS: Dual-View XLM-R With Calibration, Selective Classification, and Open-Set Detection
authors: Ahammad Hossen Eazaz, Md Intishar Ahmed, Golam Md. Muradul Bashir
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rise of mobile phishing via SMS (smishing) has become a pressing cybersecurity concern, particularly in multilingual regions like South Asia where messages frequently appear in Bangla, English, or their noisy code-mixed forms. Traditional models often deliver high accuracy in controlled settings but fail when confronted with real-world challenges such as spelling variations, transliteration, distributional shifts, or out-of-distribution (OOD) attacks. In this work, we present a dualview XLM-R framework that jointly learns from both original and romanized Bangla SMS, enforcing prediction consistency across views to build resilience against orthographic variation. To enhance trustworthiness, we incorporate probability calibration through temperature scaling, evaluate selective classification with risk-coverage analysis, and benchmark multiple OOD detection strategies including Energy, MSP, MC-Dropout, and Mahalanobis distance. Experiments on a newly curated Bangla-English code-mixed dataset show that our model achieves over 99.06 % accuracy, near-perfect calibration (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$E C E \approx 0.005$</tex>), and reliable selective abstention. While Energy and MSP struggle under OOD conditions, Mahalanobis distance reaches AUROC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.996$</tex>, offering strong open-set robustness. These findings highlight not only superior performance over strong baselines but also improved reliability and transparency, positioning our approach as a practical and trustworthy solution for smishing defense in low-resource, multilingual contexts.

title: Wind Power Forecasting Using Deep LSTM and Hybrid Quantum Neural Networks
authors: Insaf Ghouaoua, Mohamed Tarek Khadir
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Wind power has become a crucial source of renewable energy, yet its variability poses significant challenges to maintaining a stable balance between electricity generation and demand. Accurate forecasting is therefore essential to support efficient grid management and enable the integration of renewable resources. This study evaluates two advanced approaches for wind power forecasting: a deep Long Short-Term Memory model and a deep hybrid Quantum Neural Network. Both models were trained and tested on wind power data to assess their predictive performance. Results show that the Long Short-Term Memory model successfully captured temporal patterns and produced accurate forecasts, while the deep hybrid Quantum Neural Network achieved higher overall performance with fewer computational resources. The originality of this work lies in the design and evaluation of a hybrid quantum-classical forecasting model applied to wind power prediction, demonstrating its potential to improve accuracy and efficiency compared to conventional methods. These findings suggest that quantum machine learning could play a strategic role in future energy systems by enhancing reliability and reducing the cost of renewable energy integration.

title: MOOD-TUNES: A Music & Movie Recommendation System Based on People's Facial Expressions and Hand Gestures
authors: Md. Jisan Ahmed, Sajadul Islam Akash, Isha Das, Md. Shahriar Hussain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: In this digital era, music or movies are essential part of our day to day life. They are very powerful in expressing emotion, distressing, and communication with society and culture. However, people have problems finding appropriate music and movies that go with their moods. That's why we have prepared a project for you that, while innovative, makes it easy by creating a recommendation system that provides perfect content based on your mood via facial expressions and hand gestures. Our Project reads expressions and gestures in realtime, and we have developed it with deep learning approaches integrating two different models. One is the traditional CNN model, and another is the CNN model with the MediaPipe pipeline. The training accuracy of Model-1(CNN) is 86%, and the validation accuracy is 84%. The training accuracy of Model2 (CNN with MediaPipe) is 98%. We have integrated these two models into a website named “Mood-Tunes.” We also trained an attentional neural networks model but implemented the first two models mentioned above. Our models will help users find their preferred movie or music according to their mood.

title: Electrical Consumption Data Aggregation Impact on REC Analysis: A Real Case Study Including Industrial and Commercial Users
authors: Valentina Consolo, Roberto Rugani, Elena Mura, Marco Raugi
abstract: Renewable energy communities (RECs) are emerging as key contributors to grid decarbonization and energy poverty alleviation. However, the accuracy of REC performance assessments strongly depends on the resolution of input consumption data. Since shared energy is defined hour by hour, an accurate analysis of a REC should be based on an hourly simulation of the community’s energy flows. While detailed hourly data are ideal, they are often unavailable in practical applications. This study addresses the gap by evaluating how different levels of consumption data aggregation affect both technical and financial outcomes in REC feasibility studies. A real-world REC case study in central Italy, including commercial and industrial users, was simulated using a MATLAB-based model across four scenarios: hourly measured data and three synthetic profiles based on monthly time bands, monthly totals, and annual consumption. The results show that higher aggregation leads to significant losses in hourly accuracy, particularly in the presence of large prosumers with seasonal load variations. Still, monthly aggregated models—especially those disaggregated by time bands—yield robust approximations of long-term financial metrics. As expected, the estimation of hourly shared energy from aggregated data introduces information loss and unrealistic dynamics. Nonetheless, monthly banded profiles provide a reliable compromise. Future work should improve disaggregation methods—e.g., through typical load curves by building use—and extend validation across diverse REC configurations to refine control strategies and planning.

title: Automated Carotid Intima-Media Complex Segmentation Using Multi-Stage Deep Learning with Attention-Guided Feature Fusion
authors: Abdus Salam, Oishi Jyoti, Md. Nahiduzzaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease remains the leading cause of mortality worldwide, with carotid intima-media thickness (CIMT) serving as a critical biomarker for early atherosclerotic detection. Traditional manual measurement approaches suffer from significant inter-observer variability and time-intensive analysis protocols. This study presents a novel automated segmentation framework combining YOLOv9-based region of interest detection with a hybrid ResNet-50/SelfONN-UNet++ architecture enhanced by squeeze-and-excitation attention mechanisms. Evaluation on the CUBS dataset comprising 2,176 carotid ultrasound images demonstrates robust performance metrics: Dice coefficient of 85.32%, intersection over union of 74.41%, and sensitivity of 86.87%. The proposed methodology reduces false negative rates compared to conventional approaches while maintaining clinical accuracy standards. These findings establish a foundation for standardized automated CIMT assessment in cardiovascular risk stratification protocols.

title: AI-Driven Multimodal Framework for Environmental Monitoring and Conservation
authors: Jayabhaduri Radhakrishnan
abstract: Climate change-induced environmental degradation, pollution, deforestation, and ocean litter require smart, scalable, and real-time monitoring systems. Conventional methods based on manual data gathering or fixed sensor networks are usually characterized by spatially limited coverage, latency, and inefficiency in high-resolution environmental analysis. This article suggests a modular and scalable framework taking advantage of Artificial Intelligence (AI), edge computing, and multi-modal data streams-satellite imagery, unmanned aerial systems (UAS)/unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), and Internet of Things (IoT) sensors together for combined environmental monitoring and conservation. The framework has six major layers: data acquisition, ingestion and storage, preprocessing and feature engineering, AI/ML model processing, deployment, and visualization with real-time alerts. It enables the classification of water bodies and vegetation with CNNs, marine debris identification using YOLOv5, forecasting of air quality using LSTM models in time-series, and classification of pollution levels with Random Forests. Edge deployment on NVIDIA Jetson Nano allows for real-time inference with a latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$65 ~\text{ms} /$</tex> frame and power usage of 6.5 W, guaranteeing cost-effective field deployment. From the cloud perspective, Google Vertex AI allows for scalable model retraining and inference in sub-second latency. Visualization dashboards created with Grafana and Google Data Studio provide minute-by-minute reports, whereas the alert system provides 95.2 % accuracy with response times less than three seconds. The suggested system showcases real-world relevance through several case studies and proves its ability to enable responsive, energy-conscious, and scalable environmental intelligence. The current work provides a foundation for future AI-driven conservation projects and the implementation of sustainable environmental policies.

title: Transformer-Based Multimodal Fusion Technique for Bengali Aggressive Meme Classification
authors: Anirban Barua, Binoy Das Gupta, Hasan Murad
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Memes have become a familiar feature on social media, but their quick rise has led to concerns about how easily they can be used to spread harmful content and hate speech. It becomes particularly challenging to handle such content in lowresource languages like Bengali due to script complexity, and existing approaches mostly use conventional multimodal fusion techniques. In this work, we present an efficient multimodal meme classification framework that identifies aggressive memes and categorizes them into five classes based on their targeted hate group. Our method combines textual embeddings from MuRIL with visual embeddings from CLIP to capture crossmodal relationships. Moreover, we utilize a compact transformerbased fusion strategy for aligning image and text features, achieving notable improvements over common fusion methods across multiple encoder combinations. We also explore the use of state-of-the-art multimodal large language models for this task. We achieve a weighted F1 score of 0.772 using our proposed method, indicating its effectiveness for aggressive meme classification in the Bengali language.

title: Robust Variational Identification of LPV Systems With Asymmetric Measurement Noise
authors: Xiang Chen, Ke Li, Peng Su, Fei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This paper considers the problem of parameter identification for linear parameter-varying (LPV) systems with asymmetric noise. Commonly employed noise distributions exhibit symmetric statistical properties that make it difficult to describe asymmetric measurement noise. This leads to a degradation in the performance of the identification algorithm. To cope with the asymmetric noise, the measurement noise is characterized by the generalized hyperbolic skew Student’s t (GHS) distribution. Then, the robust variational algorithm for the LPV system is proposed within the Bayesian framework. The variational Bayesian (VB) algorithm estimates the parameters of the model and the asymmetric noise from the measured data. The proposed algorithm is validated through a numerical example and a mass–spring–damper system. The results and findings show that the proposed algorithm can effectively address the problem caused by asymmetric measurement noise. It can accurately identify the model parameters and suppress the influence of the asymmetric noise.

title: EfficientNet with Channel-Spatial Attention for Accurate Brain Tumor Detection
authors: Nagifa Nujhat Easha, Mohiuddin Ahmad
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate and reliable classification of brain tumors from MRI scans is essential for timely clinical intervention, yet the heterogeneous appearance of tumors and the presence of visually similar anatomical structures make this task highly challenging. To address these limitations, we propose a lightweight attention-guided framework that integrates EfficientNet-B0 with a Convolutional Block Attention Module to enhance both channel-wise and spatial feature discrimination. The incorporation of attention refinement enables the model to focus on tumor-relevant regions while reducing background interference, improving robustness across diverse MRI patterns. Evaluated on a benchmark brain tumor dataset, the proposed approach achieves superior performance compared to standard CNN baselines, attaining a test accuracy of 97.1 % and a macro-averaged AUC-ROC of 0.987. These results highlight the effectiveness of combining principled model scaling with attention mechanisms for advancing automated brain tumor analysis and demonstrate the potential of the proposed framework as a reliable and scalable clinical decision-support tool.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of Grid-Connected Microgrid with PV and Battery Storage
authors: Tonmoy Roy Akash, Md. Sabbir Alam, Md. Naiemur Rahman, Bivas Sarkar, Ahmed Al Mansur
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This paper presents an analysis and simulation of a grid-connected PV microgrid system considering real constraints like environmental conditions and consumer load consumption. As demand for renewable energy systems continues to grow, grid stability and reliability are significant challenges. The grid-connected PV microgrid system and storage are considered as a continuous power supply. The developed microgrid system has sufficient capacity to meet the load requirements without interruption. The model also follows the defector industry-standard real and reactive power injection to the grid. The paper presents a detailed design of the system's components, including passive components, the controller, PV panel sizing, and battery storage. Several case studies were presented to demonstrate the system's control functionalities in various scenarios. Simulation results show that a 50 kW PV array with a 268 kWh battery bank can reliably supply the community load, maintaining grid frequency within 59.8-60.2 Hz and stable three-phase voltages. The proposed model provides a scalable and sustainable solution for communityscale renewable integration, highlighting both its technical feasibility and its practical deployment potential.

title: Design and Analysis of a Novel Permanent-Magnet Vernier Machine as an Electromagnetic Damper
authors: Congqi Lu, Fengbin Cai, Qifang Lin, Yurong Huang, Yuhao Wang, Xinhua Guo, Shuangxia Niu
abstract: This article addresses the limitations of conventional electromagnetic dampers, such as low torque density and nonlinear damping characteristics, by proposing a novel dual-excitation permanent magnet Vernier machine (DEPMVM) integrated with a resonant circuit. The rotor of the DEPMVM combines spoke-type magnets and a central magnetic ring to enhance torque density (38.27 kNm/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>) and power factor (0.88) under motor operating mode. The resonant circuit compensates for the synchronous reactance of armature winding in the generator mode, improving the linearity of the damping force versus rotational speed. The flux barrier effect is further mitigated through split-teeth stator design. In addition, with the parallel flux paths, the sum of the dominant harmonic torques in the DEPMVM is increased by 26.99% compared with the conventional model. Experimental results indicate that under the optimal resonant parameters (R = 2 Ω, C = 0.9 mF), the fitting degree (FD) of the T-n curve reaches 0.953. Prototype testing reveals a back-EMF deviation of 3.9%.

title: El Niño/La Niña-driven spatiotemporal heterogeneity of the Eastern Tropical South Pacific Oxygen Minimum Zone with satellite-based long-term records
authors: Zhang Yifan, Bai Yan, He Xianqiang, Li Teng, Gong Fang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The Eastern Tropical South Pacific (ETSP) contains one of the largest oxygen minimum zones (OMZs) globally and is strongly influenced by El Niño-La Niña variability. Yet, long-term OMZ depth variability and its controlling processes remain poorly understood due to limited continuous observations. We develop a machine learning-based inversion model to estimate the depth of hypoxia (DOH, the shallowest OMZ depth) using satellite products and biogeochemical Argo oxygen profiles from 2008 to 2019. The model combines physical and biogeochemical predictors, including satellite-derived net community production, to represent the nonlinear regulation of subsurface hypoxia. Validation against independent observations (n = 809) demonstrates robust performance, with a coefficient of determination of 0.86 and a root mean square error of 22.7 m. Using this framework, we reconstruct a 17-year (2003-2019) monthly DOH dataset for the ETSP, enabling a high-frequency satellite-based assessment of OMZ vertical variability across recurrent El Niño-La Niña events. The reconstructed record reveals pronounced contrasts between offshore and nearshore systems. During 2003-2012, thermocline-driven offshore DOH shoaling contrasted with nonlinear nearshore fluctuations modulated by biological processes. The extreme 2012-2016 El Niño interrupted OMZ expansion, triggering pan-ETSP DOH deepening through divergent mechanisms—intensified stratification offshore versus reduced biological oxygen consumption nearshore. Following 2017, rapid offshore shoaling contrasted with delayed nearshore recovery. For the first time, we reveal the high-frequency temporal evolution of OMZ depth (DOH) in response to recurrent El Niño/La Niña events, and elucidate the El Niño/La Niña-driven heterogeneous hypoxia responses and associated environmental controls between nearshore and offshore upwelling systems.

title: Detecting Fresh & Rotten Fruits: A Comprehensive Study Using Deep & Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Fahmida Tahmin, Md. Musfiqur Rahman Mridha, Sakib Imtiaz, Fatin Nibbrash Nakib, Md. Shahid Ahammed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The global production of fresh fruits and the spoiled percentage pose significant challenges to public health, the economy, the environment, and food security. Accurate and efficient recognition of fruit quality, especially at the margin between fresh and rotten, is therefore essential. This study explores the application of advanced Deep Learning (DL) and Machine Learning (ML) techniques for the automated classification of fresh and rotten fruits. Several pretrained DL models as feature extractors, including members of the DenseNet family, Xception, and ResNet50V2, are evaluated alongside ML classifiers such as Support Vector Machine (SVM), XGBoost, Random Forest (RF), and a custom DL-based classifier. Experiments are conducted on a publicly available Kaggle dataset comprising two classes: fresh and rotten. The primary contribution of this work is the demonstration of superior performance by the DenseNet169SVM model in binary fruit classification, which achieves the highest accuracy of 99.76 % with the lowest error rate. The findings highlight the potential of DL-ML models for reliable fruit quality assessment, contributing to enhanced food safety, waste reduction, and supply chain optimization. The proposed approach can be extended to other perishable food categories for broader food quality monitoring. Future work may involve real-time deployment and integration with smart agricultural or retail systems.

title: Transformer-Assisted Recommendation System with Apriori-Enhanced Behavior Mining in Decision Models for Engineering
authors: Mariyam Ahmed, Jainish Roy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Recommender systems still have a long way to go before they can accurately and consistently predict users’ future preferences. To address these challenges, this study proposes a Transformer-Assisted Recommendation System based on Association and Enhanced Behavior Modeling (TARSAEBM). The core objective of this approach is to integrate deep learning transformers with association rule mining to better capture and interpret complex user behavior patterns and dynamic preferences. By leveraging the sequential modeling power of transformers and the relational insights of association rules, TARS-AEBM enhances the understanding of contextual dependencies and item correlations. Experimental results demonstrate that TARS-AEBM significantly outperforms traditional recommendation methods in terms of F1-score, recall, and accuracy, proving its effectiveness in providing more personalized, adaptive, and precise recommendations across diverse user environments in decision model.

title: A 3-D Magneto-Thermal Coupling Model for Ferrite Toroidal Cores Based on Vector Magnetic-Circuit Theory
authors: Haodong Wang, Zheng Wang, Wei Qin, Ming Cheng, Xiang Ma, Chengbo Li
abstract: Accurate prediction of magnetic loss and temperature rise is critical for design of ferrite toroidal cores. Compared to three-dimensional finite-element analysis (FEA), equivalent magnetic circuit (EMC) based modeling methods map regular-shaped geometries into lumped magnetic circuit elements, thus achieving higher computation efficiency while maintaining good accuracy for field analysis. However, conventional magnetic-circuit models with only reluctance is unavailable for magneto-thermal coupling analysis. Based on the vector magnetic-circuit theory, this paper proposes a three-dimensional (3-D) magneto-thermal coupling model for ferrite toroidal cores. By constructing a discrete magnetic-circuit mesh containing both reluctance and equivalent magnetic reactance, the proposed modeling method achieves fast solutions for power loss and the 3-D distribution of magnetic flux density simultaneously. Furthermore, the magnetic-thermal coupling analysis is achieved by exchanging data with a similar 3-D thermal network. Both simulation and experiments have verified that the proposed modeling method enables accurate prediction of magnetic and thermal fields, but also significantly reduces computation time and memory requirements compared with FEA.

title: Assessment of YOLO Models for Real-Time Vehicle Detection in the Complex Urban Settings of Bangladesh
authors: Ha Meem Hossain, Pritam Nath, Md Imtiaz Uddin, Ishrat Jahan Eiste, Mahitun Nesa Mahi, Syed Nasibur Rahman Ratul, Md Naim Uddin Mozumdar, Asif Mohammed Saad
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study assesses the efficacy of YOLOv8 and YOLOv11 architectures, including medium (m) and extra-large (x) variants, for real-time vehicle detection in Bangladesh's complex urban environments. A custom dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1920 \times 1080$</tex> images was compiled, annotated with 23 classes encompassing local vehicles such as Desi Nosimon, Leguna, and Battery Rickshaw. Data were collected using mobile cameras, annotated with LabelImg, and divided into 75 % training, 5 % validation, and 20 % testing sets. Models were trained at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$640 \times 640$</tex> resolution and evaluated using precision, recall, F1 score, mean average precision (mAP@0.5), and inference time. Results indicate that YOLOv8x achieves the highest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> of 0.733 and F1 score of 0.681, with an inference time of 40.255 ms, while YOLOv8m balances accuracy (mAP@0.5: 0.709) with a faster 12.1 ms. YOLOv11m variants exhibit competitive performance but higher latency (e.g., 16.6 ms for YOLOv11m). Medium variants are recommended for real-time applications, enhancing road safety through autonomous driving and traffic enforcement. Qualitative analysis reveals challenges with rare classes, necessitating dataset refinement. Future research will explore additional models and optimize datasets to improve detection accuracy in diverse Bangladeshi road conditions.

title: Improving phase difference estimation by optimizing the condition number
authors: Yuwei Qiao, Datong Liu, Liansheng Liu, Yu Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Phase difference estimation is a core component of measurement and calibration in multi-channel systems. For two channels with the same nominal frequency, a joint sinusoidal model with seven parameters is employed to estimate the phase difference, thereby mitigating estimation bias from minor frequency mismatches. The parameters are obtained by solving the least-squares (LS) problem via singular value decomposition (SVD). Analysis shows that when the system matrix has a high condition number (CN), matrix computations are prone to illconditioning, leading to numerical instability in the iterative process. To alleviate this problem, this paper derives a closed-form scaling factor that minimizes the system matrix CN while preserving the LS solution, thereby ensuring numerical stability. Based on different amplitude relationships between channels, the CN under coherent sampling can be optimized to the range of (14, 110). Simulation results show that the ill-conditioned risk of the original SVD-LS algorithm increases significantly as the CN enters the medium-to-high range. After introducing the scaling factor derived in this paper, the CN of the system matrix decreases significantly, the iterative stability and convergence rate are systematically improved, and the LS optimal solution remains unchanged.

title: Decision Analytics in Climate Diplomacy: Pathways Toward Global Sustainability
authors: Muhammed Nawar Hussein, Abdulrhman Huseein Ali, Fouad Jameel Khalaf, Humam Mahdi Salih, Zeyad Khalaf Ismail, Marwan Mohammed Abbood
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This electronic Climate change is one of the most pressing global challenges, with profound economic, political, and environmental consequences. This study aims to analyze the role of climate diplomacy as a mechanism for enhancing international cooperation and promoting sustainability. The research hypothesis assumes that climate diplomacy is a key tool for reducing emissions, integrating climate policies into development plans, and fostering technological innovation. It relies on negotiations, agreements, and multilateral dialogues to balance national interests. The study concludes that its effectiveness remains constrained by tensions and limited financing, recommending stronger commitments, expanded climate finance, and deeper regional cooperation to ensure global sustainability.

title: Benchmarking GAN-Based Synthetic Data Augmentation for Task-Driven Handwritten Digit Recognition
authors: Jannathul Maowa Hasi
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study evaluates the practical utility of synthetic handwritten digits generated by four Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) architectures: DCGAN, CGAN, Conv-CGAN, and WGAN-GP. The models are assessed using both established image-quality metrics—Fréchet Inception Distance (FID), Inception Score (IS), and Structural Similarity Index (SSIM). Moreover, a task-oriented evaluation is employed, in which a lightweight Optical Character Recognition network (OCRNet) is trained on generated synthetic data. Conv-CGAN consistently produces the most realistic and semantically coherent samples across all metrics. When these samples are combined with the MNIST training set(real data), OCRNet's accuracy improves from an intentionally constrained baseline of 89.5 % (trained only on real data) to 94.1 % (trained on real and synthetic data together). This demonstrates that the generated data provides meaningful new information beyond the original dataset. Our findings show that conditional generative models with enhanced capacity can deliver both visually high-quality images and functionally useful training signals for downstream recognition. This work highlights the importance of evaluating generative models by their real-world impact and points to the broader potential of synthetic data in resource-limited machine learning settings.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Methods with Enhanced Feature Extraction to Detect Deepfake Images
authors: Fahim Foysal Abak, M. Morsedur Rahman, S. M. Mahedy Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rapid advancement of generative models has accelerated the spread of deepfake technology, enabling the creation of highly realistic synthetic images that threaten digital content security, privacy, and trust. While deep learning (DL) approaches have shown promise in deepfake detection, conventional methods often fall short in robustness and accuracy. This study presents a comparative analysis of eight convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures, such as EfficientNetB4 (EB4), EfficientNetB7 (EB7), DenseNet121 (DN121), DenseNet169 (DN169), Xception (XP), InceptionV3 (IV3), MesoNet (MN), and NasNet (NN) for deepfake image detection. A large-scale Kaggle dataset derived from the OpenForensics benchmark is utilized for training and evaluation. To enhance discriminative feature learning, preprocessing techniques such as Local Binary Patterns (LBP) and Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) are applied, alongside a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) for adaptive attention. Experimental results demonstrate that EfficientNetB7 achieves the highest performance and outperforms other CNN architectures, with 93.99 % accuracy and an AUC of 0.9812. These findings highlight that integrating advanced preprocessing and attention mechanisms with CNN architectures significantly improves the reliability of deepfake detection.

title: Enhancing Bangla Sentiment Analysis Through Data Balancing and Preprocessing: A Comparative Insight from Classical and Deep Learning Approaches
authors: Md. Anowar Hossen Labu, Most. Tazfia Sultana, Md. Mahmudul Hassan, Md. Afridi Chowdhury, Adiba Mahjabin Nitu, Md. Arshad Ali
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Sentiment analysis in Bangla remains a challenging task due to the scarcity of labeled data, class imbalance, and the noisy nature of user-generated social media text. Unlike the languages with extensive resources such as English, Bangla lacks large-scale, high-quality datasets that can support robust natural language processing models. In this study, we focus on the SentNoB dataset, which contains 12,575 comments across 13 domains, annotated into positive, negative, and neutral classes. A key limitation of this dataset is the scarcity of neutral samples, which significantly affects model performance and generalization. Initial experiments confirmed these challenges, with Support Vector Machine (SVM) achieving 66 % accuracy and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) reaching only 57.86 % of accuracy. These results highlight the adverse effects of both class imbalance and noisy textual features. To address these issues, the authors enriched the dataset with 2,000 additional neutral and 200 negative samples collected from social media, followed by extensive preprocessing that included text cleaning, normalization, stopword removal, and noise reduction. Retraining with this improved dataset yielded substantial gains, with SVM achieving 76.94% and BiLSTM reaching 78.37 % accuracy. The authors findings demonstrate that dataset balancing and robust preprocessing are critical for Bangla sentiment analysis and emphasize the complementary strengths of classical machine learning and deep learning in low-resource contexts.

title: Attention-Guided Invariant Feature Learning for Clothes-Changing Person Re-Identification
authors: Raisa Begum, Kaushik Deb
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Person re-identification is crucial in advanced surveillance systems, striving to identify and track a query person across cross-camera views, times, and locations within a gallery of images. The presence of occlusion, multiple camera views, background interference, etc., induce further complexities in the approach. Nonetheless, notable progress has been procured on conventional person re-id tasks inadvertently presuming that query's clothes are static during the entire retrieval procedure within a short time period. However, we cannot overlook addressing the cloth-changing aspect in case of re-id over a long period of time. Cloth-invariant feature learning is the key to withdrawing its bias while handling clothing changing Re-ID (CC Re-ID). To improve the reliability of surveillance systems, in this paper, we propose an innovative framework called Non-Cloth Relevant Feature Mining (NCR-FM) to decouple clothing information and ID-inherent details in order to secure robust re-id of individuals regardless of alterations in their clothing. Separate feature mining modules are employed to squeeze the precise identity and clothing information. The mined pieces of information are further polished and disjointed by aiding feature blending and level-based pyramid approaches followed by specialized classifiers. The end-to-end collective training with the weighted sum of the multi-functional criterion functions gradually strips the clothing bias from potential biometric clues. Extensive experiments on publicly available benchmark cloth-changing dataset, PRCC further validates the disentanglement potency of the proposed framework over the recent approaches, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 2. 9 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 3. 1 \%}$</tex> of Top-1 and mAP CC Re-ID scores respectively.

title: Delay-Compensated Double-Loop IDFD Controller for EV-Assisted Renewable-Integrated Thermal Power Systems Amid Cyber Threats
authors: Deepak Kumar, Mohamed Alkhatib, G. Lloyds Raja, Omar Al Zaabi, Khalifa Al Hosani, Utkal Ranjan Muduli
abstract: Modern automatic generation control (AGC) systems suffer from instability as well as delayed corrective actions in response to frequency and tie line power aberrations due to communication time delays (TD) and cyber threats. Additionally, the sporadic nature of the renewable energy sources (RES), electric vehicles (EVs) and energy storage systems further introduces complexities in AGC. Therefore, this work proposes a double-loop-based integral dual fractional derivative (IDFD) controller to enhance robustness and stability in EV-assisted dual-area thermal power systems (TPS) under such adverse conditions. The IDFD controller is developed via the internal model control theory grounded on the fractional-order model of the TPS. The performance-robustness tradeoff is improved through the judicious selection of the single tuning parameter (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha$</tex-math></inline-formula>) based on gain and phase margin specifications with birds-of-prey-based optimisation by minimising integral time absolute error of area control error. The proposed controller is validated using both simulation and hardware-in-the-loop implementation to show its practical feasibility. Case studies are performed on dual-area TPS and a realistic IEEE 39- bus test system, demonstrating superior dynamic response and enhanced resilience against cyber threats, TD, parametric uncertainties, multiple-step load disturbance, and non-linearities.

title: NextSeed: An IoT Based Precision Agriculture System for Soil Analysis and Crop Recommendation
authors: Md. Abdur Rakib, Md. Mohidul Alam, Dabasis Das, Golam Md. Muradul Bashir, Md. Mahbubur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Agriculture is an important sector for the economy and food security of Bangladesh, which is facing several challenges due to climate change, lack of cultivable land and inadequate access to advanced farming technologies. To overcome the above challenges, this paper proposes NextSeed, which is an IoT based precision agriculture system that integrates realtime soils monitoring with machine learning for intelligent crop suggestion. The system uses a 7-in-1 soil sensor to measure NPK nutrients, pH, moisture, temperature and electrical conductivity, and some environmental sensors for rainfall, water level and atmospheric conditions. Data goes through anomaly detection before the cloud processing where a Naive Bayes classifier performs 99.77% accuracy in crop prediction. The system also offers fertilizer recommendations and allows automated irrigation control depending on soil conditions and water availability. NextSeed helps to cover significant gaps in solutions on the market by providing integrated automation that combines soil nutrition analysis with crops prediction and water management for agricultural decision support.

title: SelfBlending: Artificial Intelligence-driven Augmentation with Hand Interactions for Seamless Reality Blending in Virtual Environments
authors: Ahmed Elsharkawy, Bocheon Gim, Aya Ataya, SeungJun Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Accessing real-world objects during immersive virtual reality (VR) experiences remains challenging, as current cross-reality systems often rely on predefined interaction steps, tracking devices/markers, or fixed object setups. They also lack support for personalized object recall, where users can add, remove, or modify real-world items blended into the virtual environment (VE). Many head-mounted displays (HMDs) include passthrough technology to switch between virtual and real worlds, but it often disrupts immersion by requiring a full shift from virtual to real. Thus, maintaining an optimal balance between virtuality and reality is difficult. To address these challenges, we developed SelfBlending, a framework that uses AI-based hand tracking to let users label physical objects through freehand gestures, then blends the selected item into the VE using object recognition, enabling interaction with the relevant real-world object. SelfBlending was evaluated against two common interaction conditions: the default passthrough feature in VR HMDs and the conventional approach of physically removing the HMD to access real-world objects. Results from seated, single-object interactions with tabletop-placed items showed that SelfBlending enhanced user experience by boosting presence, supporting efficient physical interaction, and improving cross-reality continuity. It also enabled selective interaction with real objects while minimizing the disruption of VR experience.

title: Adversarial Decision-Making in Partially Observable Multi-Agent Systems: A Sequential Hypothesis Testing Approach
authors: Haosheng Zhou, Daniel Ralston, Xu Yang, Ruimeng Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: Adversarial decision-making in partially observable multi-agent systems requires sophisticated strategies for both deception and counter-deception. This paper presents a sequential hypothesis testing (SHT)-driven framework that captures the interplay between strategic misdirection and inference in adversarial environments. We formulate this interaction as a partially observable Stackelberg game, where a follower agent (blue team) seeks to fulfill its primary task while actively misleading an adversarial leader (red team). In opposition, the red team, leveraging leaked information, instills carefully designed patterns to manipulate the blue team's behavior, mitigating the misdirection effect. Unlike conventional approaches that focus on robust control under adversarial uncertainty, our framework explicitly models deception as a dynamic optimization problem, where both agents strategically adapt their policies in response to inference and counter-inference. We derive a semi-explicit optimal control solution for the blue team within a linear-quadratic setting and develop iterative and machine learning-based methods to characterize the red team's optimal response. Numerical experiments demonstrate how deception-driven strategies influence adversarial interactions and reveal the impact of leaked information in shaping equilibrium behaviors. These results provide new insights into strategic deception in multi-agent systems, with potential applications in cybersecurity, autonomous decision-making, and financial markets.

title: Differential RF-MEMS Enabled Power Divider Based Phase Shifters
authors: Zehan Guo, Padraig Fitzgerald, Dimitra Psychogiou
abstract: New types of co-designed differential RF-MEMS enabled power divider-based phase shifters (PDPSs) are reported. They are based on T-junction power dividers co-integrated with single-pole N-throw (SPNT) RF-MEMS switches that simultaneously control the power division and differential phase shift (Δφ) between adjacent antenna elements, allowing to steer the beam of an antenna array to N distinct directions. Scalability to alternative levels of Δφ and number of antenna elements are demonstrated through the implementation of four- and eight-way PDPSs. Practical realization aspects are discussed and validated through the design, manufacturing, and testing of two PDPS prototypes at X-band using commercially-available RF-MEMS switches. They exhibit the following RF performance characteristics: i) four-way PDPS: tunable Δφs: –39.2°, –106.2°, +105.1°, +38.8° and maximum phase shift range: 314.2° at 8 GHz with an insertion loss (IL): 3.09 ± 2.04 dB; ii) eight-way PDPS: tunable Δφs: –38.1°, –106°, +106.3°, +38.2° and maximum phase shift range: 741° at 8 GHz with an IL: 4.57 ± 2.08 dB.

title: Switching Rollout-Enhanced Value Iteration With Stabilization Guidance for Active Vehicle Suspension Systems: Theory and Experiment
authors: Liangju Zhang, Xiang-Peng Xie, Ju H. Park
abstract: Active vehicle suspension systems (AVSSs) are critical to ride comfort and road-holding performance. However, conventional learning-based methods for solving optimal control problems often suffer from slow convergence and overly restrictive stability requirements, thereby limiting their practical applicability. To address these issues, a three-stage learning scheme, termed switching rollout-enhanced value iteration (SRVI), is proposed to obtain the optimal control policy for AVSSs. Unlike conventional value iteration methods that bound successive value function (VF) differences by the instantaneous utility, the proposed approach adopts a less conservative finite-window stability criterion, enabling earlier identification of stabilizing policies. Building on this, a rollout-based incomplete policy evaluation mechanism is developed to reduce redundant VF exploration by rapidly steering the VF estimate toward a near-optimal region. Subsequently, a monotonic value update scheme is employed to ensure stable convergence to the optimal solution. The effectiveness of the proposed SRVI method is validated through hardware-in-the-loop experiments on a nonlinear active suspension system. Moreover, the proposed method reduces training time by 38.8% compared with existing approaches, while the learned control policy improves ride comfort and road-holding performance, reducing vertical suspension cost by up to 9.9% and control energy consumption by up to 9.0%.

title: A Multi-View Feature Fusion Approach with XLM-Roberta for Spam Email Detection
authors: Bipasha Majumder, Antu Chowdhury, A R M Mahamudul Hasan, Didarul Islam Didar, Bashirul Islam, Shekh Maruf
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Spam and phishing emails remain significant problems in email security and consumer privacy. This article proposes a spam classification model using XLM RoBERTa transformer and a Multi-View Feature Fusion approach to combine CLS token, mean-pooled, and attention-weighted embeddings to enhance the classification performance. Training and evaluation of the model were conducted using the Enron-Spam dataset and achieved its highest accuracy at 99.54%, with precision, recall, and F1-score all above 99%. This performance beats the previously reported performance on the same dataset. Data preprocessing included duplicates removal, cleaning, and tokenization using the XLM-RoBERTa tokenizer. The model underwent training for 10 epochs, incorporating hyperparameter optimization and early stopping techniques to mitigate overfitting. These results demonstrate the validity and suitability of the model for actual spam detection tasks. Future extensions include deploying the model on other datasets as well as deploying it for low-resource languages such as Bangla.

title: Predicting Enhancer DNA Utilizing $k$-mer Features and Multi-Headed CNN
authors: Priya Barua, Faisal Ahmed, Muhammad Anwarul Azim, Mohammad Khairul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Enhancers are non-coding DNA elements that play a critical role in regulating gene expression. Accurately predicting DNA enhancers is essential for understanding genetic regulatory mechanisms and their implications for health and disease. However, enhancer identification remains challenging due to the heterogeneous and complex nature of sequence motifs. To address this, we propose a novel DNA sequence encoding technique based on multi-k-mer features and a multi-headed Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture that processes multi-scale features simultaneously with four parallel branches to capture complementary local and global sequence patterns for enhancer prediction. Empirical results on a test set show the superiority of our method, achieving 77.25% accuracy, 80% sensitivity, 74.5% specificity, AUC of 0.84, and MCC of 0.55. These findings indicate the effectiveness of multi-scale k-mer feature representations and a CNN architecture for DNA enhancer prediction.

title: THz Transistor Design and Applications
authors: M. J. W. Rodwell, Miguel Urteaga, Yihao Fang, Logan Whitaker, Johann Rode, Amirreza Alizadeh, Utku Soylu, Zach Griffith, Yuya Nemoto
abstract: We consider the present performance, design principles, and prospects for further improvement, in THz transistor technologies, addressing how transistor characteristics limit IC performance, and showing several 100-300 GHz IC and systems examples.

title: Critical End-Effect at Sea-to-Land Transition of Three Core Submarine Cables
