This module generates time-aligned physical sequences, including meshing stiffness and fault-induced impulses, which are injected into the latent space via a dual-path conditioning mechanism. This design ensures spectral–temporal consistency between generated signals and underlying physical processes. Experimental results on both self-collected and public datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves improved generation quality and computational efficiency. Specifically, the model achieves a root-mean-square error of 0.0497, a peak signal-to-noise ratio of 26.17 dB, and a frequency spectrum cosine similarity of 0.98 or higher. The method also shows strong generalization across multiple datasets, including Southeast University Gearbox and Case Western Reserve University. The proposed approach provides an effective and practical solution for data augmentation in industrial fault diagnosis.

title: Adaptive Gait Assistance for Foot Drop Rehabilitation Based on Uncertainty Fusion of Contralateral Limb Information
authors: Kehan Xu, Jun Huo, Yize Zheng, Zixin Chi, Yu Cao, Zhaohui Yang, Jian Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Foot drop resulting from neurological injury severely compromises mobility and gait stability, yet existing assistive solutions often overlook physiological bilateral coordination and lack adaptability to individual gait variability. This study introduces a novel adaptive gait assistance framework for a soft exosuit that simultaneously aims to restore inter-limb coordination and generate personalized ankle joint assistance. First, we design a contralateral-guided adaptive phase synchronization engine that transforms healthy-limb information into an ideal phase reference for the impaired limb, enabling active correction of gait asymmetry. Second, to reconcile stability and personalization, we propose an uncertainty fusion-based adaptive gait assistance that estimates the real-time confidence of each model, arbitrates and fuses their predictions, and produces assistance trajectories that are both robust and individualized. After demonstrating high predictive accuracy and robustness in validation experiments with healthy subjects, a clinical evaluation involving five individuals with foot drop showed significant improvements in ankle kinematics, walking speed, and gait symmetry. Subjective feedback confirmed superior comfort, balance, and confidence over baseline and preset assistance. These preliminary results suggest that the proposed framework can improve gait kinematics, symmetry, and user-perceived walking performance, and support its potential as a personalized assistance strategy for foot-drop rehabilitation.

title: Toward Sustainable Environmental Intelligence: A Comprehensive Survey of Federated Learning Applications and Technical Challenges
authors: Sara Yarham, Mehran Behjati, Haider A. H. Alobaidy
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: Environmental monitoring systems increasingly face the challenge of processing vast volumes of distributed, heterogeneous, and sensitive data in real time. Federated Learning (FL), a decentralized machine learning paradigm, offers a compelling solution by enabling collaborative model training across edge devices while preserving data privacy. This paper presents a comprehensive review of recent advancements in FL-driven environmental monitoring across domains such as air quality forecasting, traffic flow optimization, solar energy prediction, water quality assessment, carbon footprint analysis, and disaster response. We analyze how state-of-the-art FL methodologies, including diverse machine and deep learning models, hierarchical and multi-task architectures, semantic communication, and privacy-preserving aggregation, enhance prediction accuracy, communication efficiency, and robustness. However, FL adoption still faces critical challenges including communication overhead, non-IID data, sensor unreliability, and adversarial threats. To address these, we highlight key research directions such as energy-aware FL protocols, scalable and secure aggregation, real-world testbeds, and incentive mechanisms. By bridging insights from AI, wireless networking, and environmental science, this review lays the groundwork for establishing FL as a cornerstone of sustainable, privacy-aware, and intelligent environmental monitoring systems.

title: AGP: Auto-Guided Prompt Refinement for Personalized Reranking in Recommender Systems
authors: Chen Wang, Mingdai Yang, Zhiwei Liu, Pan Li, Linsey Pang, Qingsong Wen, Philip Yu
publish: Proceedings of the Nineteenth ACM International Conference on Web Search and Data Mining
abstract: None

title: HeteroMILE: a Multi-Level Graph Representation Learning framework for Heterogeneous Graphs
authors: Yue Zhang, Yuntian He, Saket Gurukar, Srinivasan Parthasarathy
publish: Proceedings of the Nineteenth ACM International Conference on Web Search and Data Mining
abstract: None

title: Advancements in AI-Based Approaches for Early Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy: A Review
authors: Ayush Ohal, Arnav Bhalgaonkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a major cause of impaired vision globally, necessitating timely and accurate diagnosis. This review examines major advancements in AI-based approaches—particularly Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and emerging Generative AI—for early DR detection. It highlights key retinal features, classification systems, and model performance on public datasets. Deep learning models consistently surpass traditional methods in diagnostic accuracy and sensitivity, while hybrid and generative approaches show promise in improving lesion detection and computational efficiency. The review underscores AI's potential to transform DR screening and outlines future directions for enhancing clinical applicability.

title: Sentiment Analysis of IMDB Movie Dataset Using Logistic Regression
authors: Wafa Kanwal, Jasmine Garnaik, Sanjit Kumar Dash, Princy Diwan, Banaja Basini Rath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: The extraction of emotion and opinion from text data known as sentiment analysis is a significant contributor in revealing audience perception especially in the entertainment sector where online reviews have a great impact on the decisionmaking process of both the creative and the strategic teams. The process of hand analysis along with the accelerating usergenerated content has made the former unattainable due to the cost. This paper presents a sentiment analysis model for movie reviews which is fully automated based on the use of NLP and machine learning, particularly logistic regression and it aims to classify the reviews as positive or negative. The IMDB dataset containing 50,000 reviews of very high quality and wellbalanced sentiment is employed by the model after extensive preprocessing steps including HTML tag stripping, special characters filtering, stop-words removal, lemmatisation, and enhanced negation processing. TF-IDF vectorisation with 10,000 features is used for the transformation of textual data into a numerical one. For performance enhancement, the training and tuning applied the same hyper-parameters (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C$</tex>= 0.8, ngram_range <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=(1,2)$</tex>) which were previously optimized and assigned to the model. The model demonstrated superior accuracy in the training set with 92.24 % and a very good level of accuracy in the test set with 89.98 %. Therefore, the model excelled in generalization and classification performance.

title: Plant Leaf Disease Detection and Classification Using Deep Learning Models: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Murali Krishna Nangedda, Sivaiah Bellamkonda, Sivadi Balakrishna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Plant leaf diseases pose a major danger to food security throughout the world, inflicting an estimated 20– 40% loss of output each year. Early and accurate detection is crucial for effective management; however, conventional approaches rely heavily on manual scouting, expert interpretation, and laboratory analysis, rendering them inefficient for large-scale monitoring. Deep learning (DL) has made significant progress in a relatively short period. It now enables automated, scalable, and highly accurate disease detection and classification in plant leaves, leveraging large datasets and powerful neural architectures. This survey presents a comprehensive analysis of state-of-the-art DL models applied to plant leaf disease detection, including convolutional neural networks (CNNs), transformer-based architectures, hybrid models, and lightweight networks for edge deployment. The authors comprehensively analyse publically accessible datasets, data pre-treatment methodologies, performance measures, model comparisons, and deployment frameworks incorporating IoT sensors and agriculture systems. Furthermore, the survey highlights current limitations, such as including dataset bias, lack of generalization, interpretability challenges, and real-field deployment constraints and proposes promising future research directions, such as multimodal fusion, self-supervised learning, and multi-disease, multi-label classification frameworks.

title: UniGaze: Towards Universal Gaze Estimation via Large-scale Pre-Training
authors: Jiawei Qin, Xucong Zhang, Yusuke Sugano
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Despite decades of research on data collection and model architectures, current gaze estimation models encounter significant challenges in generalizing across diverse data domains. Recent advances in self-supervised pre-training have demonstrated remarkable generalization across various vision tasks. However, their effectiveness in gaze estimation remains unexplored. We propose UniGaze, which for the first time leverages large-scale in-the-wild facial datasets for gaze estimation through self-supervised pre-training. Through systematic investigation, we clarify critical factors essential for effective pre-training in gaze estimation. Our experiments reveal that self-supervised approaches designed for semantic tasks fail when applied to gaze estimation, while our carefully designed pre-training pipeline consistently improves cross-domain performance. Through comprehensive experiments of the challenging cross-dataset evaluations and novel protocols, including leave-one-dataset-out and joint-dataset settings, we demonstrate that UniGaze significantly improves generalization across multiple data domains while minimizing reliance on costly labeled data. Source code and model are available at https://github.com/ut-vision/UniGaze.

title: End-to-End Optical Characterization of 12-Channel VCSEL Transmitter and TIA-PD Arrays for 28 Gbps Optical Interconnects
authors: Y Seetha, Dondapati Naveen, C.Sri Vaishnavi, Ritusree Mahapatra, P.V.S. Anil Patnaik, Pamidighantam V Ramana, Y Rohin Kumar
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: This work presents a complete high-speed characterization methodology for a $\mathbf{1 2}$-channel VCSEL-based optical transmitter and a matched 12-channel photodiode/TIA receiver designed for short-reach multimode-fiber links. The transmitter integrates a VCSEL driver, an $\mathbf{8 5 0}-\mathbf{n m}$ VCSEL array, and a polymer microlens assembly within a compact LGA package, enabling passive coupling to standard MPO/MTP connectors. The receiver incorporates a 28 -Gbps TIA flip-chip bonded on LTCC and a wire-bonded photodiode array with an integrated aspheric microlens array to match the transmitter’s optical footprint and ensure efficient multimode coupling. A set of dedicated optical-validation procedures was developed to verify transmitter launch quality, assembly tolerances, near-field and far-field beam behaviour, and modal propagation through OM4 fiber. Complementary receiver-side validation using RSSI monitoring confirmed uniform photodetector response and stable optical-to-electrical conversion. An end-to-end OptiSys simulation of the complete optical path predicted a raw BER of $1 \times 10^{-5}$, and experimental measurements over a $\mathbf{1}-\mathbf{m}$ fiber link demonstrated identical BER level without equalization, pre-emphasis, or FEC. This close agreement between simulation and measurement validates both the modelling approach and the robustness of the VCSELbased optical front end, providing a comprehensive methodology for qualifying multi-channel optical links in highspeed interconnect architectures.

title: SGD-Mix: Enhancing Domain-Specific Image Classification with Label-Preserving Data Augmentation
authors: Yixuan Dong, Fang-Yi Su, Jung-Hsien Chiang
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Effective data augmentation for domain-specific image classification must balance three competing objectives: diversity, faithfulness, and label clarity. However, current methods, including state-of-the-art diffusion models, struggle to achieve this balance and are further limited by issues such as stochastic outputs under strong transformations. We propose SGD-Mix, a novel framework that systematically reconciles these objectives. Our approach employs saliency-guided mixing to preserve foreground semantics while introducing diverse backgrounds, followed by a domain-specific fine-tuned diffusion model that refines the output to ensure high fidelity and strict label consistency. Extensive experiments across fine-grained, long-tail, few-shot, and background robustness tasks demonstrate that SGD-Mix achieves state-of-the-art performance, surpassing existing diffusion-based and non-generative methods by notable margins. Our code is available at https://github.com/RadonDong/sgdmix

title: How to Design and Train Your Implicit Neural Representation for Video Compression
authors: Matthew Gwilliam, Roy Zhang, Namitha Padmanabhan, Hongyang Du, Abhinav Shrivastava
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Implicit neural representation (INR) methods for video compression have recently achieved visual quality and compression ratios that are competitive with traditional pipelines. However, due to the need for per-sample network training, the encoding speeds of these methods are too slow for practical adoption. We develop a library to allow us to disentangle and review the components of methods from the NeRV family, reframing their performance in terms of not only size-quality trade-offs, but also impacts on training time. We uncover principles for effective video INR design and propose a state-of-the-art configuration of these components, Rabbit NeRV (RNeRV). When all methods are given equal training time (equivalent to 300 NeRV epochs) for 7 different UVG videos at 1080p, RNeRV achieves +1.27% PSNR on average compared to the best-performing alternative for each video in our NeRV library. We then tackle the encoding speed issue head-on by investigating the viability of hyper-networks, which predict INR weights from video inputs, to disentangle training from encoding to allow for real-time encoding. We propose masking the weights of the predicted INR during training to allow for variable, higher quality compression, resulting in 1.7% improvements to both PSNR and MS-SSIM at 0.037 bpp on the UCF-101 dataset, and we increase hyper-network parameters by 0.4% for 2.5%/2.7% improvements to PSNR/MS-SSIM with equal bpp and similar speeds. Our code is available at https://github.com/mgwillia/vinrb.

title: RACE-Fusion: Reliability- and Calibration-Enhanced Tri-Modal Learning from ECG, Echocardiography, and EHR
authors: Basetty Naveen Kumar, T Narayanan, B. Narendra Kumar
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: Multimodal clinical information is crucial for reliable cardiovascular risk assessment. We propose RACEFusion, a reliable and calibrated tri-modal learning approach that integrates electrocardiogram (ECG) recordings, ultrasound representations and electronic health record (EHR) plus laboratory features for accurate prediction of cardiovascular risk. The proposed architecture employs modality-specific encoders to learn latent representations of each modality and proposes a reliability gated fusion strategy to dynamically balance the contributions of different modalities depending on their informativeness at the sample level. To enhance robustness against missing clinical data, modality-based dropouts are applied during model training to ensure that the model can perform well when only a subset of modalities is present. Furthermore, a calibration-aware loss is applied to ensure that the predicted probabilities of risk are discriminative, as well as medically reliable for thresholding-based risk classification. Trimodal reproducibility experiments reveal that the proposed model significantly outperforms unimodal models and simple feature concatenation in AUROC, F1-score and probability calibration. The ablation study also verifies the efficacy of reliability gating and modality dropout. These findings show that explicitly accounting for trust and calibration can lead to a more reliable and practical multi-modal cardiovascular risk prediction model.

title: IDEAL-M3D: Instance Diversity-Enriched Active Learning for Monocular 3D Detection
authors: Johannes Meier, Florian Günther, Riccardo Marin, Oussema Dhaouadi, Jacques Kaiser, Daniel Cremers
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Monocular 3D detection relies on just a single camera and is therefore easy to deploy. Yet, achieving reliable 3D understanding from monocular images requires substantial annotation, and 3D labels are especially costly. To maximize performance under constrained labeling budgets, it is essential to prioritize annotating samples expected to deliver the largest performance gains. This prioritization is the focus of active learning. Curiously, we observed two significant limitations in active learning algorithms for 3D monocular object detection. First, previous approaches select entire images, which is inefficient, as non-informative instances contained in the same image also need to be labeled. Secondly, existing methods rely on uncertainty-based selection, which in monocular 3D object detection creates a bias toward depth ambiguity. Consequently, distant objects are selected, while nearby objects are overlooked.To address these limitations, we propose IDEAL-M3D, the first instance-level pipeline for monocular 3D detection. For the first time, we demonstrate that an explicitly diverse, fast-to-train ensemble improves diversity-driven active learning for monocular 3D. We induce diversity with heterogeneous backbones and task-agnostic features, loss weight perturbation, and time-dependent bagging. IDEAL-M3D shows superior performance and significant resource savings: with just 60% of the annotations, we achieve similar or better AP<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3D</inf> on KITTI validation and test set results compared to training the same detector on the whole dataset.

title: Scalable Video Action Anticipation with Cross Linear Attentive Memory
authors: Zeyun Zhong, Manuel Martin, David Schneider, David J. Lerch, Chengzhi Wu, Frederik Diederichs, Juergen Gall, Jürgen Beyerer
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Recent advances in action anticipation rely heavily on Transformer architectures to learn discriminative representations of the past observation, incurring high computational and memory overhead that limits their applicability to long videos. While temporal processors with linear complexity like RNNs and state-space models offer efficient alternatives, their sequential nature risks overlooking subtle cues in observed frames that could enhance future anticipation. We address this limitation with Cross Linear Attentive Memory (CLAM), a memory module that selectively retrieves complementary context cues from frame features. By reformulating linear attention to replace traditional cross-attention, CLAM achieves linear computation complexity and constant memory usage relative to input length. Finally, by fusing the outputs of the temporal processor and CLAM, a non-autoregressive Transformer decoder generates future actions in one shot with high accuracy. Experiments on egocentric (EpicKitchens100 and Ego4D) and third-person (Thumos14) benchmarks demonstrate our model’s superior anticipation accuracy and scalability, processing longer sequences with significantly less latency growth than alternatives. Our approach also achieves promising results in online action detection.

title: GDoFS: Gaussian DoF Separation for Plausible 3D Geometry in Sparse-View 3DGS
authors: Yongsung Kim, Jooyoung Choi, Sungroh Yoon
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: While learning-based Multi-View Stereo (MVS) excels in sparse-view reconstruction, refining its output with 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) remains challenging. Excessive positional degrees of freedom (DoFs) in Gaussians often cause instability and geometric artifacts, sometimes distorting geometry to represent texture patterns. To address this issue, we propose GDoFS (Gaussian DoF Separation), a strategy that divides positional DoFs into two categories—image-plane-parallel and ray-aligned—based on their uncertainty. For each category, GDoFS introduces tailored optimization techniques, including bounded offsets for low-uncertainty DoFs and a visibility-guided loss for ray-aligned DoFs. Experiments on standard benchmarks demonstrate that GDoFS effectively mitigates geometric artifacts and produces reconstructions that are both visually coherent and structurally accurate.

title: Conversational Image Generation: Towards Multi-Round Personalized Generation with Multi-Modal Language Models
authors: Haochen Zhang, Animesh Sinha, Felix Juefei-Xu, Haoyu Ma, Kunpeng Li, Zhipeng Fan, Xiaoliang Dai, Tingbo Hou, Peizhao Zhang, Zecheng He
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Recent advancements in diffusion models have significantly enhanced personalized image generation, enabling high-fidelity synthesis of human-subject-specific images. However, existing approaches are constrained by the inherent limitations of diffusion models, which lack conversational capabilities, and operate in a single-round setting, restricting user interaction. In this work, we propose a novel framework that integrates multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) for multi-round conversational personalization. To achieve this, we identified a performance bottleneck in the detokenizer of current MLLMs, which struggles to reconstruct fine-grained facial identity details. Thus, we enhance the detokenizer with a personalization-enhaced Diffusion Transformer (DiT). We also introduce a multi-stage instruction fine-tuning strategy to balance face preservation and prompt alignment effectively. To support multi-round generation, we implement a chat-history caching mechanism and construct the first multi-round personalization dataset from video clips. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance among MLLM-based personalization methods. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to enable conversational personalization, unlocking new capabilities for MLLMs in personalized image generation.

title: MANTA: Physics-Informed Generalized Underwater Object Tracking
authors: Suhas Srinath, Hemang Jamadagni, Aditya Chandrasekar, Prathosh A P
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Underwater object tracking is challenging due to wavelength-dependent attenuation and scattering, which severely distort appearance across depths and water conditions. Existing trackers trained on terrestrial data fail to generalize to these physics-driven degradations. We present MANTA, a physics-informed framework integrating representation learning with tracking design for underwater scenarios. We propose a dual-positive contrastive learning strategy coupling temporal consistency with Beer–Lambert augmentations to yield features robust to both temporal and underwater distortions. We further introduce a multi-stage pipeline augmenting motion-based tracking with a physics-informed secondary association algorithm that integrates geometric consistency and appearance similarity for re-identification under occlusion and drift. To complement standard IoU metrics, we propose Center–Scale Consistency (CSC) and Geometric Alignment Score (GAS) to assess geometric fidelity. Experiments on four underwater benchmarks (WebUOT-1M, UOT32, UTB180, UWCOT220) show that MANTA achieves state-of-the-art performance, improving Success AUC by up to 6%, while ensuring stable long-term generalized underwater tracking and efficient runtime. Code available at https://github.com/Kazedaa/MANTA.

title: Graph Query Networks for Object Detection with Automotive Radar
authors: Loveneet Saini, Hasan Tercan, Tobias Meisen
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Object detection with 3D radar is essential for 360<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">○</sup> automotive perception, but radar’s long wavelengths produce sparse and irregular reflections that challenge traditional grid and sequence-based convolutional and transformer detectors. This paper introduces Graph Query Networks (GQN), an attention-based framework that models objects sensed by radar as graphs, to extract individualized relational and contextual features. GQN employs a novel concept of graph queries to dynamically attend over the bird’s-eye view (BEV) space, constructing object-specific graphs processed by two novel modules: EdgeFocus for relational reasoning and DeepContext Pooling for contextual aggregation. On the NuScenes dataset, GQN improves relative mAP by up to +53%, including a +8.2% gain over the strongest prior radar method, while reducing peak graph construction overhead by 80% with moderate FLOPs cost.

title: LVM-Lite: Training Large Vision Models with Efficient Sequential Modeling
authors: Xianhang Li, Hongru Zhu, Sucheng Ren, Linjie Yang, Peng Wang, Heng Wang, Xiaohui Shen, Qing Liu, Cihang Xie
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Large Vision Models (LVMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities by leveraging large-scale generative pre-training on visual sequences. However, training these models on massive datasets of single images and image sequences can be computationally expensive and limit their accessibility to researchers without substantial computational resources. This paper introduces LVM-Lite, a new two-stage learning pipeline for more efficient and effective LVM training. In the first stage, the model is pre-trained on a large corpus of single images. Subsequently, in the second stage, the model is fine-tuned on curated long image/video sequences. This decoupled training approach substantially accelerates the training process, achieving up to 2.7× speedup compared to baseline LVM training. Extensive experiments demonstrate that LVM-Lite achieves competitive performance on various generative and discriminative benchmarks while maintaining high training efficiency and strong scalability. https://github.com/UCSC-VLAA/LVM-Lite

title: Single-step Diffusion for Image Compression at Ultra-Low Bitrates
authors: Chanung Park, Joo Chan Lee, Jong Hwan Ko
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Although there have been significant advancements in image compression techniques, such as standard and learned codecs, these methods still suffer from severe quality degradation at extremely low bits per pixel. While recent diffusion-based models provided enhanced generative performance at low bitrates, they often yield limited perceptual quality and prohibitive decoding latency due to multiple denoising steps. In this paper, we propose the single-step diffusion model for image compression that delivers high perceptual quality and fast decoding at ultra-low bitrates. Our approach incorporates two key innovations: (i) Vector-Quantized Residual (VQ-Residual) training, which factorizes a structural base code and a learned residual in latent space, capturing both global geometry and high-frequency details; and (ii) rate-aware noise modulation, which tunes denoising strength to match the desired bitrate. Extensive experiments show that ours achieves comparable compression performance to state-of-the-art methods while improving decoding speed by about 50× compared to prior diffusion-based methods, greatly enhancing the practicality of generative codecs.

title: Intelligent Retail Shelf Management Using Transfer Learning and YOLOv8n
authors: Bagyammal Thirumurthy, Nithin Ravi, Yoshni Nandha Kishore, Latha Parameswaran, Karthikeyan Vaiapury
publish: 2026 World Conference on Computational Science and Technology (WcCST)
abstract: This article describes a deep learning (DL) based approach that uses object detection and product categorization to automate inventory management in retail settings. Traditional inventory systems, which are dependent on manual scanning techniques such as barcodes and radio frequency identification (RFID), are limited by human error and inefficiency, often leading to inaccurate stock counts, stockouts, or overstock situations. For addressing these issues, the Grocery Store dataset with about 5,125 images on 81 product classes under varied real-world scenes is used to develop a robust product detection and classification framework. Three pre-trained DL models, viz. MobileNetV2, ResNet50, and DenseNet169 were trained to categorize products, while the You Only Look Once, version 8, nano (YOLOv8n) model was used for object detection to accurately locate items on retail shelves. MobileNetV2 achieved a test precision of 84.47%, ResNet50 reached 84. 43%, DenseNet169 outperformed both with 86.77%. YOLOv8n showed high-precision object identification with a mean average precision (mAP) of 69%.

title: Efficient Lung Segmentation from Chest Radiographs Using a MobileUNet Architecture for Resource-Constrained Environments
authors: G Sriram, Sujith Bysani Santhosh, Priyanka Nallusamy, R Arumuga Arun, R Adaline Suji, C Chandru Vignesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Chest X-rays are one of the most popular techniques used in the diagnosis of lung disease, but precise diagnosis typically requires the ability to clearly segment the lung fields. Manual segmentation is both time-consuming and inaccurate, while many highly accurate deep learning models are too computationally intensive to be used in real-time or mobile applications. To address this gap, a new lightweight framework for efficient lung segmentation was developed. Our model combines the encoder-decoder structure of the U-Net framework with depth-wise separable convolutions, which reduces the number of parameters and computational cost without a significant loss of accuracy. The model is trained and tested our model on the Kaggle available Chest X-ray Masks and Labels dataset. The proposed method showed robust performance with Intersection over Union (IoU) of 0.9214, Dice coefficient of 0.9589, accuracy of 0.9790, precision of 0.9741, and recall of 0.9445. These results are comparable to the state-of-theart architecture but with lower computational complexity. The capability of our MobileUNet framework for use in rural areas such as hospitals, mobile X-ray vans, and mobile health solutions. This Research aims at development of real-time diagnostic tools that brings medical imaging techniques to poor communities by providing them consistent lung segmentation on low-power hardware resources.

title: A Lightweight CNN Framework with SAM-Based Preprocessing for Luffa Disease Detection
authors: Manish Kumar Sharma, Sumeet Saurav, Sanjay Singh
abstract: The accurate and automatic detection of diseases in Luffa is crucial for safeguarding crop yield. While deep learning offers promising solutions, many models suffer from high computational complexity. This paper introduces a robust and efficient pipeline for automated identification of Luffa leaf diseases. It consists of the Segment Anything Model (SAM)-based image preprocessing and a lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). The use of a zero-shot segmentation tool, such as SAM, helps the proposed pipeline isolate the leaf from the background and enhance the performance of the CNN. The proposed CNN combines modified MBConv blocks with a dual-branch Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) mechanism, achieving a remarkably small footprint of just 166K parameters, 204.5 MMac FLOPs, and 0.58 MB of memory footprint. Evaluated on the benchmark LuffaFolio dataset, our method demonstrates that SAM-based preprocessing improves accuracy by 19.73% and F1-score by 20.98% compared to using the original images. The result demonstrates the critical role of advanced segmentation in achieving robust classification. Also, the proposed CNN is optimal for deployment on low-powered edge and TinyML devices. In summary, the proposed framework provides a practical solution for in-field plant disease diagnosis, enabling timely intervention to minimize crop yield losses.

title: AI-Guided Approaches for Siting EV Charging Infrastructure in Urban Transport Networks
authors: Bhargav Chebrolu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: The transition toward sustainable urban mobility requires not only the adoption of electric vehicles but also the strategic integration of charging facilities within existing transport systems. This paper investigates how artificial intelligence can support data-driven decisions on the placement of charging stations in metropolitan contexts. Using open spatial and mobility datasets from Berlin, the research applies machine learning methods-including supervised models, unsupervised clustering, and neural networks-to identify patterns shaped by traffic flows, parking constraints, and demographic distributions. Heatmaps and GIS-based visualizations highlight areas of unmet demand and infrastructure imbalance. While real-world validation remains limited by incomplete utilization data, the comparative evaluation of AI techniques demonstrates their potential to enhance traditional planning methods. The findings contribute to urban transportation research by offering a scalable framework for integrating EV infrastructure into broader mobility planning and congestion management strategies.

title: Machine Learning–Based Earthquake Prediction Using Global Seismic Data
authors: Allacheruvu Brahmaiah, Kondragunta Rama Krishnaiah, Kattika Koteswara Rao, Nanduri Srinivas, Shahanaz Shaik, Venneti Murali Krishna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: As earthquakes may endanger people and their possessions, using accurate predictions can help in addressing risks. Lately, global earthquakes that have occurred since 2020 have been explored by researchers using the KNN method for studying and forecasting patterns. Using the model, scientists can predict if earthquakes are likely to occur in different regions globally during the next month, six months and a year. MSE, RMSE, Accuracy Percentage and MAPE are used to evaluate the model, which have values of 0.198, 0.445, 98.57% and 1.16%, respectively. Based on the results, it appears that machine learning with KNN can show the frequency of earthquakes over time. The findings suggest that AI can help forecast earthquakes and assess the dangers that follow. While the model accurately predicts, future advancements using deep learning and quick service will help it in warning systems.

title: Design and Development of Enhanced Ultrasound Vibration Potential Imaging Process Using MobileNetV2 and Efficient Stability Analysis
authors: M Chiranjeevi, Ahmad Al-Qerem, D V Ashok, Akila Venkatraman, S. Kaliappan, Sreeja Vijay
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Ultrasound Vibration Potential Imaging uses ultrasound waves in a nanoparticle suspension, where charged particles vibrate according to their polarity. This haunting work proposes a new technological concept: A hybrid framework EUVMES (Enhanced Ultrasound Vibration Potential Imaging-Machine-Enhanced System), which dramatically increases the non-invasive biomedical imaging through physics-based principles and AI. In order to do this, the system sends ultrasound through a charged nanoparticle colloidal suspension thus polarizing dipoles of induced charges that generate local electric fields which can be detected; therefore, the electrodes for detection serve as the means of recording these fields. A mathematical model of bubble dynamics along with the stability analysis to generate the potential distribution maps from the acoustic signals. Convolutional neural networks (CNN) such as MobileNetV2 for efficient feature extraction and EfficientNet-B4 for better classification accuracy were employed to process the signatures and identify the changes in the medium. The performance analysis of the EUVMES model comparison of accuracy, loss, average run time, average RAM time, and accuracy vs image throughput calculations.

title: A Deep Ensemble Learning System for Smart Helmet Detection and Automated Traffic Fine Notification
authors: M.R. Nithya, K Naveen, N Satish, D J Praveen, V Barath, V Sanjay
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The use of helmets by riders of two-wheelers is mandatory across many countries, but non-adherence is still predominant, resulting in thousands of deaths that can be avoided. We would use a hybrid system of deep ensemble learning to solve this problem by proposing a unique deep ensemble learning that incorporates real-time object detection with intelligent decision fusion to automatically detect and notify about the usage of the helmet and trigger a fine notification on the traffic. The system combines video data of urban traffic surveillance systems, and uses a semantic preserving preprocessing pipeline, which adapts to changeable light and occlusion conditions. Graph-Augmented Attention Pooling, Capsule Networks with part-aware attention, StyleMix++ augmentation and Graph-Augmented Attention Pooling are rare methods that are used to learn features in a robust way and multi-modal fusion. The baseline model is a weighted combination of YOLOv7-Tiny, EfficientDet-D2, and Swin Transformer, combined through a Dynamic Ensemble Weighting strategy, based on a Genetic Algorithm. It reached a peak F1-Score of 94.67 with precision of 95.12 and recall of 94.22, which allows it to be effectively accurate in detecting at heterogeneous traffic conditions. Moreover, automated fine notification modules are connected to government databases, which are enforced at the end. The suggested solution provides a scalable and flexible platform of smart city management, minimizing the human contribution and facilitating road safety with the help of AI-based automation.

title: AI-Powered Consumer Sentiment Analytics to Enhance Brand Loyalty in the Confectionery Industry
authors: Yuvaraj. R, R Priyadarshini, Hariprasath S, Jestin Joshva S, Ramalekshmi M, Santhosh Kumar N
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: As a result of digital platform influence on buying decisions and the rapid shifting preferences of consumers, the confectionery business is experiencing increasing challenges maintaining customer loyalty. With regards to detecting complicated emotions and instant views expressed on internet forums, traditional methods of gauging customer attitudes sometimes remain short. In order to create inferences about consumer sentiment in the confectionery market based on various sources, such as social media, product reviews, and consumer forums, this current study proposes an artificial intelligence-driven consumer sentiment analytics platform. In order to use these ideas in desirable brand traits like flavor, packaging, health reputation, and moral purchasing, the suggested approach utilizes deep learning algorithms with sophisticated natural language processing (NLP) to identify both overt opinions and more subtle emotional signals. Based on the findings of the experiments, this technique can be used by brand managers to design personalized engagement strategies that build stronger emotional bonds with customers and generate loyalty in the long term. As a move towards improving customer-driven decision-making in candy brand management, this study offers a novel AI-based methodology. The accuracy has achieved 92.3% for the model.

title: Physisolver: An LLM Based Model for Physics Queries
authors: KrishnaBhargavi Yerraganti, Rudraraju Bhupesh Varma, Rohan Chadha, Mariyala Rohith, Chintakunta Vagmi Reddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: The paper examines the development of an intelligent system that is able to solve multi-demanding physics issues using the large language model (LLM). The goal is to create subject-specific accessories that could understand the concept of physics and respond with accurate answers to help people trying to solve physics problem. To achieve this, a customized data set is prepared which includes physics questions obtained from standard academic text-books and by web-scraping. Further questions were generated with similar lines to ensure wide and balanced coverage of subjects from different topics of physics. These questions were structured in a MCQ format, each record in the dataset included record id, question, a set of options, correct answers and explanation.Two popular transformer-based models-GPT-2 and Flan-T5 base were selected from the Hugging Face Library and were fine-tuned on our dataset using monitored learning techniques. After training, both models were evaluated based on their accuracy in predicting the right choices. Among the two, the GPT-2 model performed much better, which shows its ability for domain-specific tasks even with decoder only architecture. A simple online front-end was developed to interact with the GPT-2 model so that users can allow input questions and receive real-time response. The final product acts as an evidence-of-concept to integrate properly set language model in especially for physics, especially in physics. This task sheds light on how LLM can be made to open paths for their application in different educational and learning environments, to understand and solve domain specific activities.

title: A Hyperparameter-Tuned and Explainable ML Framework for Accurate Heart Disease Diagnosis
authors: Samhitha Ramarathnam, Sayed Sabeeh Ahamed Airani, Sachin M N, Shruthi M L J
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Heart diseases are still among the common causes of death in the world and thus early and accurate diagnosis is critical in the clinical management of this disease. Over the last few years, machine learning methods have demonstrated great potential in aiding the automated cardiovascular risk assessment based on structured clinical data. The paper introduces a machine learning predictor of heart disease that is optimized and explainable on hyperparameters using the Cleveland Heart Disease dataset. These are proposed pipeline, systematic preprocessing of data, normalization of features, stratified sampling, and large scale model tuning to enhance predictive stability. Four supervised learning models, including Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, XG Boost, and k-Nearest Neighbor, are compared on the basis of class-wise F1-scores in order to eliminate the possibility of skew between positive and negative cases. Even though statistical feature selection had been studied, it was not included in the end model because it performed poorly with the small dataset. Based on experimental results, tuned Random Forest classifier provides the most consistent performance with equal F1-scores of the two classes. In order to provide transparency and clinical reliability, several techniques of interpretability, such as Permutation Importance, SHAP, LIME, and Partial Dependence Plots, are used to evaluate both global and local model behavior. The results indicate that a rigorous model optimization implementation together with multi-level explainability offer a strong and interpretable decision-support system, which can be utilized in the practical setting in healthcare facilities.

title: A Vision-Based Assistive Navigation System for Visually Impaired Individuals Using CNN–SSD
authors: Jayakumar M, Anu G Kumar, Deepshiga M, Manu Neethi Chozhan A M
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Physiological or neurological conditions leading to visual impairments significantly hamper independent mobility and safe navigation. This work introduces a low-cost, user-friendly smart navigation system for indoor applications to support people with visual impairments. The system concept involves a wearable camera on smart glasses with a Raspberry Pi 4B processor, allowing real-time obstacle detection and audio-based guidance. The camera constantly picks up the visual data from the environment and processes it through the deep learning framework made upon CNN and SSD. Through real-time processing of those detected objects and obstacles, some relevant audio cues will be generated to keep the user informed through earphones. It is a way of providing hands-free mobility to the users themselves while detecting stationary and moving obstacles. The proposed solution is affordable, portable, and can be used easily, thus being relevant for daily indoor navigation assistance. This work emphasizes practical system design and real-time validation on embedded hardware rather than proposing a novel object detection algorithm. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the system to detect common indoor objects with timely auditory feedback, thereby enhancing environmental awareness and user safety.

title: Crop Yield Prediction Using Federated Learning: A Privacy-Preserving Approach for Decentralized Agriculture Data
authors: Satyajit Pujapanda, Sriya Mishra, Debasish Mahapatra, Gyanendra Sahoo, Neelamadhab Padhy, Pradeep Kumar Mahapatro
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Food security and economic growth of the world depend on agriculture. It is also significant to anticipate rich crops well since this will bring good management and favourable allocation of resources. It is a prerequisite in all the classic practices of machine learning approaches that all data be gathered together in one place. The storage gives rise to problems such as a lack of privacy and sharing of huge data sets, especially in instances where data is harvested at varying locations. That is the issue that the present paper attempts to address with the help of Federated Learning (FL). This method gives farmers the ability to create a model of machine learning without the need to disclose their data. We developed and tested the project on real data in Python and TensorFlow. These findings indicated that FL could be used as an alternative to conventional methods, and it is best suited to agriculture.

title: CarbonSense360: A Self-Learning AI–Blockchain Ecosystem for Predictive Emission Analytics and Sustainable User Engagement
authors: R Thendral, M Abinanthida
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: Climate change continues to accelerate due to rising greenhouse gas emissions driven by transportation, energy consumption, and unsustainable lifestyle patterns. Existing carbon tracking tools often rely on manual data entry, provide static calculations, and lack transparency or behavioral engagement. To address these limitations, this research proposes CarbonSense360, an AI–Blockchain integrated web platform that enables end-to-end carbon footprint monitoring, predictive analytics, and sustainable behavior reinforcement. By leveraging OCR and NLP for automated data capture, AI for emission forecasting and personalized recommendations, and blockchain for transparent and tamper-proof activity validation, the platform enhances user engagement through gamified sustainability challenges and community dashboards. CarbonSense360 aims to provide accurate, transparent, and action-oriented insights promoting long-term environmental responsibility.

title: Federated Multi-Modal Learning System for Privacy-Preserving Medical Diagnostics in Next-Generation Healthcare
authors: M. Dharma Teja, Hayel Khafajeh, Ankur Bahl, Preethi S, Akila Venkatraman, Priyadharisini
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The high-growth rate of multi-modal clinical data has necessitated the need to develop diagnostic models that are both patient confidential and provide high predictive accuracy. The proposed work presents a Federated Multi-Modal Learning System that incorporates imaging, clinical text, and structured EHR capabilities through decentralized optimization to avoid sharing raw data. The system uses secure aggregation, multi-branch encoders, and an attention-based fusion unit, which is trained using privacy-sensitive gradient updates. Experimental analysis shows significant improvements in the performance of 97.4% diagnostic accuracy, 96.1% F1-score, 0.982 AUC, and 28% of latency reduction over local uni-modal baselines. The model achieves a 35% enhancement in multi-modal features, a 42% reduction in communication cost, and 0% risk of data exposure, making it feasible in a heterogeneous clinical node. The findings verify that federated multi-modal integration increases diagnostic reliability and ensures a high level of confidentiality. The findings indicate that the proposed system will be a scalable backbone to the next-generation privacy-preserving medical diagnostics.

title: An Explainable Deep Learning Approach for Chronic Kidney Disease Prediction in Clinical Systems
authors: Kalyani Chapa, Bhramaramba Ravi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The paper presents developing interpretable and deep prediction models for chronic kidney disease with the assistance of human intelligence as an important new avenue of research. The study reported on a neural network-based model that uses both convolutional and attention layers to process heterogeneous clinical data from hospitals. The results of tests performed on multiple representative clinical datasets showed that this neural network-based model had a very accurate ability to predict chronic kidney disease with a high degree of robustness and that the rules and interpretability derived from the key features of the input provided confidence that clinicians would understand the rationale behind the predictions generated by this model. The increased sensitivity and specificity exhibited in the studies conducted indicated that this model could be integrated into the current clinical system as a predictive model of future chronic kidney disease diagnoses. he results of tests performed on [N=12,400] patients across [3] clinical sites showed that this neural network-based model achieved an Accuracy of [94.2%], Sensitivity of [91.5%], and an AUROC of [0.96], significantly outperforming baseline models (p < 0.01). Furthermore, the interpretability suite achieved a clinical relevance score of [4.5/5.0] as rated by specialist nephrologists.

title: RPT-SR: Regional Prior attention Transformer for infrared image Super-Resolution
authors: Youngwan Jin, Incheol Park, Yagiz Nalcakan, Hyeongjin Ju, Sanghyeop Yeo, Shiho Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: General-purpose super-resolution models, particularly Vision Transformers, have achieved remarkable success but exhibit fundamental inefficiencies in common infrared imaging scenarios like surveillance and autonomous driving, which operate from fixed or nearly-static viewpoints. These models fail to exploit the strong, persistent spatial priors inherent in such scenes, leading to redundant learning and suboptimal performance. To address this, we propose the Regional Prior attention Transformer for infrared image Super-Resolution (RPT-SR), a novel architecture that explicitly encodes scene layout information into the attention mechanism. Our core contribution is a dual-token framework that fuses (1) learnable, regional prior tokens, which act as a persistent memory for the scene’s global structure, with (2) local tokens that capture the frame-specific content of the current input. By utilizing these tokens into an attention, our model allows the priors to dynamically modulate the local reconstruction process. Extensive experiments validate our approach. While most prior works focus on a single infrared band, we demonstrate the broad applicability and versatility of RPT-SR by establishing new state-of-the-art performance across diverse datasets covering both Long-Wave (LWIR) and Short-Wave (SWIR) spectra. Code is available at https://github.com/Yonsei-STL/RPT-SR.git.

title: STRinGS: Selective Text Refinement in Gaussian Splatting
authors: Abhinav Raundhal, Gaurav Behera, P. J. Narayanan, Ravi Kiran Sarvadevabhatla, Makarand Tapaswi
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Text as signs, labels, or instructions is a critical element of real-world scenes as they can convey important contextual information. 3D representations such as 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) struggle to preserve fine-grained text details, while achieving high visual fidelity. Small errors in textual element reconstruction can lead to significant semantic loss. We propose STRinGS, a text-aware, selective refinement framework to address this issue for 3DGS reconstruction. Our method treats text and non-text regions separately, refining text regions first and merging them with non-text regions later for full-scene optimization. STRinGS produces sharp, readable text even in challenging configurations. We introduce a text readability measure OCR Character Error Rate (CER) to evaluate the efficacy on text regions. STRinGS results in a 63.6% relative improvement over 3DGS at just 7K iterations. We also introduce a curated dataset STRinGS-360 with diverse text scenarios to evaluate text readability in 3D reconstruction. Our method and dataset together push the boundaries of 3D scene understanding in text-rich environments, paving the way for more robust text-aware reconstruction methods.

title: The Effect of e-WOM, Price Perception, and Trust on Shopee Users' Purchase Decisions in Bekasi
authors: Evans, Rafael Newantona Sinurat, Danang Prihandoko, Leo Andri Yulius Caesar
publish: 2026 8th International Symposium on Computational and Business Intelligence (ISCBI)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of digital marketing factors and consumer perceptions on purchasing decisions of Indonesian e-commerce users in Bekasi. The research explores the determinants of purchasing decisions on the Shopee platform, examining the direct relationships between Electronic Word of Mouth (e-WOM), Price Perception, and Trust as key drivers in the online shopping environment. This quantitative study used 405 survey responses from active Indonesian e-commerce consumers in Bekasi, aged 17–59, who participated in online shopping between November and December 2025. The data were analyzed using SmartPLS 4.0 with Structural Equation Modeling Partial Least Squares (SEM-PLS). The results show that trust had the strongest direct effect on purchasing decisions compared to price perception and e-WOM, although all three variables demonstrated a positive and significant influence. This study provides practical insights for researchers and industry practitioners regarding the importance of consumer trust, pricing strategies, and digital reviews in sustaining competitiveness in the e-commerce sector.

title: Analyzing the Impact of Early Adoption of Post Quantum Cryptography in Apache Kafka
authors: Saahil Pakhare, Shreya Hegde, Animesh Giri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Next-Gen Quantum and Advanced Computing: Algorithms, Security, and Beyond (NQComp)
abstract: Today, data is the forefront of modern decision making, it forms the foundational piece for a majority of business decisions. With the rising importance of data, real-time streaming platforms like Apache Kafka play a crucial role as they enable data movement across systems, ensuring its timely availability, and with its value increasing it is of utmost importance ensure it is secured. While data can be considered secure at the source of its generation, it is most vulnerable when in transit. While traditional encryption techniques like Advanced Encryption Standard are currently considered secure, the pace at which quantum computers are evolving presents a growing threat. This paper presents an experimental study examining the real-world impact of securing data transmission pipelines against post quantum adversaries through the use of Post Quantum Cryptography. To ensure a fair comparison, identical payloads were transmitted through three distinct pipelines, one transmitting plaintext, another employing AES-256 encryption, and a third utilizing a hybrid quantum-safe model which combines Kyber512 based key encapsulation with AES-256 based data encryption.

title: IoT Driven Intelligent safety helmet for Adaptive accident Prevention and Real time Alerting
authors: A Benisha, M Infant Marissa, J Shannya, T. Sripriya, S. Arul, H. Shine
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The safety issue with road accidents in the case of two wheelers is still a significant problem since there is no smart monitoring and emergency response system. The present paper describes an IoT-based intelligent safety helmet that will be used to prevent accidents adaptively and real-time alerting. The suggested system will combine the concept of multi-sensor data collection, assessment of the rider condition, and severity-sensitive accident detection with the use of intelligence embedded within it. Cloud-based IoT infrastructure creates real-time communication that allows automated creation of emergency alerts with accurate location tracking. Accuracy of accident detection, average alert transmission latency, and successful alert delivery are shown to be 95.7, 2.1 seconds and 98.9 respectively suggesting system reliability and responsiveness. The power efficiency analysis proves that the solution can be used daily with more than 14 hours of continuous operation. The findings show that the helmet under consideration contributes greatly to the safety of the riders, as it allows taking crucial measures to reduce the risks and be able to respond to the accident. This study offers a useful wearable safety system that can be effectively applied to large-scale use in smart transportation networks.

title: Hybrid Stock Market Forecasting: Transformers, Reinforcement Learning, and Sentiment-Driven Insights
authors: Kumbham Bhargavi, Hema Sree Guddanti, Praneeth Venkata Reddy Kallam, Yadaboyina Rishitha, Desetty Kavya Sri, Krishna Sai Gangishetty
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Predicting stock market movements is one of the most challenging tasks in financial research due to high volatility, nonlinear dependencies, and sensitivity to external events. Traditional models like ARIMA, LSTM, and GRU focus mainly on limited temporal patterns and fail to generalize well across varying market conditions. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a hybrid forecasting model that integrates Transformer, sentiment analysis of financial news, and Reinforcement Learning (RL) through Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO). The Transformer model captures long range dependencies in historical stock data, while sentiment analysis enhances predictive features by incorporating investor psychology and macroeconomic signals from news headlines. Our experiments show that the standalone Transformer model achieved a predictive accuracy of 52.88% on test data. These enriched features were then fed into a PPO agent, which dynamically learned optimal trading strategies under uncertain conditions. When integrated with reinforcement learning, the PPO agent significantly boosted performance, attaining 84% accuracy. These results highlight the potential of combining Transformers, sentiment-driven insights, and reinforcement learning for building robust and adaptive algorithmic trading systems that achieve higher predictive accuracy and better risk-adjusted profitability compared to conventional statistical and deep learning approaches.

title: Emergent Behaviour Recognition using Reinforcement Learning-Driven Control in Complex Cybernetic IoT Architectures
authors: S. Swapna, Kathari Santosh, Yentrapati Sabitha Yali, Bobonazarov Abdurasul, Krishnammal N, B.Anitha Vijayalakshmi
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: In massive cybernetic IoT architectures, interconnected devices can exhibit emergent behavior, or unintended behaviors that arise as a result of interacting with other devices, which can create challenges in stability, security and performance. Rule-based or fixed control methods are inadequate when it comes to real-time adaptation; this will lead to inefficiency and vulnerableness to an abnormal situation. In this study, a reinforcement learning-informed control approach to discovering and managing emergent behavior in IoT dynamic systems is proposed. The strategy employs dynamic feedback and active redefinition of policy to detect unnatural working conditions and to optimize the response of the system. Multi-agent reinforcement learning framework is a device-level approach, which provides local discretion and connectivity to a global system. The plan capitalizes on the existence of feedback loops and adjustment of policies to detect abnormalities in the manner in which systems should operate and how to best respond. A multi-agent reinforcement learning model coordinates device-level decision-making supporting local adaptability and global system concordance.

title: Efficient Task Execution Model with System Safety Using Edge Convolutional Neural Networks in Computer Vision Cloud Service
authors: K Ganapathi, R Manasa, Ashraf Shaqadan, Srilakshmi Aluri, Maranan Ramya, Vijaya Vardan Reddy S. P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: The efficiently determines the task’s location by examining various factors such as the deadline time, resource availability, and communication delay. It makes a choice between local edge servers or the cloud. This paper introduces the ETSECC model, which stands for Efficient Task Execution and Secure Collaboration in Cloud. It is a model framework that enables efficient collaboration between local and cloud computing for faster task execution, system monitoring in real-time, and data security management for resource-constrained environments. The two-architecture system processes sensor, image, and system data through either cloud or edge pipelines depending on network conditions. Cloud processing refers to the scenario when data from Autonomous Underwater Vehicle (AUVs) is sent to a remote location, which results in longer response times, whereas edge processing diminishes delays and improves stability by processing locally. A Task Execution Model (TEM) smartly outlines how to allocate tasks between the cloud data centers and the edge servers by taking into account the factors of execution times and resource availability, in real-time, to meet the deadlines effectively. The simulation analysis comprises prediction accuracy, delay, remaining battery, task processing time, and average processing cost calculations.

title: SOPHY: Generating Simulation-Ready Objects with PHYsical Materials
authors: Junyi Cao, Evangelos Kalogerakis
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We present SOPHY, a generative model for 3D physics-aware shape synthesis. Unlike existing 3D generative models that focus solely on static geometry or 4D models that produce physics-agnostic animations, our method jointly synthesizes shape, texture, and material properties related to physics-grounded dynamics, making the generated objects ready for simulations and interactive, dynamic environments. To train our model, we introduce a dataset of 3D objects annotated with detailed physical material attributes, along with an efficient pipeline for material annotation. Our method enables applications such as text-driven generation of interactive, physics-aware 3D objects and single-image reconstruction of physically plausible shapes. Furthermore, our experiments show that jointly modeling shape and material properties enhances the realism and fidelity of the generated shapes, improving performance on both generative geometry and physical plausibility. Project page: https://xjay18.github.io/SOPHY.

title: Tactile Feature Changes are Detected Faster than Tactile Location Changes
authors: Lauren E. Horde, J. Michael Bertsch, Sara L. Riggs
abstract: Vibrotactile stimuli are increasingly being deployed in applied settings to convey information. Because novel tactile displays often depend on a user's ability to accurately detect when a change occurs, understanding what drives change detection performance is critical. This work compares the change detection performance of tactile stimuli defined by featural properties—i.e., decreases in intensity, pulse number, or pulse width/interpulse interval-to stimuli that change in spatial location, using a two-alternative forced choice paradigm. Results show that, across the board, changes in featural properties are detected more rapidly than changes in location. However, sensitivity and response bias values remain largely comparable for both featural tactile changes and location changes. This pattern may indicate a positive priming effect resulting from repeated tactile stimulation of the fingertips, or alternatively, the effects of temporal summation for featural conditions or a distributed attentional demand elicited by spatial stimuli. Overall, these findings reinforce prior work and demonstrate a significant reaction time advantage for featural tactile changes, offering meaningful insights for the design of future tactile displays that need to prioritize faster responses.

title: Legalese Explainer: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence to Simplify Legal Language and Improve Public Understanding
authors: Faseeha Nazli K R, Reena Roy R, Chandratapa Dewan, Anika Singhal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Legal contracts sometimes contain complex clauses that are hard to understand specially for non-legal users. They may hide many risks. These hidden risks can lead to confusion and potential legal consequences. This research presents a "Legalese Explainer", an intelligent system developed to identify and analyze risky clauses in legal documents and explains the clauses in a simplified manner. The system uses transformer-based models and natural language processing (NLP). The LEDGAR dataset, which is a large-scale legal corpus dataset of contract clauses, has been used to fine-tune pre-trained language models Legal-BERT and RoBERTa. This allows accurate classification and contextual understanding of complex legal text. Attention-based mechanisms are then used to extract important phrases showing contractual risk. These extracted phrases are then processed by using a generative model that rewrites the clauses in plain-language and assigns risk scores based on the possible legal and financial implications. The results are finally combined into a structured JSON report containing the clause-level annotations, phrase-level explanations, and the aggregate risk scores. Overall, the framework proposed represents a notable step towards developing smarter, clearer, and reliable AI-driven tools for document analysis.

title: ControlEvents: Controllable Synthesis of Event Camera Data with Foundational Prior from Image Diffusion Models
authors: Yixuan Hu, Yuxuan Xue, Simon Klenk, Daniel Cremers, Gerard Pons-Moll
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Event cameras have gained significant attention due to their bio-inspired properties, such as high temporal resolution and high dynamic range. However, obtaining large-scale labeled ground-truth data for event-based vision tasks remains challenging and costly, which hinders the development of the event-based algorithm. In this paper, we present ControlEvents, a diffusion-based generative model designed to synthesize unlimited high-quality event data guided by diverse control signals such as class text labels, 2D skeletons, and 3D body poses. Our key insight is to leverage the diffusion prior from foundation models, such as Stable Diffusion, enabling high-quality event data generation with minimal fine-tuning and limited labeled data. Our method streamlines the data generation process and significantly reduces the cost of producing labeled event datasets. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach by synthesizing event data for visual recognition, 2D skeleton estimation, and 3D body pose estimation. Our experiments show that the synthesized labeled event data enhances model performance in all tasks. Additionally, our approach can generate events based on unseen text labels during training, illustrating the powerful text-based generation capabilities inherited from foundation models.

title: A Hybrid and Optimal Daubechies-Stationary Wavelet Transform-Based Multi-Modal Medical Image Fusion: A Bio-Inspired Approach
authors: K. Udaya Kiran, P. V. Gopi Krishna Rao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Multimodal Medical Image Fusion (MMIF) has been employed for deriving the crucial information from distinctive modalities of medical images. Fusion of images such as Computer Tomography (CT) and Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) can maximize the image information as it also retains more information for effective diagnosing by medical professionals. In this research study, a MMIF is implemented by combining a Daubechies (db4) Wavelet Transform and Stationary Wavelet Transform (SWT) techniques, where the innovation is adopted with the optimization of decomposition levels in both techniques is conducted by Wombat Optimization Algorithm (WOA). This proposed model acquires high precise and accurate results with Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) and Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), comparing to traditional wavelet transform methods.

title: Procedure Learning via Regularized Gromov-Wasserstein Optimal Transport
authors: Syed Ahmed Mahmood, Ali Shah Ali, Umer Ahmed, Fawad Javed Fateh, M. Zeeshan Zia, Quoc-Huy Tran
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: We study self-supervised procedure learning, which discovers key steps and their order from a set of unlabeled videos. Previous methods typically learn frame-to-frame correspondences between videos before determining key steps and their order. However, their performance often suffers from order variations, background/redundant frames, and repeated actions. To overcome these challenges, we propose a self-supervised framework, which utilizes a fused Gromov-Wasserstein optimal transport with a structural prior for frame-to-frame mapping. However, optimizing only for the above temporal alignment may lead to degenerate solutions, where all frames are mapped to a small cluster in the embedding space and thus every video is assigned to just one key step. To address that issue, we integrate a contrastive regularization, which maps different frames to various points, avoiding trivial solutions. Finally, extensive experiments on egocentric and third-person benchmarks demonstrate our superior performance over prior works, including OPEL which relies on a classical Kantorovich optimal transport with an optimality prior.

title: Improving Head Detection and Counting Accuracy via Patch-Wise YOLOv8 Inference
authors: Saw Mya Nandar, Soe Mya Thu, Ah Nge Htwe
abstract: Accurate head detection and counting in crowded scenes remains a significant challenge in environments such as classrooms, lecture halls, and public gatherings. Object recognition models that are used today often have problems with absence, scale variation, and finding small objects, which makes counting less accurate. So, the system proposed a patch-wise YOLOv8 (You Only Look Once version 8) inference approach to improve head detection and counting accuracy. The YOLOv8 detector is applied to each patch, and patch-level predictions are merged using non-maximum suppression (NMS) and coordinate re-mapping to obtain the final detection results. The proposed system is evaluated on the SCUT-HEAD dataset and CCTV dataset. The experimental results demonstrate that the patchwise YOLOv8 inference system outperforms higher than the original YOLOv8 model and other methods achieving Average Precision (mAP) 91.4 %, recall 89.6 %, and counting accuracy 88.2%.

title: A Cost-Effective System Design and Monitoring for Analog and Digital Sensors with Key Exchange Algorithm for Smart City Applications
authors: N. Nagaraju, Ja’Far A. Aldiabat Al-Btoosh, T. Sreenivasa Murthy, Bhavani G, Aman Mittal, D. Siri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: Analog sensors are devices th sense a physical quantity, such as temperature or light, and produce an output level that varies in a linear fashion with voltage or current. In this paper, we present a Cost-Effective Structural Assessment and Digital Key Security (CSADKS) model that combines structural sensing, light Internet of Things (IoT) automation, and elliptic-curve cryptographic (ECC) security. The proposed method takes measurements of strain in a cantilever beam with a single strain gauge. Through the use of bending equations, these measurements of strain can be converted into equivalent values of force, deflection, and stress. The IoT system consists of the ESP32 and Raspberry Pi Pico W microcontrollers coupled with sensors that are connected via MQTT for real-time monitoring. Communication is handled via a Narada MQTT broker running on a smartphone that also performs data visualization in a very efficient manner. In order to have secure sessions, the ECC key exchange method that was originally designed for IoT devices which are low-resource is modified by means of efficiency optimizations so that it can work for secure sessions. The simulation parameters are figured as MAE of 0.33 to 0.28, RMSE of 0.39,0.36, transmit power of 1.5 W at (0,1), sensing power of 1.5 W to 3 W, and accumulated reward computations of 37, 000 and drops to 35, 000.

title: Electrothermal Simulation of AlGaN/GaN HEMT for Assessment of Electrical Response of the Device
authors: Mahua Raha Patra, Kalyan Biswas
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: Gallium Nitride (GaN) is a popular semiconductor having a large band gap and very high electron mobility. In recent times AlGaN/GaN “High Electron Mobility Transistor (HEMT)” is extensively used. In this paper, a HEMT design is proposed where heterostructure of AlGaN/GaN is used. Firstly AlGaN/GaN HEMT model is designed by using Silvaco TCAD. Then the self-heating effect of the device is explored. Effect of Lattice heating on different device parameters is engineered to study its performance parameters. Effect of lattice heating on drain current od the device with variation of drain voltage and gate voltage is observed. The effect of lattice heating on the transconductance of the device is also observed in this paper. This proposed work explained the influence of selfheating on cut off frequency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{f}_{\mathbf{T}})$</tex> and maximum oscillation frequency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{f}_{\max})$</tex>. This study and analysis will be helpful in designing new device structure for its application in Analog/RF applications.

title: Integrate Machine Learning to Industrial Automation: Sensor-Based Predictive Quality Control and Assembly Line Workflow Optimization
authors: T. S. Balaji, A. Rajalingam, Shazia Kausar, J Lalitha, S. Jayanthi, G. Karthikeyan
publish: 2026 1st International Electronics &amp; Packaging Technologies Conference: Bridging Skills &amp; Innovation for India’s Industry (EPT India)
abstract: The application of new digital technology in industrial automation has brought about a huge change. Industries have gone from doing things by hand and responding to things to adopting smart systems that can forecast what will happen. Machine learning is currently a very important tool for improving workflow and predicting quality on assembly lines. Standard industrial automation systems use rule-based systems that are great for jobs that happen again and over again, but they aren’t flexible enough for new scenarios. Using machine learning and sensor data, manufacturing lines might perform better, more correctly, and more quickly. This research examines the application of machine learning models in industrial automation, particularly for predictive quality control and workflow enhancement. Using data from sensors on machines, production equipment, and product inspections, machine learning algorithms seek for patterns, outliers, and possible problems in real time.
