Predictive quality control helps us detect poor things early on. This cuts down on the quantity of work that needs to be done again, the amount of waste, and the total yield. Machine learning models also improve workflows by looking at problems on the assembly line, how resources are used, and changes to how things are done. We can make changes in real time that speed up production and reduce down on downtime. The suggested system uses a mix of supervised and unsupervised machine learning techniques, such as classification, anomaly detection, and clustering, to get useful information from different types of industrial data streams. When connected to Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) networks, sensor data can be handled either at the edge or on centralized servers. This depends on how much processing power and latency need. Reinforcement learning methods are also used to plan how to put things together. This makes it possible to utilize adaptive optimization algorithms that speed up cycle times and make better use of resources. Tests have shown that this kind of automation is much superior for product quality, manufacturing efficiency, and cost savings than traditional automation. With predictive quality control based on machine learning, there are fewer mistakes, and dynamic workflow optimization makes the whole process more stable. The results reveal that adding machine learning to industrial automation makes it work better and is better for the environment since it uses less energy and makes less trash. This study demonstrates the significance of machine learning as a transformative element in industrial automation. By using advanced algorithms to combine data from sensors, companies can move closer to self-optimizing and predictive systems. This will lead to a new era of smart manufacturing. The results help industries be more competitive, more productive, and more adaptable to changes in their environments.

title: AI-Powered Learning Difficulty Prediction Using Federated Analytics for Early Academic Risk Detection in STEM Students
authors: Ayesha Ambreen, CH.N.V.Jyothirmai, Prasanta Chatterjee Biswas, Kathari Santosh, Chinmaya Dash, Ponni Valavan M
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: The purpose of this research is to develop an effective and privacy-preserving framework for early prediction of learning difficulties among STEM students. Early identification of academic challenges is essential for improving learning outcomes. Existing approaches rely on centralized data storage with limited temporal modeling, raising concerns about privacy, scalability, and interpretability. To address these issues, this study proposes FedSTAR, a Federated Spatio-Temporal Attention Risk Predictor that combines federated analytics with attention-based spatio-temporal learning. Student data are processed locally at institutional nodes, and a global model is trained collaboratively without sharing raw records. The system was implemented using Python and evaluated with real educational datasets. Experimental results achieved strong performance with Accuracy 0.86, Precision 0.84, Recall 0.82, F1-score 0.83, and AUC-ROC 0.91, outperforming logistic regression, random forest, and centralized LSTM models. The findings confirm that the framework enables privacy-aware, scalable, and proactive academic risk detection in practical learning environments, enabling intervention.

title: Study of Conductivity of Metal Thin Films Composed of Biogenic Silver Nanoparticles Prepared Using Bermuda Grass (Cynodon Dactylon) Extract
authors: Kaushik Roy, Surajit Basak, Avali Banerjee, Moumita Pal, Ranjana Ray
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Emerging Trends in Electronic Devices and Computational Techniques (EDCT)
abstract: This work presents an easy, eco-friendly and sustainable approach to synthesize silver nanoparticles using aqueous extract of Bermuda grass (Cynodon dactylon) and silver nitrate. The formation of nanoparticles was confirmed through ultraviolet-visible (UV-Vis) spectroscopy. Their crystalline nature and morphological features were examined using X-ray diffraction (XRD) and high resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM), while Fourier-transform infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy identified surface functional groups responsible for nanoparticle stabilization. Thin films fabricated from these green-synthesized nanoparticles were subjected to controlled DC bias to evaluate their electrical behavior. The results demonstrated that the films exhibited semiconducting-like characteristics at room temperature, indicating potential for applications in electronics and sensing devices.

title: Exploiting GAI Tools in Remote Online Logic Design Labs
authors: Mazen Alkhatib, Yanzhen Qu, Richard Cai, Ricardo Unglaub
publish: 2025 Conference on AI, Science, Engineering, and Technology (AIxSET)
abstract: This paper presents an enhanced remote online lab prototype that enables learners to design and experiment with digital circuits realizing minimized logic expressions. Building on our previous work with AND and OR logic gates, we introduce Generative Artificial Intelligence (GAI) powered tools to facilitate the minimization process. Learners are provided with a logic expression to minimize before constructing the corresponding circuit. To validate their work, we implement GAI-driven checks using state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs), including OpenAI ChatGPT3.5/4.0, and Meta AI/ Meta Llama 3.1-405B, trained to minimize logic expressions through iterative refinement. In a conducted study, learners built circuits for minimized logic expressions virtually and tested their operation in an on-campus lab using a webcam. Survey results showed that the application is user-friendly, allowing learners to complete the experiment within a reasonable time frame. Notably, the Meta AI tool demonstrated reliability and effectiveness in supporting the minimization of 4-variable logic expressions, where 100% of participating learners successfully completed the expression minimization experiment.

title: Subspace-Guided Knowledge Distillation for Efficient Model Transfer
authors: Zeeshan Hayder, Ali Cheraghian, Lars Petersson, Mehrtash Harandi
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Compact models can be effectively trained via Knowledge Distillation (KD), where a lightweight student model learns to replicate the behavior of a larger, high-performing teacher. A persistent challenge in KD lies in the misalignment between the representational spaces of teacher and student networks, especially when they differ in architecture or capacity. To address this, we propose Subspace-Driven Knowledge Distillation (SDMD), a novel framework that mitigates representational disparity by projecting features into an indefinite inner product space. This relaxation from traditional Hilbert spaces enables more flexible geometric alignment, capturing transformations such as rotations and reflections that are often necessary for accurate knowledge transfer. By learning a subspace that bridges the semantic gap between teacher and student, SDMD facilitates more effective distillation without increasing model complexity. We validate SDMD through extensive experiments on large-scale image classification (ImageNet-1K) and object detection (COCO), where it consistently outperforms existing distillation methods. Notably, SDMD-trained models not only achieve state-of-the-art results in distilled settings but also surpass the performance of equivalent models trained from scratch, highlighting the strength of our subspace-based alignment strategy.

title: Novel Approach for Design of Autonomous Multi-Robot Networks with Efficient Deployment and Obstacle Avoidance in Unknown Environments
authors: R Premalatha, A. Vanathi, Arun Kumar Selvam, Venkatesh Vakucherla, S.A Shifani, R Kamalakannan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electronic Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICESIC)
abstract: An Autonomous Multi-Robot (AMR) system is a network of robots that are connected through ad hoc networks and thus collaborate for different tasks such as exploration and search/rescue. A model named the Autonomous Multi-Robot Energy-efficient Deployment and Docking Optimisation (AMREDO) has been developed which combines several modules such as multi-robot network deployment, LiDAR pre-processing, hybrid docking control, and SNN-based navigation. Through a weighted utility function, the components of the model command navigation, network formation, and docking in complex environments, thereby increasing the efficiency of operations. They minimize the noise in LiDAR scans. The hybrid docking control method integrates visual perception and LiDAR alignment for staging docking phases based on a Finite State Machine (FSM) architecture. The visual PID controller helps the drone in its movements through detection and alignment, whereas the SNN-based Navigation module changes the sensor inputs into spiking neural signals for enhanced decision-making. The evaluation of the method is a precision & recall graph of 0.98, a precision of 1.00, a recall of $0.03, \mathrm{~F} 1$-score graph of 0.88.

title: Finite State Machines in the Context of Knowledge Based Transformations
authors: Johannes Schick, Marc Wagner, Wolfram-Manfred Lippe
abstract: In this paper we apply a novel approach for transforming models between textual and graphical representations using knowledge graphs within the sphere of meta-modeling and model transformations. We will use an example involving a finite state machine (FSM). FSMs are basic components in many modeling languages. Unlike traditional transformation methods, our technique uniquely addresses the limitations in transforming complex models between modalities, specifically textual and graphical representations. This approach has significant implications for the development of advanced AI-driven multi-modal transformation tools and knowledge graphs, providing a robust framework for future research and practical applications in various domains.

title: A PCA-Enhanced Pruned CNN-BiLSTM Framework for Automated Prostate Cancer Detection and Gleason Grading Using mp-MRI and Histopathology
authors: Mamata Beura, Partha Sarathi Pattnayak, Pradeep Kumar Mallick, Debahuti Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: Prostate cancer is one of the most common cancers that is afflicting men in the world today and therefore requires proper and prompt diagnostic methods. The paper introduces a new hybrid model that combines synergistically and using a principal component analysis (PCA) and state-of-the-art deep learning structures to automatically detect prostate cancer and grade it using multi-parametric magnetic resonance imaging (mpMRI) and histopathological data. The suggested architecture utilizes a two-way pathway of feature extraction that incorporates both PCA-based dimensionality reduction and convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with the help of transfer learning using the pre-trained models, such as ResNet-50 and EfficientNets. A feature fusion module combines complementary data though both pathways and then classifies it. For all benchmark datasets, ProstateX (n=1,400), TCGA-PRAD (n=499) and a multi-center clinical dataset (n=2,000), comprehensive experiments were done. The proposed hybrid framework is significantly better in performance with 96.8% accuracy, 95.2% sensitivity, 97.1% specificity, and area under the ROC curve (AUC) of 0.98. The work sets the current standard of the diagnosis of prostate cancer with AI assistance.

title: MMHOI: Modeling Complex 3D Multi-Human Multi-Object Interactions
authors: Kaen Kogashi, Anoop Cherian, Meng-Yu Jennifer Kuo
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Real-world scenes often feature multiple humans interacting with multiple objects in ways that are causal, goal-oriented, or cooperative. Yet existing 3D human-object interaction (HOI) benchmarks consider only a fraction of these complex interactions. To close this gap, we present MMHOI – a large-scale, Multi-human Multi-object Interaction dataset consisting of images from 12 everyday scenarios. MMHOI offers complete 3D shape and pose annotations for every person and object, along with labels for 78 action categories and 14 interaction-specific body parts, providing a comprehensive testbed for next-generation HOI research.Building on MMHOI, we present MMHOI-Net, an end-to-end transformer-based neural network for jointly estimating human–object 3D geometries, their interactions, and associated actions. A key innovation in our framework is a structured dual-patch representation for modeling objects and their interactions, combined with action recognition to enhance the interaction prediction. Experiments on MMHOI and the recently proposed CORE4D datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance in multi-HOI modeling, excelling in both accuracy and reconstruction quality. The MMHOI dataset is available at https://zenodo.org/records/17711786.

title: Privacy-Preserving Facial Emotion Recognition Using Federated Few-Shot Learning
authors: G. Jyothi, P. Nirmala
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Facial Emotion Recognition (FER) is an important sub-field of affective computing, and is used in medicine, education, human-computer interaction, and security. Although variability in expressions, limited labeled data, and privacy concerns were crucial to the existing FER systems, even though considerable advances have been made, the systems remain limited. To overcome these issues, this study designs a Federated Few-Shot Ensemble Learning (FFSL) system that combines three complementary methods namely (i) Ensemble Learning, which ensures high robustness and accuracy through a combination of different base classifiers including (ii) Support Vector Machines (SVM) and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and (iii) Federated Learning (FL) which provides privacy-preserving and scalable learning through aggregation of model updates by distributed clients without explicit information sharing. Experimental studies on CK+ dataset show that the suggested framework works with better recognition accuracy where the ensemble+FSL module provides the highest confidence (90 percent) of target classes than individual models. The FFSL model therefore provides a privacy preserving, data saving and correct FER solution which can be generalized to a wide range of settings. This contribution supports the creation of ethically oriented, real time and scalable emotion recognition systems towards real world implementation.

title: DuPLUS: Dual-Prompt Vision-Language Framework for Universal Medical Image Segmentation and Prognosis
authors: Numan Saeed, Tausifa Jan Saleem, Fadillah Maani, Muhammad Ridzuan, Hu Wang, Mohammad Yaqub
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Deep learning for medical imaging is hampered by task-specific models that lack generalizability and prognostic capabilities, while existing ’universal’ approaches suffer from simplistic conditioning and poor medical semantic understanding. To address these limitations, we introduce DuPLUS, a deep learning framework for efficient multimodal medical image analysis. DuPLUS introduces a novel vision-language framework that leverages hierarchical semantic prompts for fine-grained control over the analysis task, a capability absent in prior universal models. To enable extensibility to other medical tasks, it includes a hierarchical, text-controlled architecture driven by a unique dual-prompt mechanism. For segmentation, DuPLUS is able to generalize across three imaging modalities, ten different anatomically various medical datasets, encompassing more than 30 organs and tumor types. It outperforms the state-of-the-art task-specific and universal models on 8 out of 10 datasets. We demonstrate extensibility of its text-controlled architecture by seamless integration of electronic health record (EHR) data for prognosis prediction, and on a head and neck cancer dataset, DuPLUS achieved a Concordance Index (CI) of 0.69. Parameter-efficient fine-tuning enables rapid adaptation to new tasks and modalities from varying centers, establishing DuPLUS as a versatile and clinically relevant solution for medical image analysis. The code for this work is made available at: Code

title: Generalizing Sports Feedback Generation by Watching Competitions and Reading Books: A Rock Climbing Case Study
authors: Arushi Rai, Adriana Kovashka
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: While there is rapid progress in video-LLMs with advanced reasoning capabilities, prior work shows that these models struggle on the challenging task of sports feedback generation and require expensive and difficult-to-collect finetuning feedback data for each sport. This limitation is evident from the poor generalization to sports unseen during finetuning. Furthermore, traditional text generation evaluation metrics (e.g., BLEU-4, METEOR, ROUGE-L, BERTScore), originally developed for machine translation and summarization, fail to capture the unique aspects of sports feedback quality. To address the first problem, using rock climbing as our case study, we propose using auxiliary freelyavailable web data from the target domain, such as competition videos and coaching manuals, in addition to existing sports feedback from a disjoint, source domain to improve sports feedback generation performance on the target domain. To improve evaluation, we propose two evaluation metrics: (1) specificity and (2) actionability. Together, our approach enables more meaningful and practical generation of sports feedback under limited annotations.

title: A Data-Driven Approach to Customer Churn Prediction in Banking: A Comparative Study of Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Sathyadevi G, Heltin Genitha C
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: Customer churn remains a major challenge for banks, Since customer retention typically incurs lower costs than customer acquisition, banking sectors prioritize retaining existing clients. Accurate prediction of churn enables financial institutions to design focused retention programs and reduce revenue leakage. Predicting potential churners allows banking sectors to take proactive measures and enhance customer loyalty. This study develops a comprehensive churn prediction framework using datasets [2] derived from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) repositories. A unified preprocessing and evaluation pipeline was employed here to implement and compare the different machine learning models: Logistic Regression classifiers, Random Forest classifiers, XGBoost and CatBoost. The dataset undergoes systematic preprocessing that includes imputation of missing values, normalization of numeric variables and encoding of categorical attributes. A time-based train validation test division is adopted to ensure temporal consistency and prevent data leakage. Each model is fine-tuned using optimal hyperparameters and evaluated through comprehensive performance metrics. Experimental findings indicate that the Random Forest model achieved enhanced performance with respect to ROC curve and test accuracy, demonstrating strong generalization and robustness. CatBoost and XGBoost also achieve high predictive efficiency, while Logistic Regression remains valuable for its transparency and interpretability. Feature-importance and SHAP-based analyses further identify the most influential factors driving customer churn.

title: Safe Zone Estimation For Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Sreenivasa Chakravarthi Sangapu, Rudraraju Vignesh Varma, Pulusu Venkata Naga Varshith
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Computing and Intelligent Technologies (ICoECIT)
abstract: With the rise of autonomous vehicles in our transport systems, the development of intelligent systems for automated collision avoidance and advanced navigation in complex traffic situations is vital. This paper presents the implementation of a Safe Zone Estimation System, assessing via real-time traffic systems and estimations of the safe operating area for autonomous vehicles. Real-time traffic simulator SUMO, and Tkinter for visualizing safe zones, as well as, visual interface for hazardous traffic situations are integrated. A safe distance is calculated using a distance calculation, vehicle speed, reaction time, and road friction are considered, while a kinematic set of equations verifies if the adjacent cars infringe the triangular safety zone using a geometric shape. A passage decision-aiding warning is produced if a boundary is breached. The proposed system increases the safety of the driver or control system by providing advanced situational awareness and can be integrated into the Safe Autonomous Vehicle Driving Safety Framework while retaining its functionality.

title: PSDiffusion: Harmonized Multi-Layer Image Generation via Layout and Appearance Alignment
authors: Dingbang Huang, Wenbo Li, Yifei Zhao, Xinyu Pan, Yanhong Zeng, Bo Dai
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Transparent image layer generation plays a significant role in digital art and design workflows. Existing methods typically decompose transparent layers from a single RGB image using a set of tools or generate multiple transparent layers sequentially. Despite some promising results, these methods often limit their ability to model global layout, physically plausible interactions, and visual effects such as shadows and reflections with high alpha quality due to limited shared global context among layers. To address this issue, we propose PSDiffusion, a unified diffusion framework that leverages image composition priors from pre-trained image diffusion model for simultaneous multi-layer text-to-image generation. Specifically, our method introduces a global layer interaction mechanism to generate layered images collaboratively, ensuring both individual layer quality and coherent spatial and visual relationships across layers. We include extensive experiments on benchmark datasets to demonstrate that PSDiffusion is able to outperform existing methods in generating multi-layer images with plausible structure and enhanced visual fidelity.

title: Countering Multi-modal Representation Collapse through Rank-targeted Fusion
authors: Seulgi Kim, Kiran Kokilepersaud, Mohit Prabhushankar, Ghassan AlRegib
publish: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
abstract: Multi-modal fusion methods often suffer from two types of representation collapse: feature collapse where individual dimensions lose their discriminative power (as measured by eigenspectra), and modality collapse where one dominant modality overwhelms the other. Applications like human action anticipation that require fusing multifarious sensor data are hindered by both feature and modality collapse. However, existing methods attempt to counter feature collapse and modality collapse separately. This is because there is no unifying framework that efficiently addresses feature and modality collapse in conjunction. In this paper, we posit the utility of effective rank as an informative measure that can be utilized to quantify and counter both the representation collapses. We propose Rank-enhancing Token Fuser, a theoretically grounded fusion framework that selectively blends less informative features from one modality with complementary features from another modality. We show that our method increases the effective rank of the fused representation. To address modality collapse, we evaluate modality combinations that mutually increase each others’ effective rank. We show that depth maintains representational balance when fused with RGB, avoiding modality collapse. We validate our method on action anticipation, where we present R3D, a depth-informed fusion framework. Extensive experiments on NTURGBD, UTKinect, and DARai demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms prior state-of-the-art methods by up to 3.74%. Our code is available at: https://github.com/olivesgatech/R3D.

title: Deep Latent Representation Learning for Pan-Cancer Classification with Explainable Boosting from High-Dimensional Gene Expression Data
authors: Diptadip Maiti, Madhuchhanda Basak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Emerging Systems and Intelligent Computing (ESIC)
abstract: High-dimensional gene expression data pose substantial challenges for predictive modeling due to noise, redundancy, and the curse of dimensionality. This work introduces a hybrid machine learning framework that integrates a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) for nonlinear dimensionality reduction with an Explainable Boosting Machine (EBM) for transparent multiclass cancer classification. The VAE compresses over 20,000 transcriptomic features into a 64-dimensional latent space while preserving biologically meaningful structure, yielding improved separability across tumor types. These latent representations are then classified using an EBM, enabling inherently interpretable predictions supported by global feature importance and SHAPbased local explanations. Experiments on the TCGA Pan-Cancer dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed pipeline, achieving 0.98 accuracy, 0.99 macro-precision, 0.97 macro-recall, and 0.98 macro-F1, with per-class F1-scores ranging from 0.95 to 1.00. The results highlight that the VAE-EBM framework provides both high predictive performance and clinical interpretability, offering a promising direction for precision oncology.

title: Energy-Aware RISC-V SoC with Integrated AI Accelerator for Edge Computing Applications
authors: Balamurugan. M, Mahmoud Odeh, Atul Kumar Singla, Preethi S, Ramya M, D. Siri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on AI Engineering and Innovations (AIEI)
abstract: Edge intelligence requires processing that is very efficient on-device, with the capability to run neural loads with very tight requirements in terms of energy and latency. A 16x16 quantized tensor accelerator and multi-bank on-chip memory subsystem is made energy-aware with an energy-efficient RISC-V System-on-Chip to meet these requirements. Some of the architecture features used are fused micro-operations, dataflow-based scheduling, workload-based DVFS, and quantized systolic computation. Based on experimental assessment, it has achieved significant improvements: 9.8 TOPS/W energy efficiency, 62% drop in off-chip memory access, 17.4% fall in micro-op energy, 94.7% PE utilization, 31% idle-power, and 11.3 ms edge workloads inference latency. These findings prove significant changes in the computational density, dataflow performance, and the responsiveness of the system. The proposed SoC provides a scalable platform to support advanced edge-AI applications with the need to execute high-performance, low-power, and real-time inferences.

title: Improved Ensemble Machine Learning Framework for Superior Smart Grid Stability Prediction
authors: Md. Shovon Rahman, Md. Tanjil Islam Aronno, Md. Ahsan Kabir, Shaikh Anowarul Fattah
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: As the population is increasing, energy regulatory bodies are facing difficulties in managing consumer demand alongside the production rate. Therefore, incorporation of more sources which are renewable in nature, along with intelligent generation-demand control, is required. Smart grid technology addresses this problem by the inclusion of advanced metering infrastructures (AMIs) as well as IoT devices. As the smart grid offers many functionalities, its stability is a major concern. Stability can be analyzed from different aspects, such as the security of the grid from various cyber-physical attacks or maintaining a balance between demand and prediction. Machine learning and artificial intelligence techniques are successfully implemented on smart grids to achieve perfection in customer demand prediction. In this paper, we propose a improved stacking ensemble model that combines multiple classifiers with optimized hyperparameters. This approach achieved an accuracy of around 99%, showing its effectiveness in ensuring smart grid stability. The experiments were performed on a publicly available dataset from the UC Irvine (UCI) machine learning repository. Robustness of the model is further demonstrated through the analysis of performance metrics, including F1-score, recall, precision, and accuracy, along with ROC-AUC analysis.

title: Collaborative Semiconductor Production Economic: Mobile Demand Fulfillment via Leakage Predictive Model and Device Technology Optimization
authors: Rami Salem, Songtao Wo, Ting Chen, Ahmed Mohamed, Huy Do, Anand Subramaniam, Tsung-Han Lee
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper addresses critical challenges in meeting mobile unit demand while minimizing production costs through an advanced thermal design power predictive model and concurrent device process optimization. The integrated approach increased mobile unit allocation from 15% to 70%, thereby making the mass production more cost effective while meeting customer specifications, demonstrating effective collaboration in semiconductor yield enhancement.

title: A Blockchain-Oriented Software Engineering Architecture for Carbon Credit Certification Systems
authors: Matteo Vaccargiu, Azmat Ullah, Pierluigi Gallo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: Carbon credit systems have emerged as a policy tool to incentivize emission reductions and support the transition to clean energy. Reliable carbon-credit certification depends on mechanisms that connect actual, measured renewable-energy production to verifiable emission-reduction records. Although blockchain and IoT technologies have been applied to emission monitoring and trading, existing work offers limited support for certification processes, particularly for small and medium-scale renewable installations. This paper introduces a blockchain-based carbon-credit certification architecture, demonstrated through a 100 kWp photovoltaic case study, that integrates real-time IoT data collection, edge-level aggregation, and secure on-chain storage on a permissioned blockchain with smart contracts. Unlike approaches focused on trading mechanisms, the proposed system aligns with European legislation and voluntary carbon-market standards, clarifying the practical requirements and constraints that apply to photovoltaic operators. The resulting architecture provides a structured pathway for generating verifiable carboncredit records and supporting third-party verification.

title: Multi-Instrument Investigation of Lightning-Induced Ionospheric Disturbances Over Bangladesh
authors: M.H. Rafi, M.G. Mostafa, M.N. Nahar
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study investigates the influence of both Cloud-to-Ground(CG) and Intra-Cloud (IC) lightning on ionospheric irregularities over Bangladesh during 2023. Lightning observations were obtained from the World Wide Lightning Location Network (WWLLN), Earth Network Total Lightning Network (ENTLN), and International Space Station Lightning Imaging Sensor (ISSLIS). Ionospheric parameters were derived from six Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) stations across Bangladesh. The geomagnetically quiet days of 2023 are calculated having a Kpindex <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<\mathbf{4}$</tex>, Dst-Index <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<-\mathbf{2 5}$</tex> nanotesla (nT), and also considering solar flares and earthquake events. The data from GNSS is processed through the GNSS TEC algorithm to derive the Vertical Total Electron Content (VTEC) data, which is used to compute the rate of the TEC index (ROTI). During the geomagnetically quiet days, the lightning was simultaneously detected by WWLLN, ENTLN, and ISS-LIS, and significant ionospheric disturbances were observed in ROTI values exceeding 0.15 TECU/min. Three representative case studies illustrate the typical response: On 6 March 2023, lightning peaks at 09:00 UT and 13:00 UT coincided with enhanced ROTI values reaching 0.6 TECU/min and VTEC around 120 TECU. On 3 June 2023, the highest lightning count of the year was recorded, with stroke peaks at 09:00 UT and 12:00 UT, during which ROTI reached TECU/min and VTEC rose to 80 TECU. On 1 September 2023, lightning peaked at 09:00 UT, with concurrent ionospheric disturbances where ROTI exceeded 0.55 TECU/min and VTEC was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 80 \text{TECU}$</tex>. These results confirm that lightning activity is strongly correlated with ionospheric irregularities and plays a significant role in perturbing ionospheric conditions.

title: From Blueprint to Genome: A Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis Framework for Ethical and Risk-Aware Decision-Making in Genetic Engineering
authors: Hagar M. Mohamed, Mahmoud Khalifa, Amina Toumi, Shama Musabah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The rapid advancement of genetic engineering, from recombinant DNA to CRISPR-Cas9, has consistently outpaced the development of robust regulatory and ethical frameworks. This lag creates a critical challenge for policymakers, engineers, and scientists who must navigate complex trade-offs between innovation, safety, equity, and public trust. Traditional decision-making processes, often reliant on expert committees alone, can lack transparency, struggle to incorporate diverse stakeholder values, and fail to quantitatively integrate ethical principles. To address this gap, this paper proposes a structured Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (MCDA) framework tailored for the evaluation of genetic engineering projects. Our framework operationalizes abstract ethical principles—Beneficence, Non-Maleficence, Justice, and Autonomy—into a actionable hierarchy of weighted criteria. We illustrate the application of this framework through a case study on a gene drive for malaria control, demonstrating how it can transparently evaluate alternatives and clarify trade-offs. By providing a systematic, transparent, and adaptable tool, this research aims to bridge the gap between ethical discourse and practical engineering governance, fostering responsible innovation in genetic technologies.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning for Litchi Leaf Analysis: Health Monitoring and Growth Stage Detection
authors: Bishwa Das, Md. Asiful Islam, Md Assaduzzaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Proper evaluation of the health and growth phases of the litchi leaves is crucial to proper management of the crop and prevention of diseases, and optimization of yield. The traditional practices are based on manual inspection, which is time-consuming and error-prone. We address these constraints by suggesting a CNN-ViT hybrid model, which integrates MobileNetV2 with an efficient local feature extractor and a Vision Transformer (ViT) as a global context extractor. The model was trained and tested on a selected set of leaf images of litchi with various health conditions and levels of development, achieving a peak accuracy of 99.74 % with high precision, recall, and F1-scores across all five classes. The hybrid architecture was shown to be better in generalization and robustness as compared to ResNet121, InceptionV3, and MobileNetV2. Ablation experiments also demonstrated that batch size 16 is optimal and provides the highest generalization. The results demonstrate the potential that the proposed framework has to automated monitoring of the leaves of the litchi tree and its relevance in the real-life context of precision agriculture systems.

title: From Prediction to Causation: An Interpretable Diagnosis of Procrastination Using XAI with ML
authors: Maria Afrin Khan, Anwar Hossain Efat, Mariam Sarker, Jubair Ahmed Nabin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Procrastination is a pervasive behavioral issue, particularly among students, leading to reduced academic performance and increased stress. Traditional analysis methods often fail to capture its complexity, necessitating advanced machine learning approaches. This study introduces a Feature-optimized Explainable Support Vector Machine (FoX-SVM) framework to predict and interpret procrastination behaviors. Using a dataset of 674 responses from diverse individuals, we applied extensive hyperparameter tuning and synthetic oversampling (SMOTE) to handle class imbalance. Our dual-layer explainability approach integrates seven Explainable AI techniques—including SHAP, LIME, Anchors, and Integrated Gradients—to identify key features and enhance model interpretability. Experimental results demonstrate that FoX-SVM achieves superior performance (97.18% accuracy after feature elimination) compared to six other classifiers. The study highlights critical procrastination indicators, such as task delay initiation, and provides actionable insights for targeted interventions, contributing to both ML and educational psychology fields.

title: LoRA-Adapted Vision Transformers for Efficient and Explainable Colorectal Cancer Classification
authors: Nafisa Binte Ghulam Kibria, Hafsa Binte Kibria, Md. Nahiduzzaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Colorectal cancer is one of the leading causes of cancer-related deaths, where an early and accurate diagnosis is vital for effective treatment. Conventional histopathological analysis is manual, time-consuming, and subject to inter-observer variability. That is why automated diagnostic tools come in handy. Deep learning has shown great potential to address these challenges by enabling fast and reproducible image-based classification. In this study, Vision Transformers (ViTs) are used for the classification of colorectal cancer tissue using the NCT-CRC-HE100 K dataset. A baseline ViT demonstrates strong performance but requires high computational resources. To enhance efficiency, Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method, is introduced. By updating only 3.25% of the total parameters, LoRA improves classification performance, achieving 0.9949 accuracy and a 0.9949 weighted F1-score. Gradientweighted Class Activation Mapping (GradCAM) is integrated to provide visual explanations of predictions, supporting clinical interpretability. External validation on the EBHI-Seg dataset further demonstrates generalizability, with over 90% accuracy and F1-score across six tissue classes. Overall, the LoRA-adapted ViT offers an accurate, efficient, and interpretable solution for colorectal cancer diagnosis.

title: CNN-RSA Federated Learning Framework: Secure Deployment for Robotic Engineering Applications
authors: Rajvir Saini, Ravinder Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Secure and collaborative deployment in robotics requires advanced mechanisms that ensure both efficient In addition to a robust sense of safety. Deep learning and cryptography are two technologies that, when combined, can result in decreased system vulnerabilities and increased system resilience. Existing systems in federated robotics have a number of drawbacks, such as the fact that they are susceptible to assaults from adversaries, that they have a high communication overhead, and that they do not have a robust encryption system for model updates. Within the scope of this investigation, a Secure Federated Deployment Framework (SFDF) is suggested. RSA cryptography is utilized in this framework to ensure that model transfer and aggregation are carried out in a secure manner. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are incorporated into this framework to facilitate effective feature learning. The framework was developed with the intention of addressing the difficulties that were highlighted earlier and aiming to provide satisfactory answers for those challenges. The approach that has been proposed guarantees that federated training in robotic systems will continue to be resistant to tampering, scalable, and capable of protecting user privacy. The findings of the experiments reveal that SFDF significantly improves accuracy, reduces the chance of data leakage, and strengthens the overall security of the system when applied to situations that involve remote robotic settings.

title: Ray-Tracing Simulation and Calibration for Indoor Hall Scenarios at 330–500 GHz
authors: Ben Chen, Ke Guan, Danping He, Muhammad Qamar, Akram Alomainy, Lawrence Carslake, Tian Hong Loh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: Terahertz (THz) communication systems are considered a key technology for achieving ultra-high data rates and low-latency wireless links. To support their deployment, the reliable characterization of THz propagation in realistic indoor environments is required. This paper presents calibrated ray-tracing (RT) simulations of an indoor hall scenario at 330–500 GHz using the CloudRT simulation platform. Material parameters are optimized through calibration against measured data, resulting in close agreement between simulated and experimental power delay profiles. The calibrated RT can accurately reproduce line-of-sight and first-order reflected components, demonstrating its capability to predict multipath behavior in sparse THz channels. The results provide insights into indoor THz propagation characteristics and establish a foundation for developing precise and standardized indoor THz channel models in future research.

title: GhostNet-Based Peach Tree Disease Detection with Class-Aware Augmentation
authors: Kalikiri Niranjan, Seeniappan Kaliappan, R. Priyadharshini, Ritika Joshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper evaluates the performance of GhostNet, a lightweight CNN, for four-class peach health prediction using a curated dataset of 4,307 images (Healthy: 3,569; Anarsia lineatella: 370; Grapholita molesta: 304; Dead Trees: 64). Images were rescaled to 224x224 px and normalized using ImageNet statistics. A stratified random split of 70/15/15 was used; augmentation (which was class-aware, and limited to minority classes) and a balanced sampler (targeting B = 360 samples per class per epoch) were applied (using RandomResizedCrop, color jitter, RandomPerspective, CLAHE, MixUp, and CutMix). GhostNet (ImageNet-pretrained) was end-to-end fine-tuned using AdamW and cosine-annealed learning rates. On the held-out test set (n = 644), a top-1 accuracy of 92%, and macro precision/recall/F1 of 0.70/0.90/0.76 was obtained. Recall is good for Anarsia lineatella and Grapholita molesta, and there is residual loss in precision only for the rare class of Dead-Trees. Results suggest that GhostNet provides an effective accuracy vs. efficiency trade-off for field-deployable peach disease monitoring.

title: Securing TLS in the Quantum Era: A Robust Hybrid Protocol with Kyber, X25519, and Dilithium
authors: Abu Sayem, Tahmid Enam Shrestha, Farzana Rahman, Md Asif Khan, Md. Asraf Ali
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rise of quantum computers threatens the security of current TLS protocols, which rely on elliptic curve and RSA-based primitives that quantum algorithms like Shor's can break. This creates a “store now, decrypt later” risk, where attackers may collect encrypted data today to decrypt it in the future once quantum capabilities advance. This study introduces a novel hybrid TLS protocol that seamlessly integrates classical (ECDSA and X25519) and post-quantum (CRYSTALS-Dilithium5 and CRYSTALS-Kyber1024) primitives. The proposed method generates a composite X. 509 certificate dual-signed with ECDSA and Dilithium5. It conducts a key exchange that concatenates an X25519 Diffie-Hellman shared secret with a Kyber KEM shared secret, followed by HKDF-SHA256 key derivation. The entire protocol is implemented in Go, leveraging the OQS liboqs-go library alongside Go's standard crypto packages. Experimental validation via a proof-of-concept demonstrates successful hybrid certificate validation and secure session establishment with both classical and post-quantum peers. These results confirm that hybrid cryptographic integration can provide quantum-resilient security while preserving compatibility with existing TLS deployments.

title: Ground-Based Beam Position Estimation Methods for Low Earth Orbit Satellite
authors: Xin Jiang, Lei Sun, Wei Deng
abstract: Beam position estimation is crucial for the coverage and service quality of low-orbit satellites. Traditional beam position estimation is applied in the radar field and are specifically for estimating the spatial beam shape. However, low-orbit satellites focus on estimating the beam shape on the terrestrial surface. This paper proposes a method to estimate the shape and area of beam projected on to the terrestrial surface. Based on the principle of beam consistency in sine space and leveraging the straight-line propagation characteristics of electromagnetic waves, this method combines the half-power ellipse equation with the spherical equation of the Earth to solve for the coordinates of the edge curve of the projected beam footprint, thereby enabling parametric estimation of the terrestrial surface beam coverage. This approach precisely describes the projected shape and area of satellite beam, which offers new insights for addressing challenges in beam position planning, beam management, and optimization of satellite networks.

title: SBEX: Noise-Robust Speech Bandwidth Expansion Through Mamba-Inspired Residual Time-Frequency Learning
authors: Sayeda Rahman Ananna, Taieba Athay, Nursadul Mamun
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Robust speech super-resolution aims to reconstruct high-frequency cues from low-bandwidth speech while preserving intelligibility in real-world acoustic conditions. Although state-of-the-art methods perform well in clean scenarios, they often fail to recover high-frequency components under noisy environments. To address this challenge, SBEX is proposed as a noise-robust framework for speech super-resolution. Unlike conventional approaches that degrade under acoustic distortions, SBEX leverages a state-space-inspired core to capture both fine spectral details and long-range temporal dependencies. In addition, a noise-aware encoder is integrated to ensure stable and accurate reconstruction of high-frequency harmonics. Experimental results demonstrate that SBEX produces more natural and intelligible speech across diverse noisy conditions, highlighting its potential for deployment in telecommunications, speech recognition, and assistive hearing technologies.

title: MEMS Load Cell Based on the Nanomechanical Membrane-Flexure (NMF) Sensor for Sub-Micronewton Thrust Measurement
authors: Haneen Shameem, Amba Agnes. R. P, Seena. V
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper reports the developement of a highly sensitive MEMS load cell for measuring sub-micronewton level thrust from micro propulsion systems. The device is based on a novel Nanomechanical Membrane-Flexure (NMF) structure, which mechanically transduces the membrane deflection into an amplified uniaxial stress on the sensing flexures. To achieve high sensitivity, the electromechanical transduction is performed by integrating a high gauge factor (-430) Indium Tin Oxide (ITO) thin film as the piezoresistive sensing element. This approach simplifies the fabrication process by obviating the need for silicon doping. Finite element analysis (FEA) was performed on two scaled designs: (A) fabricated NMF version with 250 μm radius membrane for high-resolution point-force sensing and (B) 2.5 mm radius membrane optimized for capturing plumes from various micro-thrusters. Simulations for both point and boundary load conditions show significant stress concentration in the flexures, resulting in a promising high sensitivity suitable for characterizing next-generation micro-thrusters.

title: Language Models - Large and Small
authors: Gihan Dias
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)

title: Risk-Aware Hybrid CNN-LSTM for Stock Forecasting with Macro-Technical Integration
authors: Ravi Bandara, Maninda Edirisooriya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Stock markets exhibit non-linear dynamics influenced by both technical patterns and macroeconomic regimes. While Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks capture temporal dependencies effectively, they often neglect macroeconomic drivers that account for 15 to 20 percent of price variance. This paper proposes a hybrid CNN-LSTM framework integrating technical indicators with Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) macroeconomic variables for short-horizon S&P 500 price forecasting. A Principal Component Analysis (PCA) preprocessing step orthogonalizes 45 engineered features into 14 principal components (95 % variance retention), mitigating multicollinearity. Bayesian hyperparameter optimization tunes a dual-output architecture that simultaneously optimizes price regression (primary task) and direction classification (auxiliary regularization task). Evaluated on 10 years of daily data (20152024) via walk-forward validation, the model achieves Rsquared <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.86$</tex>, Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) = 3.98%, and 18% Mean Absolute Error (MAE) reduction versus LSTM baselines. Direction prediction accuracy of 45.44 % reflects inherent unpredictability of short-term movements. Risk-aware outputs including confidence scores and volatility bands support practical decision-making. Results validate that systematic macroeconomic integration substantially enhances forecasting accuracy beyond technical indicators alone.

title: Detecting Depression in Social Media Posts Using SBERT-CNN: A Transformer-Based Semantic Neural Network Approach
authors: Shaikh Ibna Sazzad, Sumaiya Rahman, Shyla Afroge
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Depression, a silent and pervasive mental health crisis, continues to be one of the foremost causes of emotional suffering, disability, and loss of life across the world. In today's digital era, individuals often disclose their emotional struggles through social media, turning platforms like Reddit into unintended yet valuable sources for early psychological insight. Leveraging this phenomenon, our study proposes a novel deep learning architecture, SBERT-CNN, that fuses the semantic encoding strength of Sentence-BERT with the hierarchical feature extraction of Convolutional Neural Networks. In contrast to prior studies that utilize lighter SBERT models such as all-MiniLM-L6-v2, we employ the more semantically expressive all-mpnet-base-v2 model, which significantly enhances the contextual understanding of user-generated posts. Utilizing a balanced dataset collected from Reddit, the proposed model obtains an accuracy of 94.5%, demonstrating its strong capability in capturing intricate linguistic indicators associated with depressive behavior. This study presents a scalable framework for real-time mental health screening through natural language cues in online environments, while also contributing significant advancements to the current leading approaches in depression detection.

title: Transformer-Based Manipulative Span Identification with SHAP Explainability in Multilingual Telegram Data
authors: Arupa Barua, Ifath Jeba Chowdhury, Ashim Dey
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The proliferation of manipulative content on social media poses significant challenges to information integrity, particularly in sensitive geopolitical contexts. During the Rus-sia-Ukraine war, such narratives have the potential to distort public perception and shape international opinion. To address this issue, a shared task UNLP 2025 was formulated to detect such manipulative spans in Ukrainian and Russian 9,500 Telegram posts. This study leverages the dataset provided by the task organizers and explores a range of Machine Learning (ML), Deep Learning (DL) and Transformer-based approaches to investigate the detection of manipulative spans in text. SHapley Additive ex-Planations (SHAP) are further employed to interpret token-level contributions towards manipulativeness, providing transparency into model predictions. Our experimental results reveal that the RemBERT transformer model achieves the highest F1 score of 0.5841, outperforming other architectures. These findings also underline the potential and current limitations of computational approaches for detecting manipulative spans in multilingual and geopolitically sensitive discourse.

title: A Lightweight Federated–Ensemble Deep Learning Approach for Early Detection of Alzheimer's Disease Using MRI Imaging
authors: Sagufta Sabah Nakshi, Wahia Tasnim, Rabea Khatun
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The inevitable neurological disorder known as Alzheimer's disease (AD) demands an early and precise diagnosis to enhance patient outcomes and facilitate prompt interventions. Though centralized training raises questions about data privacy, heterogeneity, and availability across institutions, recent developments in deep learning (DL) have shown powerful abilities in extracting prejudicial features from clinical, neuroimaging, and genetic data. This study proposes a federated learning (FL)-based framework that incorporates deep learning models for early detection of Alzheimer's disease while maintaining patient privacy. The framework ensures adherence to data governance as well as ethical standards by facilitating cooperative model training across dispersed datasets without involving the sharing of raw data. Our proposed lightweight hybrid model is generated by combining transfer learning and feature fusion techniques, and the training of the MRI image datasets has been done in dual stages, along with fine-tuning. The hybrid model combining CNN and MobileNet achieves an accuracy of 99.98% on the training dataset, 99.96% on the validation dataset, and 99.92% on the test dataset. After integrating with FL, the proposed system has achieved 99.93% accuracy on the test dataset, which outperforms all the recent studies and also established a scalable, clinically applicable, and privacy-preserving solution by combining deep learning along with federated learning.

title: Deep Learning Method for Schizophrenia Detection from Hilbert-Huang Transformed EEG Signals
authors: F. M. Mahmudul Haque, M. Morshed, M. S. Rana, M. A. Quaium, M. J. Rahimi, S. A. Fattah
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Schizophrenia is a debilitating neuropsychiatric disorder that profoundly affects cognition, perception, and executive function. Traditional clinical diagnosis primarily relies on subjective symptom evaluation, underscoring the urgent need for objective neurophysiological biomarkers. To detect schizophrenia, a Hilbert-Huang transform (HHT)-based deep learning framework has been addressed in this research work that investigates the non-stationary nature and transient nonlinear dynamics of electroencephalogram (EEG) signals. The proposed approach employs an empirical mode decomposition (EMD) to adaptively decompose EEG segments into intrinsic mode functions (IMFs) and applies HHT to extract instantaneous amplitude and frequency components. These components are then aggregated into Hilbert-Huang scalograms, representing a finegrained time-frequency matrix. Next, a 2D convolutional neural network (CNN) is developed in PyTorch to extract discriminative spectral-spatial representations from the scalograms. Moreover a channel and spatial attention module is provided to enhance the spatial-spectral features to improve the overall performance. A publicly available EEG dataset is employed to evaluate the proposed method, achieving a classification accuracy of 86.90%, thereby demonstrating its robustness and clinical applicability in schizophrenia detection.

title: Reliability Enhancement of 40nm RRAM Chip by Bottom Electrode Engineering
authors: Kaimeng Liu, Chengxiang Ma, Wei Chang, Jinyi Liu, Ruofei Hu, Didi Zhu, Yue Xi, Dong Wu, Bin Gao, He Qian, Jianshi Tang, Huaqiang Wu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This work proposes an electrode engineering optimization strategy to enhance the comprehensive performance and reliability of resistive random-access memory (RRAM) chip on a commercial 40nm CMOS platform. By adopting a post-deposition sequential treatment (PDST) with plasma-induced surface activation and thermal oxidation on the TiN bottom electrode (BE), the 40nm RRAM array can achieve a high endurance exceeding 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> cycles, long retention over 10 years at 85℃, and excellent uniformity in the initial state within the 16kb array. The physical mechanism underlying the impact of TiN BE on the reliability in terms of TiN layer thickness and interface treatment is clarified through comprehensive electrical tests and material characterizations. These findings provide key insights into the reliability improvement of RRAM and could facilitate the commercialization of RRAM technology.

title: Salary Prediction in Thailand’s IT Sector Using Supervised Learning Models
authors: Arocha Saengwirojanapat, Jirachaya Nongbualang, Natthida Theepakornsukkasem, Nuntanut Bhooanusas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Salary determination remains a growing concern in global job markets, as many job seekers lack accurate selfevaluation skills and often overestimate their qualifications. Consequently, salary recommendation systems have been increasingly introduced across various platforms. However, most existing models rely on candidate-centric data, which rarely reflect employers’ actual requirements. This paper proposes a salary prediction framework for Thailand’s IT industry using employeroriented job postings collected from real-world portals. As ITrelated positions in Thailand continue to expand due to digital transformation, our study also provides data-driven insights into emerging job trends. A key challenge in data collection is the rarity of explicit salary offers. To address this, reference salaries from the ISM-Tech Salary Report 2024 were adopted, and controlled noise was introduced via an exponential distribution to improve model generalization. The proposed method achieved promising performance, with a median percentage error of 16% overall and only 8% for software development roles using the default XGBoost model.

title: A Tissue-Integrated Rectenna for Efficient and Safe Wireless Power Harvesting
authors: Raisul Ismail Apon, S.K. Tanchin Rahman, Md Azad Hossain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The integration of radio frequency (RF)-based wireless power transfer (WPT) systems into implantable medical devices (IMDs) represents a transformative step toward realizing fully autonomous, battery-free biomedical implants. This study presents the design, simulation, and optimization of a miniaturized implantable rectenna operating at the 2.4 GHz ISM band, aimed at addressing the critical challenges of power delivery, biocompatibility, and electromagnetic safety. A multi-layered human tissue phantom model incorporating realistic dielectric properties is developed to assess in-situ antenna performance and ensure compliance with specific absorption rate (SAR) regulations. The antenna is geometrically optimized for low return loss, minimal volume, and efficient power harvesting under physiological constraints. Concurrently, the rectifier circuit is designed using harmonic balance analysis in ADS, with various diode models evaluated for maximizing RF-to-DC conversion efficiency. The proposed system achieves a peak efficiency of 88.7%, with SAR values maintained well below regulatory limits across multiple implant positions. These findings underscore the potential of the proposed rectenna architecture to enable safe and efficient wireless powering of next-generation IMDs, thereby advancing the frontier of biomedical telemetry and implantable electronics.

title: Real-Time Student Attention Monitoring in Classrooms Using Computer Vision
authors: Md. Abdul Moeid, Md. Mokammel Haque
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Monitoring student attention in classrooms is vital for improving learning outcomes, yet traditional observation methods are often subjective and impractical for large classes. This paper presents a real-time student attention monitoring system that leverages computer vision techniques and existing CCTV infrastructure to provide an objective, scalable, and costeffective solution. The system integrates dlib and Mediapipe libraries to detect and analyze key visual cues-head pose, eye blinks, sleep indicators, and mouth movements-captured from multiple classroom camera feeds. A region-based segmentation approach ensures comprehensive coverage and reduces occlusion issues. Experimental evaluation demonstrated high detection accuracy for head position (99.86%), mouth movement (99.86%), and sleep detection (up to 99.86% at 100 cm), with slightly reduced accuracy at longer distances. A real-classroom trial with six participants confirmed the system's ability to differentiate attentiveness levels and detect disengagement patterns. The proposed framework offers potential applications in personalized learning, early intervention for at-risk students, and enhanced teacher-student interaction, while adhering to privacy and ethical considerations.

title: An Inverse-Time Hybrid Characteristic Design for Communicationless Overcurrent Relay Coordination in Microgrids
authors: Mehran Abdi, Reza Mohammadi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: Microgrids present considerable challenges for conventional protection systems due to the significant variations in fault current levels between grid-connected and islanded modes. To overcome this issue, this paper introduces an innovative inverse-time characteristic that leverages both current and voltage signals. The core of this characteristic lies in the integration of an exponential term, which depends on the fault current, and a logarithmic term, which depends on the voltage. This combination significantly enhances the relay's sensitivity to various fault conditions. It allows for faster and more accurate fault detection without relying on costly communication infrastructure or the need for dynamic setting updates. The optimal parameters for this characteristic are determined using an efficient hybrid approach combining Genetic Algorithm and Linear Programming. This characteristic effectively achieves two opposing objectives: minimizing relay operating time while maintaining full coordination between all primary and backup relays, regardless of the microgrids operating mode (grid-connected or islanded). Simulations conducted on a real 18-bus microgrid show that this method significantly reduces the total relay operating time when compared to the standard IEC characteristic, while ensuring complete coordination between primary and backup relays across all operational scenarios.

title: Predicting High Fatigue Risk in Workers Using Machine Learning: A Comparative Study of Logistic Regression and Random Forest
authors: MD. Zubaer Rahman Sajib
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Worker fatigue is a major contributor to workplace incidents and reduced productivity. This study develops and evaluates machine learning models to predict high fatigue risk using a compact set of work- and lifestyle-related features. A synthetic dataset of 3,000 samples was generated to simulate realistic distributions of work hours, sleep hours, task load, break frequency, caffeine intake, and stress level. We compare Logistic Regression (LR) and Random Forest (RF) trained on both full and minimal feature sets; hyperparameter tuning was applied using GridSearchCV with F1 as the optimization metric. On the minimal feature set, the tuned Logistic Regression achieved an accuracy of 0.895, a cross-validated F1 of 0.739, and ROC-AUC 0.95; the tuned Random Forest achieved an accuracy of 0.878, a cross-validated F1 of 0.738, and ROC-AUC 0.94. Baseline ROCAUC values for the untuned models were 0.95 (LR) and 0.93 (RF). Feature-importance analysis indicates that sleep_hours, stress_level, and work_hours are the most predictive features. Results show that minimal-feature models, when tuned appropriately, give strong predictive performance with better interpretability—suitable for practical fatigue-risk monitoring in industrial settings.

title: FedProx-IDS: A Stable and Scalable Federated Learning Framework for Intrusion Detection
authors: Farhan Abid Ahmed, Aurprita Mahmood, Meherin Sultana, Shamim Akhter
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) are vital for protecting digital infrastructures. However, traditional centralized approaches struggle with data privacy, scalability, and communication overhead, particularly when faced with nonindependent and identically distributed (non-IID) data across networks, which is very common in this domain. Federated Learning (FL) offers a decentralized alternative, enabling collaborative model training without sharing raw data. Yet, existing research remains largely theoretical, lacking practical implementation. Our method involves carefully preparing the data with a privacy-preserving, schema-aligned categorical encoding process, employing a structured data preprocessing pipeline, and utilizing a feed-forward neural network designed for distributed learning. We use FedProx to train local models, which helps to minimize the negative impact of differing data distributions across the network, including an intentionally extreme non-IID configuration used as a stress-test scenario, and addressing these data heterogeneity challenges. Furthermore, we use WebSockets for efficient and reliable model sharing, ensuring smooth communication. The global model achieves exceptional performance, with 96.12% accuracy, 95.28% precision, 94.90% recall, and a 95.09% weighted F1-score, validating the framework's effectiveness. The stability of the training process is further demonstrated through convergence analysis, and a hyperparameter study validates the choice of the FedProx regularization term. By transitioning from theory to practice, this work demonstrates the feasibility of deploying FL-based IDS in real-world environments, offering a scalable, privacy-preserving solution for intrusion detection.

title: Investigation of Post-Programming Characteristics for Charge-Trap Transistors in Standard 28nm CMOS
authors: Ye Lin, Jun Ma, Xiaoyong Song, Anying Jiang, Yuan Du, Li Du
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this paper, the Charge-trap transistor (CTT) family is extended to include Ultra-Low Voltage Threshold (ULVT), Standard Voltage Threshold (SVT), and High Voltage Threshold (HVT) transistors in standard 28nm bulk-Si CMOS technology. Measurements across 45 transistor types and channel widths (W) under various bias stresses indicate that W and transistor type have a minimal effect on the maximum threshold voltage shift (∆V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th,max</inf>). After the programming (PRG) operation, CTTs exhibit reduced low-frequency noise (LFN) possibly due to charge filling of interface traps, stable on-resistance (Ron) under identical V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">GS</inf> − V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf> bias conditions, and enhanced anti-aging characteristics. These results highlight the potential of the CTT family as fundamental components for nonvolatile storage and analog circuits.

title: Senseoftense: A Machine Learning Framework for Identifying Tense Errors in Bangla
authors: Pavel Ferdous, K. M. Azharul Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Tense error detection in Bangla language is a significant task in Natural Language Processing (NLP) that aims to identify whether the tense used in a sentence is appropriate and grammatically correct. Bangla language has a rich and complex verb morphology that changes based on tense, aspect, person, number, and honorifics, making tense error detection challenging and crucial for many applications such as grammar correction, automated essay writing, and intelligent tutoring systems. In this paper, we propose a machine learning-based framework to detect tense errors for Bangla. We create a tense dataset that contains 20,030 correct Bangla sentences having possible tense forms of Bangla text labeled according to their tense, that is, past, present, and future. The dataset is created considering the syntactic variability, morphological richness, and ambiguity that may occur. The sequence-aware LSTM autoencoder and a Dense autoencoder are used to train the dataset for tense error detection. The LSTM model outperforms the Dense model and shows the benefits of sequence modeling. This work provides a useful resource and a baseline for detecting tense errors, especially for error detection in Bangla grammar.

title: Digital Transformation in GCC Higher Education: A Decision Aid Framework for Institutional Success
authors: Abdulla Alkhaja, Colin Turner, Laura Galloway, Mohamed Salama
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper presents the Successful Digital Transformation Framework for Higher Education (SDTF-HE), a conceptual and empirically grounded model designed to guide higher education institutions (HEIs) across the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) in achieving effective digital transformation (DT). The study addresses persistent fragmentation in DT initiatives and the absence of coherent institutional strategies, governance mechanisms, and adoption pathways. Drawing on multiple case studies from seven HEIs across Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Oman, and the United Arab Emirates, the research integrates organizational change, technology adoption, and resource-based perspectives. Findings identify six key capability enablers: DT Strategy, Governance and Leadership, Communication and Culture, Training and Development, DT-Specific Investments, and Ecosystem Partnerships, that influence DT outcomes through the mediating mechanism of Digital Adoption. The research demonstrates how these enablers collectively enhance academic quality, operational efficiency, and decision transparency. It provides actionable guidance for HEI leaders seeking to progress from isolated digital projects toward comprehensive, strategically aligned transformation initiatives.

title: Slack-Jawed and Breathless
authors: Shawn Powers
publish: 2025 Artificial Intelligence x Humanities, Education, and Art (AIxHEART)
abstract: In this invited position paper, the author explores the interrelationship between aesthetic experience, consciousness, and intelligence in the age of artificial intelligence (AI). Grounded in philosophical traditions as well as personal observation, the author argues that intense aesthetic experiences provoke cognitive engagement and embodied awareness, serving as a foundation for human intelligence. It is noted that language doesn't serve how we think about AI systems creating a default to anthropomorphizing algorithms. The author asserts that human intelligence is not congruent with AI as it is rooted instead in lived experience, cultural contexts, and the capacity for wonder.

title: Process Voltage Temperature Performance Comparison of CMOS and GDI Based Arithmetic Circuits in 45 nm and 180 nm Technologies
authors: Sadia Tasnim Shara, Hamim Ibrahim, Shahriyar Masud Rizvi, Md. Mortuza Ahmmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Nowadays, maintaining dependable and energy efficient digital performance is getting harder under Process, Voltage, and Temperature (PVT) variations as technology nodes get smaller and circuits get more crowded. Three popular arithmetic circuits a 2-to-1 multiplexer, a 2-bit comparator, and a full adder are designed with both CMOS and Gate Diffusion Input (GDI) logic styles across 45 nm and 180 nm nodes and they are compared in this paper. Cadence Virtuoso post layout simulations were performed under realistic PVT corners <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(-40^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> to 125° C), examining average power, area, transistor count, propagation delay, and leakage power. The findings demonstrated that 180 nm GDI circuits offered comparatively lower power consumption, leakage, and improved thermal stability which made them more appropriate for low power, reliable embedded systems, even though 45 nm CMOS circuits produced faster speeds. Without sacrificing functional reliability, these findings offer engineers useful design insights for energy conscious VLSI applications.

title: Green Transitions and Financial Development: A Study of Emerging Asian Economies
authors: Ala Fathi Assi, Hayder Abdel Moniem Omer
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study aims to identify and analyze the complex links between green transformation and financial growth in emerging Asian economies between 1995 and 2020. MMQR model were used to explore the role of green growth, sustainable energy, natural resources, and green growth in the financial development process. Based on the results, the use of sustainable energy, natural resources, and green growth appears to have negative effects on financial development in emerging Asian economies (β1 = -0.086, β3 = -0.010, β4 = -0.221). On the other hand, green technology has a positive effect on financial development (β2 = 0.000) because it recognizes the important role that environmentally sustainable economic processes play in achieving financial stability and progress. This finding highlights the challenges associated with the transition to greener methods in economies that rely on traditional energy- and resource-intensive industries.

title: Ensemble Deep Learning Framework for Accurate Prediction of Gestational Delivery Week Using Electronic Health Records
authors: Anika Bintee Aftab, Md. Ashab Mohiuddin, Subah Nawar, Tasnuva Binte Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate prediction of gestational delivery week is vital for prenatal care and clinical decision-making but remains challenging due to data imbalance, limited features, and reliance on invasive methods in conventional approaches. While numerous studies have focused on classification tasks—such as distinguishing preterm, term, and post-term births—regressionbased prediction of the exact delivery week has received little to no significant attention in the existing literature, and the few available attempts often yield suboptimal performance. To address this gap, this study proposes an ensemble learning framework that integrates deep learning models—including Feedforward Neural Networks (FNN), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Wide & Deep Learning—with traditional machine learning algorithms to predict gestational delivery week from non-invasive electronic health record (EHR) data. Dataset imbalance was handled using SMOTER and controlled synthetic oversampling, followed by polynomial feature transformation and Bayesian hyperparameter optimization. Individual deep learning models achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> scores of 0.445-0.15, while ensemble approaches consistently outperformed them. Notably, Ensemble Model 2 achieved an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.6454 with an RMSE of 1.6987 and MAE of 1.2764. These results demonstrate that combining diverse models with tailored preprocessing substantially improves prediction accuracy. The proposed framework is scalable, non-invasive, and particularly suitable for low- and middle-income settings, enabling personalized prenatal care and timely interventions for high-risk pregnancies.

title: An Efficient DRAM RowHammer Defense Circuit Based on a Novel In-Situ Monitoring Method
authors: Lingyun Hu, Yulong Tan, Yuansheng Zhao, Zixuan Shen, Yanqing Wu, Chao Wang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper proposes an efficient DRAM RowHammer defense circuit based on a novel in-situ monitoring method. A defense method based on dummy cell is proposed, which employs dummy cells with faster voltage decay to monitor storage-node voltage and trigger refreshes when they cross preset dual thresholds, effectively reducing redundant operations and bandwidth overhead. An in-situ monitoring circuit with adjustable threshold is designed, where a threshold adjustment circuit tunes the inverter’s equivalent switching voltage under PVT variations to enhance robustness. An 8 × 8 DRAM macro integrating the proposed monitoring circuit is implemented in a 28 nm CMOS @ 0.9 V, 200 MHz, achieving 83% bandwidth overhead reduction and 41% normalized area overhead reduction compared with state-of-the-art design.

title: Female Leadership in HR Decision Models for Employee Satisfaction in a Cement Company
